symptom: Disk export/import fails with error: dataAccessAuthorizationMode is not supported for this subscription/region when setting DataAccessAuthMode to AzureActiveDirectory
rootCause: AFEC (internal feature flag) is not enabled for the subscription or region
solution: Enable AFEC flag for the subscription/region. See https://aka.ms/afec
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/AAD Auth for Managed Disk Import Export_Announcement

symptom: Cannot change dataAccessAuthorizationMode on disk while there is an active SAS. Error: PropertyChangeNotAllowedWithActiveSAS
rootCause: An active SAS token exists on the disk (granted via Get-AzDiskAccess, az disk grant-access, or Portal Export)
solution: Call EndGetAccess on the disk or wait for the SAS to expire before retrying the operation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/AAD Auth for Managed Disk Import Export_Announcement

symptom: Azure VM deployed in Extended Zone has no internet outbound connectivity - all outbound internet connections fail
rootCause: VMs in Azure Extended Zones do not have default outbound access unlike standard Azure regions
solution: Configure explicit outbound connectivity methods (e.g., NAT Gateway, Load Balancer with outbound rules, explicit public IP) for VMs in Extended Zones
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Azure Extended Zones_Announcement

symptom: Deployment error when deploying VM or service in Azure portal Other (non-recommended) region; some services unavailable or features missing in selected region
rootCause: Azure portal reclassified regions: Recommended = regions with Availability Zones, Other = regions without AZ. Services requiring AZ features cannot deploy in Other regions
solution: Deploy in a Recommended region (one with Availability Zones) or verify service availability at https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/explore/global-infrastructure/products-by-region/; existing resources are unaffected by the classification change
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Azure Portal Recommended Other Region Classification Changes_Announcements

symptom: Burstable VM (B-series / Bsv2 / Basv2 / Bpsv2) CPU performance suddenly drops or throttles, workload slows down unexpectedly
rootCause: CPU credit balance exhausted. Burstable VMs use a credit-based model: credits accumulate when CPU < baseline, are consumed when CPU > baseline. Once credits run out, the VM is throttled to its baseline CPU percentage.
solution: 1) Verify via Azure Metrics: check 'CPU Credits Remaining' metric. 2) If credits depleted, this is expected platform behavior — not a platform fault. 3) Recommend customer: (a) resize to a non-burstable SKU if sustained CPU is needed, (b) review workload to reduce CPU spikes, (c) wait for credit replenishment if spikes are intermittent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Burstable VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Customer reports poor CPU performance on B-series VM, comparing it unfavorably to other VM SKUs of same vCPU count
rootCause: B-series VMs have a baseline CPU percentage (e.g., 10-40% of vCPU). Only when credit balance > 0 can the VM burst to 100% of vCPU. Customer may not understand the burstable model vs. standard VMs.
solution: Educate customer on Burstable VM credit model: baseline CPU %, credit accumulation rate, and burst behavior. B-series is designed for workloads with low-to-moderate baseline CPU and occasional spikes (web servers, small DBs, dev/test). For sustained high CPU, recommend non-burstable SKUs. Check 'CPU Credits Remaining' and 'CPU Credits Consumed' metrics to confirm expected behavior. Bsv2/Basv2/Bpsv2 are Gen2 replacements for original B-series with higher network/storage performance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Burstable VM Series_Announcements

symptom: VM resize from retiring D/Ds/Dv2/Dsv2/Ls series to v6 series fails due to OS incompatibility — v6 requires NVMe-supported OS, Gen2 VM, and MANA-supported OS
rootCause: v6 VM series has strict prerequisites: NVMe interface (requires supported OS), Generation 2 VM only, and MANA (Microsoft Azure Network Adapter) driver support. Legacy OS images on D/Ds/Dv2/Dsv2 VMs often lack these.
solution: 1) Check OS compatibility at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/enable-nvme-interface. 2) If OS not supported for v6, fall back to v5 or v4 series. 3) Resize flow: Stop (deallocate) → Resize → Start. 4) Check quota for target series before resize. See migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/migration/sizes/d-ds-dv2-dsv2-ls-series-migration-guide
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/D Ds Dv2 Dsv2 and Ls series Migration Guide_Announcement

symptom: Confidential VM (DCasv5/DCadsv5/ECasv5/ECadsv5) deployment fails with 'Creation of VMs with ConfidentialVM security type is not supported in this region' or 'Use of setting is not supported for the provided image'
rootCause: CVM requires specific AMD SEV-SNP clusters only available in certain regions, and only ConfidentialVMSupported tagged images can be used
solution: Deploy in a CVM-supported region and use an image tagged with ConfidentialVMSupported. Check supported regions in Azure documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCasv5 DCadsv5 CVM Series_Announcements

symptom: CVM deployment fails with 'Required parameter for Confidential VMs is missing (null)' or 'should have both secureBootEnabled and vtpmEnabled set to true'
rootCause: Confidential VM requires managedDisk.securityProfile.securityEncryptionType to be set. DiskWithVMGuestState mode additionally requires secureBootEnabled=true and vtpmEnabled=true in UEFI settings.
solution: 1) Set securityEncryptionType to DiskWithVMGuestState or VMGuestStateOnly. 2) For DiskWithVMGuestState: set both secureBootEnabled and vtpmEnabled to true. 3) For VMGuestStateOnly: set vtpmEnabled to true.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCasv5 DCadsv5 CVM Series_Announcements

symptom: Cannot resize CVM or change security properties — errors: 'Cannot resize a VM with confidential computing type to different type' or 'Changing property SecurityType/securityEncryptionType is not allowed'
rootCause: CVM securityEncryptionType and SecurityType are immutable after creation. CVM SKUs are exclusive to CVM families and cannot be resized to non-CVM families or vice versa.
solution: Cannot change security properties or resize across CVM/non-CVM families. Must delete the VM (keeping disks) and recreate with desired settings. For resize within same CVM family: Stop-Deallocate → Resize → Start.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCasv5 DCadsv5 CVM Series_Announcements

symptom: CVM disk/snapshot operations fail: 'Cannot create disk or snapshot by copying CVM disk from different subscription' or 'should have same SecureVM DiskEncryptionSet as source'
rootCause: CVM CMK-encrypted disks cannot be copied cross-subscription. Snapshot/copy operations require matching SecurityType and SecureVM DiskEncryptionSet between source and target.
solution: 1) Copy/snapshot CVM CMK disks within same subscription only. 2) Do not change secureVMDiskEncryptionSet or SecurityType in the template. 3) For CVM CMK encryption use DES type 'ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey'; for SSE CMK use 'EncryptionAtRestWithPlatformAndCustomerKeys'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCasv5 DCadsv5 CVM Series_Announcements

symptom: Cannot deploy DCasv6/ECasv6 Confidential VM — SKU not available or deployment rejected during gated public preview
rootCause: DCasv6 and ECasv6 CVM series are in gated public preview; subscription must be explicitly allowlisted via sign-up form before deployment
solution: Customer must fill out the preview sign-up form: https://forms.microsoft.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=v4j5cvGGr0GRqy180BHbR_jUkzPLMttGkJglmhXbLJ9UNDNRRkEyUURCR1ZCTEFLWkI3OUJDQVhZUy4u. For access issues contact acc-pm@microsoft.com or Rakesh Ginjupalli (raginjup@microsoft.com)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCasv6 ECasv6 CVM Series_Announcements

symptom: Azure Backup, Azure Site Recovery, Shared disks, Accelerated Networking, or Live migration fails on DCasv6/ECasv6 Confidential VM
rootCause: Confidential VMs (all generations including v6) do not support these features due to SEV-SNP memory encryption architecture constraints
solution: Inform customer these are known CVM platform limitations, not a bug. Unsupported features: Azure Backup, Azure Site Recovery, Limited Azure Compute Gallery support, Shared disks, Accelerated Networking, Live migration, Boot diagnostics screenshots. Customer must use alternative approaches or non-CVM SKUs if these features are required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCasv6 ECasv6 CVM Series_Announcements

symptom: CVM DiskEncryptionSet type mismatch: 'The type of the Disk Encryption Set in the request is ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey, but this DES was created with type X' or vice versa
rootCause: User used a DES of type 'ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey' for SSE CMK encryption, or used a non-CVM DES type for CVM CMK encryption. CVM CMK requires DES type 'ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey'; SSE CMK requires 'EncryptionAtRestWithPlatformAndCustomerKeys'.
solution: For CVM CMK encryption: use DES of type 'ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey'. For SSE CMK encryption on CVM: use DES of type 'EncryptionAtRestWithPlatformAndCustomerKeys'. Do not mix DES types across encryption scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCesv5 and ECesv5 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Auto-key rotation for CVM DiskEncryptionSet fails: 'Auto-key rotation for DiskEncryptionSet with EncryptionType as ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey is not allowed'
rootCause: Automatic key rotation is not supported for DES with EncryptionType 'ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey' — this is a CVM platform limitation
solution: Set rotationToLatestKeyVersionEnabled to false on the DES. Manual key rotation must be performed instead of automatic rotation for CVM CMK-encrypted disks.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCesv5 and ECesv5 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: CVM disk creation fails: 'OS type must be specified for Trusted VM and Confidential VM disks'
rootCause: When creating managed disks for Trusted Launch or Confidential VMs, the osType property must be explicitly set in the disk creation request
solution: Specify the osType (Windows or Linux) in the managed disk creation template/API call. This is required for all Trusted VM and CVM disk operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCesv5 and ECesv5 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: DCesv6/ECesv6 Linux VM time drifting - system time drifts by seconds on long-running VMs
rootCause: Intel TDX 5th Gen (Emerald Rapids) architecture time sync bug affecting Linux only; Windows unaffected
solution: Manual workaround per FAQ: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/confidential-computing/confidential-vm-faq#what-can-i-do-if-the-time-on-my-dcesv6-ecesv6-series-vm-differs-from-utc-for-linux-
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCesv6 and ECesv6 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Deployment of DCsv3 or DCdsv3 VM fails when using Gen1 image; only Gen2 images are supported for this series
rootCause: DCsv3 and DCdsv3 series VMs require Gen2 images due to confidential computing (SGX) hardware requirements
solution: Use a Gen2 OS image (Ubuntu Server 18.04/20.04 LTS or Windows Server 2019 Datacenter) when deploying DCsv3/DCdsv3 VMs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCsv3 and DCdsv3 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Accelerated Networking fails to enable or does not work on Linux DCsv3/DCdsv3 VMs; live migration fails for DCsv3/DCdsv3 VMs
rootCause: DCsv3/DCdsv3 series does not support Accelerated Networking on Linux or Live Migration due to confidential computing hardware constraints; Hyper-Threading is also disabled
solution: Do not enable Accelerated Networking on Linux DCsv3/DCdsv3 VMs; plan for VM downtime during maintenance events as live migration is not supported; use standard networking without AN on Linux
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCsv3 and DCdsv3 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: PaaS solutions fail to deploy on diskless Dv4, Dsv4, Ev4, and Esv4 VMs; IaaS workloads unaffected
rootCause: Known platform issue with PaaS on diskless (no local temp disk) v4 series VMs
solution: Use disk VMs (Ddv4, Ddsv4, Edv4, Edsv4) instead for PaaS workloads until the issue is resolved
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DV4 EV4 Series_Announcement

symptom: Windows VM on diskless Dv4/Ev4 series appears to have no pagefile; pagefile.sys not visible in File Explorer and system tools report no page file
rootCause: Bug in Windows provisioning causes pagefile.sys to be created as a hidden + system-protected file, invisible by default
solution: In File Explorer → View → Options → View tab: enable 'Show hidden files' and uncheck 'Hide protected operating system files'. The pagefile.sys exists but is hidden.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DV4 EV4 Series_Announcement

symptom: Linux VM kernel panic on boot when deploying RHEL 7.x, CentOS 7.x, or Oracle Linux 6.x on Dv4/Ev4 VM sizes; serial console shows kernel panic early in boot process
rootCause: Azure host software issue causes Linux kernel version 4.6 or earlier to fail to boot on Dv4/Ev4 hardware; affects RHEL 7.x, CentOS 7.x, Oracle Linux 6.x and similar older kernels
solution: Use RHEL 8.x, CentOS 8.x, or Oracle Linux 7.x (or newer) images. Other Linux distros (Ubuntu, SLES, Debian) are NOT impacted. A Hyper-V host fix was planned to resolve this for older kernels.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DV4 EV4 Series_Announcement

symptom: Accelerated Networking toggle is ON during VM provisioning but no Mellanox entry appears in Device Manager (Windows) or lspci output (Linux) on Dv4/Ddv4 series VMs
rootCause: Sporadic platform issue where Accelerated Networking NIC (Mellanox ConnectX-4 Lx VF) is not properly attached to the VM despite being enabled; ICM 188744989
solution: Disable Accelerated Networking, or redeploy the VM (may not always work). Permanent Mellanox driver fix was pending from Azure Networking team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DV4 EV4 Series_Announcement

symptom: az vm list-vm-resize-option shows incorrect resize targets for diskless Dv4/Ev4 VMs, listing older generation and disk-based SKUs that are actually not allowed
rootCause: CLI bug in az vm list-vm-resize-option returns wrong resize options; actual resize between diskless and disk families is correctly blocked at platform level
solution: Ignore misleading CLI output; diskless VMs can only resize to other diskless SKUs, disk VMs can only resize to other disk SKUs. The resize functionality works correctly despite incorrect CLI listing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DV4 EV4 Series_Announcement

symptom: Azure VM loses internet outbound connectivity; new VMs created without explicit outbound method have no internet access after September 30 2025
rootCause: Azure retired default outbound access for VMs on Sept 30 2025. VMs in virtual networks without explicit outbound connectivity method no longer get auto-assigned default outbound public IP
solution: Configure explicit outbound connectivity: Azure NAT Gateway, Azure Load Balancer outbound rules, or directly attached Azure public IP. Existing VMs using default outbound continue to work but should transition. Cloud Services (extended support) unaffected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Default outbound access for VMs in Azure will be retired_Announcement

symptom: Docker image pulls from Docker Hub fail with rate limiting errors (HTTP 429) on Azure VMs; anonymous users limited to 100 pulls/6hr, free accounts 200 pulls/6hr
rootCause: Docker Hub rate limits container pull requests; Azure partner agreement with Docker (exempting Azure IPs) ended June 30 2022
solution: Authenticate Docker pulls with a Docker account, use Azure Container Registry (ACR) to cache/mirror images, or upgrade to paid Docker subscription (5000 pulls/24hr). Docker Hub is 3rd party; fixing CI/CD pipelines to reduce pulls is customer responsibility.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Docker_Hub_Rate_Limiting

symptom: Deployment of v6/v7 series VMs (Dasv6, Easv6, Fasv6, Dasv7, Easv7, Fasv7, Dpsv6, Dsv6, Dlsv6, Esv6) fails with error when using Gen1 image or OS image not tagged with NVMe support
rootCause: All v6 and v7 series VMs require Generation 2 IaaS VM images with NVMe tag; Gen1 images and non-NVMe-tagged images are not compatible with the NVMe storage controller
solution: Use a Gen2 OS image tagged with NVMe support. Check NVMe-compatible images at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/enable-nvme-interface. Convert existing Gen1 images to Gen2 if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Dasv6 Easv6 and Fasv6 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Local NVMe disk data lost after unexpected VM reboot or live migration on v6/v7 series VMs; local NVMe disk appears uninitialized/empty after restart
rootCause: Local NVMe disks on v6/v7 series VMs are ephemeral and do not persist data across VM reboots or server migrations; they require manual initialization and formatting
solution: Initialize and format local NVMe disks before use. Expect re-initialization after any unexpected reboot or migration. Do not store persistent data on local NVMe disks; use Azure Managed Disks for persistent storage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Dasv6 Easv6 and Fasv6 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Dpsv6/Dplsv6/Epsv6 VM deployment fails when using RedHat Linux Enterprise marketplace images; NVMe remote disks also not supported on this series
rootCause: Dpsv6 ARM64-based series does not support RedHat Linux Enterprise guest OS images from Azure Marketplace during preview; NVMe remote disk interface also not available
solution: Use a different supported Linux distribution (Ubuntu, SLES, etc.) for Dpsv6 series VMs. For storage, use SCSI-based remote disks instead of NVMe remote disks.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Dpsv6 Dplsv6 and Epsv6 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) fails or is not available on Dsv6/Dlsv6/Esv6 series VMs
rootCause: Dsv6/Dlsv6/Esv6 series does not support Azure Disk Encryption (ADE); also does not support Ephemeral OS or Live Migration
solution: Use server-side encryption (SSE) with customer-managed keys instead of ADE for Dsv6/Dlsv6/Esv6 VMs, or use a different VM series that supports ADE if disk encryption is required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Dsv6 Dlsv6 and Esv6 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: DCsv3 or DCdsv3 confidential VM deployment fails — only Gen2 images supported, Gen1 images rejected
rootCause: DCsv3 and DCdsv3 series VMs only support Generation 2 VM images. Attempting to deploy with a Gen1 image will fail
solution: Use a Gen2 OS image (Ubuntu 18.04/20.04 LTS or Windows Server 2019 Datacenter). Convert existing Gen1 images to Gen2 before deployment. Check supported images at https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/dcv3-series
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCsv3 and DCdsv3 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Accelerated Networking not working or unavailable on DCsv3/DCdsv3 Linux VM
rootCause: Azure Accelerated Networking (AN) is not supported on DCsv3/DCdsv3 Linux Virtual Machines (preview limitation)
solution: This is a known limitation. Do not enable AN on DCsv3/DCdsv3 Linux VMs. If network performance is critical, consider using a non-confidential VM series that supports AN, or wait for GA support
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCsv3 and DCdsv3 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Live Migration not supported for DCsv3/DCdsv3 confidential VMs — VM may experience downtime during host maintenance
rootCause: DCsv3 and DCdsv3 series do not support Live Migration due to SGX enclave memory isolation requirements. Hyper-Threading is also disabled for security hardening
solution: Plan for maintenance downtime. Use Availability Sets or Availability Zones for HA. For planned maintenance, use scheduled events API to prepare. Escalate CVM issues via CVM ICM Template: https://aka.ms/Confidential_VMs-ICM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/DCsv3 and DCdsv3 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Customer reports CVE-2021-44228 (Log4j RCE 0-day) affecting Java/Apache services running on Azure VMs; JNDI lookup allows remote code execution via crafted log messages
rootCause: Apache Log4j2 versions 2.0-beta9 through 2.15.0 do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP/JNDI endpoints in log messages, enabling arbitrary code execution
solution: Upgrade Log4j2 to version 2.16.0+; if on 2.10+, set log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true or LOG4J_FORMAT_MSG_NO_LOOKUPS env var; if below 2.10, remove JndiLookup class: zip -q -d log4j-core-*.jar org/apache/logging/log4j/core/lookup/JndiLookup.class. Note: most CVE cases are misroutes for IaaS VM team - confirm the actual service and engage that team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/EmergingIssue CVE 2021 44228 Log4j RCE 0 Day Vulnerability_Announcement

symptom: VM running SLES 12 SP5 no longer receives security updates or standard support after Oct 31 2024 (End of Standard Support)
rootCause: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 12 SP5 reached end of standard support lifecycle on October 31, 2024
solution: Three options: (1) In-place upgrade to SLES 15 per SUSE upgrade guide; (2) Fresh install of supported SLES version and migrate apps/data; (3) Purchase LTSS (Long Term Service Pack Support) for SLES 12 from Azure Marketplace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/End Of Support SLES 12

symptom: FX-series VM deployment fails or Availability Zone option is missing; zero quota error when attempting to create FX VM
rootCause: FX VM has zero default quota, supports Gen2 images only, is available in limited regions (East US, West US2, EU West at initial GA), and does not support Availability Zone option (single AZ per region at launch)
solution: Request quota via Azure Support ticket before deployment; use Gen2 VM image only; deploy in supported region; Availability Zone option is not available at initial launch
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/FX Series_Announcement

symptom: FXmsv2/FXmdsv2 VM deployment fails or Live Migration/Ephemeral OS Disk feature not working during Preview
rootCause: FXmsv2 and FXmdsv2 series are initially available only in West US3 region; Live Migration and Ephemeral OS Disks are not supported during Preview phase
solution: Deploy in West US3 (initially); avoid Live Migration and Ephemeral OS Disks during Preview; for issues contact Arpita Chatterjee (archat@microsoft.com); troubleshooting follows standard x86/x64 VM TSGs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/FXmsv2 and FXmdsv2 VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Customer inquires about Intel Processor MMIO Stale Data Vulnerabilities (CVE-2022-21123, CVE-2022-21125, CVE-2022-21127, CVE-2022-21166) affecting Azure VMs
rootCause: Vulnerabilities in certain Intel processor models allow MMIO stale data attacks; affects many modern Intel processors
solution: Azure infrastructure already mitigated; no proactive customer outreach needed; customers need firmware/microcode updates from OEM + Windows security updates; refer to ADV220002 and https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/mitigate-se
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Intel Processor MMIO Stale Data Vulnerabilities

symptom: VMs lose outbound internet connectivity; new VMs created without explicit outbound method have no internet access after September 30, 2025 retirement date
rootCause: Azure retired default outbound access for VMs. VMs in a virtual network without an explicit outbound connectivity method (NAT Gateway, Load Balancer outbound rules, or public IP) no longer receive a default public IP for internet access.
solution: Transition to an explicit outbound connectivity method: (1) Azure NAT Gateway, (2) Azure Load Balancer outbound rules, or (3) directly attached Azure public IP address. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/ip-services/default-outbound-access#how-can-i-transition-to-an-explicit-method-of-public-connectivity-and-disable-default-outbound-access
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Default outbound access for VMs in Azure will be retired_Announcement

symptom: Container image pulls from Docker Hub fail with HTTP 429 rate limit errors; anonymous pulls limited to 100 per 6 hours, free authenticated users to 200 per 6 hours
rootCause: Docker Hub enforces rate limiting on container image pull requests. Anonymous users are limited to 100 pulls/6h by IP, free authenticated users to 200/6h. Azure partner agreement with Docker (waiving throttling for Azure IPs) ended June 30, 2022.
solution: Authenticate Docker pulls with a Docker account to increase limits. Use Azure Container Registry (ACR) as an alternative registry. Upgrade to a paid Docker subscription (5000 pulls/24h). Guide customers to redesign CI/CD pipelines to reduce pull frequency.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Docker_Hub_Rate_Limiting

symptom: Large number of Windows Azure CRP Certificate Generator certificates accumulate in the VM certificate store (MMC Personal store on Windows, /var/lib/waagent on Linux)
rootCause: Each time a VM is stopped and started, the Tenant is recreated with a new DeploymentID, causing a new CRP certificate to be issued. Old certificates are not automatically cleaned up.
solution: Delete old certificates and keep only the latest ones. VM Agent will recreate the certificate if needed. Engineering fix (PR 2239105, commit e76149cf) adds automatic cleanup of old CRP certificates in future GA releases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/CRP Certificate_AGEX

symptom: Customer attempts to use Standard SSD or Standard HDD disk with Ebdsv5/Ebsv5 (Ebv5) series VM and encounters deployment or attach failure
solution: Use Premium SSD (P-series), Premium SSD v2, or Ultra Disk instead of Standard SSD (E-series) or Standard HDD (S-series) when attaching data disks to Ebv5 series VMs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Ebdsv5 and Ebsev5 VM Series_Announcement
rootCause: Ebv5 series VMs only support Premium Storage. Standard SSDs and Standard HDDs disk storage are not supported on these VM sizes.
NVMe is only supported on Gen 2 VMs. There are no plans to support NVMe on Gen 1 VMs
solution: Convert Gen 1 VM image to Gen 2 or create a new Gen 2 VM. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/generation-2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Dasv6 Easv6 and Fasv6 VM Series_Announcements
symptom: Using Gen 1 VM image with NVMe-capable v6 VM series (Dasv6/Easv6/Fasv6 etc.) fails with error: The selected image is not supported for NVMe. See supported VM images

rootCause: v6 VMs use raw unformatted NVMe local disks instead of previous SCSI-based temp disks. Unlike previous D/E series VMs, v6 VMs do not auto-initialize the local disks
solution: Initialize and format the NVMe disks into preferred file system after VM boots up. After stop/deallocate/planned maintenance and auto-recovery events, disks need to be re-initialized. Windows pagefile will be on OS disk by default and can be moved to local NVMe manually. Multiple NVMe drives can be spanned into one as needed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Dasv6 Easv6 and Fasv6 VM Series_Announcements
symptom: After deploying v6 series VMs (Dasv6/Easv6/Fasv6 etc.), local temp disk is not visible or shows as raw/unformatted. No temp disk available to applications after VM boot, stop/deallocate, or maintenance events

rootCause: New v6 sizes use NVMe protocol for local storage instead of SCSI. The API field name changed from MaxResourceVolumeMB to NVMEDiskSizeinMiB
solution: Update scripts/automation to use NVMEDiskSizeinMiB field instead of MaxResourceVolumeMB for v6 and newer VM sizes. Check Resource Skus - List REST API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/compute/resource-skus/list
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Dasv6 Easv6 and Fasv6 VM Series_Announcements
symptom: Resource SKUs API returns empty/null MaxResourceVolumeMB for v6 VM sizes (Dasv6/Easv6/Fasv6). Customer scripts relying on MaxResourceVolumeMB to determine temp disk size break for v6 VMs

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/AAD Auth for Managed Disk Import Export_Announcement
symptom: Setting DataAccessAuthorizationMode on managed disk fails with error: dataAccessAuthorizationMode is not supported for this subscription/region
rootCause: AFEC (Azure Feature Exposure Control) flag for DataAccessAuthMode is not enabled for the subscription or region
solution: Register the AFEC flag for the subscription. See https://aka.ms/afec for the feature registration process

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/AAD Auth for Managed Disk Import Export_Announcement
symptom: Changing dataAccessAuthorizationMode on managed disk fails with error: Property dataAccessAuthorizationMode cannot be changed on disk while there is an active SAS on it. Please call EndGetAccess or wait for the SAS to expire
rootCause: An active SAS token exists on the disk (generated via Get-AzDiskAccess, az disk grant-access, or Portal Export). Azure blocks property changes while SAS is active
solution: Wait for the SAS token to expire, or explicitly revoke SAS access using EndGetAccess (Revoke-AzDiskAccess / az disk revoke-access), then retry the operation

symptom: Lsv3/Lasv3 VM local NVMe disk shows error 'The format did not complete successfully' when customer tries to mount and format the temp disk
rootCause: Hardware cluster deployed with incorrect cluster settings (unlikely but known scenario)
solution: Redeploy the VM to another Availability Zone. If persists, engage HostAgent team via IcM: OneFleet Node/AzureHost-Agent-Sev-1-2 or Sev-3-4
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Lsv3 Lasv3 VM Series_Announcement

symptom: Lsv3/Lasv3/Lsv2 VM deployment fails with provisioning exception when Premium data disk caching is enabled; or VM deployed via CLI with caching succeeds provisioning but fails to start later
rootCause: Premium storage caching is not supported on Lsv3/Lasv3/Lsv2 storage-optimized SKUs. Platform bug allows CLI deployment validation to pass despite unsupported config (bug 13229774)
solution: Disable caching (set to None) for all attached data disks on Lsv3/Lasv3/Lsv2. If disk caching is required, choose an alternate VM SKU that supports it
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Lsv3 Lasv3 VM Series_Announcement

symptom: Customer deploys Nested Confidential VM (DCas_cc_v5/DCads_cc_v5/ECas_cc_v5/ECads_cc_v5) outside AKS and expects confidential computing features (attestation, memory encryption) but these are not available
rootCause: Nested CVM confidential computing features (SEV-SNP attestation, memory encryption via nested child VMs) are only supported when deployed through AKS. Outside AKS, these VMs function as regular general-purpose VMs without security assurances.
solution: Inform customer that Azure does not provide troubleshoot guidance or incident support for Nested CVMs deployed outside AKS. For confidential computing assurances, deploy through AKS. Contact azconfidentialpm@microsoft.com for enablement in regular VM deployments. Escalate via IcM template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=e134KF
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Nested Confidential VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Azure Site Recovery, Azure Dedicated Host, Customer-managed keys for OS disk pre-encryption, or TrustedLaunch security configuration fails or is unavailable on Nested Confidential VMs (DCas_cc_v5/ECas_cc_v5 series)
rootCause: These features are known preview restrictions for Nested Confidential VM series and are not currently supported: ASR, Dedicated Host, CMK for OS disk, TrustedLaunch SecurityType.
solution: These are known preview limitations. Customer must wait for GA support or use alternative VM series (e.g., DCasv5/DCadsv5 standard Confidential VMs) that support these features.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Nested Confidential VM Series_Announcements

symptom: Customer in Qatar Central region asks about GRS, region pairing, or cross-region replication for disaster recovery — GRS and region pairing are not available
rootCause: Qatar Central is a 3+0 Geo (single region with 3 Availability Zones, no paired region). Only LRS and ZRS storage redundancy options are available. No in-country region pairing exists.
solution: Advise customer: (1) Use zone-to-zone DR via ASR/Backup within Qatar Central's 3 AZs for HA. (2) For full BCDR requiring GRS, data must leave the Qatar geo — customer needs to accept cross-geo replication to another region. See: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/availability-zones/cross-region-replication-azure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Qatar Central_Announcement

symptom: Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) extension installation blocked on new VMs/VMSS, or ADE-encrypted VMs fail to unlock disks after reboot with Key Vault access errors
rootCause: ADE is being retired on 15 Sep 2028. Timeline: Sep 2026 → new enablement blocked (portal and extension install); Sep 2028 → Key Vault secret access removed, disks fail to unlock after reboot.
solution: Migrate from ADE to Encryption at Host before Sep 2028. Follow migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disk-encryption-migrate. View impacted resources via Azure Advisor Service Retirement Workbook. Contact: Jack Richins (jackr@microsoft.com) or Edgar Garcia Mujica (edgar@microsoft.com) for migration questions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Retirement Azure Disk Encryption_Announcement

symptom: VM extensions fail to install on Linux VM - agent cannot find WireServer
rootCause: DHCP is disabled on all NICs. The Linux agent discovers WireServer via DHCP, so if DHCP is not enabled on at least one NIC, extension processing cannot work.
solution: Enable DHCP on at least one NIC on the VM so the Linux agent can locate WireServer and process extensions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Install Extensions_AGEX

symptom: VM extension minor version not auto-upgrading despite autoUpgradeMinorVersion set to true
rootCause: A goalstate change alone does not trigger a minor version update. An explicit PUT request against the extension resource is required to update the minor version.
solution: Run Set-AzVMExtension (PowerShell) or az vm extension set (CLI) to generate an empty PUT against the extension, which triggers the minor version upgrade. For major version upgrades, specify the new major version in the PUT request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Update Extension_AGEX

symptom: Kaspersky VM extension (ksws/kesl) removed from Azure VMs or extension deployment fails for Kaspersky Endpoint Security
rootCause: U.S. Commerce Department banned Kaspersky products (June 2024). Microsoft removed the Kaspersky extension (publisher: kasperskylab.securityagent) from all Azure regions as of January 10, 2025 in compliance with CELA guidance.
solution: No customer action needed - the extension was forcibly removed. VMs that had the extension had their model updated to exclude it. For escalations, contact Support/EEE GA/PA via https://aka.ms/CRI-GAPA. Customers should migrate to alternative endpoint security solutions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Kaspersky VM Extension Removal_AGEX

symptom: VM operation (extension install, VM start, resize) takes exactly 90 minutes to complete; subsequent operations blocked for 90 minutes
rootCause: An extension is stuck in 'Transitioning' state, hitting the 90-minute timeout. This blocks all subsequent extension operations and VM-level operations that depend on extension readiness.
solution: 1) Check ASC Resource Explorer → Operations tab for 90-min timing. 2) Use Kusto: cluster('azcrp').database('crp_allprod').ContextActivity | where activityId =~ '<operationId>' to identify stuck extension. 3) Have customer remove the stuck extension (uninstall CSE/RunCommand/etc) and retry. 4) If extension is needed, reinstall after removal. See Extension Workflow for specific extension troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/90 minute extension timeout_AGEX

symptom: AKS VMSS node fails to provision; vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) reports failure with exit code (e.g. 50, 85)
rootCause: AKS uses CSE to install Kubernetes components on VMSS nodes. Exit codes indicate specific failures: ERR_OUTBOUND_CONN_FAIL=50 (NSG/UDR blocking outbound), ERR_GPU_DRIVERS_INSTALL_TIMEOUT=85 (GPU driver install timeout), etc.
solution: 1) ASC → VM → Extensions → expand failed CSE → Status Message for error code. 2) Cross-reference exit code: https://github.com/Azure/acs-engine/blob/master/parts/k8s/kubernetesprovisionsource.sh. 3) Code 50 → check NSG/UDR for outbound connectivity. Code 85 → GPU driver may complete if given time. 4) Check AKS Wiki for details. 5) Route to AKS team: SAP Azure/Kubernetes Service (AKS)/Extensions, Policies and Add-Ons/Customer script extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/AKS Custom Script Extension_AGEX

symptom: Azure Performance Diagnostics (PerfInsights) fails with 'Failed to upload result to storage account' / 'The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure' / SSL/TLS trust relationship error
rootCause: NSG rule or NVA/firewall blocks HTTP (port 80) traffic to Certificate Authority CRL/OCSP endpoints, or SSL packet inspection tool is intercepting HTTPS and breaking the certificate chain.
solution: 1) Check for SSL packet inspection tools on VM. 2) Allow outbound HTTP (port 80) to CRL/OCSP endpoints: crl3.digicert.com, crl4.digicert.com, ocsp.digicert.com, crl.microsoft.com, oneocsp.microsoft.com, ocsp.msocsp.com. 3) Add IPs to Allow list: 192.229.211.108, 204.79.197.203, 23.53.122.133, 104.18.25.243. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/security/fundamentals/tls-certificate-changes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Azure Performance Diagnostics Fails with Invalid Remote Certificate_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM Guest Agent or extension issues on 3rd-party NVA image (Fortinet/Barracuda/Palo Alto/Cisco/Kemp) — agent logs missing from standard paths, outdated agent version, limited or no GuestOS access
rootCause: 3rd-party vendor has forked (customized) the open-source Linux Guest Agent in their image. Forked agents use non-standard log locations, cannot auto-update from Azure repositories, and the OS may restrict shell/filesystem access.
solution: Microsoft cannot support forked Linux Guest Agents. Customer must engage the VM image vendor (3rd party) for agent and extension troubleshooting. Identify forked GA by: known NVA vendors, logs not in standard locations, limited GuestOS access, very outdated agent version. Minimum supported version: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/support-extensions-agent-version
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Forked GA_AGEX

symptom: VM Guest Agent not communicating with Azure platform — extensions fail, VM shows 'Not Ready', agent can't reach WireServer 168.63.129.16 on ports 80/32526
rootCause: Firewall, proxy, security software, or network configuration (e.g. multiple IPs on single NIC) blocking connectivity from VM to WireServer IP 168.63.129.16 on ports 80 and 32526.
solution: 1) Test connectivity: PowerShell Test-NetConnection 168.63.129.16 -Port 80/32526, or Linux traceroute/curl. 2) Check local firewall, proxy, security software not blocking 168.63.129.16. 3) Multiple IPs on single NIC? See TSG for that specific scenario. 4) Check WaAppAgent.log (Windows) or waagent.log (Linux) for errors. 5) Kusto heartbeat check: cluster('AzCore.centralus').database('Fa').WireserverHeartbeatEtwTable with NodeId. 6) WireServer REST logs: look for VM PA IP in Rest.log, HTTP 200 = success.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/WireServer Troubleshooting_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM with forked (3rd-party customized) Guest Agent - extensions not working, agent outdated, logs missing or inaccessible
rootCause: 3rd-party vendors (Fortinet, Barracuda, Palo Alto, Cisco, Kemp) customize the open-source Linux agent for their NVA images. The forked agent cannot auto-update from standard repositories, and log locations may differ from defaults.
solution: Microsoft cannot support forked Guest Agents. Customer must engage the vendor of their VM image for troubleshooting. Signs of forked agent: NVA image from known vendors, logs not in standard locations, limited/no GuestOS access, very outdated agent version. Escalation: https://aka.ms/CRI-GAPA
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Forked GA_AGEX

symptom: Windows VM Guest Agent not auto-upgrading on custom VM images deployed outside Azure Marketplace
rootCause: Custom VMs and manually provisioned VMs do not auto-upgrade the Guest Agent from Azure Marketplace channels. ProvisionVMAgent and EnableAutomaticUpdates must be set to True, and the VM needs access to Azure Storage for agent binaries.
solution: 1) Verify ProvisionVMAgent=True and EnableAutomaticUpdates=True via Get-AzVM OSProfile 2) Ensure no firewalls blocking Azure Storage 3) For manual upgrade, download latest MSI from https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=394789 4) If agent shows Ready but not upgrading, check for manifest timestamp issue per TSG
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Manually Upgrade VMAgent on CustomVMs_AGEX

symptom: Linux agent error 'Failed to copy ovf-env' on a VM that was previously provisioned successfully
rootCause: The /var/lib/waagent/provisioned marker file is missing on a previously provisioned VM, causing the agent to attempt re-provisioning and fail.
solution: Run: sudo touch /var/lib/waagent/provisioned
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Read Linux Guest Agent Logs_AGEX

symptom: Linux agent warning 'Too many files under /var/lib/waagent/events' causing agent performance issues
rootCause: Event telemetry files accumulate in the /var/lib/waagent/events directory faster than they are consumed, eventually impacting agent performance.
solution: Run: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/events/*
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Read Linux Guest Agent Logs_AGEX

symptom: VM operations (start, extension install, update) take exactly 90 minutes to complete
rootCause: An extension is stuck in 'Transitioning' state and hitting the 90-minute timeout. This blocks all subsequent VM operations that depend on extension completion.
solution: 1) Check ASC Resource Explorer Operations tab for 90-min operations 2) Query CRP ContextActivity with operationId to identify the stuck extension 3) Have customer remove the stuck extension(s) or fix via Extension workflow TSG 4) Retry the original operation. For RunCommand/CSE stuck extensions, uninstall and reinstall if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/90 minute extension timeout_AGEX

symptom: Error 'Subscription X is not registered with the feature Microsoft.Compute/Architecture. Please register the subscription before retrying the call' when creating an Arm64 OS image or deploying Arm64 VM
rootCause: Customer's subscription is not registered to the 'Architecture' AFEC flag required for Arm64 VM support
solution: Register the subscription to the 'Architecture' AFEC flag; use the onboarding link https://aka.ms/arm64onboarding to request whitelisting (takes ~48 hours)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error 'Architecture.Arm64 image requests to be deployed through only managed disk. Please set properties.disallowed.vmDiskType to Unmanaged' when deploying Arm64 VM or creating Arm64 image with unmanaged disk
rootCause: Arm64 VMs only support managed disk scenarios; unmanaged disk is not supported on Arm64 architecture
solution: Customer must use managed disk with Arm64 VMs; set VM disk type to Managed and retry deployment
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error 'Cannot create a VM of size X because this VM size only supports a CPU Architecture of Arm64, but an image or disk with CPU Architecture x64 was given' (or vice versa) when creating VM
rootCause: The selected VM size CPU architecture (Arm64 vs x64/x86) does not match the architecture of the provided image or OS disk
solution: Use Arm64 images/disks with Arm64 VM sizes (sizes with 'p' suffix, e.g. Standard_D2ps_v5), and x64 images with x64 VM sizes; architectures must match
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error 'Cannot resize a VM of size X with CPU Architecture Arm64 to a VM of size Y with CPU Architecture x64 due to different CPU Architectures' when resizing Arm64 VM
rootCause: Azure does not support resizing between different CPU architectures; Arm64 and x64/x86 are incompatible resize targets
solution: Arm64 VMs can only be resized to other Arm64 VM sizes (suffix 'p'); to switch to x64 architecture, customer must redeploy a new VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error 'Swapping OS Disk is not allowed since CPU Architecture property for the VM size is not supported by the OS Disk' when swapping OS disk on an Arm64 VM
rootCause: The replacement OS disk uses a different CPU architecture (e.g. x64) from the Arm64 VM; architecture must match for OS disk swap
solution: When swapping OS disk on an Arm64 VM, the replacement OS disk must be an Arm64-compatible OS disk; cannot swap to x64 OS disk
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error: 'Subscription is not registered with the feature Microsoft.Compute/Architecture. Please register the subscription before retrying the call' — when creating an Arm64 OS image or Arm64 VM
rootCause: Customer's subscription is not enrolled in the 'Architecture' AFEC flag required for Arm64 image creation (public preview requirement)
solution: Register the subscription to the 'Architecture' AFEC flag. Submit an onboarding request via https://aka.ms/arm64onboarding (takes ~48 hours to complete).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error: 'Architecture.Arm64 image requests to be deployed through only managed disk' when deploying or using an Arm64 VM/image
rootCause: Customer attempted to use an unmanaged disk with an Arm64 image; Arm64 VMs only support managed disks
solution: Use managed disk (not unmanaged disk) when deploying Arm64 VMs. Ensure vmDiskType is not set to Unmanaged in the deployment config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error: 'Cannot create a VM of size X because this VM size only supports a CPU Architecture of Arm64, but an image or disk with CPU Architecture x64 was given' (or vice versa)
rootCause: CPU architecture mismatch: an Arm64 VM size (suffix 'p', e.g. Standard_D2ps_v5) was paired with an x86/x64 image, or an x64 VM size was used with an Arm64 image
solution: Ensure architecture compatibility: select Arm64 OS images for Arm64 VM sizes (sizes with 'p' suffix), and x86/x64 images for standard VM sizes. Arm64-compatible images are available in Azure Compute Galleries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error: 'Cannot resize a VM of size X with CPU Architecture Arm64 to a VM of size Y with CPU Architecture x64 due to different CPU Architectures' when resizing a VM
rootCause: Customer attempted to resize an Arm64 VM (sizes with 'p' suffix) to an x86/x64 VM size, or vice versa; cross-architecture resize is not supported
solution: Arm64 VMs can only be resized to other Arm64 VM sizes (sizes with 'p' suffix). Switching CPU architecture via resize is not supported — customer must redeploy with desired architecture.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: Error: 'Swapping OS Disk is not allowed since CPU Architecture property X for the VM size Y is not supported by the OS Disk Z' when swapping OS disk on an Arm64 VM
rootCause: Customer tried to swap the OS disk on an Arm64 VM with an x86/x64 OS disk; the replacement disk architecture must match the VM architecture
solution: When swapping the OS disk on an Arm64 VM, the replacement OS disk must also be an Arm64 OS disk (Gen2, with Arm64-compatible OS image).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Arm64 VM Series

symptom: VM or resource creation in Azure Public MEC (Edge Zone) fails; deployment error or resource not found in Edge Zone location
rootCause: Resources in Azure Public MEC require the 'extendedLocation' property (type: EdgeZone, name: <edgezone-id>) in ARM templates or the --edge-zone parameter in CLI; this property is missing from the deployment
solution: Add extendedLocation to every resource in the ARM template: {"type": "EdgeZone", "name": "<edgezone-id-lowercase>"}. In CLI use: az vm create ... --edge-zone <edgezone-id> --public-ip-sku Standard. Edge zone ID must be lowercase (e.g., attatlanta1)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Azure Public MEC_Announcement

symptom: VM creation in Azure Public MEC Edge Zone fails with public IP-related error or VM creation error related to IP SKU
rootCause: Azure Public MEC only supports Standard SKU for public IP addresses; Basic SKU public IPs are not supported in Edge Zones
solution: Specify Standard SKU for Public IP: in CLI use --public-ip-sku Standard; in ARM template set sku.name = 'Standard' for Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses resource
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Azure Public MEC_Announcement

symptom: Standard Load Balancer in Canada Central Availability Zones cannot fully redistribute traffic when an entire AZ suffers an outage
rootCause: During Canada Central AZ Managed Access period, SLB service capabilities were not fully built out; in a full-AZ outage scenario, SLB cannot absorb the complete workload across remaining zones
solution: Known platform limitation during Managed Access phase only; issue was expected to be mitigated at Unrestricted Access. For production critical workloads during Managed Access, consider alternative HA designs. If still occurring, verify Canada Central is in Unrestricted Access; escalate to Azure Global team if issue persists
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Canada Central Availability Zones_Announcement

symptom: Customer unable to create new subscriptions or access resources in Korea South or West India regions
rootCause: Korea South and West India satellite regions have been restricted for new customers (effective Nov 2, 2021 and Dec 7, 2021 respectively) as part of Azure's initiative to simplify customer experience by focusing on Hub regions (Korea Central, Central India) with Availability Zones. Existing customer subscriptions and billing IDs are auto-allow-listed.
solution: New customers must request access via Azure region access request process: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/general/region-access-request-process. Existing customers are automatically allow-listed and can continue using these regions normally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/KoreaSouth_WestIndia_RegionRestrictions_Announcement

symptom: 客户新订阅无法在 Korea South 区域部署 VM（Compute），收到配额或权限拒绝错误
rootCause: Korea South 区域已实施 Offer Restrictions 模型（2022年2月起）：为将新部署引导至 Korea Central（具备3个AZ），新非高级订阅默认无 Korea South 的 Compute 访问权限
solution: 客户需通过 Azure Portal 提交 Korea South 区域的配额增加请求以申请 allow list：https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-portal/supportability/regional-quota-requests。已有 approved quota 的订阅仍可正常使用。建议优先引导新客户使用 Korea Central（具备3个可用区，提供更好的 HA/DR 能力）
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/KoreaSouth Access Restriction Rollback_Announcement

symptom: 需要确认 VM 使用的 DiskControllerType（SCSI 或 NVMe），但 Azure Portal 尚未显示该属性
rootCause: Azure Portal 暂不显示 DiskControllerType，需通过后端查询获取
solution: 方法一（Kusto）：查询 moseisley.kusto.windows.net AzureCM.LogContainerSnapshot，extend ext_prop = parse_json(additionalContainerProperties)，取 diskController = tostring(ext_prop.DiskControllerType)，按 roleInstanceName/Tenant/nodeId 过滤。方法二（fcshell）：在节点上通过 Fabric JIT Access 执行 vmadmin listDetail，VM Details 的 AdditionalContainerProperties 中包含 DiskControllerType 字段。方法三（ASC）：VM Properties 中显示 Disk Controller Type。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/NVMe Disk Controller_Announcement

symptom: 客户希望将现有 VM 的磁盘控制器从 SCSI 切换到 NVMe，但不清楚步骤或操作后 VM 无法正常启动
rootCause: 切换 DiskControllerType 需要 VM 处于 deallocated 状态；仅 Ebsv5/Ebdsv5 VM 系列支持 NVMe；且 OS 镜像必须支持 NVMe 驱动，否则切换后无法启动
solution: 前提条件：VM 为 Ebsv5 或 Ebdsv5，OS 镜像支持 NVMe，VM 已 deallocate。步骤：1) 停止并 deallocate VM；2) 在 storageProfile 中将 diskControllerTypes 设为 ["NVMe"]；3) 确认 OS 镜像标记为 NVMe-supported。需要停机。如需回退到 SCSI，同样需 deallocate，但须确认 OS 存在 SCSI 驱动（否则可能导致启动问题）。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/NVMe Disk Controller_Announcement

symptom: 客户在 NVMe-enabled VM 上配置 Ephemeral OS Disk 失败，或在 Portal 中无法对 NVMe VM 执行 Ultra DD resize
rootCause: Ephemeral OS disk 和 Ultra DD resize（Portal 中）是 NVMe-enabled VM 的已知不支持功能
solution: Ephemeral OS disk 不支持 NVMe-enabled VM：当 OS disk 配置为 ephemeral 时，data disk 仍会使用 NVMe（如果已配置），但 OS disk 本身不适用 NVMe。Ultra DD resize 当前在 Portal 中被阻止（仅 Preview 阶段可用）。客户需使用支持的替代方案或等待功能 GA。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/NVMe Disk Controller_Announcement

symptom: 客户使用 DCsv2-series VM 遇到容量不足（2025年7月后）或面临 2026年6月30日退役
rootCause: DCsv2-series 将于 2026-06-30 退役；2025-07-01 起开始实施容量限制，可能导致 allocation failure
solution: 在 2026-06-30 前迁移：(1) 继续 Intel SGX 路径 → DCdsv3（性能更高、内存更大）；(2) lift-and-shift → DCasv5/DCadsv5/ECasv5/ECadsv5，或 DCasv6/ECasv6（Preview），或 DCesv6/ECesv6（Preview）；(3) 容器化工作负载 → C-ACI 或 AKS C-VN2 Virtual Nodes。迁移指南：https://aka.ms/dcsv2-series-retirement。ICM 路由：OneFleet Node\ConfidentialComputing-Sev-1-2 或 Sev-3-4。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Retirement of DCsv2 series Virtual Machines_Announcement

symptom: 客户使用 F、Fs、Fsv2、Lsv2、G、Gs、Av2、Amv2 或 B 系列 VM，遇到 Reserved Instance 无法续期，或面临 2028年10月15日退役
rootCause: 上述 VM 系列将于 2028-10-15 退役；3年 Reserved Instance 自 2025-10-15 起不可购买或续期；到期合同将以 PAYG 费率计费
solution: 建议客户：1) 检查当前 RI 订单及到期日期；2) 对现有预留执行 exchange（换购新 VM 系列 RI）或 trade-in（换 Azure savings plan for compute）；3) 1年期 RI 至 2027-10-15 前仍可购买；4) 参考迁移指南进行 VM 系列升级（learn.microsoft.com: d-ds-dv2-dsv2-ls-series-migration-guide）。联系 Esther Ehindero（eehindero@microsoft.com）。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Retirement of F Fsv2 Lsv2 G Gs Av2 Amv2 B series Virtual Machines_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Changing the logical sector size of a disk is not permitted' when creating a disk from Ultra/Premium SSD v2 snapshot with a different logical sector size (e.g., attempting to create 4K sector size disk from a 512-byte sector size snapshot)
rootCause: Logical sector size cannot be changed when creating a disk from a snapshot; the new disk must use the same sector size as the source snapshot
solution: Create the new disk with the same logical sector size as the source snapshot. Creating a 4K logical sector size disk from a 512 logical sector size snapshot is not supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Import of disk {id} is in progress. Creation of snapshot is not allowed until completion' when attempting to create a snapshot of an Ultra or Premium SSD v2 disk
rootCause: The target disk's import or creation operation has not yet completed (completion percentage < 100%)
solution: Wait until the disk completion percentage reaches 100% before creating a snapshot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Cross subscription incremental snapshots are not supported. Copy source is in subscription {A} but snapshot creation request is with different subscription {B}' when creating incremental snapshot of Ultra/Premium V2 disk across subscriptions
rootCause: Incremental snapshots of Ultra Disk and Premium SSD v2 across different subscriptions are not supported
solution: Create the incremental snapshot in the same subscription as the source disk. Cross-subscription incremental snapshots are not supported for Ultra/Premium V2 disks.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Only incremental snapshots are supported for disks of Sku {sku}' when attempting to create a full snapshot of Ultra Disk or Premium SSD v2
rootCause: Full (non-incremental) snapshots are not supported for Ultra Disk and Premium SSD v2 disk types; only incremental snapshots are supported
solution: Use incremental snapshots instead of full snapshots when snapshotting Ultra Disk or Premium SSD v2 disks.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Source incremental snapshot {id} copy is still in progress. Please retry after source snapshot copy has completed' when creating a disk from an incremental snapshot of Ultra/Premium V2 disk
rootCause: The source incremental snapshot has not finished replicating/copying; the copy operation is still in progress
solution: Wait for the source snapshot copy to complete before creating a disk from it. Monitor the snapshot provisioning state and retry once copy is done.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Minimum api-version of {version} required to create snapshots on ultra or premiumV2 disks' when creating incremental snapshot on Ultra Disk or Premium SSD v2
rootCause: The API version specified in the request is below the minimum required (2022-03-22) for snapshot operations on Ultra/Premium V2 disks
solution: Upgrade to Azure compute REST API version 2022-03-22 or later when creating snapshots of Ultra Disk or Premium SSD v2.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Minimum api-version {version} is required to restore an Ultra or PremiumV2 disk from restore point' when restoring Ultra/Premium V2 disk
rootCause: API version used is below the minimum required (2022-03-22) for restore operations on Ultra/Premium V2 disks
solution: Upgrade to Azure compute REST API version 2022-03-22 or later when restoring Ultra Disk or Premium SSD v2 from a restore point.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'Minimum api-version of {version} required to create incremental restore points on ultra or premiumV2 disks' when creating incremental restore points
rootCause: API version used is below the minimum required (2022-03-22) for incremental restore point creation on Ultra/Premium V2 disks
solution: Upgrade to Azure compute REST API version 2022-03-22 or later when creating incremental restore points for Ultra Disk or Premium SSD v2.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error 'The move resources request contains Ultra or PremiumV2 disk created from snapshot and still getting hydrated. Disks cannot be moved while they are in hydrating state' when moving disk to another resource group or subscription
rootCause: The disk was created from a snapshot but the data hydration (copy from snapshot) is still in progress; Azure blocks resource moves during hydration
solution: Wait for disk hydration to complete (monitor provisioning state until disk is fully provisioned) before attempting to move the disk to another resource group or subscription.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Error indicating too many snapshots are already pending for export when trying to create snapshots of Ultra Disk or Premium SSD v2
rootCause: Platform limit reached: too many snapshot copy/export operations are concurrently in-flight for this subscription or disk
solution: Click the link in the error message to view snapshots pending export; wait for existing snapshot copies to complete before creating new snapshot export requests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Cannot attach Ultra/Premium V2 disk to VM while disk is still being provisioned/created from a snapshot
rootCause: Disk provisioning operation has not yet completed; disk is still being created and cannot be attached
solution: Wait for disk provisioning to finish before attaching to VM. This was a known limitation during public preview of Ultra/Premium V2 snapshot restore.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Snapshots Ultra&Premium SSDv2_Announcement

symptom: Customer running Windows Server 2008/R2 workloads in Azure asks about ESU support eligibility or entitlement verification
rootCause: Azure customers are automatically entitled to ESU + Support for actively running WS2008/R2 workloads in Azure; no separate entitlement purchase or verification needed
solution: Azure customers with ability to open Azure support case are entitled to ESU + Support on actively running Azure workloads. No entitlement verification needed. Support INCLUDES: ESU configuration assistance, ESU install/download/performance issues, non-ESU Windows issues (recommend upgrade if issue doesn't reproduce on supported OS). Support EXCLUDES: design changes, non-ESU bugs, feature requests, RCA, systems not configured for ESU (must configure and patch first before support). On-prem workloads must submit via Support for Business/Services Hub with ESU obtained via procurement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Windows Server 2008 ESU_Announcement

symptom: Windows Server 2008/2008R2 ESU MAK key is missing or incorrect in Azure Portal
rootCause: ESU MAK in Azure Portal is managed by Volume Licensing Service Center (VLSC), not by Azure Support; Windows 7 ESU MAK is intentionally not available in Azure Portal by design
solution: Route to VLSC: https://www.microsoft.com/Licensing/Servicecenter/Help/Contact.aspx. For Windows 7 ESU MAK: direct customer to VLSC or M365 Admin Center (https://admin.microsoft.com). Note: WS2008/R2 ESU MAK is visible in Azure Portal only for customers who purchased ESU in advance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Windows Server 2008 ESU_Announcement

symptom: Cannot install prerequisite hotfixes for ESU on Windows Server 2008/R2, or cannot install/activate ESU MAK on Windows system
rootCause: ESU MAK installation and activation issues are handled by the Devices and Deployment team, not Azure VM support
solution: Route to Devices and Deployment team. Support paths: (1) Prerequisite hotfixes: Windows Servers > <OS> > <OS Edition> > Installing Windows Updates, Features, or Roles > Non-cumulative update installation. (2) Cannot install MAK: Windows Servers > <OS> > <OS Edition> > Licensing and Activation > Windows product key issues. (3) Cannot activate MAK: Windows Servers > <OS> > <OS Edition> > Licensing and Activation > Windows activation issues. (4) VAMT issues: Windows Servers > <OS> > <OS Edition> > Licensing and Activation > Volume Activation Management Tool (VAMT). Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/deployment/volume-activation/install-vamt
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Windows Server 2008 ESU_Announcement

symptom: VMCU (VMware Cloud Universal) customer contacts Azure support for a technical issue with Azure VMware Solution (AVS) reservations or billing
rootCause: VMCU is a transactional licensing/credit program between VMware and customers, not a technical Azure feature; VMCU customers are billed via VMware under a special 0-meter or $0 price lock arrangement; these are CSP partner customers
solution: No technical support scope for VMCU program questions. Direct customer to open a Billing support ticket via Azure Portal (https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Support/HelpAndSupportBlade/~/overview): Issue Type = Billing, Subscription = <customer sub>, Summary = 'VMCU | Cancel existing AVS RI', Problem Type = Reserved instance requests, Problem Subtype = Cancel or change an existing reservation. Escalation contacts: Markus Hain mahain@microsoft.com (Specialized Support PM), Azar Jindal Azar.Jindal@microsoft.com (Azure Specialized Planner).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/VMware Cloud Universal (VMCU)_Announcement

symptom: Customer questions unexpected Ultra Disk billing charges or notices billing amount changed after October 2025
rootCause: Ultra Disk billing model changed from tiered billing to per-GiB billing effective October 15, 2025. Old model: provisioning 7 GiB was billed as 8 GiB (next tier). New model: 7 GiB provisioned = billed exactly 7 GiB.
solution: No customer action required; billing switched automatically on October 15, 2025. Customers have the option to continue using tiered pricing or use the new per-GiB pricing model. Feature rollout began August 25, 2025; GA targeting end of October 2025. Pricing calculator and pricing page updated at GA. Azure Update published at GA. Pricing change only affects how provisioned GiB maps to billing tiers — customers who prefer tiered model can continue using it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/UltraSSD Per GiB Billing_Announcement

symptom: Windows Update client is downloading update content via WUfB Deployment Service but does not progress past 50%; or client Update Health Tool does not initiate Express Update installation; or client does not honor restart schedule for Express Updates
rootCause: null
solution: Route to Windows Client queue. Support path: Routing Windows v3 > Installing Windows Updates, Features, or Roles. More info: https://aka.ms/wufbsupport. WUfB DS allows specifying update deferral: feature updates up to 365 days, quality updates up to 30 days.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Windows Update for Business Deployment Service_Announcement

symptom: ISV Graph API calls to Windows Update for Business Deployment Service return 403 response; or ISV reports persistent WUfB Deployment Service unavailability
rootCause: null
solution: Route to MSaaS Developer App and Services Dev queue. REST API and PowerShell/Azure CLI endpoints are exposed for IT pros and 3rd party management tool vendors via the WUfB Deployment Service. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/windowsupdates-concept-overview and https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/deployment/update/deployment-service-overview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Announcements/Windows Update for Business Deployment Service_Announcement

symptom: VM or resource exists in the resource provider (RP) but is not visible in ARM cache, causing it to not appear in subscription-level or tenant-level resource list calls
rootCause: ARM cache entry was not created or was lost; subscription/tenant-level resources are still subject to ARM internal storage replication delays (the cross-regional read fix from Nov 2022 only applies to resource-group-scope reads, not subscription/tenant scope)
solution: Execute ARM Sync (Create mode) via Jarvis action: Azure Resource Manager > Resource Synchronization > Synchronize resource state. Required fields: SubscriptionId, ResourceGroup, ResourceName, Provider (e.g. Microsoft.Compute), ResourceId (e.g. virtualMachines/myVM), ResourceLocation, ApiVersion, ProvisioningState=Create. ARM expects RP to return HTTP 200. Completes within minutes for single resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/How It Works/ARM Sync_How It Works

symptom: ARM-tracked fields of a resource (e.g. Storage Account SKU, tags, or other metadata outside the 'properties' element) are out of sync with the actual state in the resource provider after a backend migration or update
rootCause: Backend migration or platform update changed resource state in the RP (e.g. ZRS migration changed SKU) without updating ARM's Regional Store cache; ARM only caches metadata outside 'properties' for tracked (routing type: Default) resources
solution: Execute ARM Sync (Create mode) via Jarvis: Azure Resource Manager > Resource Synchronization > Synchronize resource state for the specific resource. ARM re-reads from RP and updates cache. Only applicable to Tracked resources (routing type: Default), not ProxyOnly resources. Use batch sync for multiple resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/How It Works/ARM Sync_How It Works

symptom: VM fails to start/redeploy/reapply with 'Provisioning failed with Internal execution error' (Provisioning State = Failed), VM remains in Stopped (Deallocated) state
rootCause: CRP calls Madari Service during VM lifecycle operations when IsEnabledForNodeBasedCrashConsistentRestorePoint flag is set (non-null) on the VM. If the Madari call fails (service unavailable, auth errors, timeouts), CRP aborts the operation immediately with no fallback path back to normal flow.
solution: 1. Query VMAllocationInfo.IsEnabledForNodeBasedCrashConsistentRestorePoint via Kusto (azcrpbi cluster, bi_allprod database) to confirm flag state and when it changed. 2. If flag is set, follow dedicated TSG at: SME-Topics/Cant-Start-Stop/TSGs/VM-Start-Failure-Madari-NodeBasedCrashConsistentRestorePoint_Start-Stop.md. 3. Cross-ref: AzureBackup wiki for crash-consistent snapshot errors (XDiskSvc, DiskRP, XStore signatures).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/How It Works/How Madari Works_How It Works

symptom: Microsoft engineer submits Customer Lockbox access request via ASC but customer never receives notification email; request does not appear in customer's Lockbox blade in Azure portal under 'pending'
rootCause: Customer has PIM (Privileged Identity Management) enabled but the eligible role (Subscription Owner or Global Admin) was NOT activated before the Lockbox request was initiated. Customer Lockbox ignores role assignments or PIM activations made after request processing has started.
solution: 1. Customer must activate their PIM eligible Subscription Owner or Global Admin role BEFORE the Lockbox request is initiated. 2. If already submitted without activation: wait 4 days for request to expire, then ask customer to activate role first, then re-request. 3. Note: role assignments scoped to management groups are NOT supported by Customer Lockbox.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Azure Customer Lockbox_Process

symptom: User with inherited subscription owner role does not receive Customer Lockbox notification email and cannot approve or reject the Lockbox access request in Azure portal
rootCause: Customer Lockbox does NOT recognize inherited owner roles. Only users with explicitly (directly) assigned Subscription Owner or Global Admin roles receive notification emails and have approval rights.
solution: Ensure the approving user has a direct (non-inherited) Subscription Owner or Global Admin role assignment on the subscription. Inherited role assignments (e.g., inherited from management group) are not sufficient for Lockbox.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Azure Customer Lockbox_Process

symptom: Microsoft engineer cannot re-submit a second Customer Lockbox request for the same support case; second request fails or is blocked
rootCause: Customer Lockbox enforces a per-case cooldown: a second request for the same case cannot be initiated until 4 days have passed from the original request. The request itself also only remains active for 4 days.
solution: Wait 4 days before re-triggering. If urgent, escalate via IcM to 'Azure LockBox\Triage' team with: Customer Tenant ID, Lockbox Request Timestamp, screenshots of customer portal Lockbox blade (pending requests + history) and ASC view.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Azure Customer Lockbox_Process

symptom: VM deployment fails with error 'Plan ID: cis-rhel8-1l cannot be purchased due to validation error' when using CIS marketplace image automation
rootCause: CIS (Center for Internet Security) changed their offerings in Feb 2024 and removed old plan information from Azure Marketplace, creating a breaking change for customers using automation that references the old CIS RHEL plan ID
solution: Request customer to reach out to CIS directly to correct the issue, or create a new VM image using an available and new deployable Marketplace image. CIS Contact: https://azuremarketplace.microsoft.com/en-us/marketplace/apps/center-for-internet-security-inc.cis-windows-server?tab=Overview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/3rd Party OS Support Boundaries_Process

symptom: 在 DfM 全局搜索框中无法找到刚创建的 Case（使用顶部 Global Search 搜不到）
rootCause: DfM 全局搜索通过 Azure Search 实现，存在最长 15 分钟的索引延迟（15 min SLA），刚创建的 Case 尚未被索引
solution: 改用 Cases Tab 的 All Cases 视图：点击 + 新建标签页 → 选择 Cases → 切换为 All Cases 视图 → 右上角搜索框输入 Case 编号并按 Enter。或从 Dashboard → See all records → My Open Cases 下拉选 All Cases 视图进行搜索
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/DfM/DfM Delayed Search_Process

symptom: 通过 ASC 创建 CRI/ICM 后立即失去访问权限（Restricted CRI），提交者包括 case owner 本人也被锁定
rootCause: ASC 近期变更了访问模型，新创建的 ICM 默认为 Restricted CRI，当前行为已知在调查中
solution: 1. 先在关联的 DFM case 上申请 JIT（DFM JIT 审批通过后即可访问 linked ICMs）；2. 若 JIT 失败，在 aka.ms/icmbug 提交 bug 并取得 bug WI #，EEE/OnCall 凭 WI# 提供临时访问权限；3. 不要等待模板修复，继续按流程创建 ICM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/Escalation (EEE, PG)/How to Create a CRI (ICM)_Process

symptom: Customer requests Azure datacenter energy/water sustainability data (PUE/WUE) or detailed Cloud ESG disclosure information not available in public documents
solution: Engage internal CO+I Sustainability & Cloud ESG team: (1) Submit a Disclosure Request via GetHelp at https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/sites/COISustainabilityCloudESG/SitePages/Disclosure-Management.aspx, OR (2) Submit the Customer Cloud ESG Disclosure Request Intake Form (for customer/potential customer requests). For non-customer requests (blog posts, media, reports, whitepapers) use the Non-customer Cloud ESG Disclosure Request Intake Form.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/Handling Cloud ESG Disclosure_Process

symptom: Delayed Assignment in SBAManager/VDM ignores the scheduled time and immediately assigns the case to the next available support engineer instead of waiting
rootCause: The 24x7 flag was not removed from the DFM case before setting up Delayed Assignment. The 24x7 flag overrides Delayed Assignment behavior and forces immediate assignment.
solution: Before setting Delayed Assignment in SBAManager: (1) Open the case in DFM, (2) Remove the 24x7 flag from the case, (3) Update Country/Time Zone to match customer's requested callback time, (4) Then proceed to configure Delayed Assignment in SBAManager Admin tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/Handover Process/How to Add a Delay Assignment VDM_Process

symptom: After manually taking ownership of a case in DfM and meeting IR, the case gets reassigned to another engineer, OR an additional unexpected case gets assigned to the engineer
rootCause: VDM/SBAManager Bot was not notified of the manual case assignment. VDM is unaware the engineer owns the case and treats it as unassigned, potentially reassigning it or counting the engineer as available for another case.
solution: After manually taking ownership in DfM and meeting IR, immediately tell the SBAManager Bot: 'assign case {caseID} to {alias}@microsoft.com'. This registers the case with VDM so it does not attempt to reassign it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/IR Best Practices_Process

symptom: IR SLA email is not recognized by DfM in a timely manner — delays of up to 15 minutes before DfM registers the email as meeting IR SLA
rootCause: The IR SLA email was created from Outlook instead of from within DfM. Emails sent from Outlook are not immediately recognized by DfM and can take up to 15 minutes to be processed.
solution: Always create the IR SLA email from within DfM (not from Outlook). Requirements: (1) Email must be sent from DfM, (2) Use your @microsoft.com account, (3) Primary customer contact must be on the To line, (4) TrackingID# (CaseID) must be included in the Subject line.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/IR Best Practices_Process

symptom: Support engineer's GME/AME (*ME) account is not found, disabled, or cannot be located
rootCause: *ME accounts that are inactive for more than 90 days are automatically disabled, and deleted 15 days after being disabled. An active Cloud screen is also required to maintain *ME accounts.
solution: Recreate the *ME account following steps at https://dev.azure.com/msazure/AzureWiki/_wiki/wikis/AzureWiki.wiki/644686/Create-User-Account
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Case Handling/OneVM AME GME Guide and FAQ_Process

symptom: Pull request (PR) in Azure DevOps wiki incorrectly marks a wiki page as deleted when the page was actually just edited and renamed/moved
