rootCause: Git loses file tracking when you simultaneously rename/move a file AND edit more than a certain threshold percentage of its content in the same commit. Git reports the original as deleted and the result as a new file. Azure DevOps then assigns a new Page ID, breaking all existing wiki links, ASC insight references, and bookmarks pointing to the old Page ID.
solution: Split the change into two separate PRs: (1) First PR: revert the rename/move back to original location but keep the content edits; complete and merge. (2) Second PR: on a new branch, rename/move the page to the desired location without further content changes; complete and merge. This lets Git fully track the file and DevOps maintain the original Page ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Knowledge Management (KM)/Resolve PR Incorrectly Thinks a Page Was Deleted_Process

symptom: Defender for Cloud 显示 'Windows/Linux virtual machines should enable Azure Disk Encryption or EncryptionAtHost' 漏洞警告
rootCause: VM 未配置系统分配的托管标识（System-assigned managed identity），或未在 Extensions + applications 中启用 Azure Automanage Machine Configuration（原 Azure Policy）
solution: 1. 为 VM 启用系统分配的托管标识；2. 在 VM 的 Extensions + applications 下启用 Azure Policy（Azure Automanage Machine Configuration）；详细选项参见内部 TSG：https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureIaaSVM/_wiki/wikis/AzureIaaSVM/958762/
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Security Cases Guidance/Defender for Cloud Encrypted VMs_Process

symptom: VM 可能遭受 RDP 暴力破解攻击（VM potentially under attack via internet-exposed RDP）
rootCause: NSG 规则开放了 RDP 端口（3389），且 Source IP 为通配符（*），任意互联网 IP 均可连接，攻击者可暴力破解后横向移动
solution: 将 NSG 规则的 RDP 来源 IP 限制为本地公网 IP（非 *）；或启用 Just-In-Time (JIT) VM Access 替代开放 RDP 端口
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Security Cases Guidance/Incident Response Concerns_Process

symptom: 客户询问 Azure VM 是否受 LogoFAIL 固件漏洞（UEFI boot loader firmware vulnerability）影响
rootCause: LogoFAIL 漏洞需攻击者预先获得 OS 完整控制权（admin/root 级别）才可利用；大多数 Azure 裸金属机器不使用 boot loader 镜像，因此不受影响
solution: Azure VM 不受 LogoFAIL 影响。使用 Surface 等硬件设备的客户需联系硬件厂商获取固件更新。建议遵循 Zero Trust 安全模型（https://aka.ms/zerotrust）和强健的更新管理策略
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Security Cases Guidance/LogoFail Security Vulnerability_Process

symptom: Need to search previous IcMs by error message, support ticket ID, title, or alias to find related escalations or known precedents
solution: Use ICM portal search at https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/search, or run Kusto: cluster('Icmcluster').database('IcmDataWarehouse').Incidents | where Summary has 'error message' | extend linktoicm = strcat('https://icm.ad.msft.net/imp/v3/incidents/details/', IncidentId, '/home') | project CreateDate, IncidentId, Title, OwningTenantName, linktoicm, SupportTicketId | order by CreateDate desc | limit 1000
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Searching IcMs_Process

symptom: Customer receives Azure Safety Guard / Microsoft Azure Safeguards email warning that their Azure resources may be under active attack
rootCause: Azure Safeguards team detected active security threat on customer subscription and sent notification; handling is outside CSS scope
solution: Inform customer this request is outside CSS scope. Direct customer to reply to azsafety@microsoft.com referencing their case/SIR ID from the warning email. Proactively contact CSAMs to follow up with Microsoft Azure Safeguards Team <azsafety@microsoft.com>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Security Cases Guidance/Azure Safety Guard_Process

symptom: Customer reports vulnerability scanner findings or alerts on Azure VM (Microsoft Defender or 3rd-party scanner tool)
solution: 1) Gather: scanner name/vendor, Microsoft vs 3rd-party, version (if Defender), alert details, OS versions, number of VMs affected, alert type, screenshots and alert IDs. 2) If 3rd-party scanner → refer customer to vendor. 3) If Microsoft Defender → contact Azure VM Security team to identify which Defender version/team supports it, then transfer case or open collaboration task with that team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Security Cases Guidance/Vulnerability Scanner Triage_Process

symptom: Engineer needs overview of Sovereign Cloud support coverage (Azure Government, Mooncake, Black Forest)
solution: Sovereign Cloud includes Azure Government (Fairfax), Azure China 21Vianet (Mooncake), Azure Germany (Black Forest). See sub-pages for specific process guides per cloud.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Sovereign Cloud

symptom: ACIS operations (serial console logs, VM screenshot, InspectIaaS Disk, Brooklyn diagnostics) unavailable or not shown when working on Azure Government (Fairfax) cases
rootCause: Azure Government requires JIT (Just-in-Time) access for ACIS operations due to compliance requirements; persistent access is not permitted; Azure Support Center (ASC) is not supported in Azure Government
solution: 1) Create IcM using POD-specific template (e.g., http://aka.ms/vmaacisjit for VM Availability POD). 2) Submit JIT request at https://jitaccess-validation.security.core.usgovcloudapi.net/ referencing the IcM, using Access Level PlatformServiceOperator. 3) Wait for escort assignment → launch RDP to jumpbox → request JIT again through escort session. Full guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-Azure-Government-Escort-JIT-Process.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Sovereign Cloud/Azure Government Escort JIT_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow submission fails with error when customer already has 20 workflows running concurrently
rootCause: Microsoft Genomics enforces a quota of 20 concurrent workflows per Genomics account; submissions fail when this limit is reached
solution: Wait for existing workflows to complete before resubmitting; both the concurrent workflow quota (20) and the account-per-region quota (1 Genomics account per region per subscription) can be increased by submitting a quota increase request to Azure Support
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Default Quotas_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Error downloading/uploading <blob>: Access to storage account <account> has been unavailable for too long (may have been throttled due to too many requests)'
rootCause: Customer submitted too many concurrent workflows reading from or writing to the same storage account simultaneously, causing storage throttling; all retries to retrieve inputs or upload results exhausted
solution: Use a different storage account for workflow inputs/outputs, or wait for current workflows to complete and storage quota to replenish; customer may also submit a quota increase request to Azure Support
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Scenarios_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Access denied to <blob>; the SAS token may have expired since submission' or 'Access denied to output container; the SAS token may have expired since submission or the access key is invalid'
rootCause: Workflow stayed in the queue too long before processing started, or the -sas/--sas-duration argument was set to a value smaller than the actual total processing time (default is 48 hours)
solution: Resubmit the workflow; if recurring, increase the SAS token duration using -sas/--sas-duration argument when running msgen. Run 'msgen list' to check the failed workflow's wall clock time and compare with the configured SAS duration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Scenarios_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with "Blob <blob> doesn't exist", "Source blob does not exist: <blob>", or "Couldn't reach these blobs: <blobs>. Make sure they exist in container <container> and their SAS tokens are not expired and are generated with the correct storage account key. Note that blob names are case-sensitive."
rootCause: Input blob does not exist in the specified storage container; incorrect storage account key was used to generate the SAS signature; or blob name has case mismatch (blob names are case-sensitive in Azure Storage)
solution: 1) Verify input blobs exist in the specified container with the correct case-sensitive name. 2) Ensure the correct storage account key (from Access keys page in Azure portal) was used for SAS generation via -ik/--input-storage-account-key or -ok/--output-storage-account-key. Note: storage account keys differ from Genomics account keys (-k/--subscription-key).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Scenarios_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Error uploading <blob>: <storage status message> This most likely happened because two workflows tried to write output files with the same names to the same container at the same time'
rootCause: Multiple concurrent Genomics workflows writing to the same output container using the same output base name, causing a write collision
solution: Resubmit the failed workflow with a unique output base name to avoid conflicts with concurrent workflow outputs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Scenarios_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Error uploading <blob>: <storage status message for BlockListTooLong or BlockCountExceedsLimit> This most likely happened because the output file is too large'
rootCause: Genomics workflow output file exceeds Azure Blob Storage's maximum block list or block count limits
solution: Reduce the size of the output by splitting the sequencing workload into smaller jobs with less input data per submission
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Scenarios_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Blob <blob> already exists and the overwrite option was set to False'
rootCause: Output blob with the same name already exists in the output container and the overwrite option is disabled (default behavior)
solution: Delete the existing output blob before resubmitting, or resubmit the workflow with the --overwrite flag set to True, or specify a different output container/name prefix
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Scenarios_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Unable to reach the storage account <account>. Make sure the name is correct'
rootCause: Incorrect storage account name was specified in the workflow submission command
solution: Copy the exact storage account name directly from the Access keys page of the storage account in Azure portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Genomics Scenarios_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Error reading an input file'; workflow log (standardoutput.log) shows 'SNAP exited with exit code 512 from line 1898 of file DataReader.cpp'
rootCause: Input genomics file (FASTQ, BAM, or SAM) is corrupted or invalid
solution: 1) Retrieve log archive from output container (file named *.<workflow-ID>.logs.zip) and check standardoutput.log for SNAP exit code 512. 2) Verify local file integrity: use fastaq for FASTQ files on Linux/Bash on Windows, or samtools for BAM/SAM files. 3) If local files are intact, re-upload inputs to the input storage container and resubmit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Submit IcM for Genomics_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Read length is too long', 'Encountered an invalid CIGAR value', or 'CIGAR string is too long'
rootCause: Input genomics file (FASTQ, BAM, or SAM) is corrupted
solution: Verify local file integrity using fastaq (for FASTQ files) or samtools (for BAM/SAM files) on Linux or Bash on Windows. If local files are valid, re-upload inputs to the input container and resubmit the workflow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Submit IcM for Genomics_Process

symptom: Genomics workflow fails with 'Error reading a FASTQ file; make sure the input files are valid and paired correctly' or 'Encountered an unmatched read'
rootCause: Genomics service performs paired-end read alignment requiring exactly two matched FASTQ files as inputs; the provided FASTQ files are not properly paired with each other or are corrupted
solution: Verify locally that both FASTQ input files are valid and correctly paired using fastaq on Linux or Bash on Windows. Ensure inputs are not mixed formats (FASTQ and BAM/SAM cannot be mixed). Resubmit after confirming proper pairing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/Processes/Swarming/Genomics/Submit IcM for Genomics_Process

symptom: AzureSecurityLinuxAgent extension install fails with Non-zero exit code 55: 'failed to parse handler settings: error deserializing public settings for handler: unexpected end of JSON input'
rootCause: The az vm extension set command was executed without the required --settings parameter containing enableGenevaUpload and enableAutoConfig JSON configuration
solution: Add the settings section to the install command: --settings '{"enableGenevaUpload":true,"enableAutoConfig":true}'. If still fails after correct syntax and waagent is working, engage security team via SAP: Azure/Microsoft Defender for Cloud/Onboarding and Settings/Agent Provisioning Configuration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Install AzureSecurityLinuxAgent_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) with Managed Identity fails: 'Unable to get managed identity. Please make sure that system-assigned managed identity is enabled on the VM or user-assigned identity is added to the system: unable to get msi, metadata service response code 400'
rootCause: VM has no managed identity assigned, or the specified objectId/clientId is incorrect, or the CSE Type Handler version is too old (requires Windows 1.10+ or Linux 2.1+)
solution: 1) Ensure managed identity (system or user-assigned) is created and assigned to the VM/VMSS. 2) Upgrade CSE to minimum version 1.10 (Windows) or 2.1 (Linux). 3) Use correct syntax: managedIdentity=@{} for system-assigned, or managedIdentity=@{objectId='<id>'} / managedIdentity=@{clientId='<id>'} for user-assigned
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Managed Identity Custom Script Extension_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) with Managed Identity fails to download blob file: 'unexpected status code: actual=404 expected=200' or 'Failed to download all specified files. The remote server returned an error: (404) Not Found'
rootCause: The managed identity specified (or implicitly selected) does not have Storage Blob Data Reader access to the target blob in Azure Storage. When managedIdentity parameter is not specified, or when VM has multiple identities and the wrong one is implicitly selected
solution: 1) Specify the managed identity explicitly using objectId or clientId. 2) Grant Storage Blob Data Reader role to the managed identity on the storage account/container. 3) If VM has multiple identities, use managedIdentity=@{objectId='<id>'} to select the correct one
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Managed Identity Custom Script Extension_AGEX

symptom: VMWatch provisioning fails with ExitCode 1: 'could not initialize output outputs.event_hubs: illegal base64 data at input byte 8. Exiting VMWatch...'
rootCause: The base64 encoding for Event Hub authentication parameter (SAS token or connection string) is incorrect or corrupted
solution: Verify and fix the base64 encoding for EVENT_HUB_OUTPUT_SAS_TOKEN_BASE64 or EVENT_HUB_OUTPUT_CONNECTION_STRING_BASE64. Re-encode the token/connection string and update vmWatchSettings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/VM Watch EventHub_AGEX

symptom: AzureSecurityLinuxAgent extension install fails with 'Non-zero exit code: 55' and error 'failed to parse handler settings: error deserializing public settings: unexpected end of JSON input'
rootCause: The az vm extension set command was run without the --settings section containing enableGenevaUpload and enableAutoConfig parameters
solution: Include the settings JSON when installing: az vm extension set -n AzureSecurityLinuxAgent --publisher Microsoft.Azure.Security.Monitoring --version 2.0 --vm-name <VM> --resource-group <RG> --enable-auto-upgrade true --settings '{"enableGenevaUpload":true,"enableAutoConfig":true}'. If still failing after correct syntax and waagent is working, engage security team via SAP: Azure/Microsoft Defender for Cloud/Onboarding and Settings/Agent Provisioning Configuration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Install AzureSecurityLinuxAgent_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails with 'Unable to get managed identity. Please make sure that system-assigned managed identity is enabled on the VM or user-assigned identity is added to the system: unable to get msi, metadata service response code 400'
rootCause: VM/VMSS has no managed identity assigned, or the managedIdentity property specifies incorrect client/object ID, or CSE type handler version is below minimum (Windows < 1.10, Linux < 2.1)
solution: 1. Ensure CSE version is at minimum Windows 1.10+ or Linux 2.1+. 2. Assign a managed identity (system or user-assigned) to the VM/VMSS. 3. Ensure the identity has Reader/Storage Blob Data Reader access to the storage account. 4. Use correct managedIdentity format: {"objectId":"<id>"} for user-assigned by objectId, {"clientId":"<id>"} for user-assigned by clientId, or {} for system-assigned (also works when only one user-assigned identity exists)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Managed Identity Custom Script Extension_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) not working as expected, unresponsive, or multiple archive folders accumulate under C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension indicating repeated failures
rootCause: CSE binaries on the VM are stale or corrupted; previous troubleshooting attempts may have left inconsistent handler state
solution: Clean reinstall CSE on Windows: 1. Delete registry key: reg delete "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\HandlerState\Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension" /f. 2. Remove extension folder: rd /s /q c:\packages\plugins\Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension. 3. Remove from Azure: Remove-AzVMCustomScriptExtension -ResourceGroupName <RG> -VMName <VM> -Name CustomScript -Force (or az vm extension delete). 4. Restart VM. 5. Reinstall CSE as needed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Manually Reinstall CSE_AGEX

symptom: VM extension removal via Portal, PowerShell, or CLI fails; extension is stuck and cannot be uninstalled through normal methods
rootCause: Extension handler state is corrupted or stuck in the guest agent, preventing normal removal flow from completing
solution: Windows manual removal: 1. Delete registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\HandlerState\<extension name>. 2. Delete folder c:\packages\plugins\<extension name>. 3. Trigger new goal state: Set-AzVM -ResourceGroupName <RG> -Name <VM> -Reapply (ARM) or az vm reapply (CLI); for Classic VMs add an endpoint. 4. Force remove: Remove-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName <RG> -VMName <VM> -Name <Ext> -Force. Linux: contact AGEX SME channel for guidance (procedure under PG review)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/Manually Remove Extension_AGEX

symptom: Error 1638 'Another version of this product is already installed' when manually reinstalling Azure VM Guest Agent via MSI. msiexec.log shows reconfiguration error status 1638.
rootCause: A previous version of Azure Guest Agent MSI package is still registered in Windows (product GUID starting with {5CF exists in registry) preventing the new version from installing.
solution: 1) Find the Product GUID (starts with {5CF) from C:\Windows\Panther\msiexec.log. 2) Navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall and find the matching GUID key. 3) Copy the UninstallString value and execute it in an admin command prompt to remove the old version. 4) Retry Guest Agent installation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Another version of this product is already installed_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent (WaAppAgent.exe or WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe) crashes, hangs, or exhibits unexpected behavior. 3rd-party DLLs are found loaded in the guest agent process.
rootCause: 3rd-party DLLs (e.g., antivirus like Kaspersky/Symantec, tools like EaserSoft ExtremeCopy, .NET profilers) are injected into the Guest Agent process, interfering with its operation.
solution: Check for 3rd-party modules via PowerShell: (get-process waappagent).modules | where {$_.company -ne 'microsoft corporation'} and (get-process windowsazureguestagent).modules | where {$_.company -ne 'microsoft corporation'}. If found, temporarily uninstall the 3rd-party software and test reproduction. Module prxyqry.dll is safe to ignore (Microsoft internal). Also use TSSv2: .\TSSv2.ps1 -sdp perf to collect module info.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Process Explorer_AGEX

symptom: Guest Agent manual reinstallation fails with error 1638 'Another version of this product is already installed' - msiexec.log shows Product {5CF...} already registered
rootCause: An older version of the Azure Guest Agent MSI package is still registered in the Windows registry (HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall) and blocks the new installation
solution: 1. RDP to VM, open msiexec.log (C:\Windows\Panther\msiexec.log) and find the Product GUID starting with {5CF. 2. Navigate to HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall and locate that GUID. 3. Copy the Uninstall String value and run it from an admin command prompt to remove the old version. 4. Retry Guest Agent installation using the standard installation TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Another version of this product is already installed_AGEX

symptom: VMWatch process not running or missing VMWatch telemetry/metrics in GuestAgentGenericLogs - AppHealth extension logs show 'VMWatch is not running' or 'Leaving VMWatch in failed state. Exhausted retry limit of 3'
rootCause: VMWatch exceeded resource governance limits (CPU 1%, memory 80MB) and was terminated. After 3 failed restart attempts, it enters a 3-hour cooldown period and stops retrying.
solution: 1. Check AppHealth extension logs for 'VMWatch exited. Attempt: N PID: PID ExitCode: ExitCode' messages. 2. Review exit code and VMWatch startup parameters. 3. Query GuestAgentGenericLogs in azcore Fa database with TaskName=='VMWatch' to verify telemetry gaps. 4. If VMWatch consistently fails, escalate to EEE GA/PA via IcM with template oC1C2i.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/Extension/VM Watch_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent (WaAppAgent.exe/WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe) or extensions crash, hang, or exhibit unexpected behavior - 3rd-party DLLs found loaded in guest agent processes
rootCause: Third-party software (antivirus like Kaspersky/Symantec, .NET profilers, or other tools) injects DLLs into Guest Agent processes via hooks, Image File Execution Options, or COM interception, interfering with normal operation
solution: 1. Run PowerShell: (get-process waappagent).modules | where {$_.company -ne 'microsoft corporation'} to check for 3rd-party modules. 2. Similarly check windowsazureguestagent process. 3. Collect Procmon trace filtered on WaAppAgent.exe and WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe. 4. Ask customer to temporarily uninstall suspected 3rd-party software and retest. 5. Alternatively run TSSv2: .\TSSv2.ps1 -sdp perf for comprehensive process/driver info.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/How Tos/GA/Process Explorer_AGEX

symptom: Azure Performance Diagnostics extension fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError. Handler log shows System.InvalidOperationException: This implementation is not part of the Windows Platform FIPS validated cryptographic algorithms, at MD5CryptoServiceProvider.
rootCause: FIPS mode is enabled on the VM. PerfInsights uses MD5CryptoServiceProvider which is not a FIPS-validated cryptographic algorithm, causing it to throw InvalidOperationException.
solution: 1. Check if FIPS is enabled: REGEDIT -> HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FipsAlgorithmPolicy -> Enabled value (1=enabled). 2. Take backup of registry and OS disk. 3. Disable FIPS mode (set Enabled to 0). 4. Restart the server. 5. Reinstall the extension. Engage Windows Guest OS support if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Azure Performance Diagnostics fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError_AGEX

symptom: Azure Performance Diagnostics fails with 'The table does not exist' error in Azure portal (HTTP 404), or 'System.IO.FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly System.Net.Http, version=4.2.0.0' when running PerfInsights manually on Windows.
rootCause: Required .NET packages and dependencies are missing from the system. PerfInsights requires .NET to execute properly. VMs without SQL Server or other .NET-dependent applications lack these dependencies.
solution: Install the latest version of .NET on the affected machine: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/install/windows#net-installer. Retry PerfInsights after installation to confirm resolution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Azure Performance Diagnostics failure dueTo dotnet dependencies_AGEX

symptom: Performance diagnostics extension (AzPerfDiagExtension) fails with 'Failed while running the command for extension: AzPerfDiagExtension' and substatus shows 'Failed to upload the PerfInsights result to Azure storage account' when using a custom storage account with firewall enabled.
rootCause: When installing Performance Diagnostics with a custom storage account that has firewall enabled, the VM's subnet is not in the storage account's allow list, so the extension cannot upload results to storage.
solution: 1. Find the custom storage account from Portal -> Performance diagnostics -> Settings. 2. Uninstall the extension. 3. Add VM's subnet to the storage account firewall allow list (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-network-security). 4. Reinstall the perf diagnostic extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/AzurePerformanceDiagnostics fails to enable log collection_AGEX

symptom: BGInfo extension fails to display Deployment Id, Internal IP, Public IP. CommandExecution.log shows 'System.Xml.XmlException: Data at the root level is invalid' when reading Rcf file (ExtensionConfig JSON file parsed as XML).
rootCause: BGInfo extension attempts to load JSON config files (ExtensionConfig*.json) in C:\WindowsAzure\Config\ as XML Rcf files, causing XmlException parse failure.
solution: 1. Delete JSON files in C:\WindowsAzure\Config\: del C:\WindowsAzure\Config\*.json. 2. Manually run enable command: cd C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Compute.BGInfo\2.2.2 && ./BgInfoHandler.exe enable && ./BGInfo.exe config.bgi /NOLICPROMPT /timer:0
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/BGInfo_fails_to_display_deploymentid_internalip_publicip AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails with 'Exceeded maximum file download time' exception in handler.log. System.TimeoutException thrown by DownloadManager.
rootCause: CSE has a hardcoded 30-minute maximum download time for script files. The customer's script files exceed this download time limit due to large size or quantity.
solution: Inform customer of the 30-minute download time limit. Customer should reduce the size or number of script files being downloaded by CSE to stay within the limit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/CSE Exceeded maximum file download time_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails on AKS VMSS with ExitCode 50 ('Connection reset by peer') or ExitCode 124 (timeout utility terminated command). VMExtensionProvisioningError reported for vmssCSE extension.
rootCause: Firewalls, NSGs, or bad networking routing block outbound connectivity from AKS nodes to AKS control plane or package repositories. CSE bootstrapping AKS nodes cannot establish required outbound connections.
solution: 1. Check DNS resolution, routing table, and NSG rules for blocked connectivity. 2. Verify outbound requirements per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/outbound-rules-control-egress. 3. Collect guest logs from /var/log/azure/ within 15-30 min before AKS deletes failing instance. 4. For deeper networking investigation, engage OPEX AzNet team. 5. If outbound is OK and issue is AKS-scoped, engage AKS Support via SAP: Azure/Kubernetes Service (AKS)/Create, Upgrade, Scale and Delete operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/CSE ExitCode50 Failed to Execute command_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) on Linux fails with 'Timed out waiting for lock on custom-script-extension' and exit status=4. Handler.log shows the extension retrying 10 times (3 second intervals) to acquire lock on the custom-script-extension binary.
rootCause: A previous CSE execution (customer script) is still running and holding a lock on the custom-script-extension executable. May also be caused by AV/anti-malware product scanning the binary (race condition).
solution: Customer should review their script to ensure it completes in a reasonable time. The lock contention is a race condition that may be caused by AV/anti-malware products. The CSE has built-in backoff and retry logic (10 attempts, 3s intervals) to handle this.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/CSE Timed Out Waiting for Lock_AGEX

symptom: Extension installation fails with error: 'Current sequence number, 0, is not greater than the sequence number of the most recently executed configuration, 0. Exiting...' ErrorCode: VMExtensionProvisioningError.
rootCause: VM Extensions use sequence numbers to detect configuration changes. When the same extension is run with identical settings, the handler sees the same sequence number and decides nothing has changed, refusing to re-execute.
solution: 1. Modify some extension settings so the handler detects changes and assigns a new sequence number. 2. Or use PowerShell with -ForceRerun parameter: Set-AzVMExtension ... -ForceRerun (get-date).ticks
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Current Sequence Number 0_AGEX

symptom: Azure Performance Diagnostics (PerfInsights) extension fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError. Handler log shows System.InvalidOperationException: This implementation is not part of the Windows Platform FIPS validated cryptographic algorithms at MD5CryptoServiceProvider constructor.
rootCause: FIPS mode is enabled on the VM (registry key HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FipsAlgorithmPolicy\Enabled=1). PerfInsights uses MD5CryptoServiceProvider which is not FIPS-compliant.
solution: 1) Check FIPS status: REGEDIT > HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FipsAlgorithmPolicy\Enabled (1=enabled). 2) Take backup of registry and OS disk. 3) Disable FIPS mode (set Enabled=0). 4) Restart the server. 5) Retry extension installation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Azure Performance Diagnostics fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError_AGEX

symptom: Azure Performance Diagnostics (PerfInsights) fails. Portal shows The table does not exist (HTTP 404). Running PerfInsights manually shows System.IO.FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly System.Net.Http, version=4.2.0.0.
rootCause: Required .NET packages and dependencies are missing from the Windows VM. PerfInsights requires .NET to execute properly.
solution: Install the latest version of .NET on the affected machine: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/install/windows#net-installer. Then retry PerfInsights.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Azure Performance Diagnostics failure dueTo dotnet dependencies_AGEX

symptom: Azure Performance Diagnostics extension fails with Failed while running the command for extension: AzPerfDiagExtension and substatus Failed to upload the PerfInsights result to Azure storage account when using a custom storage account with firewall enabled.
rootCause: Custom storage account has firewall enabled without adding the VM subnet to the allow list. The extension cannot upload results due to network access restrictions.
solution: 1) Identify the custom storage account from Portal > Performance diagnostics > Settings. 2) Uninstall the extension. 3) Add VM subnet to storage account firewall allow list. 4) Reinstall the performance diagnostics extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/AzurePerformanceDiagnostics fails to enable log collection_AGEX

symptom: BGInfo extension fails to display Deployment Id, Internal IP, and Public IP. CommandExecution.log shows Exception in reading Rcf: System.Xml.XmlException: Data at the root level is invalid. Line 1, position 1 when loading JSON config file as Rcf (XML).
rootCause: JSON configuration files in C:\WindowsAzure\Config\ are being loaded as Rcf (XML format) by BGInfo handler, causing XML parsing exception.
solution: 1) Delete JSON files: del C:\WindowsAzure\Config\*.json. 2) Re-run enable: cd C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Compute.BGInfo\2.2.2 && ./BgInfoHandler.exe enable. 3) Run BGInfo: ./BGInfo.exe config.bgi /NOLICPROMPT /timer:0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/BGInfo_fails_to_display_deploymentid_internalip_publicip AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails to provision with Exceeded maximum file download time timeout. Handler.log shows System.TimeoutException at DownloadManager.DownloadAsyncWithBlobClientDownloader.
rootCause: CSE has a hard-coded 30-minute maximum download time for script files. The customer script files exceed this limit due to large file size or too many files.
solution: Inform customer of the 30-minute hard-coded download time limit. Advise to reduce the size or number of script files being downloaded by the Custom Script Extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/CSE Exceeded maximum file download time_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails on AKS VMSS instances with ExitCode 50 (Connection reset by peer) or ExitCode 124 (timeout utility terminated command). Occurs during AKS cluster create/start/deploy operations.
rootCause: Network connectivity issues - firewalls, NSGs, or bad networking routing blocking outbound connectivity from AKS nodes to AKS control plane or package repositories. ExitCode 50 = ERR_OUTBOUND_CONN_FAIL, ExitCode 124 = command timeout.
solution: 1) Check DNS resolution, routing table, NSG rules. 2) Collect guest logs from /var/log/azure/ within 15-30 min. 3) For managed OS disk: collect IID via ASC. For ephemeral: SSH to VMSS instance, check /var/log/azure/cluster-provision.log. 4) If platform network OK, engage AKS Support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/CSE ExitCode50 Failed to Execute command_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) on Linux fails with Timed out waiting for lock on custom-script-extension and exit status=4. Handler.log shows the extension retrying 10 times (3s intervals) waiting for file lock on the executable.
rootCause: Customer script is taking too long to run or failed with a non-zero error code, holding the lock. May also be caused by AV/anti-malware product creating a race condition on the extension binary.
solution: Customer should review their script for long execution time or errors. If AV/anti-malware is suspected, investigate if it is scanning/locking the extension binary.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/CSE Timed Out Waiting for Lock_AGEX

symptom: VM extension installation fails with Current sequence number, 0, is not greater than the sequence number of the most recently executed configuration, 0. Exiting... (VMExtensionProvisioningError). Occurs when reinstalling extension with same settings.
rootCause: VM Extensions use sequence numbers to detect configuration changes. When reinstalling with identical settings, the extension handler sees the same sequence number and refuses to re-run.
solution: Option 1: Modify extension settings so the handler assigns a new sequence number. Option 2: Use PowerShell parameter -ForceRerun (get-date).ticks to force re-execution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Current Sequence Number 0_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails to download script with error 'Invalid length for a Base-64 char array or string' (Windows) or 'blob host not in *.blob.* format' (Linux)
rootCause: The storage account key provided in the CSE configuration is incorrect or invalid.
solution: Have the customer verify and correct the storage account key used in the CSE deployment command. Ensure the key matches the actual storage account key from the Azure portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails to download script with '(404) Not Found / The specified blob does not exist' (Windows) or 'blob host not in *.blob.* format' (Linux)
rootCause: The storage account name provided in the CSE configuration is incorrect, or the storage account has a firewall blocking access.
solution: Have the customer verify the storage account name is correct, check there is no firewall on the storage account blocking access, and if the storage account name is correct, also verify the storage account key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails with '(400) Bad Request' when attempting to get MSI from IMDS (Windows), or 'command terminated with exit status=127' (Linux). MsiNotFoundException is thrown.
rootCause: The User-Assigned Managed Identity specified in the CSE command was removed from the VM, or the managed identity does not have Storage Blob Reader role on the storage account.
solution: Verify the managed identity (System or User-Assigned) is still assigned to the VM and has Storage Blob Reader role on the target storage account. Re-assign if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension on VMSS fails with '(403) Forbidden - Server failed to authenticate the request. Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature.'
rootCause: The managed identity on the VMSS was recently updated but the CSE extension was not updated with the new managedIdentity property, or the managedIdentity property format is incorrect (clientId vs objectId).
solution: 1) Verify the managedIdentity property in the CSE settings matches the correct identity type (System vs User-Assigned). 2) If the managed identity was recently changed, update the extension with the new identity details using az vmss extension set.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Debian Linux VM extensions not reporting status, VM stuck in 'updating' status, password reset/RunCommand/extension operations fail. Extensions.Enabled=n in /etc/waagent.conf.
rootCause: Debian changed their Azure platform images (28 July - 14 December 2023) to disable VM extension handling by default (Extensions.Enabled=n in waagent.conf).
solution: For new VMs: add cloud-init script to set Extensions.Enabled=y during deployment. For existing VMs: edit /etc/waagent.conf and change Extensions.Enabled=n to Extensions.Enabled=y, then restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Debian Extensions Not Working_AGEX

symptom: Azure Diagnostic Extension fails with 'Failed to generate a unique diagnostics storage account name' when enabling diagnostics, regardless of what metrics/logs are selected.
rootCause: The boot diagnostics storage account has Storage Account Firewall enabled, which blocks the diagnostic extension from accessing the storage account. Not all required exceptions are configured.
solution: Navigate to the boot diagnostics storage account > Firewall and Virtual Networks > enable all three exceptions: (1) Allow trusted Microsoft services, (2) Allow read access to storage logging from any network, (3) Allow read access to storage metrics from any network.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Diagnostic Extension Failed to Generate SA_AGEX

symptom: Domain join extension (JsonADDomainExtension) fails with 'Failed to initiate system shutdown'. VM restarts but remains in workgroup state. Netlogon shows successful join followed by DeviceUnjoin.
rootCause: (1) The SYSTEM account does not have the 'Shut down the system' privilege (removed from Local Policy > User Rights Assignment). (2) Another script or scheduled task triggers a restart during the domain join process, interrupting the extension.
solution: 1) Verify SYSTEM account has 'Shut down the system' privilege: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > User Rights Assignment. 2) Review System event logs for external restart events initiated before domain join completes. Remove conflicting restart triggers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Domain_Join_Failed_to_initiate_system_shutdown_AGEX

symptom: EnableVMAccess / Reset Password fails with 'The password does not meet the password policy requirements. Check the minimum password length, password complexity and password history requirements.' even with 12-character password.
rootCause: Local or domain group policy on the VM sets 'Minimum password length' higher than 12 (e.g. 14 characters), or 'Password must meet complexity requirements' rejects the password because it contains the username (samAccountName or displayName).
solution: Use a password of at least 14 characters (maximum configurable by policy) and ensure the password does not contain the user's account name or display name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Fails The password doesn't meet the password requirements_AGEX

symptom: After using Reset Password (EnableVMAccess extension) on a Linux VM, ChallengeResponseAuthentication is set to 'no' in sshd_config, potentially breaking authentication workflows that rely on it.
rootCause: By design — the EnableVMAccess extension explicitly sets ChallengeResponseAuthentication to no in sshd_config as a security measure (response to prior security incident ICM 596270614).
solution: This is expected behavior. If the customer requires ChallengeResponseAuthentication=yes, they need to manually re-enable it in /etc/sshd_config after the password reset. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/extensions/vmaccess-linux
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Modifies sshd configuration

symptom: VM extension deployment fails with 'RequestDisallowedByPolicy: Resource <ExtensionName> was disallowed by policy' referencing 'Only approved VM extensions should be installed' policy.
rootCause: Azure Policy assignment 'Only approved VM extensions should be installed' (policyDefinition c0e996f8-39cf-4af9-9f45-83fbde810432) is enforced on the subscription/resource group, and the extension being deployed is not in the approved list.
solution: Navigate to Azure Portal > Policy > Assignments > find the policy > Edit assignment > add the required extension name to the 'Approved Extensions' list > Save. Then retry extension deployment. For policy-specific issues, collaborate with Azure Policy team via SAP: Azure/Azure Policy/Policy behavior not as expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Extension DeploymentFailed DueToAzurePolicy_AGEX

symptom: Customer wants to remove the legacy fixEmulatedIO extension (deployed for Spectre mitigation) from their VM.
rootCause: The fixEmulatedIO extension was deployed by Microsoft to mitigate the Spectre security vulnerability. It is no longer needed but was not removed from PIR to avoid breaking VMs that still have it.
solution: Remove via PowerShell: Get-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName <RG> -VMName <VM> -Name fixEmulatedIO | Remove-AzVMExtension -Force. If that fails, use CLI: az vm extension delete --resource-group <RG> --vm-name <VM> --name fixEmulatedIO. If 404 error occurs, create a collaboration request to ARM team with SAP: Azure/Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Resource Management/Resource not showing up.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Fix Emulated IO Removal_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails to download script with error '(404) Not Found' / 'The specified blob does not exist' (Windows) or 'blob host not in *.blob.* format' (Linux)
rootCause: The storage account name provided in the CSE command is incorrect, or a firewall on the storage account is blocking access
solution: Have the customer verify the storage account name is correct, check there is no firewall blocking access on the storage account, and also verify the storage account key is correct.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: CSE on VMSS fails with '(403) Forbidden. Server failed to authenticate the request. Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature'
rootCause: The managedIdentity property in the CSE settings is incorrect (wrong clientId/objectId for System-Assigned vs User-Assigned), or the managed identity was recently updated on the VMSS without updating the extension settings
solution: 1) Ensure the correct managedIdentity property format per identity type (see CSE docs property-managedidentity). 2) If the VMSS managed identity was recently changed, update the extension with the new identity details using az vmss extension set.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: VM extensions not reporting status, VM stuck in 'Updating' state on Debian VMs deployed after July 28, 2023. Extensions cannot be installed/removed, Azure Portal operations (reset password, RunCommand) fail.
rootCause: Debian changed Azure platform images after July 28, 2023 to disable extension handling by default (Extensions.Enabled=n in /etc/waagent.conf)
solution: For new VMs: add cloud-init script in 'Custom data and cloud init' during deployment: #!/bin/sh\nsed -i -e 's/^Extensions\.Enabled=.*$/Extensions.Enabled=y/' /etc/waagent.conf. For existing VMs: edit /etc/waagent.conf and change Extensions.Enabled=n to Extensions.Enabled=y. Note: As of Dec 14 2023, Debian re-enabled extensions for new VMs going forward.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Debian Extensions Not Working_AGEX

symptom: Azure Diagnostic Extension fails with error 'Failed to generate a unique diagnostics storage account name'
rootCause: The storage account used for boot diagnostics has Storage Account Firewall enabled, which blocks the diagnostic extension's IP from accessing the storage account. Insufficient firewall exceptions are configured.
solution: Navigate to the boot diagnostics storage account > Firewall and Virtual Networks, and enable all three exceptions: 1) Allow trusted Microsoft services to access this storage account, 2) Allow read access to storage logging from any network, 3) Allow read access to storage metrics from any network.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Diagnostic Extension Failed to Generate SA_AGEX

symptom: Domain join extension (JsonADDomainExtension) fails with error 'Failed to initiate system shutdown'. VM restarts but remains in workgroup state. Netlogon shows domain join succeeded then was unjoined.
rootCause: 1) The SYSTEM account does not have the required 'Shut down the system' privilege. 2) Another script, extension, or scheduled task is triggering a restart during the domain join process, interrupting the extension before it completes.
solution: 1) Verify SYSTEM account has 'Shut down the system' privilege in Local Policy > User Rights Assignment. 2) Review System event logs to identify any external restart operations initiated around the time of domain join. Remove conflicting restart triggers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Domain_Join_Failed_to_initiate_system_shutdown_AGEX

symptom: Reset password fails with 'The password does not meet the password policy requirements' via EnableVMAccess extension, even when using 12+ character passwords
rootCause: Local or domain password group policy on the VM requires minimum password length greater than 12 characters (up to 14), or the password contains the user's samAccountName/displayName which violates complexity requirements. The enablevmaccess extension only enforces 'Minimum password length' and 'Password must meet complexity requirements' policies.
solution: Use a password with 14 characters (maximum configurable by Minimum password length policy) and ensure the password does not contain the username (samAccountName or displayName).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Fails The password doesn't meet the password requirements_AGEX

symptom: After using Reset Password on Linux VM, ChallengeResponseAuthentication is changed to 'no' in sshd_config by the EnableVMAccess extension, potentially breaking SSH authentication methods
rootCause: This is by-design behavior. The EnableVMAccess extension intentionally sets ChallengeResponseAuthentication=no in sshd_config as a security measure, implemented in response to a prior security incident (ICM 596270614).
solution: This behavior is by design. If customer requires ChallengeResponseAuthentication=yes, they must manually re-enable it in /etc/ssh/sshd_config after the password reset operation and restart sshd. Inform the customer this is an expected security hardening step.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Modifies sshd configuration

symptom: VM deployment or extension installation fails with error 'Resource was disallowed by policy' / 'RequestDisallowedByPolicy' referencing 'Only approved VM extensions should be installed' policy
rootCause: An Azure Policy assignment ('Only approved VM extensions should be installed') is blocking the extension because it is not in the approved extensions list
solution: 1) Navigate to Azure Portal > Policy > Assignments > find the policy. 2) Click 'Edit assignment'. 3) Add the blocked extension name to the 'Approved Extensions' list. 4) Save and retry the extension deployment. For Azure Policy issues, collaborate with Azure PaaS/Dev team using SAP: Azure/Azure Policy/Policy behavior not as expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Extension DeploymentFailed DueToAzurePolicy_AGEX

symptom: Customer wants to remove the legacy fixEmulatedIO extension (deployed for Spectre security mitigation) from their VM
rootCause: The fixEmulatedIO extension was deployed by Microsoft on customer's behalf during the Spectre security vulnerability mitigation. It is no longer needed but was not deleted from PIR to avoid breaking existing deployments.
solution: Remove via Azure Cloud Shell PowerShell: Get-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName <RG> -VMName <VM> -Name fixEmulatedIO | Remove-AzVMExtension -Force. If that fails, try Azure CLI: az vm extension delete --resource-group <RG> --vm-name <VM> --name fixEmulatedIO. If 404 'ResourceNotFound' error occurs, open a Collaboration request to ARM team with SAP: Azure/Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Resource Management/Resource not showing up.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Fix Emulated IO Removal_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails to download script with 'Invalid length for a Base-64 char array or string' (Windows) or 'blob host not in *.blob.* format' (Linux) error.
rootCause: The storage account key provided in the CSE command is invalid or incorrect.
solution: Have customer verify the storage account key used in the CSE command is correct. Fixing the storage account key should resolve the download failure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails to download script with '(404) Not Found' / 'The specified blob does not exist' (Windows) or 'blob host not in *.blob.* format' (Linux) error.
rootCause: The storage account name provided in the CSE command is incorrect, or the storage account has a firewall blocking access.
solution: 1. Have customer verify the storage account name in the CSE command is correct. 2. Check if the storage account has a firewall enabled that may block the extension access. 3. Also verify the storage account key is correct (Linux error is identical for wrong name and wrong key).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails with '(400) Bad Request' when getting MSI from IMDS (Windows) or 'command terminated with exit status=127' (Linux). MsiNotFoundException thrown.
rootCause: The User Assigned Managed Identity referenced in the CSE command was removed from the VM or is not assigned to both the storage account (Storage Blob Reader) and the VM.
solution: 1. Check ASC Properties > VM Properties > Resource Identity to verify Managed Identity assignment. 2. Ensure the Managed Identity used in the CSE command is assigned to both the storage account (Storage Blob Reader Access) and the VM. 3. Re-assign the Managed Identity if it was removed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) on VMSS fails with '(403) Forbidden. Server failed to authenticate the request. Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature.'
rootCause: The Managed Identity (System-Assigned or User-Assigned) used in the CSE protected settings does not match the identity currently assigned to the VMSS, or was recently updated without updating the extension configuration.
solution: 1. Verify correct managedIdentity property format per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/extensions/custom-script-windows#property-managedidentity. 2. If Managed Identity was recently changed on the VMSS, update the extension with the new identity details using az vmss extension set.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Debian Linux VM (deployed between July 28 - December 14, 2023) cannot use any VM extensions. Extensions not reporting status, VM stuck in Updating status. Password reset, RunCommand, and other extension operations fail. /etc/waagent.conf shows Extensions.Enabled=n.
rootCause: Debian changed their Azure platform images to disable extension handling by default (Extensions.Enabled=n in waagent.conf) starting July 28, 2023. This was reversed on December 14, 2023 for new deployments.
solution: For new VMs: Add cloud-init script in Custom data to set Extensions.Enabled=y. For existing VMs: Edit /etc/waagent.conf and change Extensions.Enabled=n to Extensions.Enabled=y, then restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Debian Extensions Not Working_AGEX

symptom: Domain join extension (JsonADDomainExtension) fails with 'Failed to initiate system shutdown' error. VM restarts but remains in workgroup state. Netlogon.log shows domain join succeeded but was then unjoined (DeviceUnjoin).
rootCause: 1. The SYSTEM account does not have the required Shut down the system privilege. 2. Another script/extension/scheduled task triggers a restart during the domain join process, interrupting the extension.
solution: 1. Verify SYSTEM account has Shut down the system privilege in Local Policies > User Rights Assignment. 2. Review System event logs for any restart operations initiated just before the domain join extension restart attempt. Remove conflicting automation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Domain_Join_Failed_to_initiate_system_shutdown_AGEX

symptom: Reset password fails with 'The password does not meet the password policy requirements' error via EnableVMAccess extension, even when using passwords with 12+ characters as required by Azure portal.
rootCause: The EnableVMAccess extension enforces OS-level password policies: Minimum password length and Password must meet complexity requirements. If OS has local/domain group policy requiring >12 characters (up to 14), or password contains the username, the reset fails.
solution: Use a password with at least 14 characters (maximum configurable by Minimum password length policy) and ensure the password does not contain the username (samAccountName or displayName).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Fails The password doesn't meet the password requirements_AGEX

symptom: After using Reset Password feature on a Linux VM, ChallengeResponseAuthentication is set to no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config. Customer reports unexpected SSH authentication behavior change.
rootCause: The EnableVMAccess extension explicitly sets ChallengeResponseAuthentication to no in sshd_config as a security measure, implemented in response to a prior security incident (ICM 596270614). This is by design.
solution: Inform customer this is by design - a security measure implemented by the VMAccess extension. If customer requires ChallengeResponseAuthentication=yes, they will need to manually re-enable it after each password reset. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/extensions/vmaccess-linux
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Modifies sshd configuration

symptom: VM deployment or extension installation fails with RequestDisallowedByPolicy error: Resource was disallowed by policy. Policy assignment: Only approved VM extensions should be installed.
rootCause: An Azure Policy assignment restricts which VM extensions can be installed. The extension being deployed is not in the approved extensions list of the policy.
solution: 1. Navigate to Azure Portal > Policy > Assignments. 2. Search for the blocking policy by name. 3. Click Edit assignment. 4. Add the blocked extension name to the Approved Extensions parameter list. 5. Save and retry. For deeper issues, collaborate via SAP: Azure/Azure Policy/Policy behavior not as expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Extension DeploymentFailed DueToAzurePolicy_AGEX

symptom: Customer wants to remove the fixEmulatedIO extension (Spectre security mitigation) from their VM. Extension may show 404 ResourceNotFound error when attempting removal.
rootCause: The fixEmulatedIO extension was deployed by Microsoft to mitigate the Spectre security vulnerability. It is no longer needed but has not been removed from PIR. ARM may return 404 when the extension resource record is inconsistent.
solution: 1. Try PowerShell: Get-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName {rg} -VMName {vm} -Name fixEmulatedIO | Remove-AzVMExtension -Force. 2. If PowerShell fails, try CLI: az vm extension delete --resource-group {rg} --vm-name {vm} --name fixEmulatedIO. 3. If 404 error, open collaboration to ARM team. SAP: Azure/Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Resource Management/Resource not showing up.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Fix Emulated IO Removal_AGEX

symptom: Customer cannot remove FixLinuxDiagnostic extension from Linux VM; removal command fails because the ARM relationship is broken.
rootCause: The ARM relationship for the FixLinuxDiagnostic extension is broken, preventing direct removal via Remove-AzureRmVMExtension.
solution: First reinstall the extension to recreate the ARM relationship using one of these commands: (1) Set-AzureRmVMExtension with the original GUID name and -ForceRerun, (2) without -ForceRerun, (3) with -Name FixLinuxDiagnosticTest and -ForceRerun, (4) without -ForceRerun. If one succeeds, then remove using Remove-AzureRmVMExtension -Name <matching-name> -Force. If none succeed, engage backend support to remove the extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Fix Linux Diagnostic Removal_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM extension installation fails with 'ImportError: No module named xml.sax.saxutils' (commonly seen with VMAccessForLinux extension).
rootCause: The python-xml module is not installed on the system. It is not part of the python-base package, so the import of xml.sax.saxutils fails when extensions try to use it.
solution: Install the python-xml package for the distro: SUSE: 'sudo zypper install python-xml'; RHEL: 'sudo dnf install python-xml'; Ubuntu: 'sudo apt-get install python-xml'. Then retry the extension operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/ImportError No module named xml-sax-saxutils_AGEX

symptom: VM extension deployment fails with 'Publisher and type are not supported for OS type', 'The specified executable is not a valid application for this OS platform', or 'Non-zero exit code: 127' / 'No such file or directory' when using an extension designed for a different OS.
rootCause: Extension is incompatible with the VM's operating system — using a Linux extension (e.g., Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript) on Windows, or a Windows extension (e.g., Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension) on Linux.
solution: Use the correct OS-specific extension version. Windows: Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension, Microsoft.Azure.NetworkWatcher.NetworkWatcherAgentWindows. Linux: Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript, Microsoft.Azure.NetworkWatcher.NetworkWatcherAgentLinux. Full compatibility list at https://aka.ms/vmextensionspublishers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Installing Extensions on wrong OS_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension domain join fails with error code 1332: 'No mapping between account names and security IDs was done.'
rootCause: The 'Options' parameter value used for domain join is incorrect. Using option 1 (NetSetupJoinDomain only) can lead to failure.
solution: Use Options value 3 (NetSetupJoinDomain + NetSetupAcctCreate) instead of 1 in the Set-AzureRmVMExtension -Settings parameter. For error codes reference: http://aka.ms/errors. For other common error codes (1323=wrong password, 2202=invalid username), verify credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension domain join fails with error code 1355: 'The specified domain either does not exist or could not be contacted.'
rootCause: Wrong domain name provided, or VM cannot resolve domain DNS because domain controller IP is not configured in the VNET DNS settings, or network communication blocked by firewall/NSG.
solution: Verify the domain name is correct. Ensure VNET DNS servers point to domain controller IPs. Check that no firewall/NSG rules block communication between VM and domain controller on required AD ports.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension domain join fails with error code 2732: 'An account with the same name exists in Active Directory. Re-using the account was blocked by security policy.'
rootCause: A computer account with the same name already exists in Active Directory, and the security policy prevents reuse of the existing account.
solution: Ask the customer's AD team to remove the existing computer name from the Active Directory Users and Computers console, then retry the domain join operation. For VMSS, each instance creates a new computer object — stale objects from previous instances must be cleaned up.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: Old extension files accumulate in /var/lib/waagent on Linux VM, consuming disk space. WALA agent does not clean up old extension zip files and directories.
rootCause: Older versions of the Windows Azure Linux Agent (WALA) did not perform cleanup of old extension files — all previous version zips and directories were retained.
solution: Update the WALA agent to version 2.2.44 or later. This version includes automatic cleanup: keeps only the current version zip and directory, deletes the previous version upon successful update.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Linux GA Cleanup Extension Files_Agex

symptom: VMSnapshotForLinux (Azure Backup) extension fails with 'ManagedRPCreationFailedInCrpButExtensionSucceeded'. CRP logs show 'BlobTooOld' and 'VM status download failed'. Extension itself runs but CRP times out waiting for status.
rootCause: The VMSnapshotLinux extension's pre_check() calls getpwuid() which triggers LDAP lookups to Active Directory. UID conflicts in AD cause delays exceeding the 20-minute timeout, making the status blob stale (BlobTooOld) and CRP to report failure despite the extension succeeding.
solution: Configure pre/post backup scripts to stop and start the LDAP service (e.g., lsass) around backup operations. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/backup-azure-linux-app-consistent. This prevents LDAP lookups from blocking the snapshot operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Managed RP Creation Failed in CRP but Extension Succeeded_AGEX

symptom: Any Linux VM extension fails with 'Non-zero exit code: 126' (e.g., RunCommandLinux, CustomScript, or others).
rootCause: The /var mount point in /etc/fstab has the 'noexec' option set (e.g., '/dev/mapper/rootvg-varlv /var xfs defaults,noexec 0 0'), preventing execution of binaries from /var/lib/waagent where extensions are stored.
solution: 1. Remove the 'noexec' option from the /var mount entry in /etc/fstab. 2. Unmount and remount: 'umount /var && mount -a'. 3. Retry the extension operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Non zero exit code 126_AGEX

symptom: VM extension (RunCommand or others) fails on Windows with 'powershell is not recognized as an internal or external command' or '<command> is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file'.
rootCause: The System PATH environment variable is missing required entries (e.g., C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0 for PowerShell). Extensions run under Local System account which may have a different PATH than the interactive user session.
solution: For PowerShell: add C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0 to System PATH (Control Panel > System > Advanced > Environment Variables). Reboot or restart WindowsAzureGuestAgent service, then retry. For other commands: verify System PATH includes paths for required executables, and check for preconfigured cmd.exe startup commands that may alter the environment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Not recognized as an internal or external command_AGEX

symptom: OpenSSH extension (Microsoft.Azure.OpenSSH.WindowsOpenSSH) fails to install on Windows Server with exit code 2551.
rootCause: OpenSSH on Windows Server 2016 is not supported by CSS. For Windows Server 2019+ and Windows 10+, OpenSSH issues are owned by the Windows team, not the VM/AGEX team.
solution: For Server 2016: not supported — direct customer to GitHub issues (https://github.com/PowerShell/Win32-OpenSSH/issues). For Server 2019+/Win10+: engage Windows team via SAP 'Windows Servers/Windows Server 2022/System Management Components/OpenSSH (including SFTP)'. For ongoing collaboration: contact sshdisc@microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/OpenSSH extension troubleshooting_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails to download script on Windows with error: Invalid length for a Base-64 char array or string (System.FormatException)
rootCause: The storage account key provided in the CSE deployment command is invalid or incorrectly formatted
solution: Have the customer verify the storage account name and storage account key used in the CSE command. Fix the incorrect storage account key to resolve the Base-64 format error.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails on Linux with error: blob host not in *.blob.* format; script download fails
rootCause: The fileUri in the CSE configuration points to a non-Azure Blob Storage URL (e.g., raw.githubusercontent.com) while also specifying storageAccountName/Key, or the storage account name is incorrect
solution: Verify the fileUri uses Azure Blob Storage format (*.blob.core.windows.net). If using a public URL like GitHub, do not specify storageAccountName/Key. If using blob storage, verify both the account name and key are correct.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails on Windows with error: The remote server returned an error: (404) Not Found / The specified blob does not exist
rootCause: The storage account name configured in the CSE command is incorrect, or the blob/container does not exist. Could also be caused by a storage account firewall blocking access.
solution: Verify the storage account name is correct in the CSE command. Check that the blob/container exists. Verify there is no storage account firewall blocking the VM from accessing the storage account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) fails with error: (400) Bad Request when attempting to get MSI from IMDS, followed by MsiNotFoundException; script download fails
rootCause: The User Assigned Managed Identity specified in the CSE command was removed from the VM, or the Managed Identity does not have correct RBAC role (Storage Blob Reader) on the storage account
solution: Verify the Managed Identity (System or User Assigned) is assigned to the VM in ASC Properties > VM Properties > Resource Identity. Ensure the identity has Storage Blob Reader Access on the target storage account. Re-assign if removed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Custom Script Extension (CSE) on VMSS fails with error: (403) Forbidden / Server failed to authenticate the request. Make sure the value of Authorization header is formed correctly including the signature
rootCause: The managedIdentity property in the CSE protected settings references an outdated or incorrect identity after the VMSS managed identity was changed, or the identity type (System vs User Assigned) does not match the configuration
solution: Check if customer is using System-Assigned or User-Assigned identity and ensure the managedIdentity property matches per CSE documentation. If managed identity was recently updated on VMSS, update the extension settings with new identity details using az vmss extension set.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Custom Script Extension Storage Account Failures_AGEX

symptom: Debian Linux VM extensions not reporting status, VM stuck in Updating state, reset password/RunCommand/extension operations all fail. Deployed between 28 July and 14 December 2023.
rootCause: Debian changed Azure Platform Images (28 July 2023) to disable extension handling by default: waagent.conf has Extensions.Enabled=n. As of 14 Dec 2023 this was re-enabled for new deployments, but existing VMs from that period remain affected.
solution: For existing VMs: edit /etc/waagent.conf and change Extensions.Enabled=n to Extensions.Enabled=y, then restart waagent. For new VMs: add cloud-init custom data under Advanced tab: #!/bin/sh\nsed -i -e 's/^Extensions\.Enabled=.*$/Extensions.Enabled=y/' /etc/waagent.conf
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Debian Extensions Not Working_AGEX

symptom: Azure Diagnostic Extension fails immediately with error: Failed to generate a unique diagnostics storage account name
rootCause: The storage account used for boot diagnostics has Storage Account Firewall enabled, filtering IP ranges and blocking the Azure platform backend IPs needed by the diagnostic extension
solution: Navigate to the boot diagnostics storage account > Firewall and Virtual Networks. Enable all three exception checkboxes: (1) Allow trusted Microsoft services to access this storage account, (2) Allow read access to storage logging from any network, (3) Allow read access to storage metrics from any network.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Diagnostic Extension Failed to Generate SA_AGEX

symptom: Domain join extension (JsonADDomainExtension) fails with error: Failed to initiate system shutdown. VM restarts but remains in workgroup state. Netlogon shows NetpDoDomainJoin succeeded but DeviceUnjoin follows.
rootCause: Two possible causes: (1) SYSTEM account lacks the 'Shut down the system' privilege in Local Policy > User Rights Assignment; (2) Another script/extension/scheduled task triggers a restart during the domain join process, interrupting the extension before it completes its own restart.
solution: Step 1: Verify SYSTEM account has 'Shut down the system' privilege at Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > User Rights Assignment. Step 2: Check System event logs for restart operations initiated just before domain join extension attempts its restart. Remove conflicting restart triggers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Domain_Join_Failed_to_initiate_system_shutdown_AGEX

symptom: Reset password via Azure portal fails with error: The password does not meet the password policy requirements. Check the minimum password length, password complexity and password history requirements. EnableVMAccess extension deployment fails.
rootCause: EnableVMAccess extension respects OS-level 'Minimum password length' and 'Password must meet complexity requirements' policies. If domain/local GPO sets minimum length >12 chars (e.g., 14), Azure portal allows 12-char passwords that fail on the OS. Also, passwords containing the username (samAccountName/displayName) are rejected.
solution: Use a password that is at least 14 characters long (the maximum that can be configured by Minimum password length policy) and does not include the username. Verify the OS-level password policies via Local Security Policy or domain GPO.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Fails The password doesn't meet the password requirements_AGEX

symptom: After using Reset Password on a Linux VM, sshd_config is modified: ChallengeResponseAuthentication is set to no. Customer reports unexpected SSH configuration changes.
rootCause: By design behavior. The EnableVMAccess (VMAccessForLinux) extension explicitly sets ChallengeResponseAuthentication to no in sshd_config as a security measure, implemented in response to a prior security incident (ICM 596270614).
solution: This is expected behavior by design. Inform the customer that the EnableVMAccess extension intentionally modifies ChallengeResponseAuthentication. Logs at /var/log/azure/Microsoft.OSTCExtensions.VMAccessForLinux/ confirm this. If customer needs ChallengeResponseAuthentication=yes, they must re-enable it manually after each password reset.
