sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/EnableVMAccess Modifies sshd configuration

symptom: VM deployment or extension installation fails with error: RequestDisallowedByPolicy / Resource 'ExtensionName' was disallowed by policy. Policy name: 'Only approved VM extensions should be installed'.
rootCause: An Azure Policy assignment (built-in policy c0e996f8-39cf-4af9-9f45-83fbde810432 or custom) restricts which VM extensions can be installed. The extension being deployed is not in the approved extensions list.
solution: Navigate to Azure Portal > Policy > Assignments > find the blocking policy. Click Edit Assignment > add the required extension name to the Approved Extensions list and save. Retry extension deployment. For policy-related issues, collaborate with Azure Policy team via SAP: Azure/Azure Policy/Policy behavior not as expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Extension DeploymentFailed DueToAzurePolicy_AGEX

symptom: Customer wants to remove the fixEmulatedIO extension from their VM (deployed during Spectre security mitigation). May get 404 ResourceNotFound error when attempting removal.
rootCause: The fixEmulatedIO extension was deployed by Microsoft to mitigate the Spectre security vulnerability. It is still in PIR but no longer actively used. 404 errors during deletion indicate ARM cannot find the resource, requiring ARM team collaboration.
solution: Step 1: Try removal via Azure Cloud Shell PowerShell: Get-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName {RG} -VMName {VM} -Name fixEmulatedIO | remove-azvmextension -force. Step 2: If PowerShell fails, try Azure CLI: az vm extension delete --resource-group {RG} --vm-name {VM} --name fixEmulatedIO. If 404 error persists, send Collaboration request to ARM team with affected resource ID and correlation ID. SAP: Azure/Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Resource Management/Resource not showing up.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Fix Emulated IO Removal_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension domain join fails with error code 1323: 'Unable to update the password. The value provided as the current password is incorrect.'
rootCause: The password provided for the domain join account is incorrect or has expired.
solution: Verify the password for the domain join account is correct and has not expired. Update the -ProtectedSettings password parameter and retry the domain join operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: Customer expects VM to leave the domain after removing or deleting JsonADDomainExtension, but VM remains domain-joined.
rootCause: The JsonADDomainExtension performs a one-time Active Directory domain join. Removing or deleting the extension does not unjoin the VM from the domain — domain membership persists independently of the extension lifecycle.
solution: Domain membership must be explicitly removed at the OS level (System Properties → Change → Workgroup) or via Active Directory tools (remove the computer object from AD). The extension only handles the initial join, not ongoing management.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: Customer reinstalls or reapplies JsonADDomainExtension with a different OU path, but the VM's computer object remains in the original OU.
rootCause: OU placement is evaluated only during the initial domain join operation. The extension does not support moving an existing computer object to a different OU after the domain join is completed.
solution: Use Active Directory Users and Computers (ADUC) or PowerShell Move-ADObject to manually move the computer object to the desired OU. Reinstalling the extension will not change OU placement for an already-joined VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: Unable to remove FixLinuxDiagnostic extension from Linux VM; ARM relationship is broken preventing successful removal
rootCause: The ARM relationship for the FixLinuxDiagnostic extension is corrupted or missing, preventing the standard removal command from working
solution: Reinstall the extension first using Set-AzureRmVMExtension to recreate the ARM relationship (try with both original GUID name and 'FixLinuxDiagnosticTest' name), then use Remove-AzureRmVMExtension to remove it. If none of the 4 reinstall commands succeed, the extension may need to be removed from the backend.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Fix Linux Diagnostic Removal_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM extension fails with ImportError: No module named xml.sax.saxutils when installing extensions like VMAccessForLinux
rootCause: The python-xml module is not installed on the Linux VM. It is not part of the python-base package, so xml.sax.saxutils import fails when extensions try to use it
solution: Install the python-xml package: SUSE: 'sudo zypper install python-xml', RHEL: 'sudo dnf install python-xml', Ubuntu: 'sudo apt-get install python-xml'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/ImportError No module named xml-sax-saxutils_AGEX

symptom: Extension installation fails on Windows VM with error 'The specified executable is not a valid application for this OS platform' when using Linux extension (e.g. Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript or NetworkWatcherAgentLinux)
rootCause: Customer is attempting to install a Linux-specific extension on a Windows VM. Linux extensions use shell scripts (.sh) that cannot execute on Windows
solution: Use the correct Windows-equivalent extension. For Custom Script: use Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension instead of Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript. Check extension OS compatibility at https://aka.ms/vmextensionspublishers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Installing Extensions on wrong OS_AGEX

symptom: Extension installation fails on Linux VM with 'Non-zero exit code: 127' or 'No such file or directory' when using Windows extension (e.g. Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension or NetworkWatcherAgentWindows)
rootCause: Customer is attempting to install a Windows-specific extension on a Linux VM. Windows extensions use .cmd/.exe files that cannot execute on Linux
solution: Use the correct Linux-equivalent extension. For Custom Script: use Microsoft.Azure.Extensions.CustomScript instead of Microsoft.Compute.CustomScriptExtension. Check extension OS compatibility at https://aka.ms/vmextensionspublishers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Installing Extensions on wrong OS_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension fails to join domain with error code 1332: 'No mapping between account names and security IDs was done'
rootCause: The domain join option parameter is incorrect. Option 1 (NetSetupJoinDomain only) can lead to failures; option 3 (NetSetupJoinDomain + NetSetupAcctCreate) is recommended
solution: Use join option 3 instead of 1 in the Set-AzureRmVMExtension or ARM template Settings. Option 3 includes both NetSetupJoinDomain and NetSetupAcctCreate flags
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension fails to join domain with error code 1355: 'The specified domain either does not exist or could not be contacted'
rootCause: The domain name is wrong, or the VM cannot resolve the DC DNS name because the DC IP hasn't been added to the VNET DNS settings, or network communication is blocked by firewall/NSG
solution: 1) Verify the domain name is correct. 2) Configure VNET DNS servers to point to the domain controller IP. 3) Ensure no firewall or NSG is blocking traffic between the VM and the domain controller
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension fails to join domain with error code 1323: 'Unable to update the password. The value provided as the current password is incorrect'
rootCause: The password provided for the domain join user account is incorrect
solution: Verify and correct the password in the ProtectedSettings of the extension configuration. Ensure the domain join credential is valid and has not expired
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension fails to join domain with error code 2202: 'The specified username is invalid'
rootCause: The username provided for domain join does not exist in the target domain
solution: Verify the domain join username exists in Active Directory and is correctly formatted (e.g. domain\username or user@domain.com)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: JsonADDomainExtension fails to join domain with error code 2732: 'An account with the same name exists in Active Directory. Re-using the account was blocked by security policy'
rootCause: A computer account with the same name already exists in Active Directory, and the security policy prevents reusing it
solution: Ask the customer's AD admin to remove the existing computer name from Active Directory Users and Computers console, then retry the domain join. For VMSS, this commonly occurs when instances are reimaged and try to rejoin with the same name
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/JsonAdDomainExtension Errors_AGEX

symptom: Old extension files accumulate on Linux VM under /var/lib/waagent, consuming disk space
rootCause: Windows Azure Linux Agent (WALA) versions prior to 2.2.44 do not properly clean up old extension files after updates
solution: Update WALA agent to version 2.2.44 or later. In this version, cleanup is handled automatically: only the current version zip and directory are kept. During updates, the previous version zip is deleted upon successful update
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Linux GA Cleanup Extension Files_Agex

symptom: VMSnapshotForLinux extension fails with ManagedRPCreationFailedInCrpButExtensionSucceeded. CRP shows 'VM status download failed (BlobTooOld)' and syslog shows LDAP UID conflict errors from lsass during backup
rootCause: The VMSnapshotLinux extension calls getpwuid() during pre-checks which triggers LDAP queries. When Active Directory has UID conflicts, these calls hang or fail, causing the extension to exceed the status reporting timeout (BlobTooOld)
solution: Configure Azure Backup pre/post scripts to stop and start the LDAP service around the backup operation. See https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/azure-docs/blob/master/articles/backup/backup-azure-linux-app-consistent.md for script configuration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Managed RP Creation Failed in CRP but Extension Succeeded_AGEX

symptom: Any Linux VM extension fails with 'Non-zero exit code: 126' in /var/log/waagent.log (e.g. RunCommandLinux, CustomScript)
rootCause: The /etc/fstab has the 'noexec' mount option set on the /var partition. This prevents any executables from running under /var/lib/waagent where extensions are stored
solution: 1) Remove the 'noexec' option from the /var mountpoint in /etc/fstab. 2) Remount: 'umount /var && mount -a' for the changes to take effect
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Non zero exit code 126_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand extension fails with "'powershell' is not recognized as an internal or external command" on Windows VM. PowerShell works fine via RDP but fails when run by the VM agent under Local System account
rootCause: The PowerShell path (C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0) is missing from the System PATH environment variable. Extensions run under Local System account which uses System PATH, not the user's PATH
solution: 1) Add 'C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0' to the System PATH environment variable (Control Panel → System → Advanced → Environment Variables → System variables). 2) Reboot the VM or restart the WindowsAzureGuestAgent service, then retry RunCommand
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Not recognized as an internal or external command_AGEX

symptom: Windows VM extension fails with '<command> is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file' in CommandExecution.log
rootCause: The PATH system environment variable is misconfigured, or cmd.exe has preconfigured commands that interfere with extension execution
solution: 1) Open Command Prompt in the VM and reproduce the error to identify which command is failing. 2) Check if the PATH variable contains the required executable path. 3) Check for preconfigured cmd.exe startup commands that may interfere
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Not recognized as an internal or external command_AGEX

symptom: OpenSSH extension (Microsoft.Azure.OpenSSH.WindowsOpenSSH) fails to install on Windows VM with exit code 2551
rootCause: OpenSSH on Windows Server 2016 is not supported by CSS. Only Windows Server 2019+ and Windows 10+ are supported by the Windows team
solution: For Windows Server 2016: OpenSSH is not supported, direct customer to GitHub issues (https://github.com/PowerShell/Win32-OpenSSH/issues). For Windows Server 2019+: engage Windows team using SAP 'Windows Servers/Windows Server 2022/System Management Components/OpenSSH (including SFTP)'. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/connect-ssh
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/OpenSSH extension troubleshooting_AGEX

symptom: Error 'Operation PUT Extension is not allowed on VM extension VMAppExtension since it is marked for deletion' when trying to delete VMApps extension; extension details missing under VM Extension tab
rootCause: VMApps extension stuck in 'marked for deletion' state, CRP cannot proceed with PUT while extension is pending delete. Sometimes caused by another extension (e.g., MDE) stuck in transitioning state.
solution: Use REST API Virtual_Machine_Update (PATCH) to clear applicationProfile.galleryApplications to empty array, then run ARMSync to refresh ARM cache, then retry VMApps deletion. If another extension is stuck in transitioning state, remove that extension first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Remove VMApps Extension_AGEX

symptom: Azure VM created from NetApp CVO (Cloud Volumes ONTAP) image shows notification about unsupported VM extensions; BlueXP management operations affected by extensions on CVO VMs
rootCause: Cloud Volumes ONTAP uses customized FreeBSD derivative, not Linux. Azure VM extensions are not supported on ONTAP. Starting from BlueXP 3.9.54, NetApp enforces this limitation via notifications.
solution: Remove all extensions via PowerShell: Invoke-AzRestMethod PUT with empty resources array ($vmModel.resources = @()). Prevent recurrence: Option 1 - Azure Policy deny for Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions; Option 2 - set AllowExtensionOperations=$false. Note: removal may take 20-30 min if Guest Agent absent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Removing Extensions NetApp CVO_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand extension fails to execute when command argument contains reserved character '&' (ampersand)
rootCause: The '&' character is a cmd.exe reserved character that splits the command line, causing unexpected execution behavior when passed as RunCommand parameter.
solution: Use caret (^) to escape '&' in the argument. Example: change $paramm='abc&jj' to $paramm='abc^&jj' when passing to Invoke-AzVMRunCommand -Parameter.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Run Command fails to execute if reserved characters are present_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand extension fails when script path contains backslash-n (e.g., C:\Windows\notepad.exe) because \n is interpreted as newline in the script payload, splitting the command
rootCause:
RunCommand extension script serialization treats 
 as a newline character. Paths like C:\Windows
otepad.exe become corrupted when 
 is parsed as line break.
solution: Replace lowercase \n with uppercase \N in file paths (e.g., use C:\Windows\System32\Notepad.exe), or use single quotes around paths to avoid escape interpretation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Run Command fails to execute if reserved characters are present_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand install/uninstall fails with 'failed to parse protected settings: decrypting protected settings failed' and 'openssl: version OPENSSL_3.x.x not found' errors on Linux VM
rootCause: Required OpenSSL library version is missing or incompatible on the Linux VM. RunCommand extension requires specific OpenSSL versions for decrypting protected settings.
solution: Check OpenSSL: rpm -qa | grep openssl (RPM) or apt list --installed | grep openssl (APT). If installed, reinstall: yum reinstall openssl* or apt-get install --reinstall openssl*. If missing: yum install openssl* or apt-get install openssl*.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/RunCommand Openssl Error_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand extension fails with error 'XYZ is not recognized as an internal or external command' where XYZ is the value of the AutoRun registry key
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Command Processor\AutoRun has a non-empty value. cmd.exe executes the AutoRun value before running RunCommand install.cmd, causing the error.
solution: Clear the AutoRun registry key: ensure HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Command Processor\AutoRun has an empty value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/RunCommand with CommandProcessor AutoRun_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand execution fails with error code 'RunCommandConflict' / 'Conflict': 'Run command extension execution is in progress. Please wait for completion before invoking a run command.'
rootCause: Another RunCommand operation is already in progress on the VM. Concurrent RunCommand execution is not supported; only one RunCommand can run at a time.
solution: Wait for existing RunCommand to complete (up to 90 min default timeout). If command appears hung, guide customer to login to VM, find the process running the script (PowerShell/cmd under SYSTEM account), and terminate it. Verify via CRP Kusto logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/RunCommandConflict_AGEX

symptom: Extension installation fails with 'the system cannot find the file specified, Code: 1007' or 'Failed to launch the plugin command, Code: 1009' in WaAppAgent.log on Windows VM
rootCause: ComSpec environment variable changed from 'c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe' to another value (e.g., Java/JDK path). Windows extensions use install.cmd/enable.cmd which require cmd.exe.
solution: Reset ComSpec: PowerShell [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('ComSpec', 'c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe', 'Machine'). Or CMD: SET ComSpec=c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe. Reboot VM for changes to take effect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/System Cannot Find the File Specified_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand v2 (Managed RunCommand) upgrade fails with 'InternalExecutionError/FabricInternalOperationError' and 'A different extension upgrade info (version) with the same key has already been added to extensionVersions'
rootCause: Upgrade from RunCommand v2 extension (Windows 2.0.4->2.0.5, Linux 1.3.1->1.3.2) conflicts with previously deployed RunCommand v2 resources due to key collision in extensionVersions.
solution: Delete all RunCommand v2 resources: Get-AzVmRunCommand then loop Remove-AzVMRunCommand (ignore errors). Verify count=0. Subsequent operations use latest version (2.0.5 Windows / 1.3.2 Linux).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/The fabric operation failed RunCommand_AGEX

symptom: Extension provisioning fails with 'Timed out waiting for extension [ExtensionName] to reach a terminal state. Failing the provisioning of the extension' in CRP ContextActivity
rootCause: Extension did not update status file with terminal state within 90-minute timeout. If picked up by a handler (e.g., AzureBackupWindowsWorkload by SqlIaaSAgent), the handler extension may have timed out. NOT a guest agent issue.
solution: Investigate handler/extension logs and involve the extension team. Check CRP ContextActivity for 'was picked up for the Handler' message to identify handler. GA only reports status file contents to CRP every 15 seconds.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Timed Out Waiting for Extension_AGEX

symptom: VMAppExtension deletion fails with error: Operation 'PUT Extension' is not allowed on VM extension 'VMAppExtension' since it is marked for deletion. Customer unable to remove the VmApp extension; subsequent ARM operations also fail.
rootCause: The VMApps extension is stuck in a failed/transitioning state in the ARM cache. Sometimes caused by another extension (e.g. MDE) stuck in transitioning state, blocking VMApps deletion.
solution: 1) Remove failed extension via Remove-AzVMExtension. 2) If persists, use REST API Virtual_Machine_Update to clear applicationProfile.galleryApplications (empty array). 3) Run ARMSync to refresh ARM cache. 4) Check for other extensions stuck in transitioning state and remove them first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Remove VMApps Extension_AGEX

symptom: Azure VM extensions fail or trigger BlueXP notifications on VMs created from NetApp Cloud Volumes ONTAP (CVO) images. Extensions cannot be installed on CVO VMs.
rootCause: Cloud Volumes ONTAP uses customized FreeBSD-based ONTAP OS (not Linux). Azure VM extensions are incompatible. Starting from BlueXP 3.9.54, NetApp enforces this limitation.
solution: Remove all extensions using PowerShell Invoke-AzRestMethod PUT with empty resources array (takes 20-30 min if no Guest Agent). Prevent recurrence by setting AllowExtensionOperations=false or creating Azure Policy to deny VM extensions. For China cloud use management.chinacloudapi.cn.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Removing Extensions NetApp CVO_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand extension fails to execute when command arguments contain the ampersand '&' character or other reserved characters.
rootCause: The '&' character is interpreted as a command separator by the Windows command processor, breaking the intended command argument.
solution: Use caret (^) to escape '&' in command arguments. Example: change 'abc&jj' to 'abc^&jj' in the -Parameter value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Run Command fails to execute if reserved characters are present_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand extension fails when script contains file paths with backslash-n sequence (e.g. C:\Windows\notepad.exe). The path is split at \n which is interpreted as newline.
rootCause: The \n sequence in file paths (e.g. \notepad.exe) is interpreted as a newline character by RunCommand, causing the command to be split incorrectly.
solution: Replace lowercase \n in file paths with uppercase \N. Use C:\Windows\Notepad.exe (capital N) instead of C:\Windows\notepad.exe.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/Run Command fails to execute if reserved characters are present_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand install/uninstall fails on Linux VM with error 'failed to parse protected settings: decrypting protected settings failed' and 'OPENSSL_3.x.x not found (required by openssl)'.
rootCause: Required OpenSSL library version (libssl.so.3, libcrypto.so.3) is missing or incompatible on the Linux VM.
solution: 1) Check OpenSSL: rpm -qa | grep openssl (RPM) or apt list --installed | grep openssl (APT). 2) If installed, reinstall: yum reinstall openssl* or apt-get install --reinstall openssl*. 3) If not installed: yum install openssl* or apt-get install openssl*.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/RunCommand Openssl Error_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand extension fails on Windows VM with error 'XYZ is not recognized as an internal or external command' (where XYZ is a seemingly random value from AutoRun registry).
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Command Processor\AutoRun is set to a non-empty value. cmd.exe executes this before RunCommand install.cmd, causing unrecognized command error.
solution: Clear the registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Command Processor\AutoRun to empty value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/RunCommand with CommandProcessor AutoRun_AGEX

symptom: RunCommand execution fails with Conflict error: 'Run command extension execution is in progress. Please wait for completion before invoking a run command.' (RunCommandConflict)
rootCause: A previous RunCommand operation is still in progress. Only one RunCommand can execute at a time. The existing command may be hung or stuck in an endless loop.
solution: 1) Wait for existing RunCommand to complete (up to 90 min). 2) If hung, log into VM, find the process (PowerShell/cmd under SYSTEM account) and terminate it. 3) Verify via Kusto CRP logs and ARM Job trace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/RunCommandConflict_AGEX

symptom: Extension install fails on Windows VM with 'the system cannot find the file specified, Code: 1007' or 'Failed to launch the plugin command... Code: 1009' in WaAppAgent.log.
rootCause: ComSpec environment variable changed from c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe to something else (e.g. Java/JDK path). Windows extensions need cmd.exe for install.cmd/enable.cmd.
solution: Set ComSpec back: [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('ComSpec', 'c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe', 'Machine'). Verify and reboot VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/System Cannot Find the File Specified_AGEX

symptom: VM Access Extension fails to reset password; RDP and serial console unavailable. VMAccessAgent reports password policy violation error.
rootCause: Local security policy (inherited from base image) enforces minimum password length. Short passwords fail VMAccess reset even through Azure portal.
solution: 1) Verify password policy with 'net accounts' command. 2) Temporarily lower minimum password length and disable complexity requirements. 3) Uninstall and reinstall VMAccess Extension. 4) Reset password through Azure portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VM Access FailureRDP Serial Console and Password Reset Unavailable_AGEX

symptom: VM fails to start or extension install fails with 'No version found in the artifact repository' or 'VM extension could not be found'.
rootCause: Extension publisher/type/version specified incorrectly, or extension was accidentally deleted from PIR by publisher.
solution: Remove the extension: Remove-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName <RG> -VMName <VM> -Name '<ExtName>'. Verify correct extension exists: Get-AzVMExtensionImage -Location <Loc> -PublisherName <Pub> -Type <Type>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VM fails to start due to extension missing in PIR_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess extension fails with 'CannotModifyExtensionsWhenVMNotRunning' - cannot modify extensions when VM is not running.
rootCause: VM is deallocated or stopped; extensions cannot be modified in this state.
solution: Confirm VM is up and running in Azure portal or ASC before retrying extension operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess Extension fails with 'VMAccess Extension does not support Domain Controller' when trying to reset password.
rootCause: Microsoft does not support resetting passwords on Domain Controllers via VMAccess Extension.
solution: Use Domain Controller password reset procedure instead. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/windows/reset-rdp
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess extension fails: 'The Admin User Account password cannot be null or empty if provided the username'.
rootCause: Password field was left empty when attempting to reset or create user account via VMAccess.
solution: Provide a non-empty password for the account when using VMAccess extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess extension fails: 'User account already exists but cannot be updated because it is not in the Administrators group'.
rootCause: Target user account exists but is not a member of the local Administrators group; VMAccess cannot update non-admin accounts.
solution: Add the user to the Administrators group on the DC/VM, then retry the VMAccess operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess extension fails with 'MultipleExtensionsPerHandlerNotAllowed' - multiple VMExtensions per handler not supported for Windows.
rootCause: Duplicate enablevmaccess extension entries exist for the same handler (Microsoft.Compute.VMAccessAgent).
solution: Remove the duplicate extension: Remove-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName '<RG>' -Name '<ExtName>' -VMName '<VM>', then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccessForLinux extension fails: 'Enable failed: No password or ssh_key is specified'.
rootCause: Neither password nor SSH key was provided in the VMAccess extension settings for Linux VM.
solution: Provide password or ssh_key information for the account in the VMAccess extension configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccessAgent fails with COMException 0x800706D9: 'There are no more endpoints available from the endpoint mapper' at EnableRemoteDesktopFirewallRules().
rootCause: Windows Firewall service (MpsSvc) is disabled. VMAccess cannot enumerate firewall rules when the service is not running.
solution: 1) Run Windows Guest Analyzer from ASC RE Diagnostics tab. 2) Check if MpsSvc service is disabled. 3) Enable and start Windows Firewall service, then retry VMAccess.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccessAgent Cannot update Remote Desktop Connection settings for Administrator account_AGEX

symptom: VMAccessForLinux extension fails: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xe2 in position 852. Extension version 1.5.10.
rootCause: Non-ASCII characters (e.g., UTF-8 encoded chars) present in /etc/ssh/sshd_config file. Extension tries latin-1 encoding and fails on non-ASCII bytes.
solution: 1) Snapshot OS disk. 2) Backup /etc/ssh/sshd_config. 3) Find non-ASCII chars with regex [^x00-x7F]+ in text editor. 4) Replace with keyboard-typed equivalents. 5) Save and restart sshd/VM. Fixed in VMAccessForLinux 1.5.11+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccessForLinux fails to enable ascii codec can't decode byte 0xe2_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent stuck on 'Started scanning OS to find JIT created user accounts'. WinGA reports 'Not Ready'. Observed on Windows 2008 R2 SP1 Domain Controllers.
rootCause: Missing Windows updates on older OS versions (Windows 2008 R2 SP1) cause WinGA to hang during JIT user account scanning.
solution: Install Windows Updates KB4523204, KB4533013, and KB4523205, then reboot the VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/WinGA stuck on Started scanning OS to find JIT created user account_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent logs 'StorageException...HttpStatusCode: 403 Forbidden' repeatedly when downloading VMArtifactsProfile.
rootCause: VNET Service Endpoint Policy restricts access to only certain Storage Accounts, blocking VMArtifactsProfile download from Azure storage.
solution: VMArtifactsProfile is now covered by Get VMSettings API call from HostGAPlugin; VNET Service Endpoint Policy should no longer cause this issue. If it resurfaces, contact AGEX SMEs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/403 Forbidden StorageException Unable To Download VMArtifactsProfile_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent status not reported. TransparentInstaller.log shows TimeoutException on http://168.63.129.16/?comp=versions after 2-minute timeout.
rootCause: Third-party antivirus or web filter (e.g., Symantec, Blue Coat Web Filter/Unified Agent) is blocking Guest Agent communication to Wire Server (168.63.129.16).
solution: 1) Check for 3rd party antivirus/web filter (Symantec, Blue Coat). 2) Confirm with Fiddler/Procmon trace if interference exists. 3) Disable antivirus/Blue Coat Agent and reboot VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Agent Status Not Reported_AGEX

symptom: VM fails to start or extension install fails with error 'No version found in the artifact repository that satisfies the requested version' or 'VM extension with publisher X could not be found'
rootCause: Extension and/or version specified by customer is incorrect, or in rare cases an extension publisher accidentally deleted the extension from PIR while VMs still use it
solution: Remove the extension: Remove-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName <RG> -VMName <VM> -Name <ExtName>. Verify correct extension exists: Get-AzVMExtensionImage -location <Location> -PublisherName <Publisher> -Type <Type>. If widespread PIR deletion suspected, escalate via IcM with SME/TA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VM fails to start due to extension missing in PIR_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess extension fails with 'VMAccess Extension does not support Domain Controller' when attempting password reset
rootCause: VMAccess does not support password reset on Domain Controller VMs
solution: Use Domain Controller-specific password reset procedure. See wiki VM-Password-Reset_RDP-SSH for DC instructions. Public doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/windows/reset-rdp
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess enablevmAccess fails with 'The password does not meet the password policy requirements' (error 0x800708C5 or TargetInvocationException)
rootCause: Password provided does not comply with the VM password policy (minimum length, complexity, or history requirements)
solution: Provide a password that meets the VM password policy requirements. Check minimum password length, complexity, and history settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess fails with 'Multiple VMExtensions per handler not supported for OS type Windows' - MultipleExtensionsPerHandlerNotAllowed
rootCause: Duplicate VMAccess extension (enablevmaccess) already registered on the VM with the same handler Microsoft.Compute.VMAccessAgent
solution: Manually remove the existing duplicate extension: Remove-AzVMExtension -ResourceGroupName <RG> -Name <ExtName> -VMName <VM>, then retry the operation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccess fails with 'User account already exists but cannot be updated because it is not in the Administrators group'
rootCause: Target user account exists on the VM but is not a member of the local Administrators group, so VMAccess cannot update its credentials
solution: Add the user to the Administrators group on the Domain Controller, then retry the VMAccess credentials update
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccess Errors_AGEX

symptom: VMAccessAgent fails with 'Cannot update Remote Desktop Connection settings for Administrator account. Error: 0x800706D9 - There are no more endpoints available from the endpoint mapper'
rootCause: Windows Firewall service (MpsSvc) is disabled on the VM, causing VMAccess to fail when trying to enumerate firewall rules
solution: Enable and start the Windows Firewall service (MpsSvc), then retry VMAccess. Windows Guest Analyzer will show 'MpsSvc is set as Disabled' in Findings tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccessAgent Cannot update Remote Desktop Connection settings for Administrator account_AGEX

symptom: VMAccessForLinux extension fails to enable with 'ascii codec cannot decode byte 0xe2' encoding error in sshd_config
rootCause: Non-ASCII characters (e.g., smart quotes 0xe2) present in /etc/ssh/sshd_config file cause encoding error in VMAccessForLinux extension (bug in versions before 1.5.11)
solution: 1. Snapshot OS disk. 2. Find non-ASCII chars in /etc/ssh/sshd_config using regex [^\x00-\x7F]+. 3. Remove and re-type those characters. 4. Save, restart sshd, retry VMAccess. Issue fixed in VMAccessForLinux 1.5.11.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/VMAccessForLinux fails to enable ascii codec can't decode byte 0xe2_AGEX

rootCause: Known bug on Windows 2008 R2 SP1 Domain Controllers where Guest Agent hangs during JIT user account scanning
solution: Install Windows updates KB4523204, KB4533013, KB4523205 and reboot the VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/Extension/WinGA stuck on Started scanning OS to find JIT created user account_AGEX

rootCause: VNET Service Endpoint Policy configured on customer VNET restricts Storage Account access, blocking Guest Agent from downloading VMArtifactsProfile
solution: VMArtifactsProfile is now covered by Get VMSettings API from HostGAPlugin, so VNET Service Endpoint Policy should no longer block it. If issue resurfaces, contact AGEX SME AVA Teams channel.

rootCause: Third-party antivirus or security software (e.g., Symantec, Blue Coat Web Filter/Unified Agent) blocks communication from Guest Agent to Host Wire Server
solution: Disable the interfering antivirus/security software (e.g., Blue Coat Agent) and reboot the VM. Confirm interference with Fiddler/Procmon trace showing a process blocking Wire Server communication.

rootCause: The password provided does not comply with the VM password complexity, length, or history requirements

rootCause: The specified user account exists but is not a member of the local Administrators group, so VMAccess cannot update its credentials
solution: Add the user to the Administrators group on the VM (or DC), then retry the VMAccess operation.

rootCause: Non-ASCII characters (e.g. smart quotes 0xe2) exist in /etc/ssh/sshd_config; VMAccessForLinux 1.5.10 fails trying to encode config as latin-1. Fixed in version 1.5.11.
solution: 1) Snapshot the OS disk. 2) Backup /etc/ssh/sshd_config. 3) Find non-ASCII chars using regex [^\x00-\x7F]+ in config. 4) Replace with ASCII equivalents. 5) Save, restart sshd, retry. Or upgrade VMAccessForLinux to >= 1.5.11.

rootCause: Duplicate VMAccess extension entries exist on the VM (multiple extensions with same handler Microsoft.Compute.VMAccessAgent)

rootCause: 3rd party antivirus or web filter (e.g. Symantec, Blue Coat Web Filter/Unified Agent) is blocking Guest Agent communication with the Wire Server (168.63.129.16)
solution: 1) Check for 3rd party antivirus or Blue Coat Agent. 2) Confirm with Fiddler/Procmon trace if Blue Coat Agent is interfering. 3) Disable the anti-virus/Blue Coat Agent and reboot the VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Agent Status Not Reported_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent crashing with System.BadImageFormatException: An attempt was made to load a program with an incorrect format (HRESULT: 0x8007000B) in WaAppAgent.log during extension installation
rootCause: A 3rd-party .NET application set HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\Enable64Bit to 0, causing 64-bit .NET apps (including Guest Agent) to be treated as 32-bit, resulting in DLL format mismatch
solution: Set registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\Enable64Bit to 1, then reboot the VM. Guest Agent and extensions should work after reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Attempt was Made to Load a Program with an Incorrect Format (Exception from HRESULT0x8007000B)_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent fails to start; Azure Portal shows VM Agent: Not Ready; Event ID 7000/7009 in System Event Log; Procmon shows mscoree.dll NAME NOT FOUND
rootCause: .NET Framework 4.x runtime is missing, corrupted, or disabled on the VM; core file mscoree.dll not present, preventing CLR initialization for the managed Guest Agent process
solution: Repair or reinstall .NET Framework 4.x: Install: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/install/on-windows-and-server; Repair: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/install/repair
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Azure Guest Agent service fails to start due to missing or corrupted .NET Framework components_AGEX

symptom: BFEToolWin8.exe logs appearing in WaAppAgent.log showing filter rules for 168.63.129.16 on ports 80/32526/8080; may be mistaken for connectivity issues
rootCause: BFEToolWin8.exe is a built-in Guest Agent component that manages Base Filtering Engine rules to restrict WireServer communication to System account and Administrators only. These logs are by design.
solution: No action needed. BFE logs are normal Guest Agent behavior and NOT a sign of connectivity issues. The Guest Agent runs under System account and is unaffected by these filtering rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/BFEToolWin8_Description_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM Agent Not Ready; extensions stuck Transitioning/Failed; waagent.log shows ConnectionRefusedError [Errno 111] to WireServer; nc -v 168.63.129.16 80/32526 times out; iptables filter table has rules blocking outbound to 168.63.129.16
rootCause: iptables filter table rules blocking connectivity to WireServer 168.63.129.16: either explicit block-all-internet with no WireServer exception, or OUTPUT chain default policy DROP without allow rule
solution: Add iptables rules: sudo iptables -I OUTPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -d 168.63.129.16 -j ACCEPT && sudo iptables -I OUTPUT -p tcp --dport 32526 -d 168.63.129.16 -j ACCEPT. Persist: Ubuntu/Debian apt install iptables-persistent; RHEL/SUSE dnf install iptables-services. Check for 3rd-party software resetting rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Connectivity to WireServer is blocked in Linux VMs_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM Agent Not Ready; WireServer unreachable; iptables empty but nft list ruleset shows blocking rules; firewalld or ufw is active
rootCause: nftables (used by firewalld on RHEL/SUSE or ufw on Ubuntu/Debian) has default blocking rules preventing outbound to WireServer, even when iptables rules appear empty
solution: Disable guest OS firewall: RHEL/SUSE: sudo systemctl stop firewalld && sudo systemctl disable firewalld. Ubuntu/Debian: sudo ufw disable. If persists, another software uses nftables -- customer must engage Linux/security admin.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Connectivity to WireServer is blocked in Linux VMs_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent not ready; WaAppAgent.log shows FileNotFoundException: Could not find file C:\WindowsAzure\zip.baseline during package validation (IsValidUnZip)
rootCause: Missing zip.baseline file in C:\WindowsAzure directory; per PG this is a benign error that does not impact Guest Agent functionality
solution: This error can be safely ignored per PG. The missing zip.baseline does not affect Guest Agent or extension operations. No action required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Could not find file 'C WindowsAzure zip.baseline'_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent and RDAgent crashing repeatedly on startup; Event ID 7031/7034; TransparentInstaller.log shows ConfigurationErrorsException for Microsoft.VisualStudio.Diagnostics.ServiceModelSink.Behavior in machine.config
rootCause: WCF profiling enabled in machine.config adds reference to Microsoft.VisualStudio.Diagnostics.ServiceModelSink assembly which cannot be loaded, crashing the Guest Agent .NET process
solution: Edit machine.config (C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config): backup, remove ServiceModelSink.Behavior add element and commonBehaviors section, restart services (net stop RdAgent/WindowsAzureGuestAgent/WindowsAzureTelemetryService then net start RdAgent). May need VM reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Crashing Due to WCF Profile Enabled_AGEX

symptom: Guest Agent or extensions fail with CryptographicException: Keyset does not exist, or Failed to decode/decrypt protected settings, or Invalid provider type specified, or Self-signed Certificate Generation failed Error Code -2146893808
rootCause: Incorrect ACL permissions on Crypto RSA MachineKeys folder for the Windows Azure CRP Certificate Generator tenant certificate private key file
solution: (1) Find key: (Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My | Where {$_.Subject -eq 'DC=Windows Azure CRP Certificate Generator'}).PrivateKey.CspKeyContainerInfo.UniqueKeyContainerName (2) Fix: icacls MachineKeys\<keyname> /grant SYSTEM:(F) and /grant Administrators:(RX) (3) Try SystemKeys folder if still failing (4) Enable SChannel trace and escalate if needed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Crypto or Certificate Errors with Windows Guest Agent_AGEX

symptom: Guest Agent Not Ready; VM status blob is found but not yet populated; TransparentInstaller.log shows EndpointNotFoundException to http://168.63.129.16/?comp=versions; ipconfig shows DHCP Enabled: No on guest NIC
rootCause: DHCP disabled on the VM network interface, causing IP configuration out of sync with Azure, breaking connectivity to WireServer 168.63.129.16
solution: For single-IP NIC: enable DHCP. For multi-IP NIC: statically assign all IPs. If DHCP correct, continue with WireServer Troubleshooting guide. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/what-is-ip-address-168-63-129-16
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/DHCP Not Enabled_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM provisioning fails; waagent.log shows cloud-init does not appear to be running with repeated retries; cloud-init.log shows AttributeError: module has no attribute JSONDecodeError when parsing IMDS metadata
rootCause: Resource Group or VM tags contain Unicode characters. IMDS returns these in metadata but cloud-init (especially Python 2.7) cannot parse them, causing JSON decode failure and OSProvisioningTimedOut
solution: Remove all Unicode characters from Resource Group and VM tags. Use only ASCII. Verify via Jarvis/ARM API. Ref: https://github.com/Azure/WALinuxAgent/issues/1933
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Daemon Cloud Init Does Not Appear to be Running_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent crashes with System.BadImageFormatException: An attempt was made to load a program with an incorrect format (HRESULT 0x8007000B). Error in WaAppAgent.log during extension plugin validation (PackageExpand/ValidateExtensionZipFile).
rootCause: A 3rd-party .NET application modified registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\Enable64Bit to 0, forcing 64-bit .NET apps to run as 32-bit. 64-bit process loading 32-bit DLLs triggers BadImageFormatException.
solution: Set registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\Enable64Bit to 1 and reboot the VM. Guest Agent and extensions will work normally after reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Attempt was Made to Load a Program with an Incorrect Format (Exception from HRESULT0x8007000B)_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent fails to start. Portal shows VM Agent: Not Ready. Extensions stuck in Transitioning/Failed. Event ID 7000/7009 in System log. Core .NET file mscoree.dll missing from System32 and Framework64 directories.
rootCause: .NET Framework 4.x runtime is missing, corrupted, or disabled. Guest Agent is a managed .NET process and cannot initialize CLR without mscoree.dll.
solution: Repair or reinstall .NET Framework 4.x. Install: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/install/on-windows-and-server. Repair: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/install/repair. Use ProcMon to confirm missing files.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Azure Guest Agent service fails to start due to missing or corrupted .NET Framework components_AGEX

symptom: BFEToolWin8.exe log entries in WaAppAgent.log showing Permit/Block filter rules for WireServer 168.63.129.16 (ports 80, 32526, 8080). Appears suspicious but is expected by-design behavior.
rootCause: BFEToolWin8.exe is a built-in Guest Agent component managing Base Filtering Engine (BFE/WFP) rules. It restricts WireServer access to SYSTEM and Administrators accounts only. Logs are by design.
solution: No action needed. BFEToolWin8 logs are expected behavior. The tool limits WireServer access to System/Administrators only. Guest Agent runs as System so functionality is not affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/BFEToolWin8_Description_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM Agent shows Not Ready, extensions stuck. waagent.log shows ConnectionRefusedError [Errno 111] to 168.63.129.16. nc -v 168.63.129.16 80/32526 times out. waagent service is running.
rootCause: iptables or nftables firewall rules block outbound to WireServer (168.63.129.16) on ports 80/32526. Scenarios: (1) iptables OUTPUT DROP rules without WireServer exception; (2) OUTPUT default policy DROP; (3) nftables (firewalld/ufw) default blocking.
solution: Add iptables rules: iptables -I OUTPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -d 168.63.129.16 -j ACCEPT and --dport 32526. For nftables: disable firewalld or ufw. Make persistent with iptables-persistent (Debian) or iptables-services (RHEL). Full guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-WireServer-blocked-Linux.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Connectivity to WireServer is blocked in Linux VMs_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent Not Ready. WaAppAgent.log: Could not find file C:\WindowsAzure\zip.baseline. FileNotFoundException during PackageValidator.IsValidUnZip.
rootCause: zip.baseline file missing from C:\WindowsAzure. Per PG, this error does not impact Guest Agent functionality.
solution: This error can be safely ignored per PG confirmation. Missing zip.baseline does not affect Guest Agent. Investigate other causes if VM Agent shows other Not Ready symptoms.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Could not find file 'C WindowsAzure zip.baseline'_AGEX

symptom: Guest Agent and RdAgent crash repeatedly on startup. Event ID 7031/7034 in System log. TransparentInstaller.log: ConfigurationErrorsException - type Microsoft.VisualStudio.Diagnostics.ServiceModelSink.Behavior could not be loaded (machine.config line 242).
rootCause: WCF profiling enabled in machine.config (Framework64/v4.0.30319/Config/machine.config). References missing ServiceModelSink assembly causing all .NET services including Guest Agent to crash.
solution: Edit machine.config: remove ServiceModelSink behavior registration and commonBehaviors block. Restart services: net stop Rdagent/WindowsAzureGuestAgent/WindowsAzureTelemetryService then net start Rdagent. May need VM reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Crashing Due to WCF Profile Enabled_AGEX

symptom: VM Agent Not Ready, status blob not yet populated. TransparentInstaller.log: GetVersions() failed - EndpointNotFoundException to 168.63.129.16:80. ipconfig /all shows DHCP Enabled: No on guest NIC.
rootCause: DHCP disabled on VM network adapter. Without DHCP, VM cannot route to WireServer (168.63.129.16), Guest Agent cannot communicate with Azure platform.
solution: Re-enable DHCP on NIC for single-IP scenarios. For multiple IPs, statically assign. If DHCP correct, continue WireServer troubleshooting per ADO wiki WireServer-Troubleshooting_AGEX page.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/DHCP Not Enabled_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM provisioning fails: cloud-init does not appear to be running in waagent.log. cloud-init.log shows IMDS fetch OK (200) but _get_data FAIL with AttributeError: module has no attribute JSONDecodeError. VM enters OSProvisioningTimedOut.
rootCause: Resource Group or VM tags contain Unicode characters (e.g. accented letters). IMDS returns Unicode tags to cloud-init which fails parsing on older Python 2.7 versions lacking json.decoder.JSONDecodeError.
solution: Remove Unicode characters from Resource Group name, RG tags, and VM tags. Use only ASCII characters. Ref: https://github.com/Azure/WALinuxAgent/issues/1933
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Daemon Cloud Init Does Not Appear to be Running_AGEX

rootCause: The .NET Framework 4.x runtime is missing or corrupted (core file mscoree.dll not present in System32 or Framework64 directory), preventing the Guest Agent from initializing the CLR
solution: Repair or reinstall .NET Framework 4.x: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/install/repair and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/install/on-windows-and-server
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Azure Guest Agent service fails to start due to missing or corrupted .NET Framework components_AGEX

rootCause: iptables filter table rules (set by customer or 3rd-party software) block outbound TCP to WireServer 168.63.129.16 on ports 80 and 32526
solution: Add iptables rules: sudo iptables -I OUTPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -d 168.63.129.16 -j ACCEPT and --dport 32526. Persist with iptables-persistent (Debian/Ubuntu) or iptables-services (RHEL/SUSE).

rootCause: nftables rules (managed by firewalld on RHEL/SUSE or UFW on Ubuntu/Debian) block outbound to WireServer 168.63.129.16
solution: Disable OS firewall: RHEL/SUSE: systemctl stop/disable firewalld. Ubuntu/Debian: ufw disable. If persists, another software uses nftables - engage Linux admin.

rootCause: The zip.baseline file is missing from WindowsAzure directory, causing package validation to log error
solution: This error can be safely ignored per PG confirmation - it does not impact Guest Agent functionality.

rootCause: WCF profiling enabled in machine.config loads ServiceModelSink.Behavior which conflicts with Guest Agent .NET runtime
solution: Edit machine.config at Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config: remove ServiceModelSink.Behavior add entry and commonBehaviors block. Restart services or reboot VM.

rootCause: Incorrect ACL permissions on Crypto Keys folder (MachineKeys) prevent Guest Agent from accessing tenant certificate private key
solution: Find UniqueKeyContainerName for cert DC=Windows Azure CRP Certificate Generator. Fix ACL: icacls MachineKeys\<name> /grant SYSTEM:(F) and Administrators:(RX). If failing try SystemKeys. Last resort: SChannel trace + Windows crypto team.

rootCause: DHCP disabled on guest NIC causes loss of connectivity to WireServer 168.63.129.16 as IP settings go out of sync with Azure
solution: Single IP: enable DHCP. Multiple IPs: statically assign. If DHCP correct, continue with WireServer Troubleshooting guide.

rootCause: Resource Group or VM tags contain Unicode characters. IMDS returns them to cloud-init which fails JSON parsing under Python 2.7 (lacks json.decoder.JSONDecodeError)

symptom: Linux VM running deprecated Azure Guest Agent version (WALinuxAgent v2.2.33/v2.2.35/v2.2.39) causing agent hangs, telemetry issues, or custom image problems
rootCause: Known bugs in specific deprecated Linux GA versions: v2.2.33 (agent hangs during update), v2.2.35 (telemetry issues), v2.2.39 (issue in custom images)
solution: Update Linux Guest Agent to the latest release. Use Kusto query on Vmainsight/CAD/CADDAILY filtering GA_GAVersion for deprecated versions to identify affected VMs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Deprecated GA_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent service hangs when trying to stop/start, extensions not functioning. Dynatrace OneAgent installed on the VM. oneagentproc.dll found loaded in WindowsAzureGuestAgent process modules.
rootCause: Dynatrace OneAgent injects its DLL (oneagentproc.dll) into the Windows Azure Guest Agent process, interfering with GA operations and causing service stop/start to hang.
solution: Customer must uninstall Dynatrace OneAgent or work with Dynatrace support to configure it to exclude the Guest Agent process from DLL injection. Verify by running: get-process WindowsAzureGuestAgent | select -ExpandProperty Modules to check for oneagentproc.dll.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Dynatrace OneAgent Causing GA Start Stop Operations To Hang_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent MSI install fails with error code -1073741819 in CA2 custom action. MSIEXEC.log shows CustomAction CA2 returned actual error code -1073741819.
rootCause: Third-party tool EaserSoft ExtremeCopy (or TortoiseSvn) interferes with the MSIEXEC install operation of the Windows Guest Agent.
solution: Remove the interfering 3rd party tool during WinGA install. If no known tool is installed, capture a PROCMON trace to identify what is interfering with the operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/EaserSoft ExtremeCopy_AGEX

symptom: Linux waagent reports Exception retrieving extension handlers: [ProtocolError] /var/lib/waagent/GoalState.1.xml is missing in DMESG/waagent.log.
rootCause: Stale or corrupted XML goal state files in /var/lib/waagent/ directory prevent the Linux Guest Agent from processing extension handlers.
solution: Run: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/*.[0-9]*.xml && sudo service walinuxagent restart
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Exception Retrieving Extension Handlers_AGEX

symptom: Linux waagent.log flooded with Failed to report status: NoneType object is not iterable errors. WALA version stuck at old version (e.g. 2.3.1.1), auto-update not working.
rootCause: Bug in older WALA versions (e.g. 2.3.1.1) with NoneType error in _process_substatus_list. Agent should auto-update but the auto-update mechanism itself failed.
solution: Manually update WALA: 1) Ensure AutoUpdate.Enabled=y in /etc/waagent.conf; 2) Download latest from GitHub WALinuxAgent releases; 3) Unzip to /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x; 4) Restart walinuxagent/waagent service; 5) Verify with waagent --version; 6) Run portal Reapply to refresh goal state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/ExtHandler Failed to Report Status NoneType Object is Not Iterable_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.log shows Failed to serialize aggregate status with error about null value in FormattedMessage member. Extension status shows formattedMessage: null in substatus entries.
rootCause: An extension (e.g. AzureBackupWindowsWorkload) incorrectly populates its status file with null formattedMessage fields, breaking the AggregateStatus.Json built by the Guest Agent.
solution: 1) Open latest .Status file in C:\Packages\Plugins\<Extension>\<Version>\Status\; 2) Manually populate null formattedMessage.message fields; 3) Restart Guest Agent; 4) If extension is VM-team owned, work with TA/SME for IcM; otherwise collaborate with extension owning team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Failed to Aggregate Status_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent reports unresponsive. WaAppAgent.log shows Failed to serialize aggregate status with required member missing error related to GuestAgentUpdateState registry key.
rootCause: After VM reboot, the message Registry Key under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\GuestAgentUpdateState was lost. GA fails to serialize GA update status because the required member is missing.
solution: 1) Check Registry Key LatestExpectedVersion under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\GuestAgentUpdateState; 2) Export/backup the key; 3) Delete LatestExpectedVersion key; 4) Restart WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe or WaAppAgent.exe.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Failed to Write Aggregate Status Due To Missing Registry Key Value_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent fails at startup with FATAL: Failed to set access rules for agent directories. Exception: IdentityNotMappedException: Some or all identity references could not be translated on C:\WindowsAzure\ or C:\Packages\.
rootCause: ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES group added to C:\WindowsAzure or C:\Packages folder permissions. GA cannot remove/set ACL on these folders during startup, entering crash loop.
solution: 1) Log in as local/Domain Admin; 2) Go to C:\Packages and C:\WindowsAzure -> Properties -> Security -> Edit; 3) Remove ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES; 4) Restart Guest Agent; 5) Check GPO is not re-setting this permission.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Fails to Set Access Rules for Agent Directories_AGEX

symptom: VM Agent status Not Ready, extensions stuck in Transitioning/Failed. WaAppAgent.log shows Unable to connect to remote server with SocketException to 168.63.129.16:32526 or :80. Test-NetConnection to WireServer times out.
rootCause: Windows Firewall or 3rd party guest software blocking outbound access to WireServer 168.63.129.16 on TCP ports 80/32526 required for HostGAPlugin. 168.63.129.16 is host node virtual IP, not subject to UDR/NSG.
solution: 1) Check: Get-NetFirewallRule -Enabled True -Direction Outbound -Action Block; 2) Identify rules blocking 168.63.129.16:80/32526; 3) Disable blocking rule; 4) If GPO-defined, run gpresult /h and engage AD team; 5) If no firewall rule, check 3rd party tools via WFP drops analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Firewall Blocking WireServer_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent failing with Goal state processing encountered an exception: System.NullReferenceException at GoalStateManager.GetExtensionConfigProcessor. Also IMDS errors: underlying connection closed unexpectedly.
rootCause: Third-party software (anti-virus etc.) on the VM intercepting and closing WireServer connection before response arrives, causing NullReferenceException in goal state processing.
solution: 1) Collect Host Analyzer to verify WireServer responded correctly; 2) Collect WinGuestAnalyzer, check Software tab for 3rd party software; 3) Uninstall suspected 3rd party software (start with recently added); 4) Restart Guest Agent services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Goal State Processing Encountered an Exception - Exception: System.NullReferenceException: Object Reference Not Set to an Instance of an Object_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM running deprecated Azure Guest Agent version (v2.2.33, v2.2.35, or v2.2.39) which have known bugs including agent hangs during update, telemetry issues, or custom image failures
rootCause: Specific Linux Guest Agent versions have critical bugs: v2.2.33 (agent hangs during update), v2.2.35 (telemetry issues), v2.2.39 (custom image compatibility issue). These versions have been deprecated.
solution: Update Linux Guest Agent to the latest version from https://github.com/Azure/WALinuxAgent/releases. Use Kusto query on cluster(Vmainsight).database(CAD).CADDAILY to identify VMs still running deprecated versions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Deprecated GA_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent service hangs on stop/start operations, extensions do not function. No notable errors in WaAppAgent.log. Dynatrace OneAgent installed and oneagentproc.dll loaded in GA process modules.
rootCause: Dynatrace OneAgent injects oneagentproc.dll into the WindowsAzureGuestAgent process via DLL injection, causing the GA service to hang during start/stop operations and preventing extension processing
solution: 1) Check WinGuestAnalyzer for Dynatrace OneAgent, 2) Verify with PowerShell: Get-Process WindowsAzureGuestAgent | Select -ExpandProperty Modules | Sort ModuleName - look for oneagentproc.dll, 3) Remove or reconfigure Dynatrace to exclude GA processes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Dynatrace OneAgent Causing GA Start Stop Operations To Hang_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent MSI installation fails with CustomAction CA2 returned error code -1073741819 in MSIEXEC.log during InstallOrUpdateGA.vbs execution
rootCause: 3rd party software EaserSoft ExtremeCopy (or TortoiseSvn) interferes with the MSIEXEC install operation, causing the CA2 custom action to crash with access violation (0xC0000005)
solution: Remove EaserSoft ExtremeCopy or other 3rd party file copy/SVN tools during Guest Agent MSI installation. If no known tool is installed, capture a PROCMON trace to identify the interfering process.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/EaserSoft ExtremeCopy_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM dmesg/waagent log shows: Exception retrieving extension handlers: [ProtocolError] /var/lib/waagent/GoalState.1.xml is missing
rootCause: Stale or corrupted GoalState XML files in /var/lib/waagent/ prevent the Linux extension handler from retrieving and processing extension configurations
solution: Remove stale GoalState XML files and restart agent: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/*.[0-9]*.xml && sudo service walinuxagent restart
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Exception Retrieving Extension Handlers_AGEX

symptom: Linux waagent.log flooded with WARNING ExtHandler Failed to report status: NoneType object is not iterable, stack trace in exthandlers.py collect_ext_status/_process_substatus_list. Agent version old (e.g. 2.3.1.1 or 2.4.0.2)
rootCause: Bug in older Linux Guest Agent versions (2.3.x/early 2.4.x) where extension status substatus list parsing fails with NoneType error. Agent auto-update may have failed to trigger.
solution: Update Linux Guest Agent: 1) Ensure AutoUpdate.Enabled=y in /etc/waagent.conf, 2) Download latest from GitHub releases, 3) Unzip to /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x, 4) Restart (systemctl restart walinuxagent or waagent), 5) Verify waagent --version, 6) Test WireServer connectivity, 7) Run reapply from portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/ExtHandler Failed to Report Status NoneType Object is Not Iterable_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent WaAppAgent.log shows Failed to serialize aggregate status: Member message in type FormattedMessage cannot be serialized, null value serialization conflict. AggregateStatus.Json broken.
rootCause: An extension (e.g. AzureBackupWindowsWorkload) writes null in formattedMessage.message of its status file under C:\Packages\Plugins\<Ext>\<Ver>\Status\, causing GA aggregate status serialization to fail
solution: 1) Inspect each extension latest .Status file in C:\Packages\Plugins\<Ext>\<Ver>\Status\, 2) Find null formattedMessage.message fields, 3) Manually populate with valid string, 4) Restart Guest Agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Failed to Aggregate Status_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent unresponsive after VM reboot. WaAppAgent.log shows Failed to serialize aggregate status: Member message in type IaaS.FormattedMessage cannot be serialized, required member missing
rootCause: Registry key message under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\GuestAgentUpdateState lost during reboot, leaving only LatestExpectedVersion without required sub-keys (State, Message, Code, StartTime)
solution: 1) Check registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\GuestAgentUpdateState, 2) Export/backup, 3) Delete LatestExpectedVersion key, 4) Restart WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe or WaAppAgent.exe.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Failed to Write Aggregate Status Due To Missing Registry Key Value_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent crashes in loop at startup: FATAL Failed to set access rules for agent directories. IdentityNotMappedException: Some or all identity references could not be translated, when ACL on C:\WindowsAzure or C:\Packages
rootCause: ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES group (or other unmapped identity) in ACL of C:\WindowsAzure or C:\Packages, possibly set by GPO. GA cannot translate this identity when removing/setting permissions at startup.
solution: 1) C:\Packages and C:\WindowsAzure > Properties > Security > Edit, 2) Remove ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES, 3) Restart Guest Agent, 4) Check GPO (gpresult /h gpreport.html) and disable if re-applying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Fails to Set Access Rules for Agent Directories_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent: Goal state processing encountered an exception: System.NullReferenceException in GoalStateManager.GetExtensionConfigProcessor. IMDS calls fail with underlying connection closed unexpectedly. Cannot get goal state for extended period.
rootCause: 3rd party software (anti-virus, security tool) on VM intercepting and prematurely closing TCP connection to WireServer (168.63.129.16) before response arrives, preventing goal state retrieval
solution: 1) Collect Host Analyzer to verify WireServer responds correctly, 2) Check WinGuestAnalyzer Software tab for 3rd party software, 3) Uninstall suspected software (start with those added before issue began), 4) Restart Guest Agent services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Goal State Processing Encountered an Exception - Exception: System.NullReferenceException: Object Reference Not Set to an Instance of an Object_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent waagent.log grows abnormally large with repeated HTTP 404 errors fetching from stale RDFE blob URIs (BlobNotFound) every hour
rootCause: VM did not receive new goal state after RDFE-to-CAPS migration, causing agent to keep polling obsolete RDFE manifest URIs and logging errors repeatedly
solution: Trigger new goal state via 'az vm reapply' or PowerShell Set-AzVM -Reapply. For agents older than v2.8, also restart walinuxagent service after reapply (sudo systemctl restart walinuxagent.service)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Guest Agent Increased Log Size_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent and RdAgent services crash on startup with ConfigurationErrorsException / TypeInitializationException in Application Event Log (Event ID 1026, .NET Runtime)
rootCause: machine.config file at C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config\machine.config is missing or corrupted
solution: Copy machine.config from a working VM to C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config\, then restart RdAgent and WindowsAzureGuestAgent services (net stop/start RdAgent and WindowsAzureGuestAgent)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Guest Agent Not Starting with ConfigurationErrorExcepition_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent not auto-upgrading despite being Ready and services running; TransparentInstaller.log shows 'Manifest timestamp has not changed'
rootCause: ManifestTimeStamp registry value (HKLM\Software\Microsoft\GuestAgent) retains timestamp from a previously failed upgrade attempt, preventing new upgrade checks
solution: Delete ManifestTimeStamp registry value from HKLM\Software\Microsoft\GuestAgent, then restart RdAgent and WindowsAzureGuestAgent services from services.msc
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Guest agent not auto upgrading due to matching manifest timestamp_AGEX

symptom: Azure Portal shows incorrect OS distribution/version for Linux VM after in-place OS upgrade (e.g., CentOS 7.5 to Oracle 8.10 but portal still shows old OS)
rootCause: Multiple or outdated OS release files (/etc/os-release, /etc/redhat-release, etc.) remain on the VM after upgrade, causing Guest Agent distro detection functions to read incorrect file
solution: Remove or move outdated OS release files from /etc/ keeping only the file matching current OS, then restart walinuxagent (sudo systemctl restart walinuxagent) and verify in portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Incorrect OS Displayed Portal_AGEX

symptom: Guest Agent fails TLS connections with 'The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure' error in WaAppAgent.log
rootCause: Missing Baltimore CyberTrust Root certificate in Trusted Root Certification Authorities store, or third-party SSL inspection (e.g., Zscaler) breaking the certificate chain
solution: Install Baltimore CyberTrust Root certificate from DigiCert or copy from a working VM, then restart the VM. For SSL inspection, configure Zscaler to bypass SSL inspection for Azure traffic
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Invalid Remote Certificate_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent fails to start with 'No such file or directory' error referring to Python in waagent.log (broken python symlink)
rootCause: Python symbolic link at /usr/bin/python is broken or pointing to a non-existent Python version
solution: Remove broken symlink (rm /usr/bin/python), create correct link (ln -s /usr/bin/python2 /usr/bin/python), then restart waagent (sudo systemctl restart waagent)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux GA Not Starting No such file or directory_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent fails to start with Python import errors (IsADirectoryError on /etc/motd, missing azurelinuxagent modules) — python3 binaries missing
rootCause: Python3 binaries or packages are missing from the system, causing walinuxagent to fail at import
solution: Reinstall python3: apt install -f --reinstall python3 byobu ubuntu-server. If dpkg error, first rm /var/lib/dpkg/status && touch /var/lib/dpkg/status, then reinstall
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux GA Not Starting No such file or directory_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent fails to start after zonal migration with PermissionError removing extension config files (e.g., Qualys.QualysAgentLinux settings) — VM unique identifier changed
rootCause: Stale file handles in /var/lib/waagent from previous extension configurations cause PermissionError during provisioning cleanup after VM unique ID change during zonal migration
solution: Stop waagent (systemctl stop waagent.service), backup waagent dir (mv /var/lib/waagent /var/lib/waagent.bak), reinstall waagent (yum reinstall waagent)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux GA Not Starting No such file or directory_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM provisioning fails with 'Error mounting dvd: Failed to get dvd device from /dev' or 'Failed to mount dvd device, wrong fs type' in waagent.log
rootCause: VM created from specialized disk (VHD directly attached, not from image) which does not include ovf-env.xml provisioning file; or UDF filesystem support blocked in custom image
solution: Create VM from image instead of specialized disk. If ovf-env.xml is required, create a new VM with same SSH credentials from an image, then copy ovf-env.xml to the affected VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux Guest Agent Error Mounting DVD_AGEX

symptom: C:\WindowsAzure\Logs directory filling up with RuntimeEvents_xxxxxx.etl.old and WaAppAgent_xxxxxx.etl.old files consuming disk space on Windows VM
rootCause: Known bug in older versions of Windows VM Agent (before version 2.7.41491.969) causing excessive ETL log file accumulation without cleanup
solution: Upgrade Windows VM Agent to latest version (2.7.41491.1030+ recommended) from https://github.com/Azure/WindowsVMAgent/releases — the issue self-mitigates after upgrade
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Logs filling up with RuntimeEvents_xxxxxx.etl.old and WaAppAgent_xxxxxx.etl.old consuming disk space_AGEX
