symptom: Linux Guest Agent versions v2.2.33, v2.2.35, v2.2.39 are deprecated. v2.2.33 hangs during update, v2.2.35 has telemetry issues, v2.2.39 breaks custom images
rootCause: Known bugs in specific deprecated Linux GA versions
solution: Update Linux Guest Agent to the latest release. Check current version via Kusto query against Vmainsight CAD table
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Deprecated GA_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent service hangs during stop/start operations. Extensions not functioning. Dynatrace OneAgent installed on VM with oneagentproc.dll injected into GA process
rootCause: Dynatrace OneAgent injects oneagentproc.dll into the WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe process, causing it to hang during stop/start
solution: Uninstall Dynatrace OneAgent or work with Dynatrace support to stop DLL injection. Verify by checking loaded modules: get-process WindowsAzureGuestAgent | select -ExpandProperty Modules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Dynatrace OneAgent Causing GA Start Stop Operations To Hang_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent MSI installation fails with error code -1073741819 in MSIEXEC.log during CA2 custom action (InstallOrUpdateGA.vbs)
rootCause: 3rd party tool EaserSoft ExtremeCopy (or TortoiseSvn) interferes with the MSIEXEC install operation
solution: Remove the 3rd party tool during WinGA install. If not installed, take a PROCMON trace to identify what is interfering
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/EaserSoft ExtremeCopy_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM DMESG: Exception retrieving extension handlers: [ProtocolError] /var/lib/waagent/GoalState.1.xml is missing
rootCause: Corrupted or missing GoalState XML files in /var/lib/waagent/ directory
solution: Run: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/*.[0-9]*.xml && sudo service walinuxagent restart
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Exception Retrieving Extension Handlers_AGEX

symptom: waagent.log flooded with Failed to report status: NoneType object is not iterable. Agent version stuck at 2.3.1.1, auto-update not working
rootCause: Outdated Linux Guest Agent version (2.3.1.1) with bug in status reporting (_process_substatus_list). Agent self-update mechanism failed
solution: Manually update Linux GA: 1) waagent --version, 2) Enable AutoUpdate in /etc/waagent.conf, 3) Download latest from GitHub releases, 4) Place in /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x. Take OS disk snapshot first
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/ExtHandler Failed to Report Status NoneType Object is Not Iterable_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.log: Failed to serialize aggregate status - DataMember IsRequired conflict, member message cannot be serialized due to null value
rootCause: An extension incorrectly populates its status file with null formattedMessage.message (e.g. AzureBackupWindowsWorkload), breaking AggregateStatus.json
solution: Find offending extension .Status file in C:\Packages\Plugins\<Ext>\<Ver>\Status\, manually populate formattedMessage.message, restart GA. Escalate to extension owning team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Failed to Aggregate Status_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent unresponsive after reboot. WaAppAgent.log: Failed to serialize aggregate status, required member message missing
rootCause: After VM reboot, message Registry Key under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\GuestAgentUpdateState was lost
solution: Delete Registry Key LatestExpectedVersion under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\GuestAgentUpdateState (backup first), restart WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe or WaAppAgent.exe
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Failed to Write Aggregate Status Due To Missing Registry Key Value_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent FATAL: Failed to set access rules for agent directories. IdentityNotMappedException - Some or all identity references could not be translated
rootCause: ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES group added to C:\WindowsAzure or C:\Packages folder permissions (possibly via GPO). GA cannot translate identity references when setting ACLs
solution: Remove ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES from C:\Packages and C:\WindowsAzure Security permissions, restart GA. Check GPO is not re-adding. Verify: Get-Acl -Path c:\packages
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Fails to Set Access Rules for Agent Directories_AGEX

symptom: VM Agent status Not Ready in ASC/Portal. Extensions stuck Transitioning/Failed. WaAppAgent.log: Unable to connect to 168.63.129.16:32526 (or :80) - access forbidden
rootCause: Windows Firewall or 3rd party security software blocking outbound TCP access to WireServer 168.63.129.16 on ports 80 and 32526
solution: Check firewall: Get-NetFirewallRule -Enabled True -Direction Outbound -Action Block. Identify/disable rules blocking 168.63.129.16. If GPO-defined, customer IT admin must modify. Ref: https://aka.ms/agexTSG009
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Firewall Blocking WireServer_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent: Goal state processing exception System.NullReferenceException and IMDS connection errors (underlying connection closed unexpectedly)
rootCause: 3rd party software (anti-virus etc.) on the VM intercepting and closing connections to WireServer/IMDS before response is received
solution: Collect Host Analyzer to verify WireServer communication. Collect WinGuestAnalyzer to identify installed software. Uninstall 3rd party software (start with recent installs), restart GA services
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Goal State Processing Encountered an Exception - Exception: System.NullReferenceException: Object Reference Not Set to an Instance of an Object_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM Guest Agent logs (waagent.log/syslog) growing excessively large with repeated 404 errors fetching from rdfepirv2 blob URIs every hour (BlobNotFound). Non-endorsed distros may see higher impact.
rootCause: VMs not migrated from RDFE to CAPS still reference old RDFE manifest URIs. Agent checks for updates hourly and logs 404 errors, causing log growth (~15MB/month).
solution: Trigger a new goal state via 'az vm reapply' to update manifests from RDFE to CAPS URIs. For VMs with Guest Agent < 2.8, also restart the Guest Agent service after reapply.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Guest Agent Increased Log Size_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent and/or RdAgent services crash on startup. Application Event Log shows Event ID 1026 with System.Configuration.ConfigurationErrorsException and TypeInitializationException in WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe.
rootCause: The .NET machine.config file at C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config\machine.config is missing or corrupted.
solution: Copy machine.config from a working VM to the same path, then restart Guest Agent services: net stop/start RdAgent and WindowsAzureGuestAgent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Guest Agent Not Starting with ConfigurationErrorExcepition_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent is Ready and services are running, but the agent is not auto-upgrading. TransparentInstaller.log shows 'Manifest timestamp has not changed' with matching LastModifiedHeader and registry ManifestTimeStamp values.
rootCause: A previous failed upgrade attempt updated the ManifestTimeStamp registry value, preventing future upgrade attempts even when a newer version is available.
solution: Delete the ManifestTimeStamp value from registry key HKLM\Software\Microsoft\GuestAgent, then restart RdAgent and WindowsAzureGuestAgent services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Guest agent not auto upgrading due to matching manifest timestamp_AGEX

symptom: Azure Portal Properties page shows incorrect OS distribution/version after an in-place OS upgrade (e.g. CentOS 7.5 to Oracle 8.10).
rootCause: Multiple or outdated OS release files (/etc/os-release, /etc/redhat-release, etc.) remain after in-place upgrade, causing the Guest Agent distro detection to read stale data.
solution: Remove or relocate outdated OS release files in /etc/ (keep only the one matching the current OS), then restart agent: sudo systemctl restart walinuxagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Incorrect OS Displayed Portal_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.log shows 'The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure' (System.Net.WebException / AuthenticationException). Guest Agent cannot establish SSL/TLS connection.
rootCause: Baltimore CyberTrust Root certificate is missing from the Trusted Root Certification Authorities store (certmgr.msc).
solution: Install the Baltimore CyberTrust Root certificate from DigiCert Root Certificates page or copy from a working VM, then restart the VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Invalid Remote Certificate_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.log shows 'The remote certificate is invalid' with SSL/TLS failure, despite root certificates being present.
rootCause: Third-party SSL inspection (e.g. Zscaler) is breaking the certificate chain by intercepting traffic to Azure endpoints.
solution: Configure the SSL inspection proxy (e.g. Zscaler) to bypass SSL inspection for Azure Guest Agent communication endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Invalid Remote Certificate_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent fails with 'No such file or directory' in waagent.log when launching with 'python -u /usr/sbin/waagent -run-exthandlers'. waagent service cannot start.
rootCause: The /usr/bin/python symbolic link is broken or pointing to a non-existent Python version.
solution: Remove the broken symlink (rm /usr/bin/python), recreate it pointing to correct Python (ln -s /usr/bin/python2 /usr/bin/python), then restart: sudo systemctl restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux GA Not Starting No such file or directory_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent fails to start with 'IsADirectoryError: [Errno 21] Is a directory: /etc/motd' or 'Failed at step EXEC spawning /usr/bin/python3: No such file or directory'. waagent --version shows traceback.
rootCause: Python3 binaries are missing or corrupt on the system, preventing walinuxagent from loading its modules.
solution: Reinstall python3: apt install -f --reinstall python3 byobu ubuntu-server. If dpkg errors occur, reset dpkg status first (rm then touch /var/lib/dpkg/status), then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux GA Not Starting No such file or directory_AGEX

symptom: After zonal migration, WALinuxAgent fails to start with 'PermissionError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted' when trying to remove extension settings file (e.g. Qualys.QualysAgentLinux .settings).
rootCause: Zonal migration left stale file handles in /var/lib/waagent/ for extension config files, causing PermissionError when agent tries to deprovision/clear cached state.
solution: Stop waagent (systemctl stop waagent.service), move waagent dir (mv /var/lib/waagent /var/lib/waagent.bak), reinstall waagent (yum reinstall waagent).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux GA Not Starting No such file or directory_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM provisioning fails with 'Provisioning failed: [ProtocolError] [CopyOvfEnv] Error mounting dvd: [OSUtilError] Failed to get dvd device from /dev' or 'wrong fs type, bad superblock on /dev/sr0'.
rootCause: VM was created from a specialized disk (not an image), so no ISO with ovf-env.xml is provided. Alternatively, the custom image has UDF filesystem support disabled.
solution: Create VM from an image (not specialized disk) if ovf-env.xml is needed. If UDF is blocked, re-enable kernel UDF support. Workaround: copy ovf-env.xml from a correctly provisioned VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Linux Guest Agent Error Mounting DVD_AGEX

symptom: C:\WindowsAzure\Logs directory fills up with RuntimeEvents_xxxxxx.etl.old and WaAppAgent_xxxxxx.etl.old files, consuming significant disk space.
rootCause: Bug in older Windows VM Agent versions causing ETL log files not to be cleaned up properly.
solution: Upgrade Windows VM Agent to latest version (fix introduced in 2.7.41491.969, improved in 2.7.41491.1030+). See https://github.com/Azure/WindowsVMAgent/releases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Logs filling up with RuntimeEvents_xxxxxx.etl.old and WaAppAgent_xxxxxx.etl.old consuming disk space_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent stuck on old version; waagent --version shows outdated Goal state agent version; waagent.log shows agent permanently blacklisted after abnormal termination
rootCause: New version of Guest Agent terminated abnormally (e.g., WireServer connectivity failure), creating error.json with was_fatal:true, permanently blacklisting the newer agent version
solution: 1. Fix WireServer connectivity (168.63.129.16). 2. Find blacklisted agent folder under /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x/. 3. Delete error.json: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x/error.json. 4. Restart agent: sudo systemctl restart walinuxagent. 5. Verify with waagent --version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Old GoalState Agent Version Due To Blacklist_AGEX

symptom: VM Agent fails with 'Unable to perform the operation as the VM agent is not responsive'; waagent -version shows 'Oracle is not a supported distribution'
rootCause: Outdated Azure Linux Agent version on Oracle Linux VM does not recognize Oracle as a supported distribution
solution: 1. Enable ol7_addons repo: edit /etc/yum.repos.d/public-yum-ol7.repo, set enabled=1 under [ol7_addons]. 2. Install latest agent: sudo yum install WALinuxAgent. 3. Update: sudo yum update WALinuxAgent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Oracle is not a Supported Distro_AGEX

symptom: TransparentInstaller.log shows System.TypeLoadException: Could not load type 'System.Runtime.Diagnostics.ITraceSourceStringProvider' from assembly 'System.ServiceModel.Internals'
rootCause: Outdated .NET Framework version on the VM (below 4.5.2)
solution: Update .NET Framework to version 4.5.2 or 4.6.2 depending on OS. Download: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=42642
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Outdated .Net_AGEX

symptom: VM extension deployment fails on Linux distributions with Python 3.x (Ubuntu 20.04, RHEL 8.1, CentOS 8.1); affected extensions: ADE, Log Analytics, VM Access, Guest Diagnostics
rootCause: Linux distributions transitioned to Python 3.x and removed legacy /usr/bin/python entrypoint; extensions still transitioning to Python 3.x require this entrypoint
solution: Reinstate /usr/bin/python symlink: For Ubuntu: sudo apt update && sudo apt install python-is-python2. For RHEL/CentOS: sudo dnf install python2 && sudo alternatives --set python /usr/bin/python2. For new VMs, use cloud-init to install python2 at provisioning time.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Python 3.X_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.log shows RdCrypt Initialization failed Error Code -2147023143 (0x6d9 EPT_S_NOT_REGISTERED); Failed to get TransportCertificate with System.AccessViolationException
rootCause: CNG Key Isolation (KeyIso) Windows service is disabled or not running, causing RPC endpoint to be unavailable for certificate operations
solution: 1. Verify CNG Key Isolation service (KeyIso) status. 2. Start the CNG Key Isolation service. 3. Restart WaAppAgent.exe / WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe. Optionally run portqry -n <VMName> -e 135 to check if CNGKEYISO is in RPC endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RPC Issues_AGEX

symptom: RdAgent service (WaAppAgent.exe) fails to start with Error 1067; MonitoringAgent.log shows Access to the path C:\Windows\system32\VMAgentDisabler.dll is denied
rootCause: VMAgentDisabler.dll in C:\Windows\System32\ has the hidden attribute set, causing the Guest Agent transparent installer to fail loading it despite correct file permissions
solution: Remove the hidden attribute from VMAgentDisabler.dll: Run elevated command prompt: attrib -H c:\windows\system32\VMAgentDisabler.dll. Alternatively, right-click file > Properties > uncheck Hidden.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RdAgent Could Not Start Error 1607

symptom: Windows Guest Agent status not reported; WaAppAgent.log shows 'Received the Identity data response, environment is OnPrem' and 'Current environment is: OnPrem'; IMDS endpoint returns unexpected response instead of 404
rootCause: Highly restrictive network lockdown or proxy filtering on the VM prevents IMDS endpoint (169.254.169.254) from returning expected 404 response, causing Guest Agent to misidentify environment as on-premises
solution: Coordinate with network/security team to ensure IMDS IP 169.254.169.254 is accessible and returns expected responses. Remove proxy/firewall rules blocking this address. Collect PROCMON and FIDDLER trace while restarting Guest Agent for further diagnosis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Received the Identity Data Response Environment OnPrem_AGEX

symptom: Rocky Linux 8 VM is shown as centos in Azure Portal Operating System field; waagent.log shows OS: centos 8.7 instead of rocky 8.7
rootCause: Python version in Rocky Linux 8 is < 3.8; get_distro() with Python < 3.8 picks up /etc/centos-release (symlink to rocky-release) as first file in /etc/*-release glob, misidentifying OS as centos
solution: Remove centos-release and redhat-release symlinks: sudo rm /etc/centos-release /etc/redhat-release. Guest agent will then read /etc/rocky-release correctly. DO NOT update Python version as it causes other OS problems. Alternatively upgrade to Rocky Linux 9.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Rocky Linux 8 VM is shown as centos in Portal_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent not ready; waagent.log shows repeated WireServer endpoint not found / WireServer not responding loop; VM has RunCommandHandlerLinux (Run Command v2) installed; Guest Agent version < 2.4.0.2
rootCause: Guest Agent versions prior to 2.4.0.2 do not support multiconfig which Managed Run Command v2 (Microsoft.CPlat.Core.RunCommandHandlerLinux) requires, causing the agent to enter a failure loop
solution: Update Guest Agent to version 2.4.0.2 or above: 1. Ensure AutoUpdate.Enabled=y in /etc/waagent.conf, restart agent service. 2. If repo version is too old, copy /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x/ directory from a working VM with agent >= 2.4.0.2 to the broken VM, then restart agent service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RunCommandv2 on old Guest Agent versions_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent (waagent) stays on old version and does not auto-update; Goal state agent shows outdated version; waagent.log shows Agent WALinuxAgent-X.X.X.X is permanently blacklisted
rootCause: New version of Guest Agent terminated abnormally (e.g. due to WireServer connectivity issues at launch), creating error.json with was_fatal:true, which permanently blacklists that version
solution: 1. Fix WireServer connectivity to 168.63.129.16 first. 2. Find blacklisted version folder: ls /var/lib/waagent | grep WALinuxAgent. 3. Delete error.json: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-X.X.X.X/error.json. 4. Restart agent: sudo systemctl restart walinuxagent. 5. Optionally reapply VM from Portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Old GoalState Agent Version Due To Blacklist_AGEX

symptom: Oracle Linux VM shows Unable to perform the operation as the VM agent is not responsive; waagent --version returns Oracle is not a supported distribution
rootCause: Outdated Azure Linux Agent version does not recognize Oracle Linux as a supported distribution
solution: 1. Enable Oracle Addons repo: edit /etc/yum.repos.d/public-yum-ol7.repo, set enabled=1 under [ol7_addons]. 2. Install latest agent: sudo yum install WALinuxAgent. 3. Update: sudo yum update WALinuxAgent
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Oracle is not a Supported Distro_AGEX

symptom: TransparentInstaller.log shows System.TypeLoadException: Could not load type System.Runtime.Diagnostics.ITraceSourceStringProvider from assembly System.ServiceModel.Internals
rootCause: Outdated .NET Framework version on Windows VM
solution: Update .NET Framework to 4.5.2 or 4.6.2 depending on OS: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=42642
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Outdated .Net_AGEX

symptom: Squid proxy on VM causes HTTP 503 when Guest Agent tries to reach WireServer 168.63.129.16; wasetup.xml shows SendRequest status=503; Wireshark shows SQUID in HTTP headers
rootCause: Squid proxy intercepts and blocks HTTP requests to WireServer 168.63.129.16, returning 503 Service Unavailable
solution: Configure Squid proxy to allow direct access to WireServer 168.63.129.16 without interception or packet modification
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Proxy Issues_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.log shows repeated Response status code does not indicate success: 502 (cannotconnect) for GetVersions(); note: normal HTTP 502 says Bad Gateway not cannotconnect
rootCause: Third-party proxy (McAfee Client Proxy or Skyhigh Client Proxy) intercepts traffic to WireServer 168.63.129.16 and returns non-standard HTTP 502 cannotconnect
solution: Check WinGuestAnalyzer Software tab for installed proxies. Disable McAfee/Skyhigh Client Proxy or configure to exclude traffic to 168.63.129.16
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Proxy Issues_AGEX

symptom: VM extension deployment fails on Linux distros with Python 3.x (Ubuntu 20.04, RHEL 8.1, CentOS 8.1); affected: ADE, Log Analytics, VMAccess, Guest Diagnostics
rootCause: Linux distributions transitioning to Python 3.x removed legacy /usr/bin/python entrypoint; extensions still referencing this path fail to launch
solution: Reinstall python symlink: Ubuntu: sudo apt install python-is-python2; RHEL/CentOS: sudo dnf install python2 && sudo alternatives --set python /usr/bin/python2. For new VMs use cloud-init. RunCommand and CustomScript NOT affected
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Python 3.X_AGEX

symptom: RdAgent service fails to start with Error 1067 The process terminated unexpectedly; MonitoringAgent.log shows Access to the path C:\Windows\system32\VMAgentDisabler.dll is denied; Event 7034 repeats
rootCause: VMAgentDisabler.dll has the hidden attribute set, preventing Guest Agent from loading it despite correct NTFS permissions
solution: Remove hidden attribute from elevated cmd: attrib -H C:\Windows\System32\VMAgentDisabler.dll. Then restart RdAgent service
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RdAgent Could Not Start Error 1607

symptom: Windows Guest Agent reports environment is OnPrem in WaAppAgent.log; agent status not reported; log shows Received the Identity data response environment is OnPrem from IMDS 169.254.169.254
rootCause: Network proxy or restrictive firewall intercepts requests to IMDS endpoint 169.254.169.254, returning response instead of expected 404 Not Found, causing WinGA to think VM is on-premises
solution: Ensure IMDS endpoint 169.254.169.254 is accessible and returns expected 404 for /Microsoft.Compute/identity. Work with network/security team to whitelist. Collect PROCMON+FIDDLER while restarting GA for diagnosis
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Received the Identity Data Response Environment OnPrem_AGEX

symptom: Rocky Linux 8 VM shows as centos in Azure Portal Operating System; waagent.log shows OS: centos 8.7; Rocky Linux 9 NOT affected
rootCause: Python < 3.8 in Rocky Linux 8: get_distro() picks up /etc/centos-release symlink as first in /etc/*-release glob, reporting OS as centos instead of rocky
solution: Remove symlinks: sudo rm /etc/centos-release /etc/redhat-release, then restart waagent. Do NOT update Python (causes other OS problems). PG will NOT fix - use Rocky 9 or apply workaround
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Rocky Linux 8 VM is shown as centos in Portal_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent Not Ready with repeated WireServer is not responding and Exceeded max retry updating goal state in waagent.log; VM has RunCommandHandlerLinux installed; GA version < 2.4.0.2
rootCause: Linux GA < 2.4.0.2 does not support multiconfig required by Managed Run Command v2 (RunCommandHandlerLinux). Installing this extension on older GA causes agent to become unresponsive
solution: 1. Set AutoUpdate.Enabled=y in /etc/waagent.conf, restart waagent. 2. If repo GA still < 2.4.0.2, copy /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-X.X.X.X from a working VM with GA >= 2.4.0.2. 3. Restart waagent service
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RunCommandv2 on old Guest Agent versions_AGEX

symptom: Azure Linux Guest Agent (waagent) stuck on old version after newer version is blacklisted. waagent --version shows old version as both running and Goal state agent. waagent.log shows Agent WALinuxAgent-X.X.X.X is permanently blacklisted
rootCause: New version of Guest Agent terminated abnormally (e.g., WireServer connectivity failure at 168.63.129.16), creating error.json with was_fatal: true in the version folder, permanently blacklisting the newer version
solution: 1. Fix WireServer connectivity to 168.63.129.16 (see WireServer TSG). 2. Delete error.json in blacklisted version folder: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-<version>/error.json. 3. Restart waagent: sudo systemctl restart walinuxagent. 4. Optionally Reapply VM from portal to trigger GoalState refresh. 5. Verify with waagent --version
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Old GoalState Agent Version Due To Blacklist_AGEX

symptom: VM Agent fails with Unable to perform the operation as the VM agent is not responsive. Running waagent -version shows Oracle is not a supported distribution. Affects Oracle Linux 7.x VMs from marketplace image
rootCause: Outdated Azure Linux Agent version that does not properly recognize Oracle Linux distribution
solution: Enable ol7_addons repo: edit /etc/yum.repos.d/public-yum-ol7.repo, set enabled=1 under [ol7_addons]. Install latest agent: sudo yum install WALinuxAgent. If add-on repo missing, add [ol7_addons] section manually pointing to http://public-yum.oracle.com/repo/OracleLinux/OL7/addons/$basearch/
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Oracle is not a Supported Distro_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent extension fails. TransparentInstaller.log shows System.TypeLoadException: Could not load type System.Runtime.Diagnostics.ITraceSourceStringProvider from assembly System.ServiceModel.Internals
rootCause: Outdated .NET Framework version on the Windows VM
solution: Update .NET Framework to 4.5.2 or 4.6.2 depending on OS version. Download from https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=42642
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Outdated .Net_AGEX

symptom: VM extension deployment fails on Linux VMs running Python 3.x distributions (Ubuntu 20.04, RHEL 8.1, CentOS 8.1) where /usr/bin/python entrypoint has been removed. Affected extensions: Azure Disk Encryption, Log Analytics, VM Access, Guest Diagnostics
rootCause: Linux distributions transitioning to Python 3.x removed the legacy /usr/bin/python symlink. Extensions requiring this entrypoint fail when the symlink is missing
solution: Reinstate /usr/bin/python symlink. Ubuntu: sudo apt update && sudo apt install python-is-python2 (or python-is-python3). RHEL/CentOS: sudo dnf install python2 && sudo alternatives --set python /usr/bin/python2. Or create symlink: sudo ln -s /usr/bin/python3 /usr/bin/python. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/extensions/issues-using-vm-extensions-python-3
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Python 3.X_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent fails with RdCrypt Initialization failed Error Code -2147023143 (0x6d9 = EPT_S_NOT_REGISTERED) and Failed to get TransportCertificate in WaAppAgent.log
rootCause: CNG Key Isolation (KeyIso) Windows service is disabled or not running, causing RPC endpoint to be unavailable for certificate operations
solution: 1. Verify CNGKEYISO in RPC endpoints: portqry -n <VMName> -e 135. 2. Start the CNG Key Isolation Windows service (name: KeyIso). 3. Restart WaAppAgent.exe / WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RPC Issues_AGEX

symptom: RdAgent service (WaAppAgent.exe) fails to start with Error 1067: The process terminated unexpectedly. MonitoringAgent.log shows Access to the path C:\Windows\system32\VMAgentDisabler.dll is denied and Could not be loaded into process error code 0xe06d7363. Event log shows Event ID 7034 repeatedly
rootCause: VMAgentDisabler.dll file in C:\Windows\System32 has the hidden attribute set, preventing the RdAgent process from loading it despite correct file permissions
solution: Remove the hidden attribute from VMAgentDisabler.dll: attrib -H C:\Windows\System32\VMAgentDisabler.dll (run from elevated command prompt). Then restart the RdAgent service. Alternatively, uncheck Hidden in file Properties via Windows Explorer
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RdAgent Could Not Start Error 1607

symptom: Windows Guest Agent shows as Not Ready. WaAppAgent.log shows Received the Identity data response environment is OnPrem and Current environment is OnPrem when the VM is actually running in Azure. IMDS-related NullReferenceException errors may also appear
rootCause: IMDS endpoint 169.254.169.254/Microsoft.Compute/identity returns a non-404 response (should return 404 in Azure), causing Guest Agent to incorrectly identify environment as on-premises. Often caused by NVA, proxy, or firewall intercepting IMDS traffic
solution: 1. Test IMDS from within VM: browse http://169.254.169.254/Microsoft.Compute/identity - should return 404. 2. If non-404 response, identify what is intercepting traffic (NVA, proxy, firewall). 3. Fix routing to ensure direct access to 169.254.169.254. 4. Collect PROCMON and FIDDLER traces while restarting Guest Agent for diagnosis. 5. Restart Guest Agent after fixing routing
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Received the Identity Data Response Environment OnPrem_AGEX

symptom: Rocky Linux 8 VM is shown as centos in Operating System field in Azure Portal. waagent.log shows OS: centos 8.7. Only affects Rocky Linux 8 VMs from marketplace image; Rocky Linux 9 displays correctly
rootCause: Python < 3.8 get_distro() function picks up /etc/centos-release symlink (alphabetically first in /etc/*-release) before /etc/rocky-release, causing Guest Agent to report OS as centos. Rocky Linux 8 ships with Python 3.6.8
solution: Remove centos-release and redhat-release symbolic links: sudo rm /etc/centos-release /etc/redhat-release. Guest Agent will then correctly detect /etc/rocky-release. DO NOT update Python version as it causes other OS problems. No need to create ICM for this issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Rocky Linux 8 VM is shown as centos in Portal_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent Not Ready with repeated WireServer connection loop in waagent.log: WireServer endpoint is not found Rerun dhcp handler and Protocol endpoint not found WireProtocol Exceeded max retry updating goal state. VM has Microsoft.CPlat.Core.RunCommandHandlerLinux extension and GA version < 2.4.0.2
rootCause: RunCommandv2 (Managed Run Command / Microsoft.CPlat.Core.RunCommandHandlerLinux) extension is incompatible with Guest Agent versions prior to 2.4.0.2 which do not support multiconfig, causing GA to enter an infinite WireServer reconnection loop
solution: 1. Check /etc/waagent.conf: ensure AutoUpdate.Enabled=y. 2. Restart waagent service (systemctl restart walinuxagent or waagent). 3. If GA still not ready, copy WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x directory (version >= 2.4.0.2) from a working VM to /var/lib/waagent/ on the broken VM. 4. Restart waagent service again. Extension removal via portal will fail while GA is not ready
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RunCommandv2 on old Guest Agent versions_AGEX

symptom: Azure VM guest agent reports 503 Server Unavailable or socket connection refused to 168.63.129.16:80 in WaAppAgent.log/Telemetry.log (GetVersions/GetMachineGoalState failed)
rootCause: A proxy or firewall is intercepting/blocking traffic from the VM to WireServer IP 168.63.129.16. Network trace shows HTTP packets going to unexpected IP instead of 168.63.129.16.
solution: Disable the proxy or exclude traffic to 168.63.129.16 from the proxy. Verify access by browsing http://168.63.129.16/?comp=versions from inside the VM. Ensure NIC is DHCP-enabled with DNS configured.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Server Unavailable_AGEX

symptom: WindowsAzureGuestAgent service not installed when installing Windows Guest Agent from MSI. TransparentInstaller.log shows sc.exe create fails with exit code 1639. RDAgent installs but crashes on startup.
rootCause: sc.exe create command in ServiceHelper.cs has a syntax error (missing spaces after equals sign), causing service creation to fail with exit code 1639. Affects MSI installs on VMs from specialized VHDs (ASR migration, CreateOption:Attach).
solution: Uninstall Windows Azure VM Agent from Programs and Features. Install .1010 MSI (WindowsAzureVmAgent.2.7.41491.1010) from GitHub releases. It auto-updates to .1024+. Fixed permanently in agent version 2.7.41491.1095+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Service Not Installed When Installing WinGA from MSI_AGEX

symptom: RdAgent or Windows Azure Guest Agent service fails to start with Error 1053: The service did not respond to the start or control request in a timely fashion. Service Control Manager Event 7000/7009 with 30000ms timeout.
rootCause: Third-party firewall or antivirus software (e.g., Symantec Endpoint Protection sysfer.dll) is blocking the guest agent executables (WaAppAgent.exe, WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe) from running.
solution: Create a whitelist/exception in the customer firewall for agent paths: C:\WindowsAzure\GuestAgent_<Version>\WaAppAgent.exe and C:\WindowsAzure\GuestAgent_<Version>\WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe. Contact 3rd party vendor if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Service Not Starting Error 1053_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM agent reports Size of VM status blob is larger than allowed limit of 1 MB. Guest agent continuously sets HostGAPlugin as default channel with high CPU usage.
rootCause: Status blob exceeds 1MB size limit. Associated with high VM CPU and agent repeatedly switching to host plugin channel. Related bug in WALinuxAgent (GitHub issue #1653), fixed Oct 2019.
solution: Collect verbose Linux agent logs (enable only for a few minutes). Update agent to latest version (bug fixed Oct 2019, WALinuxAgent GitHub #1653). Escalate to Linux SME with verbose logs if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Status Blob Larger than 1MB_AGEX

symptom: SUSE Linux VM hostname keeps alternating between the configured hostname and linux (e.g., hn1-hdb0 -> linux -> hn1-hdb0). Detected hostname change events flood waagent.log.
rootCause: SUSE wicked network manager bug (SUSE bug 1167134) causes DHCP renewal to reset hostname. DHCLIENT_SET_HOSTNAME is set to yes allowing DHCP to override the hostname.
solution: 1) Update guest agent to latest version. 2) Set DHCLIENT_SET_HOSTNAME=no in /etc/sysconfig/network/dhcp. SUSE released a fix in 2020. For additional questions, redirect customer to SUSE support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/SuSE Hostname Change_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent fails to generate TransportCertificate with error code -2146893808 (NTE_PERM / Access Denied). WaAppAgent.log shows Self-signed Certificate Generation failed. Procmon shows ACCESS DENIED on c:\programdata\microsoft\crypto\SystemKeys.
rootCause: The SYSTEM account does not have permissions to the c:\programdata\microsoft\crypto\SystemKeys folder, preventing the agent from generating self-signed certificates.
solution: Restore proper permissions for the SYSTEM account on c:\programdata\microsoft\crypto\SystemKeys folder. Use Procmon with filters (Result=Access Denied, Path contains crypto\systemkeys) to confirm the root cause.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/System Keys Access Denied_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent silently crashes with System.Threading.Tasks.TaskExceptionHolder.Finalize() unhandled AggregateException in .NET Runtime Event 1026. Extensions reach Faulted state with NullReferenceException.
rootCause: Task chaining exceptions were not properly cleared by the Garbage Collector, especially when .NET Framework version is below 4.5. Bug in agent task exception handling.
solution: Update Windows Guest Agent to version .943 or later (fix for task chaining). Ensure VM is running .NET Framework 4.5+ with all Windows Updates applied. Version .911 has partial fix (Bug 3789025), .943 has complete fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/System.Threading.Tasks.TaskExceptionHolder.Finalize Error_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent update/install fails with exit code 0xc0000005 (Access Violation) or exit code 255. TransparentInstaller.log shows ServiceUninstallFailedException. .NET Runtime log shows a third-party profiler loaded (e.g., Dynatrace CLSID {B7038F67-52FC-4DA2-AB02-969B3C1EDA03}).
rootCause: Third-party .NET Core profiler (Dynatrace, IntelliTraceProfilerProxy, etc.) interferes with the Guest Agent components, causing access violations during install/uninstall operations.
solution: Disable or uninstall the third-party .NET Core profiler, then retry the Windows Guest Agent installation/update.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Third Party .Net Core Profiler_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent crashes immediately after starting. ConfigurationErrorsException: system.serviceModel/bindings/siebelBinding could not be found. Binding extension not registered in bindingExtensions.
rootCause: Third-party BizTalk adapters (Siebel, Oracle DB, SAP) have registered binding extensions in machine.config (C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config\machine.config) that are not properly installed, causing .NET configuration errors when Guest Agent loads.
solution: 1) Backup machine.config. 2) Comment out lines containing siebelBinding (and other 3rd party bindings like oracleDBBinding, sapBinding if present). 3) Restart the VM. 4) Contact CSS BizTalk team for further adapter configuration issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Third Party Binding Issues_AGEX

symptom: Linux VM guest agent (waagent) fails with TransportCert.pem is missing. waagent.log shows /usr/bin/openssl: No such file or directory and failed to create TransportPrivate.pem. Agent appears running but is unresponsive from Azure portal/ASC.
rootCause: The /usr/bin/openssl symlink is broken, pointing to /usr/local/ssl/bin/openssl which does not exist. Without openssl, the agent cannot generate transport certificates needed for WireServer communication.
solution: 1) Unlink broken symlink: sudo unlink /usr/bin/openssl. 2) Reinstall openssl: sudo yum reinstall openssl. 3) Verify Transport .pem certs regenerated: ls -al /var/lib/waagent | grep Transport. 4) Restart waagent: sudo systemctl restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/TransportCert is Missing_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent stuck on old version; waagent --version shows outdated Goal state agent; waagent.log shows Agent WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x is permanently blacklisted
rootCause: New Guest Agent version terminated abnormally (e.g., WireServer 168.63.129.16 connectivity failure), creating error.json with was_fatal:true in the agent version folder, permanently blacklisting that version
solution: 1. Fix WireServer connectivity to 168.63.129.16. 2. Find blacklisted agent folder: ls -al /var/lib/waagent | grep WALinuxAgent. 3. Confirm error.json has was_fatal:true. 4. Delete blacklist file: sudo rm -f /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x/error.json. 5. Restart agent: sudo systemctl restart walinuxagent. 6. Verify: waagent --version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Old GoalState Agent Version Due To Blacklist_AGEX

symptom: VM Agent fails with Unable to perform the operation as the VM agent is not responsive; waagent -version shows Oracle is not a supported distribution
rootCause: Outdated Azure Linux Agent version on Oracle Linux VM does not recognize Oracle as a supported distribution
solution: 1. Enable ol7_addons repo: edit /etc/yum.repos.d/public-yum-ol7.repo, set enabled=1 under [ol7_addons]. 2. Install latest agent: sudo yum install WALinuxAgent. 3. Update: sudo yum update WALinuxAgent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Oracle is not a Supported Distro_AGEX

symptom: VM extension deployment fails on Linux VMs running Python 3.x with missing /usr/bin/python entrypoint; affected extensions: Azure Disk Encryption, Log Analytics, VM Access, Guest Diagnostics
rootCause: Linux distributions transitioned to Python 3.x and removed the legacy /usr/bin/python entrypoint; affected extensions still reference /usr/bin/python
solution: Reinstall python entrypoint: For Ubuntu: sudo apt update && sudo apt install python-is-python2. For RHEL/CentOS: sudo dnf install python2 && sudo alternatives --set python /usr/bin/python2. For new deployments, use cloud-init to pre-install the symlink.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Python 3.X_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent status not reported; WaAppAgent.log shows Received the Identity data response environment is OnPrem and Current environment is OnPrem; IMDS endpoint http://169.254.169.254/Microsoft.Compute/identity returns unexpected response instead of 404
rootCause: Network proxy or firewall is intercepting/responding to IMDS requests at 169.254.169.254, causing the Guest Agent to misidentify the VM as on-premises instead of Azure
solution: 1. Verify IMDS access: test http://169.254.169.254/Microsoft.Compute/identity from within VM (should return 404 in Azure). 2. Collect PROCMON and FIDDLER trace while restarting Guest Agent to identify interfering proxy. 3. Work with customer network/security team to whitelist 169.254.169.254 and ensure no proxy intercepts IMDS traffic.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Received the Identity Data Response Environment OnPrem_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent Not Ready after installing Managed Run Command extension (Microsoft.CPlat.Core.RunCommandHandlerLinux); waagent.log shows repeated WireServer is not responding Reset dhcp endpoint and Protocol endpoint not found errors; Guest Agent version < 2.4.0.2
rootCause: Linux Guest Agent versions prior to 2.4.0.2 do not support multiconfig required by Managed Run Command v2 (RunCommandHandlerLinux); installing RunCommandv2 on old agent versions breaks the agent
solution: 1. Ensure AutoUpdate.Enabled=y in /etc/waagent.conf, restart agent (systemctl restart walinuxagent/waagent). 2. If repo lacks version >= 2.4.0.2, copy /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x directory from a working VM with newer agent to the broken VM, then restart agent service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/RunCommandv2 on old Guest Agent versions_AGEX

symptom: Azure Resource Graph (ARG) query for Advisor recommendations returns inconsistent/fluctuating counts of ResourceIDs for the same subscription when run at short intervals (~30 seconds); total row count remains the same but per-subscription resource counts differ across runs
rootCause: Known pagination limitation in Azure Resource Graph (ARG). When results are sorted only by non-unique columns, the distributed query engine returns non-deterministic ordering across pagination boundaries, causing duplicate or missing records even in static environments.
solution: Add a unique column (such as id) to the order by clause to ensure deterministic sort order. Example: | order by location asc, id asc. For dynamic environments where resources change during pagination, consider change-tracking or snapshot-based approaches instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/ARGQuery Returns_Inconsistent_Recommendation_Advisor

symptom: VM extension re-execution fails silently with message: Current sequence number is not greater than the sequence number of the most recently executed configuration
rootCause: Extension was re-executed with the exact same settings. The Guest Agent prevents duplicate execution to avoid mistakes.
solution: Use the ForceUpdateTag property to force re-execution of the extension with the same settings. Set a new unique value (e.g., timestamp) for ForceUpdateTag in the extension resource definition.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/Workflows/Extensions Workflow_AGEX

symptom: LinuxDiagnostic extension error message is encoded/not human-readable in the extension handler log (base64 + zlib compressed)
rootCause: LinuxDiagnostic extension encodes error messages using base64 + zlib compression in the log output.
solution: Decode the error using: echo "<encoded_message>" | base64 -d | python3 -c "import sys,zlib; print(zlib.decompress(sys.stdin.buffer.read()).decode())"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/Workflows/Extensions Workflow_AGEX

symptom: Azure Advisor WAF Assessment shows In progress after completion; recommendations not visible in Advisor portal
rootCause: Recommendations sync from the Learn platform (learn.microsoft.com) to Azure Advisor can take up to 8 hours after assessment completion.
solution: Wait up to 8 hours for recommendations to sync into Advisor. If still not appearing, raise an ICM to Learn Engineering Services/Learn Engineering Tier 3 Assessments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/How Tos/Azure Advisor WAF Assessments_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor WAF Assessment completed but returns zero recommendations
rootCause: Customer skipped assessment questions (jumped to View guidance) or the Learn platform has not yet generated recommendations for the assessment.
solution: Ensure all assessment questions are answered completely without skipping. If all answered, wait for Learn platform to process. For persistent issues, raise ICM to Learn Engineering Services/Learn Engineering Tier 3 Assessments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/How Tos/Azure Advisor WAF Assessments_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor recommendation missing or not visible for a subscription; customer expects to see a recommendation but it does not appear
rootCause: The recommendation may have been snoozed (postponed) by a user with appropriate RBAC permissions.
solution: Query Kusto to verify: cluster("azureadvisorpartner.centralus").database("AzEE-DB").Recommendations | where SubscriptionId == "{SubId}" | where EngagementLevel == "Snoozed". Also check KpiSnoozed table. Access requires Azure Advisor Telemetry entitlement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/How Tos/Determine if a Recommendation Is Snoozed_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor configuration Include checkbox is disabled/greyed out; unable to configure which subscriptions receive Advisor recommendations
rootCause: User does not have sufficient RBAC permissions. Reader or Contributor role is required on the subscription or resource group to manage Advisor configuration.
solution: Grant the user at least Reader or Contributor role on the target subscription or resource group. Note: Advisor configuration is persistent and differs from UI filtering which is temporary.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/How Tos/Customize Azure best practices_Advisor

symptom: Run Command extension stuck in transitioning state; extension operation does not complete or times out
rootCause: The Run Command extension operation has timed out or entered an unrecoverable state. Applies to both Action Run Command (v1) and Managed Run Command (v2).
solution: Uninstall the stuck extension and reinstall it. For v1: Microsoft.CPlat.Core.RunCommandLinux/Windows. For v2: Microsoft.CPlat.Core.RunCommandHandlerLinux/Windows.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/Workflows/Run Command Workflow_AGEX

symptom: VM not receiving expected Azure Advisor cost recommendation (shutdown or rightsize); Advisor does not show cost savings recommendation for underutilized VM
rootCause: VM utilization metrics do not meet recommendation thresholds. Shutdown requires P95 CPU<3% and outbound network<2% over 7 days. Rightsize requires CPU/network P95 at 40% (user-facing) or 80% (non user-facing) on cheaper SKU.
solution: Use CloudFit tool (aka.ms/CloudFitTool) to check VM utilization summary and verify if thresholds are met. The average CPU utilization threshold can be customized in Advisor configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/How Tos/Check VM Utilization for Cost Recommendations_Advisor

symptom: WaAppAgent.log shows 'Unable to connect to the remote server' with SocketException targeting non-WireServer address (e.g. 127.0.0.1:8888) due to machine.config proxy settings
rootCause: Incorrect proxy configuration in C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config\machine.config overrides Guest Agent WebClient behavior, redirecting connections to a local proxy instead of WireServer 168.63.129.16
solution: 1. Collect machine.config and check for <system.net> section with proxy settings pointing to non-WireServer address. 2. Remove or correct the proxy configuration. 3. Upgrade to WinGA 2.7.41491.992+ which uses a different mechanism not impacted by machine.config settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Unable to Connect to Remote Server_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent fails with UnicodeEncodeError: 'latin-1' codec can't encode character in telemetry SendTelemetryHandler; DroppedEventsCount increases
rootCause: Bug in WALinuxAgent 2.3.0.x - Unicode characters in telemetry data cause encoding failure when sending to WireServer. Fix PR: Azure/WALinuxAgent#2278
solution: 1. Upgrade WALinuxAgent to 2.4.0.0+ which contains the fix. 2. Mitigation: stop agent, remove all files in /var/lib/waagent/events directory, restart agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/UnicodeEncodeError Python Error in Linux Agent 2.3.0.x_AGEX

symptom: Windows Guest Agent hangs at 'Updating existing DACL' in WaAppAgent.log; Portal shows Not Ready but VM Aggregate Status is Ready; agent restart still stuck at same point
rootCause: A process (often 3rd-party antimalware) is blocking Guest Agent NTFS permission operations on C:\WindowsAzure and C:\Packages folders. The agent hangs waiting to set DACL and never reaches 'Finished setting access controls for directory'.
solution: 1. Use Sysinternals Handle.exe (run as admin) to identify which process holds open file handles on C:\WindowsAzure or C:\Packages. 2. If 3rd-party antimalware, ask customer to add scanning exceptions for those folders. 3. If not antimalware, verify with customer if OK to terminate the blocking process. 4. After clearing the block, restart Guest Agent services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WAAppAgent stuck on Updating existing DACL_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent daemon ends with ExpatError 'no element found' parsing ovf-env.xml; waagent.log shows 'Daemon ended with exception -- Sleep 15 seconds and restart daemon' in a loop
rootCause: Corrupted /var/lib/waagent/ovf-env.xml file - missing closing XML tags (e.g., </ns0:Environment>), causing XML parser to fail
solution: 1. Collect InspectIaaSDisk. 2. Navigate to /var/lib/waagent/ovf-env.xml. 3. Compare with a working VM's ovf-env.xml. 4. Add missing closing tags (e.g., </ns0:Environment> at end of file). 5. Restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WALinuxAgent Daemon Ended with an Exception_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent shows 'No DVD device detected, unable to provision' after VMware migration; waagent stuck on old version 2.0.18 or shows version 9.9.9.9 on RHEL EUS
rootCause: VM uses RHEL EUS (Extended Update Support) repositories which don't have access to the latest WALinuxAgent packages, blocking the agent upgrade from the very old 2.0.18 version
solution: Add non-EUS repositories: 1. rm /etc/yum/vars/releasever 2. yum --disablerepo='*' remove 'rhui-azure-rhel7-eus' 3. yum --config='https://rhelimage.blob.core.windows.net/repositories/rhui-microsoft-azure-rhel' to configure standard repos. Then upgrade WALinuxAgent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WALinuxAgent Failing to Start a Specialized VM - No DVD Device Detected Unable to Provision_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent shows JSONDecodeError 'Expecting value: line 1 column 1' in waagent.log; extension handler version not updating despite AutoUpdate.Enabled=y
rootCause: Empty error.json file exists in /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-x.x.x.x/ directory. Known bug fixed in WALinuxAgent v2.7.0.6 (PR #2433).
solution: 1. systemctl stop walinuxagent 2. rm -f /var/lib/waagent/WALinuxAgent-*/error.json 3. systemctl start walinuxagent. Permanent fix: upgrade to WALinuxAgent v2.7.0.6+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WALinuxAgent JSONDecodeError_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent fails to start with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'azurelinuxagent'; waagent service crashes repeatedly with exit-code 1
rootCause: Python symlink (/usr/bin/python or /usr/bin/python3) points to an unsupported Python version. WALA package requires specific Python version (e.g., python2.7 for SLES12, python3.6 for RHEL8/SLES15) but symlink was changed to a different version.
solution: 1. Check required Python version: rpm -q --requires WALinuxAgent | grep python (or apt-cache depends walinuxagent). 2. Check current symlink: head -1 /usr/sbin/waagent && ls -l /usr/bin/python*. 3. Fix symlink using update-alternatives --config python to point to supported version. 4. systemctl restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WALinuxAgent ModuleNotFoundError NameError_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent fails to start with NameError: name 'distro' is not defined on RHEL when /etc/redhat-release or system-release files are missing and distro module not installed
rootCause: Missing /etc/*-release files (e.g., /etc/redhat-release deleted) prevents platform module from detecting OS. The distro Python module (fallback) is also not installed, causing NameError in azurelinuxagent.common.future.py get_linux_distribution_from_distro().
solution: 1. Reinstall the release package to restore release files: yum -y reinstall redhat-release-<version> (check with rpm -qf /etc/redhat-release). 2. Alternatively install distro module: pip install distro or yum install python3-distro. 3. systemctl restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WALinuxAgent ModuleNotFoundError NameError_AGEX

symptom: WALinuxAgent fails with NameError 'distro' is not defined on Ubuntu 18.04 after python3 symlink changed from python3.6 to python3.8; platform._supported_dists missing in python3.8
rootCause: Python 3.8 removed platform._supported_dists and platform.linux_distribution() APIs. WALinuxAgent falls back to distro module which is not installed by default on Ubuntu 18.04 when using python3.8.
solution: Method 1: apt install python3-distro && systemctl restart walinuxagent. Method 2: Reset python3 symlink to python3.6: apt install --reinstall python3-minimal && systemctl restart walinuxagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WALinuxAgent NameError name distro is not defined_AGEX

symptom: VMSS instances show VMAgentStatusCommunicationError / VMExtensionProvisioningError; WaAppAgent.log loops with 'Goalstate execution is on hold, do not report status to the blob' and 'Workflow is not initialized yet'
rootCause: Bug in VMAgent - VMArtifactsExecutor never reset the onHold flag (ADO bug 7635052). Affects VMSS with overprovisioning enabled.
solution: Bug fix has been released. Workaround: 1. Disable VMSS overprovisioning (Portal: VMSS > Settings > Configuration > Enable overprovisioning > Off, or CLI: az vmss update --name <name> --resource-group <rg> --set overprovision=false). 2. Or set doNotRunExtensionsOnOverprovisionedVMs=false via CLI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WARN Goalstate Execution is on Hold Do Not Report Status to the Blob_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.exe (RDAgent service) uses a UDP port on domain-joined Windows VMs, which may conflict with customer applications needing the same ephemeral UDP port
rootCause: WaAppAgent generates a random UDP port to query Domain Controller via LDAP on domain-joined VMs. The underlying Windows API has a bug that does not release the port after use.
solution: This is by design. Options: (1) Fix app to use random unused UDP port, (2) Restart RDAgent service to get a new random port, (3) Set enablePushInVMLogs to false in CommonAgentConfig.config (not recommended/supported)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WaAppAgent UDP Port_AGEX

symptom: WaLinuxAgent fails to start with OSError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory during gen_transport_cert function. Agent status shows Not Ready and reinstallation does not resolve the issue.
rootCause: Missing or broken openssl binary. WaLinuxAgent depends on openssl at /usr/bin/openssl for certificate generation (gen_transport_cert). If openssl is not installed or the path is broken, the agent cannot generate transport certificates.
solution: If openssl not installed: apt-get install openssl (or yum/rpm equivalent). If installed but path broken: create symlink ln -s /path/to/actual/openssl /usr/bin/openssl. Then restart waagent: systemctl restart waagent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WaLinuxAgent No Such File or Directory_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.log shows warning message [WARN] Waiting for OOBE to Complete during VM provisioning
rootCause: Guest agent waits for the OOBE (Out-of-Box Experience) phase to complete before proceeding. This is an informational message during normal VM provisioning.
solution: No action required. This is an informational message. Wait time was reduced from 10 minutes to 2 minutes in the November 2019 agent release.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Waiting for OOBE to Complete_AGEX

symptom: Higher than expected Azure Site Recovery (ASR) egress fees. Windows guest agent CollectGuestLogs.exe creates temporary log copies in C:\CollectGuestLogsTemp on C: drive, which ASR replicates causing unwanted egress.
rootCause: ASR replicates all file system changes. CollectGuestLogs.exe runs hourly and creates temp log copies in C:\CollectGuestLogsTemp before zipping. These temp files on C: drive get replicated by ASR, increasing egress fees.
solution: Install Windows guest agent version 2.7.41491.1009 or later. This version puts CollectGuestLogsTemp on the temp drive (not replicated by ASR). If ASR SiteRecovery extension is detected and no temp drive exists, CollectGuestLogs does not run.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WinGA CollectGuestLogs Unwanted ASR Egress_AGEX

symptom: AzureDiskEncryption (ADE) extension PUT fails with VMAgentStatusCommunicationError when VM and ADE are deployed in the same ARM template. Guest agent is not installed on the VM.
rootCause: ADE BEK VHD attach triggers a reboot during provisioning. If this reboot happens before WinGA installation completes, the guest agent is not installed. WinPA does not wait for WinGA install completion before sending provisioning success signal.
solution: Add another extension (e.g., CustomScriptExtension running a no-op command) to the template and have ADE depend on it via dependsOn instead of depending directly on the VM. This ensures WinGA has time to install before ADE triggers the reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WinGA Install Fails When VM and ADE Deployed in Same Template_AGEX

symptom: Manual install of Windows guest agent MSI fails with error Object doesnt support this property or method: Me.Script on Windows Server 2008R2 VM
rootCause: Kaspersky antivirus (Kavfswp.exe) intercepts and interferes with .wsf/.vbs script files executed during the MSI installation process, specifically the GuestAgent.wsf script.
solution: Remove Kaspersky Security 10.1.1 for Windows Server and Kaspersky Security Center 11 Network Agent. MSI install should succeed after removal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WinGA MSI Failed Installation_AGEX

symptom: Windows guest agent MSI installation fails with Error 1722 and Installing the RdAgent service failed due to broken WMI. VmAgentInstaller.xml shows Object required error during ConfigureRdAgentService.
rootCause: WMI (Windows Management Instrumentation) StdRegProv is broken. The VM agent MSI uses WMI StdRegProv to access the registry. If WMI is not working properly, the MSI cannot set the RdAgent service path.
solution: Fix WMI: (1) Test with wmic StdRegProv call, (2) winmgmt /verifyrepository, (3) Backup WMI repo, (4) winmgmt /salvagerepository, retry MSI. If still fails: net stop winmgmt /y, winmgmt /resetrepository, net start winmgmt, retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WinGA failing to install due broken WMI_AGEX

symptom: Windows Azure Guest Agent service fails to start with Error 1053: The service did not respond to the start or control request in a timely fashion. WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe crashes with Symantec sysfer.dll as faulting module.
rootCause: Symantec antivirus (sysfer.dll module) causes the Windows Guest Agent process to crash on startup due to realtime scanning interference.
solution: Create an antivirus exclusion for Windows Guest Agent process (C:\WindowsAzure\GuestAgent_X.X.XXXXX.XXXX\WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe) to prevent Symantec realtime scanning from affecting it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Windows GA Could Not Start Error 1053 Symantec_AGEX

symptom: Windows guest agent running but extensions not working. WaAppAgent.log shows PInvoke PFXImportCertStore failed and null handle is returned. Error Code: 86. Cannot create Windows Azure CRP Certificate Generator certificate.
rootCause: SYSTEM account lacks Full Control permissions on the Crypto folders: C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\Keys, C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\RSA, C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\SystemKeys.
solution: Grant SYSTEM account Full Control permissions on: C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\Keys, C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\RSA, C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\SystemKeys. Then restart the guest agent services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Windows GA PInvoke PFXImportCertStore failed and null handle is returned Error Code86_AGEX

symptom: WaAppAgent.exe and/or WindowsAzureGuestAgent.exe crashing on startup with exception code 0x40000015 (WaAppAgent) and 0xe0434f4d (WindowsAzureGuestAgent). Application Event Log shows Event ID 1000.
rootCause: Corrupted .NET Framework, typically caused by failed installation of .NET Framework Windows Updates (e.g., KB4570506 failing with error 0x80070643).
solution: Ensure VM is fully up-to-date with all Windows Updates, especially .NET Framework updates. Run the .NET Framework Repair Tool (https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2698555).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Windows Guest Agent Crashing with 0x40000015_AGEX

symptom: Windows Provisioning Agent (WaGuest.exe) sporadically failing with error 800700cb when running /configureservicestartmode RdAgent Automatic 1; error message: The system could not find the environment option that was entered
rootCause: 3rd party application (Ivanti AppSense Application Manager / Environment Manager - AMAgent.exe) injected into WAGuest.exe process, interfering with the provisioning agent execution. Visible in Procmon trace and Windows Guest Analyzer.
solution: Ask customer to uninstall the 3rd party software (Ivanti AppSense), retry the WAGuest command, and rebuild the image without the 3rd party application.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Windows Provisioning Agent Sporadically Failing_AGEX

symptom: Windows Update registry values (AUOptions, NoAutoUpdate) unexpectedly changed after VM Guest Agent updated to version 2.7.41491.938 or 2.7.41491.940, causing VMs to automatically install Windows Updates and reboot unexpectedly
rootCause: Regression in Windows VM Agent versions .938/.940: feature for VMSS inadvertently affected single VMs, overriding enableAutomaticUpdates on existing VMs. Agent wrote WU settings assuming unchanged since provisioning.
solution: Update to agent 2.7.41491.943+. For affected VMs: reset WU registry to desired values (agent caches and won't re-override). Workaround: import .reg file with NoAutoUpdate=1. Detection via Kusto GuestAgentGenericLogs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Windows Update Registry_AGEX

symptom: Windows VM with multiple IP addresses on a NIC loses connectivity to WireServer (168.63.129.16), Guest Agent reports Not Ready; Test-NetConnection shows non-primary IP as SourceAddress
rootCause: Windows selects lowest numerical IP as primary regardless of Azure portal settings. Secondary IP (lower number) is used but not authorized to connect to WireServer.
solution: PowerShell: Set-NetIPAddress -IPAddress <PrimaryIP> -InterfaceAlias <NIC> -SkipAsSource $false; Set-NetIPAddress -IPAddress <SecondaryIPs> -InterfaceAlias <NIC> -SkipAsSource $true. Ref: docs.microsoft.com/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/no-internet-access-multi-ip
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Windows VM loses connectivity to wireserver with multiple IPs per NIC_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent (WALinuxAgent) on SUSE VM fails to communicate with WireServer; waagent.log shows WireServer is not responding, IOError timed out, ProtocolError Exceeded max retry updating goal state
rootCause: SUSE network manager (wicked) stop/start of managed network interfaces causes WALinuxAgent to lose WireServer connectivity and not recover. Confirmed in ICM 224267350.
solution: Restart WALinuxAgent service (systemctl restart waagent.service) or restart the VM. Issue under investigation by WALinuxAgent DEV team (ICM 224267350).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/WireServer is Not Responding - IOError Timed Out - Attempts to Retrieve the Goal State are Failing_AGEX

symptom: Linux Guest Agent reports ProtocolError Wireserver Failed Status Code 400 sending telemetry to http://168.63.129.16/machine?comp=telemetrydata; WireMarshal.log: Failed to load Xml Error 0xC00CE508 invalid character in text content
rootCause: Extension (Azure Guest Configuration / AzurePolicyforLinux) sends telemetry with invalid XML chars (ANSI color codes from InSpec/ChefInSpec output), causing WireServer XML parser to reject with HTTP 400.
solution: Check registry key LatestExpectedVersion under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Azure\GuestAgentUpdateState. Export/backup, delete it, restart GA processes. For Linux: restart waagent. Ref: ICM 475077035.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Agents & Extensions (AGEX)/TSGs/GA/Wireserver Exception - ProtocolError - Wireserver Failed - Status Code 400_AGEX

symptom: Azure Advisor REST API/CLI returns different recommendation count than Portal GUI. API output shows more recommendations than Portal because it lists each impacted resource individually.
rootCause: API/CLI lists recommendations individually per affected resource, while Portal groups them by recommendation type and counts as a single recommendation.
solution: Explain to customer that API counts per impacted resource while Portal groups by recommendation type. This is expected behavior. Use ARG query to get grouped count matching Portal view.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor API troubleshooting steps_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor LIST or GET recommendations REST API does not return Actions field in the response body output.
rootCause: The Actions field is hidden behind an undocumented $expand parameter. PG confirmed this is by design but not yet publicly documented.
solution: Add $expand=ibiza,details parameter to the API call. Example: https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/<sub-id>/providers/Microsoft.Advisor/recommendations?api-version=2023-01-01&$expand=ibiza,details
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor API troubleshooting steps_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor REST API shows different cost savings totals and affected resource counts compared to Portal. API seems to show only recent recommendations while Portal includes older ones.
rootCause: API does not have a time filter functionality. Portal may show recommendations from a longer lookback period than the API, causing discrepancies in total savings and resource counts.
solution: Currently no time filter in Advisor API. Customer can use ARG API to query Advisor recommendations with custom time-based filters, or accept the difference as expected behavior. Feature request for time filter submitted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor API troubleshooting steps_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor cost savings recommendation shows potential savings higher than actual resource cost. For example, Advisor suggests 150 USD/month savings but actual cost is 80 USD/month.
rootCause: Advisor calculates savings using retail pricing and assumes 24/7 resource usage. It does not factor in customer-specific pricing (EA discounts), usage patterns (start/stop), or discounted rates. Only right-size VM and certain delete recommendations show discounted pricing.
solution: Explain methodology: retail pricing x 24/7 assumption. For actual savings, use Cost Analysis or Pricing Calculator. Advisor shows discounted price only in drill-down UI for right-size VM. PG working on personalized factors but no ETA. Verify via CloudFit tool.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor Cost Savings are Higher than VM Cost_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor no longer displays Potential yearly savings based on retail pricing aggregation on cost recommendation pages. Previously shown yearly savings summary is missing from the Azure portal.
rootCause: By design change effective September 30 2024, Azure removed the aggregated yearly savings display from the Advisor Portal. Individual recommendation savings remain available.
solution: Customers can calculate aggregated yearly savings manually: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/advisor/advisor-how-to-calculate-total-cost-savings. Note: individual recommendations may show overlapping savings (savings plans vs reservations).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor No Longer Displays Yearly Cost Savings

symptom: Azure Advisor Alert not triggered or alert email not received even though a recommendation was generated and visible to the customer.
rootCause: Advisor alert rules are Activity Log scoped alerts that only trigger when a recommendation is first created. No subsequent Activity Log events are generated if the recommendation is updated. This is by design. Alternatively, alert scope may be misconfigured or AzNS transmission failed.
solution: 1. Check AzNS transmission via Kusto (aznscluster.southcentralus, AzNSPROD, AzNSTransmissions_All) using subscription and action group filter. 2. If found in AzNS but not delivered, engage Azure Monitoring. 3. If not found, verify alert scope and action group. Note: alerts only fire at recommendation creation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor Alerts not received or not triggered_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor Recommendation Digest email shows different recommendation count than the Portal UI. Digest count is higher or lower than what customer sees in Advisor portal.
rootCause: Digest calculates recommendation count using targetResourceCount property from internal Kusto query, which counts impacted resources differently than Portal grouping logic.
solution: This is by current design. Customer can verify count using ARG query: advisorresources | where type == 'microsoft.advisor/recommendations' | summarize sum(count) by category. ADO Feature 24874339 in PG backlog to align counts (no ETA).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor Recommendation Digest_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor category score is less than 100 percent but customer has no active recommendations to fix. No actionable recommendations visible in the Advisor dashboard.
rootCause: Customer dismissed some recommendations but the Advisor Score calculation via ADLS data still counts them. Score data not updated with dismissed/snoozed status.
solution: 1. Check dismissed recommendations via ARG in ASC. 2. Verify in Kusto (azureadvisorpartner.centralus, AzEE-DB, Recommendations with EngagementLevel==Snoozed). 3. Check KpiData/KpiSnoozed tables. 4. Contact MCDS team to verify ADLS data. Next score calc job will correct. ICM template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=91J304
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Advisor Score is not 100% when no recommendations_Advisor

symptom: ACSS SAP system discovery or registration fails with error code AzureVMIsNotInSupportedProvisioningState. SAP Virtual Instance (VIS) shows DiscoveryFailed provisioning state despite VM and extensions showing Succeeded. Redeploying the VIS does not resolve the issue.
rootCause: The SAP central instance was deleted. ACSS cannot find any SAP central instances for the VIS, causing the discovery process to fail with this error code.
solution: Delete the VIS and re-register the SAP system. Use Kusto queries on waasservices cluster (WaaSKustoDB.WaaSErrorEvent and WaaSDiagnosticEvent) filtering by the VIS resource ID to confirm the root cause. Then follow: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sap/center-sap-solutions/register-existing-system#register-sap-system
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/AzureVMIsNotInSupportedProvisioningState_ACSS

symptom: ACSS does not natively support Private Endpoint. Customer needs private connectivity between ACSS and the managed storage account but cannot use Service Endpoint due to network restrictions.
rootCause: Private Endpoint is not officially supported in ACSS. Only Service Endpoint is natively supported for restricting network access to the managed storage account.
solution: Workaround (unsupported, at customer risk): 1) Enable Microsoft.Storage Service Endpoint on SAP system VNET subnet. 2) Register SAP system with ACSS with restricted network access. 3) Remove Service Endpoint. 4) Remove virtual network subnets from managed storage account Networking. 5) Create Private Endpoint targeting blob subresource on SAP VM subnet. 6) Configure Private DNS zone. All ACSS functionalities work after this.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/How Tos/Enable Private Endpoint for ACSS Managed Storage Account_ACSS

symptom: Azure Advisor category score (Reliability, Security, Cost, Operational Excellence, or Performance) shows 'not available' or 'No data' for a specific subscription
