rootCause: Possible causes: (1) No applicable resources for that category to compute score, (2) Subscription status changed from Active to Disabled/Deprovisioned, (3) User lacks permission to view the data, (4) For Security Score specifically, subscription is not onboarded to Microsoft Defender for Cloud (MDC)
solution: Query Advisor Score Kusto cluster (advscoreadxwus3prod.westus3.kusto.windows.net) database AdvisorScore table Advisor_Score filtering by Category and SubscriptionId. If data exists in Kusto but not visible in portal, raise Sev 4 IcM to Advisor Score team. For Security category, verify MDC onboarding first before creating IcM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Azure Advisor Score_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor Score shows 'not available' reported by multiple customers simultaneously
rootCause: Service-side issue with Advisor Score computation pipeline
solution: Raise Sev 2 IcM to Advisor Score team using IcM template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=1g3s3H
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Azure Advisor Score_Advisor

symptom: Columns missing or data not matching when downloading Azure Advisor recommendations in CSV or PDF format compared to portal display
rootCause: Known issues: (1) Certain cost recommendations do not support cost-saving price info so it is empty in both portal and exports. (2) For right-size/shutdown VM recommendations, portal drill-down shows discounted price (from billing API, customer currency) but exports show retail price (USD) due to billing API throttling at high volumes.
solution: Compare data across Portal, CSV, and PDF. If discrepancy is only in cost/pricing for right-size VMs, this is by design (PG working to align). If columns missing for non-cost fields or other categories, escalate to Advisor PG via AVA channel. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/advisor/advisor-reference-cost-recommendations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Columns missing or not matching when downloading recommendations in CSV PDF format in Advisor_Advisor

symptom: Unable to dismiss Azure Advisor recommendations for Azure RedHat OpenShift (ARO) resources due to restricted permissions on ARO-managed resource groups
rootCause: ARO-managed resource groups have deny assignments that prevent users from modifying permissions or resources, including dismissing Advisor recommendations. This is by design per ARO support policies.
solution: This is by design. ARO deny assignment prevents dismissal. The only workaround is to delete the ARO managed resource group itself. Feature request submitted to PG but no timeline. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/openshift/support-policies-v4
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Dismissing Advisor recommendations for Azure RedHat OpenShift resources_Advisor

symptom: Customer resource is not shown in the Azure Advisor blade, or a banner message at the top indicates permission issues
rootCause: The user lacks the required RBAC role (Owner, Contributor, or Reader) for the impacted resources at Subscription, Resource Group, or Resource level
solution: Check Role Assignments in Access Control (IAM) for the subscription/resource group. Customer needs someone with assignment permissions to grant Owner, Contributor, or Reader role. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/advisor/permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/My Resource Is Not Shown in the Blade_Advisor

symptom: Customer subscription is not shown in Azure Advisor despite having multiple subscriptions
rootCause: The subscription is not selected in the Azure Portal global subscription filter settings
solution: Navigate to Portal Settings (gear icon) > expand Subscriptions > ensure the desired subscription is checked. Then navigate to Advisor overview and verify the subscription filter is correctly applied.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/My Subscription Is Not Shown_Advisor

symptom: Unable to postpone or dismiss Azure Advisor recommendations even though the user has the required RBAC permissions (Owner/Contributor role), receiving Forbidden or permission error
rootCause: A deny assignment (e.g., from Azure Databricks, ARO, or other managed services) overrides the user RBAC permissions and blocks the Microsoft.Advisor/recommendations/suppressions/write action
solution: Query ARM Kusto (armprodgbl.eastus.kusto.windows.net) HttpIncomingRequests for advisor + suppressions operations to find DenyAssignmentAuthorizationFailed errors and identify the deny assignment. Customer needs to remove or modify the deny assignment. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/advisor/view-recommendations#dismissing-and-postponing-recommendations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Not Able To Postpone or Dismiss Recommendations_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor cost recommendation amounts differ between portal UI and CSV/PDF export, or between overall view and drill-down view for right-size VM recommendations
rootCause: Portal drill-down calculates discounted price by calling billing API (shown in customer currency). Overall view, CSV/PDF export, and REST API use retail price from partner teams (always in USD). Billing API throttles at high call volumes so exports cannot use discounted pricing for all rows.
solution: This is by design. Inform customer: (1) Portal drill-down = discounted price in customer currency, (2) Export/API = retail price in USD, (3) For all recommendations except RI and right-size, retail costs shown everywhere, (4) RI always shows discounted costs from partner team. PG is working to align.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Cost recommendation_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor commitments filter (Term and Look-back Period) does not change or filter right-sizing/shutdown VM recommendations
rootCause: The commitments filter only applies to savings plan and reserved instances (RI) recommendations. It does not apply to right-sizing recommendations by design.
solution: Inform customer the commitments filter is only for savings plan/RI. For right-sizing recommendations, adjust CPU utilization threshold and Lookback period in Advisor Configuration blade at subscription level (available: 7/14/21/30/60/90 days). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/advisor/advisor-get-started#configure-recommendations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Inaccurate right sizing recommendation or consumption related issues in Advisor_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor returns zero recommendations for newly created resources or no usage/performance-based recommendations appear
rootCause: Advisor needs minimum data history: general recommendations require at least 24 hours, usage/performance-based recommendations need up to 14 days of resource data. Security Center recommendations require Security Center to be enabled on the subscription.
solution: Verify resource age. Wait at least 24 hours for general recommendations and up to 14 days for usage/performance recommendations. For Security recommendations, ensure Microsoft Defender for Cloud is enabled on the subscription.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Not Getting Any Recommendations_Advisor

symptom: Customer is not getting any Azure Advisor recommendations for SQL databases, App Services, Redis Cache, or other non-VM resources
rootCause: Advisor recommendations for these services are generated by the service itself, not by Advisor. Advisor calls the respective service API to fetch recommendations. If the service has no recommendations available, Advisor cannot display any.
solution: Check if recommendations exist within the service own blade: SQL > Performance Recommendation, App Services > App Service Advisor, Redis Cache > Redis Cache Advisor under General Settings. If the service blade shows no recommendations, there are none for Advisor to display. If recommendations exist in service blade but not in Advisor, escalate to PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Not Getting Any Recommendations_Advisor

symptom: ACSS Create deployment fails with ARMTemplateDeploymentFailed/AuthorizationFailed. The user-assigned managed identity does not have authorization to perform actions (e.g. Microsoft.Compute/availabilitySets/write) over the required scope.
rootCause: The user-assigned managed identity provided by customer does not have the necessary permissions to deploy a SAP workload via Azure Center for SAP Solutions.
solution: Update the user-assigned managed identity to use the correct roles (e.g. Azure Center for SAP solutions administrator) over the subscription. Customer can also create custom roles with required permissions. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sap/center-sap-solutions/manage-with-azure-rbac
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Create ARMTemplateDeploymentFailed AuthorizationFailed_ACSS

symptom: ACSS deployment fails with ResourceNotPermittedOnDelegatedSubnet error. NIC cannot be created in subnet that has delegation(s) to external services (e.g. Microsoft.Web/serverFarms).
rootCause: The appsubnet or dbsubnet used for ACSS deployment has a delegation setup to external services. IP configuration cannot be created on a delegated subnet.
solution: Remove the delegation from the subnet. Query delegation: az network vnet subnet show --query delegations. Remove: az network vnet subnet update --remove delegations. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/manage-subnet-delegation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Create deployment failed ResourceNotPermittedOnDelegatedSubnet_ACSS

symptom: ACSS registration fails with FailedToResolveIpAddressFromSapHostname. SAP VM OS is unable to resolve the IP address for a hostname during VIS registration.
rootCause: The ASCS instance cannot connect to the hostname mentioned in the error. Root causes include VNet issues, incorrect hosts file entries, or DNS unable to resolve SAP hostnames.
solution: Check network connectivity by pinging the hostname from the ASCS VM. Verify DNS configuration and hosts file entries on the VMs. Ensure network prerequisites are met: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sap/center-sap-solutions/prepare-network. If needed, create collaboration with Azure Networking team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/FailedToResolveIpAddressFromSapHostname_ACSS

symptom: ACSS registration fails with GivenVmIsNotAscsVm error. The VM provided is not running the ASCS instance for the given SID, or multiple MESSAGESERVER instances found on different hosts (unsupported configuration).
rootCause: Either the provided VM is not running the SAP ASCS instance, or the SAP system has an unsupported configuration with MESSAGESERVER running on multiple hosts.
solution: Provide the correct VM running the ASCS instance. If multiple MESSAGESERVER instances found, customer must correct the SAP system configuration to ensure only one ASCS instance runs on the correct host. Verify using WaaSPlatformDiagnosticEvent Kusto query with GetSystemInstanceList.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/GivenVmIsNotAscsVm_ACSS

symptom: ACSS Install deployment fails with InstallScsInstallationFailed. Ansible task has failed during SCS installation. Ansible installation logs are present in the storage account referenced in the error.
rootCause: Storage account from where the deployer VM picks up the ansible Zip file had issues, causing the SCS install to fail.
solution: Have customer upload ansible logs container (from error message) plus sapinst_dev.log and sapinst.log from SCS and ERS VMs to DTM. Escalate via ICM to: Service Azure Workloads Platform as Service / Team Azure SAP Business Platform as Service - CSI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Install Failure InstallScsInstallationFailed_ACSS

symptom: ACSS Install deployment fails with zypper return code 7: System management is locked by the application with pid (zypper). Ansible task for OS package update fails due to zypper process lock.
rootCause: System management (zypper) is locked by another running zypper process on the SLES VM, preventing the ACSS ansible installer from updating packages.
solution: Restart the VM and retry the install. If issue persists, collect Ansible installation logs from the storage account mentioned in the error and open an Ava request with ACSS SMEs for further investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/OS Process Lock_ACSS

symptom: ACSS Install deployment fails with AnsibleTaskExecutionFailed during OS package update (RHEL) or zypper repository registration check failure (SUSE). VM cannot reach OS repositories.
rootCause: Inability to reach the OS repositories (RHEL RHUI or SUSE repos) due to network restrictions, firewall rules, or other network-related issues blocking required endpoints.
solution: Review firewall rules to ensure appropriate endpoints for SUSE/RHEL are permitted per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/center-sap-solutions/prepare-network. Retry after confirming network settings. If persists, engage Linux Resources team to check for known outages on OS vendor side.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/OS Repo Connectivity Issues_ACSS

symptom: ACSS Register operation fails with AuthorizationFailed. The user-assigned identity for the VIS does not have authorization to perform required actions (e.g. Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/read) over the resource scope.
rootCause: The user assigned identity for the Virtual Instance for SAP (VIS) does not have the correct permissions assigned for ACSS registration operations.
solution: Assign the built-in role Azure Center for SAP Solutions Service role to the user assigned identity. Alternatively, create a custom role with all required permissions. Check role assignments via Access Control (IAM) > Role Assignments and verify against the required permissions list.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Register AuthorizationFailed_ACSS

symptom: ACSS registration fails with MisconfiguredSapSystem error: more than one ASCS instance found for the SID. SAP system has duplicate ASCS instances making configuration ambiguous.
rootCause: Multiple ASCS instances are present in the SAP system configuration, which is not valid. Duplicate sapstartsrv entries in /usr/sap/<SID>/SYS/global/sapcontrol directory cause the system to report multiple ASCS instances.
solution: Reconfigure SAP system to have only one ASCS instance: 1) Stop sapstartsrv (sapcontrol -nr <nr> -function StopService). 2) Go to /usr/sap/<SID>/SYS/global/sapcontrol. 3) Delete conflicting duplicate files for the affected hostname. 4) Restart sapstartsrv. 5) Verify with sapcontrol GetSystemInstanceList that only one ASCS entry appears.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Register MisconfiguredSapSystem MoreThanOneASCS_ACSS

symptom: ACSS registration fails with SAPDBHOSTNotFound error. Failed to discover the database VM; SAPDBHOST parameter is null in the application server profile.
rootCause: The SAP system is using the Java stack, which is not supported by Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS).
solution: Inform the customer that Java stack is not supported by ACSS. Refer to ACSS Supported Systems documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sap/center-sap-solutions/register-existing-system#supported-systems
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Register SAPDBHOSTNotFound_ACSS

symptom: Azure Advisor shows outdated or stale recommendations older than 72 hours, or recommendations referencing resources that have been deleted; last refresh date does not match today
rootCause: Advisor auto-refresh occurs every 24 hours by design; bug in Advisor Recommender service may fail to clear deleted-resource recommendations from cache
solution: Verify last refresh date on Advisor dashboard; if recommendation is for a deleted resource (clicking resource shows not found), file ICM to Advisor Recommender team; use Advisor Partner PowerBI Dashboard (aka.ms/advisordashboard) or SelfHelpContent repo to find owning PG team; default escalation to CRI-AdvisorRecommender or Azure Advisor / Triage
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Out of Date Recommendations Are Shown_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor recommendation is still listed after customer has implemented remediation steps; recommendation persists beyond 24-hour auto-refresh period
rootCause: Either incomplete remediation by customer (not all required steps completed), or backend issue where recommendation-owning team fails to filter out recommendations for already-remediated resources
solution: First confirm 24h refresh period has passed; validate customer completed ALL remediation steps; if persists, use ASC subscription page > Advisor Recommendations to find recommendation owner (Owner Email, Owner Team, IcM Path); engage owning PG team; if owner fields blank/invalid, file ICM with Advisor PG team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Recommendation is still listed after implementing remediation_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor recommendation "Enable Accelerated Logs for improved performance" for MySQL Flexible Server persists even after enabling the feature; recommendation also appears on service blade of Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server
rootCause: Known bug in recommendation logic: recommendation is not cleared after accelerated logs are enabled; PG team is aware and actively working on a fix
solution: If accelerated logs are already enabled on the server, customer can safely ignore this recommendation; PG is working on permanent fix; reference ICM 588077761
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Recommendation is still listed after implementing remediation_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor security recommendations intermittently disappear and reappear; security recommendations not visible at times in Advisor portal
rootCause: Known issue with service bus queues that process security recommendations; queues get clogged frequently causing recommendations to be unavailable; PG redesigning the process (ADO work item 16306284)
solution: As workaround, advise customer to use Microsoft Defender for Cloud to view security recommendations instead of Advisor; PG is redesigning the process but no ETA available
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Recommendations disappear and reappear_Advisor

symptom: Reserved Instance or Savings Plan recommendation is not present or is incorrect on Azure Advisor; customer expects RI/SP recommendations but they are missing or show wrong values
rootCause: RI/SP recommendations are generated by Reservations Core team (Billing scope), not by Advisor; Advisor only fetches and displays them; issue may be at source team (not generating) or at Advisor (not fetching/displaying correctly)
solution: Use ASC to check if recommendation exists for the subscription; compare Advisor page with Reservations/Savings Plan source pages in Azure Portal; if source page has recs but Advisor does not -> Advisor issue, engage Advisor SMEs via AVA; if neither page has recs -> Billing/Reservations issue, engage Billing team (SAP: Azure/Subscription management/Reserved instance requests)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Reserved Instance or Savings Plan recommendations incorrect or not shown_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor security recommendation "Virtual machines should enable Azure Disk Encryption or EncryptionAtHost" persists even after customer has enabled encryption at host on Windows or Linux VMs
rootCause: The recommendation requires 3 separate steps to be fully resolved, not just enabling encryption: (1) enable encryption itself, (2) enable Guest Configuration policy, (3) enable system-assigned managed identity; customers commonly miss steps 2 and 3
solution: Verify customer has completed all 3 steps: enable encryption + enable Guest Configuration policy + enable system-assigned identity; wait 24h for recommendation refresh; if still present on both Advisor and Defender -> Defender issue; if on Advisor but not Defender -> Advisor issue, engage AVA; reference Defender TSG: Encryption Policy in Defender for Cloud
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Security recommendations_Advisor

symptom: Azure Advisor Security Score displays "No Data" instead of a numerical value; security category score is missing or outdated; Advisor Security Score and Microsoft Defender for Cloud Secure Score are not aligned
rootCause: Latency refresh issue between Microsoft Defender for Cloud (MDC) Secure Score and Azure Advisor Security category; typically takes 24-48 hours to propagate
solution: Wait 24-48 hours for refresh to complete; compare MDC Secure Score with Advisor Security Score; if MDC score is correct but Advisor shows No Data -> Advisor issue, engage AVA channel for PG engagement; if MDC score is also wrong -> Defender issue, engage Defender team (Microsoft Defender for Cloud / Defenders - CRI)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/Security recommendations_Advisor

symptom: Error "Failed to dismiss recommendation" when trying to dismiss Azure Advisor recommendations related to Databricks virtual machines; unable to dismiss or postpone Databricks VM recommendations
rootCause: By design: resource locks on Databricks VMs prevent dismissal of recommendations; permanent fix is in development by Databricks PG team
solution: Create ICM to Azure Databricks RP team to remove resource locks hindering dismissal; use ICM template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=83m83Y; reference ICMs 411388317 and 412012574; do NOT escalate to WASU; permanent fix tracked in ADO work items 25113414 and 25032320
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Advisor/TSGs/UnableToDismissDatabricksRecommendations_Advisor

symptom: ACSS workloads extension installation fails on SAP VM with error indicating extension cannot communicate with managed RG storage account (WorkloadsExtensionInstallFailedWithStorageAccountNotReachable)
rootCause: SAP VM vNet/firewall is not configured to allow connectivity to the managed resource group storage account. Most common in HA scenarios where customer has not added storage account service tag in firewall rules.
solution: Configure vNet following https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/center-sap-solutions/prepare-network. In HA scenarios, add storage account service tag in firewall rules. Cross-verify with example network configuration in docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Workloads Extension TSGs/Workloads Extension InstallFailedWithStora_ACSS

symptom: ACSS WorkloadsExtensionLinux needs to be uninstalled and re-registered on all VMs when ACSS registration or extension encounters persistent errors
rootCause: Workloads extension state is corrupted or outdated, requiring clean reinstall and re-registration from ACSS portal.
solution: For each VM: go to VM > Extensions + Applications > click WorkloadExtensionLinux > Uninstall. After successful deletion from all VMs, re-register the ASCS VM from the ACSS Portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Workloads Extension TSGs/Workloads Extension Uninstall Extension and Re Register_ACSS

symptom: ACSS monitoring extension fails with HealthAndStatusConnectivityIssueToCrlEndpoint error - unable to evaluate health and status of VIS, monitoring extension cannot verify script signature due to no or delayed connectivity to CRL endpoint microsoft.com
rootCause: Connectivity issue between the ASCS VM and required CRL (Certificate Revocation List) endpoints. The monitoring extension needs to verify script signatures via CRL endpoint but network configuration blocks or delays the connection.
solution: Engage Azure Networking on collaboration. Verify customer has configured network prerequisites per public doc (Prepare Network for Infrastructure Deployment). Ensure NSG allows outbound connectivity to CRL endpoint microsoft.com. Kusto: cluster(waasservices.eastus.kusto.windows.net).database(WaaSKustoDB).WaaSErrorEvent with ErrorCodeName == HealthAndStatusConnectivityIssueToCrlEndpoint
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Monitoring TSGs/HealthAndStatusConnectivityIssueToCrlEndpoint_ACSS

symptom: ACSS Monitoring Extension installation fails and VIS Health/Status errors out after VIS deletion and re-registration - MonitoringExtensionLinux or MonitoringExtensionWindows was not cleaned up from SCS VMs before re-registering
rootCause: The Monitoring Extension was not manually uninstalled from SCS VMs after the previous VIS deletion. The stale extension conflicts with the new installation during re-registration, causing monitoring extension install failure and health/status errors.
solution: Before re-registering a VIS with ACSS after deletion: 1) Navigate to SCS VM(s) 2) Go to Settings > Extensions + applications 3) Select MonitoringExtensionLinux or MonitoringExtensionWindows 4) Click Uninstall. The extension will be reinstalled automatically when the new VIS is registered.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Monitoring TSGs/Monitoring Clean up Monitoring Extension post SVI del_ACSS

symptom: ACSS Monitoring Extension installation fails with OperationNotAllowed - ARM template deployment fails with error VM Extension with publisher Microsoft.Azure.Workloads and type MonitoringExtensionLinux does not support setting enableAutomaticUpgrade property to true
rootCause: The ACSS monitoring extension was onboarded for auto-upgrade but was not added to the GASupportedExtensions list (a requirement propagated later). This caused enableAutomaticUpgrade=true to fail on affected subscriptions.
solution: A hotfix was released to disable the enableAutomaticUpgrade flag for monitoring extensions installed from the service. Customer needs to delete the VIS and re-register it. Kusto: WaaSMetricEvent where MetricName == MonitorExtensionInstallProcessingFailed. Bug 15181603. SME: Devaishi Tiwari (dtiwari) and Subash Chandra (koredd).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Monitoring TSGs/Monitoring Extension install fail OperationNotAllowed_ACSS

symptom: SAP instance start/stop automation via Logic App and ARM template fails with AuthorizationFailed error in Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS) - Managed Identity lacks permission to perform Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/deallocate/action
rootCause: The Managed Identity associated with the Logic App did not have the Azure Center for SAP Solutions Administrator RBAC role, which is required to manage SAP workloads deployed through ACSS including start/stop operations on SAP Virtual Instances and underlying VMs.
solution: Assign the Azure Center for SAP Solutions Administrator role to the Logic App Managed Identity at the resource group or subscription level covering both SAP Virtual Instance and underlying VMs. Also consider assigning Automation Contributor role if using Azure Automation. Verify via Azure Portal > Subscriptions > Access Control (IAM). Escalation: ICM to Azure Workloads Platform as Service / Azure SAP Business Platform as Service - CSI team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Monitoring TSGs/SAP Start and Stop Automation Failure in ACSS_ACSS

symptom: Azure Advisor recommendations not visible in portal for ACSS SAP Virtual Instance (VIS) - customer cannot see advisor recommendations after registering/installing VIS
rootCause: Three possible causes: 1) Database type is empty or not among supported types (HANA, DB2, MSSQL, ORACLE). 2) Resource group or VIS was deleted and recreated with same name - Advisor treats the resource as deleted and skips recommendations (7-day delay). 3) All quality checks are passing correctly so no recommendations are generated.
solution: For cause 1: Re-register VIS with correct DB type, recommendations appear within 1 hour. For cause 2: Re-register in a different resource group or wait 7 days. Kusto diagnostics: WaaSMetricEvent where Message contains Database type is not supported for validation; WaaSMetricEvent where MetricName == ValidationFailure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Quality Insights/Insights Quality checks Azure advisor recommendations not available in Portal_ACSS

symptom: Cannot update Purchase Plan information (name, publisher, product) on an existing Azure Compute Gallery Image Definition; properties are immutable after creation
rootCause: Purchase Plan properties are immutable after the Image Definition is created. Two Image Definitions within the same ACG cannot share identical Publisher/Offer/SKU.
solution: Specify correct Purchase Plan info at Image Definition creation time (Portal Publishing options tab, CLI az sig image-definition create, or PowerShell). If wrong, delete and recreate. Ensure OS/Generation/State of source match the Definition.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Specify Purchase Plan Information_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Gallery Image Version - Conflict error: source image OS type, Hypervisor generation, or OS state does not match the parent gallery Image Definition properties
rootCause: Source image properties (OS type Windows/Linux, VM generation V1/V2, OS state Generalized/Specialized) must exactly match the Image Definition. Mismatches cause Conflict or OperationNotAllowed.
solution: Select a source image matching the Image Definition properties, or create a new Image Definition with matching OS type, VM generation, and OS state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Gallery Image Version_ACG

symptom: Gallery Image Version fails with Conflict: IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported property mismatch between source disk (null) and image definition (True)
rootCause: Source VM OS disk does not have AcceleratedNetwork capability enabled, but Image Definition requires IsAcceleratedNetworkSupported=True.
solution: Enable accelerated networking on source disk: az disk update -n <diskName> --resource-group <rg> --accelerated-network true. Then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Gallery Image Version_ACG

symptom: Gallery Image Version fails: diskControllerType mismatch - source missing NVMe support; known bug capturing VM with NVMe to image definition (ADO#71722)
rootCause: Source OS disk diskControllerTypes does not include NVMe as required by target Image Definition. Known bug: capturing VM with NVMe diskControllerType to image definition fails.
solution: Update source disk: az resource update --ids <diskId> --set properties.supportedCapabilities.diskControllerTypes=SCSI,NVMe. For NVMe capture bug workaround: create image version from OS disk directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Gallery Image Version_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Image Definition - 400 Bad Request: entity name invalid per validation rule; must match ^[^_\W][\w-._]{0,79}(?<![-.])$
rootCause: Image Definition name contains invalid characters. Only uppercase/lowercase letters, digits, dots, dashes, periods allowed, max 80 chars.
solution: Rename using only allowed characters. Must not start with underscore/non-word char, must not end with dash or dot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Definition_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Image Definition - 409 Conflict: gallery image with same publisher/offer/sku already exists in the Azure Compute Gallery
rootCause: Publisher/Offer/SKU combination must be unique within the same gallery.
solution: Change at least one of publisher, offer, or sku to make it unique. Use az sig image-definition list to check existing definitions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Definition_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Azure Compute Gallery - 409 Conflict InvalidResourceLocation: gallery name already exists in another location within same subscription
rootCause: Gallery names must be unique within a subscription. Same name in different regions causes conflict.
solution: Use a unique gallery name. Check existing galleries via az sig list or ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Gallery_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Azure Compute Gallery - 400 Bad Request: gallery name invalid per validation rule
rootCause: Gallery name contains invalid characters.
solution: Use only uppercase/lowercase letters, digits, dots and periods for gallery name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Gallery_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Gallery Image Version - 400 InvalidParameter: version name must follow Major(int).Minor(int).Patch(int) format (e.g. 1.0.0)
rootCause: Image Version name must follow semantic versioning: Major.Minor.Patch with numbers and periods only.
solution: Use correct format: e.g. 1.0.0, 2024.1.0. Only numbers and periods allowed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Version_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Gallery Image Version - NotFound: source image not found; must exist in same region and subscription as gallery image version
rootCause: Source managed image must exist, be in same region, and same subscription as the gallery.
solution: Verify source image exists, is in same region and subscription. Move or recreate if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Version_ACG

symptom: Cannot create Gallery Image Version - Conflict: source image being used by another replication currently
rootCause: Source image is locked by another concurrent replication operation.
solution: Wait for current replication to complete, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Version_ACG

symptom: Gallery Image Version replication timeout after 6 hours - TimeoutException in CopySeedBlobsIntoRegionBlock; OS disk max 2TB, data disk >1TB unsupported
rootCause: Image too large causes replication timeout (default 6h). OS disk max 2TB. Data disk >1TB may fail.
solution: For OS disk: max 2TB. Data disk <1TB: delete and recreate version. Data disk >1TB: not fully supported. Reduce target regions initially, add more later. Engineering may extend timeout to 24h on request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Create Shared Image Version_ACG

symptom: Cannot delete Azure Image Builder template - IdentityError 404: user assigned managed identity was deleted before the image template; MSI NotFound
rootCause: User Assigned Managed Identity deleted before being disassociated from Image Builder template. Template references non-existent identity.
solution: If identity still associated: az image builder identity remove. If already deleted: (1) recreate identity with same name/RG, (2) create temp identity, (3) PATCH template via ARM API setting old=null new={}, (4) delete template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Delete Image Template_ACG

symptom: Cannot delete Azure Image Builder template - OperationNotAllowed: DELETE not allowed in provisioningState creating
rootCause: Image Template stuck in creating provisioning state. DELETE blocked.
solution: Wait for provisioning to finish. If stuck indefinitely, contact AIB support for backend state change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Delete Image Template_ACG

symptom: Cannot delete Image Builder template - UAMI deleted before removal from config; identity blade greyed out; all operations fail with UAMI validation errors
rootCause: UAMI deleted before removal from template. Template in provisioningState=Failed, immutable. AIB requires existing UAMI in PATCH body.
solution: (1) Recreate deleted identity exact same name/RG. (2) Create temp identity. (3) body.json: old identity=null, new identity={}. (4) az rest --method patch with api-version=2024-02-01. (5) Delete template after PATCH.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Delete Image Template_ACG

symptom: Cannot delete Azure Compute Gallery or Image Definition - CannotDeleteResource: cannot delete before nested resources are deleted
rootCause: ACG enforces hierarchical deletion order. Gallery > Definition > Version; must delete in reverse order.
solution: Delete in reverse order: (1) Image Versions (az sig image-version delete), (2) Image Definitions (az sig image-definition delete), (3) Gallery (az sig delete). Check nested resources in ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Cannot Delete with Nested Resources_ACG

symptom: Encryption is not supported for gallery image version with shared parent gallery when community sharing is enabled. Error: Encryption is not supported for gallery image version with shared parent gallery. Permissions = Community.
rootCause: Community sharing does not support encrypted images. An image version with encryption properties is being added to a gallery enabled for community sharing.
solution: Either (1) create the image version in a new gallery that has not been shared to the community, or (2) reset the permissions of the gallery to turn off community sharing before adding encrypted image versions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Common Errors for Community Galleries_ACG

symptom: Cannot share a gallery to community because it contains encrypted image versions. Error: Cannot share a gallery with encrypted version within gallery image. Please select a different gallery without any encrypted gallery image versions or delete the encrypted version before sharing.
rootCause: Gallery cannot be enabled for community sharing when it contains image versions with encryption. Community sharing does not support encrypted images.
solution: Either (1) delete the encrypted image version(s), or (2) use a new gallery that does not have any encrypted images for community sharing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Common Errors for Community Galleries_ACG

symptom: Authorization failure when trying to enable community sharing on gallery. Error: The client does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.Compute/galleries/share/action over scope.
rootCause: The user, service principal, group, or managed identity used to enable community sharing is not a member of the Owner role on the gallery/resource group/subscription.
solution: Either (1) assign the Owner role to the user/service principal/group/managed identity, or (2) use a different identity that already has Owner permissions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Common Errors for Community Galleries_ACG

symptom: Cannot delete a gallery that has been shared to the community. Error: Gallery is still shared to Community. Please unshare the gallery first before deleting the gallery.
rootCause: Azure prevents deletion of galleries that are currently shared to the community. The gallery must be unshared before it can be deleted.
solution: Reset the permissions for the gallery to private (unshare from community) before attempting to delete the gallery.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Common Errors for Community Galleries_ACG

symptom: Failed to stop replication of image version in Azure Compute Gallery from Azure Portal. Customers trying to stop replication of image versions to different regions experience failure when updating from portal.
rootCause: Safety profile feature (allowDeletionOfReplicatedLocations=false) is enabled by default for all image versions created with API version 2022-01-03 or later, which blocks deletion of previously replicated regions.
solution: Set allowDeletionOfReplicatedLocations=true using Azure CLI: az sig image-version update --resource-group <rg> --gallery-name <gallery> --gallery-image-definition <def> --gallery-image-version <ver> --set safetyProfile.allowDeletionOfReplicatedLocations=true. Or uncheck 'Lock deleting Replicated Locations' in Portal Configuration Settings. Then proceed to delete the replicated region.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Failed to update Replication of Image Version in Azure Compute Gallery_ACG

symptom: Image version replication fails with CannotReplicateImageVersion error: Cannot replicate Image Version - Source image is missing when adding new regions.
rootCause: The source image (managed image/snapshot/VHD) used to create the image version was deleted. When adding new replication regions, Azure needs the source image to create new replicas.
solution: Keep the source image around if you need to add new replication regions in the future. If the source is already deleted, the only workaround is to recreate the image version from a new source.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/ImageNotFound_ACG

symptom: VM created from latest image version in Azure Compute Gallery deploys a much older version instead of the newest one. When using Create VM from image definition with latest option, it picks an old version.
rootCause: By design, the latest image version is determined by semantic versioning (highest MajorVersion.MinorVersion.Patch), not by creation date. For example, version 2.12.17 is considered newer than 2.2.35 because 12 > 2 in MinorVersion comparison.
solution: Ensure new image versions use a higher semantic version number (e.g., 2.13.x or 2.12.18 instead of 2.2.35). Alternatively, deploy VMs using a specific version number instead of 'latest'. Verify versions with Kusto: cluster('azcrpbifollower').database('pirbi_allprod').GalleryImageVersion | where SubscriptionId == '<sub>' | where GalleryImageName has '<def>' | distinct GalleryImageVersionName
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/LatestImageVersion_ACG

symptom: LinkedAuthorizationFailed error when creating Azure Compute Gallery image version in cross tenant using Azure CLI version 2.69.0 or 2.70.0. The current tenant is not authorized to access linked subscription.
rootCause: Known bug in Azure CLI version 2.69.0 and 2.70.0 due to a code change, confirmed by PG via ICM 602997894. The CLI fails to properly handle cross-tenant authentication for gallery operations.
solution: Upgrade Azure CLI to version 2.71.0 (which contains the fix), or downgrade to version 2.68.0 as a workaround. Note: CLI version cannot be changed in Azure Cloud Shell. First validate customer commands and app registration/SPN cross-tenant configuration are correct per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/share-using-app-registration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Linked Authorization error while creating image in cross tenant using Azure CLI_ACG

symptom: Image version creation fails with InternalOperationError: Replication failed in this region due to Contract.Assert failed: Data model has null SecurityProfile, so DiskRP SecurityProfile should also be null.
rootCause: Mismatch in security type between the image definition and the source image. The image definition was not created with Trusted Launch security type, causing a null SecurityProfile assertion failure during replication.
solution: Recreate the image definition with the correct security type set to Trusted Launch. Then create the image version under the new definition. Verify with PowerShell: Get-AzGalleryImageDefinition -ResourceGroupName <rg> -GalleryName <gallery> -GalleryImageDefinitionName <def> and check Features for TrustedLaunch.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Replication failed in this region due to Contract.Assert failed_ACG

symptom: Azure Compute Gallery image version replication fails with ReplicationJobsTimedOut error when source image has large disks (>100GB). Error: 'Replication to all the target regions not completed. The replication job has not completed at region'
rootCause: Replication times out when gallery image version contains large disks (>100GB). Large disk sizes cause replication jobs to exceed timeout thresholds, especially when replicating to distant or multiple regions.
solution: 1) Check if disks really need to be that large. 2) Keep source and destination regions close. 3) Set replicaCount to 1 initially. 4) If one region fails, remove and re-add it. 5) If multiple regions fail, replicate to home region first, then add others. Use CapsApiQosEvent and CapsContextActivityEvent Kusto queries to identify specific replication failures.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/ReplicationJobsTimedOut_ACG

symptom: Creating Azure Compute Gallery image version via Capture fails with Conflict error: 'The resource has a different plan than the parent gallery image plan [null]'. Purchase plan information mismatch between source VM and gallery image definition.
rootCause: The source VM was created from a Marketplace image with a purchase plan, but the gallery Image Definition was created without specifying the purchase plan information. The plan metadata must match.
solution: Re-create the Image Definition with the correct purchase plan information (name, publisher, product) as shown in the error message. Use ARM operations at subscription level to find the failed deployment, filter by 'Microsoft.Compute-CaptureVM' resource name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Resource has a Different Plan_ACG

symptom: Azure Compute Gallery image version update fails with error: 'The replication region and replica count cannot be changed when using shallow replication.' Cannot modify replication settings after enabling shallow replication.
rootCause: When Shallow Replication is enabled for an image version, replication settings (replica count and target regions) become immutable. Any attempt to modify them after creation results in this error.
solution: Configure all desired replication regions and replica counts BEFORE enabling the Shallow Replication option during image version creation. If customer needs to modify settings later, do not enable Shallow Replication.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Shallow Replication in Azure Compute Gallery_ACG

symptom: Publishing image to Azure Compute Gallery fails with SubscriptionNotRegistered error: 'Cannot specify region in target regions because the subscription is missing required feature Microsoft.Resources/region'.
rootCause: The subscription is not enabled/registered for the specific target region. The Microsoft.Resources provider is not registered for that region in the subscription.
solution: Create a collaboration task and engage the Quota Team (SAP: Azure/Service and subscription limits (quotas)/Other Requests) to enable the subscription for the desired region. Check enabled regions via Portal: Subscription > Resource Providers > Microsoft.Resources > Locations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/SubscriptionNotRegistered_ACG

symptom: Customer needs to uninstall VM Application from a virtual machine while keeping the application in the Azure Compute Gallery. Removing VMApp from VM without deleting gallery resource.
solution: Use PowerShell: 1) $vm = Get-AzVM -ResourceGroupName <rg> -Name <vm>  2) Remove-AzVmGalleryApplication -VM $vm -GalleryApplicationsReferenceId '<resource URI of app version>'  3) Update-AzVM -ResourceGroupName <rg> -VM $vm. The VM App is removed from VM but remains in gallery.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/Uninstall VmApps_ACG

symptom: VM Application deployment fails because the application version has been deprecated. Error: 'Current VM Application Version {name} was deprecated at {date}'.
rootCause: Attempting to deploy a VM Application version that has already passed its deprecation date.
solution: Either change the expiry/deprecation date for the application version and retry, or use a different application version that is not deprecated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Common deployment errors_ACG

symptom: VM Application deployment fails with OS mismatch error: 'Current VM Application Version supports OS {OS}, while current OSDisk OS is {OS}'. Trying to deploy Windows app to Linux VM or vice versa.
rootCause: The VM Application was created for a different OS type than the target VM's operating system.
solution: Use an application version that matches the target machine's OS type (Windows or Linux).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Common deployment errors_ACG

symptom: VM Application deployment fails: 'The maximum number of VM applications (max=5) has been exceeded.' Cannot add more than 5 VM Applications to a single VM or VMSS.
rootCause: Azure currently only supports a maximum of 5 VM Applications per VM or VMSS instance.
solution: Remove one or more existing VM Applications from the VM/VMSS before adding new ones. Current limit is 5 applications per VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Common deployment errors_ACG

symptom: VM Application deployment fails: 'Storage account in the arguments does not exist.' No applications found for this subscription when deploying VM Application.
rootCause: The specified VM Application does not exist or is not available for the current subscription.
solution: Verify that the application exists and is available for this subscription. Check the application resource URI and subscription access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Common deployment errors_ACG

symptom: VM Application deployment fails: 'The gallery image is not available in {region} region.' Gallery application version was not replicated to the target deployment region.
rootCause: The gallery application version exists but has not been replicated to the region where the VM is being deployed.
solution: Update the gallery application version to replicate it to the required region, or deploy the VM in a region where the application version is already available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Common deployment errors_ACG

symptom: VM Application deployment fails during archive extraction: 'Expand-Archive: The path <AppName>.zip either does not exist or is not a valid file system path.' VMApp extension strips .zip file extension during download.
rootCause: The VM Application extension does not always preserve the original file extension when downloading the artifact. A file uploaded as 'app.zip' may be downloaded as 'app' without the .zip extension, causing Expand-Archive to fail.
solution: Update the install command to rename the file before extraction: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command 'Rename-Item <AppName> <AppName>.zip; Expand-Archive .\<AppName>.zip -DestinationPath . -Force; Start-Process .\<installer> -ArgumentList /quiet -Wait'. Check logs at C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.CPlat.Core.VMApplicationManagerWindows\.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Expand Archive Cannot Find ZIP_ACG

symptom: Cannot create VM Application Definition in Azure Compute Gallery due to invalid name. Error: 'The entity name galleryApplicationName is invalid according to its validation rule'. StatusCode 400 Bad Request.
rootCause: The VM Application Definition name contains invalid characters. Names must start with a letter or digit and can only contain letters, digits, dots, dashes, and periods (max 80 chars).
solution: Use only valid characters for the definition name: uppercase/lowercase letters, digits, dots, dashes, and periods. Cannot start with underscore or end with dash/period.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot create application definition_ACG

symptom: Cannot create VM Application version: 'The gallery application version name is invalid. Should follow Major(int32).Minor(int32).Patch(int32) format.' StatusCode 400.
rootCause: The version name does not follow the required semantic versioning format (Major.Minor.Patch where each component is int32, 0 to 2147483647).
solution: Use version names following Major.Minor.Patch format, e.g. 1.0.0, 2018.12.1. Each component must be between 0 and 2,147,483,647.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot create application version_ACG

symptom: Cannot create VM Application version: 'The gallery application version url cannot be accessed: NotFound. Ensure blob exists and is publicly accessible or is a SAS url with read privileges.' StatusCode 400.
rootCause: The package file URL (packageFileId) points to a blob that does not exist, is not accessible, or the SAS token has expired/insufficient permissions.
solution: Verify the blob exists in the storage account, ensure it is either publicly accessible or has a valid SAS URL with read privileges. Check SAS token expiry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot create application version_ACG

symptom: Cannot create VM Application version: 'The blob referenced by source uri is too big. The maximum blob size allowed is 1 GB.' Package file exceeds size limit.
rootCause: The package file or default configuration file exceeds the maximum allowed size of 1 GB for VM Application versions.
solution: Reduce the package file size to below 1 GB. Both the package file and default configuration file must be under the 1 GB limit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot create application version_ACG

symptom: Cannot create VM Application version: 'Append blob type is not supported for PutGalleryApplicationVersion operation. Only page blobs and block blobs are supported.' StatusCode 400.
rootCause: The package file is stored as an append blob in Azure Storage. VM Application versions only support page blobs and block blobs.
solution: Re-upload the package file as either a block blob or page blob. Append blobs are not supported for VM Application packages.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot create application version_ACG

symptom: Cannot create VM Application version: 'Source Image .../applications/APPLICATION is not found. Check source application exists and is in same region as gallery application version being created.'
rootCause: The source application does not exist, is in a different region, or is in a different subscription than the gallery.
solution: Verify: 1) The source application exists. 2) It is in the same region as the gallery application version. 3) It is in the same subscription as the gallery.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot create application version_ACG

symptom: Cannot delete Azure Compute Gallery application definition; error: Gallery application still has gallery application versions. Delete the gallery application versions before deleting the gallery application. StatusCode 400 Bad Request.
rootCause: Gallery application definition still has one or more application versions underneath. ACG enforces hierarchical deletion: versions must be removed before the parent definition can be deleted.
solution: Delete all application versions under the gallery application definition first, then retry deleting the definition.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot delete with nested resources_ACG

symptom: Cannot update gallery application version properties; error: Changing property galleryApplicationVersion.properties.publishingProfile.manageActions.install (or update/delete) is not allowed. StatusCode 400 Bad Request.
rootCause: Only a limited set of properties can be changed on existing gallery application versions: target regions, replica count, and the expiry/signature of the SAS URL. All other property changes are rejected by the API.
solution: Confirm the property being changed is one of the allowed update properties (target regions, replica count, SAS URL expiry/signature). For other properties (e.g., manageActions install/update/delete commands), create a new application version instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Cannot update application definition_ACG

symptom: VM Application version publishing fails with SAS URI is not valid error when using Az.Storage PowerShell module version 3.12.0 to generate SAS tokens.
rootCause: Known bug in Az.Storage PowerShell module version 3.12.0 that generates invalid SAS URIs for VM Application publishing.
solution: Downgrade Az.Storage to version 3.11.0: 1) Uninstall-Module Az.Storage, 2) Close and reopen PowerShell session, 3) Install-Module Az.Storage -RequiredVersion 3.11.0. If already on 3.11.0, verify the SAS URL is valid per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-sas-overview#sas-token.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Sas uri is not valid_ACG

symptom: VM Application version creation fails with InvalidParameter: The SAS is not valid for source uri when the storage account hosting the application binary has firewall enabled.
rootCause: Storage account firewall blocks the Azure Compute Gallery service from downloading the application binary during the VM Application version creation and replication process.
solution: Temporarily disable the storage account firewall during VM Application version creation and replication. After the version is properly created and replicated to all target regions, the firewall can be re-enabled. VMs will later download the binary directly from the compute gallery, not from the storage account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Publishing Sas uri is not valid_SA_firewall_enabled_ACG

symptom: VM Application version replication shows provision failed status with no visible error in portal notifications; Kusto CapsApiQosEvent shows resultCode BlobNotAccessible with StorageForbiddenError 403.
rootCause: Storage account firewall blocks the ACG service from accessing the source blob for replication. ACG attempts MSI-based trusted access but fails if the gallery has no MSI configured. MSI-based access is not yet externally supported.
solution: Disable storage account firewall temporarily to allow replication to complete. Do not suggest MSI-based access to external customers as it is not yet publicly supported. Use Kusto queries on CapsApiQosEvent and CapsContextActivityEvent to confirm the 403/BlobNotAccessible root cause before advising the customer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMApps Replication Provision failed_ACG

symptom: VM deployment from Azure Compute Gallery image fails with VMMarketplaceInvalidInput: Creating a virtual machine from Marketplace image requires Plan information in the request; or plan mismatch error between source VM plan and gallery image plan.
rootCause: Purchase Plan information is missing or incorrectly specified when deploying a VM from an ACG image created from a third-party Marketplace image. First-party images do not require plan info, but third-party images do.
solution: Specify purchase plan information in the deployment template: add purchasePlan block with product, name, and publisher fields. Alternatively use PowerShell (New-AzGalleryImageDefinition) or Azure CLI (az sig image-definition create) with plan parameters. If plan was not set at image definition creation, recreate the image definition with correct plan info (update is not supported).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/TSGs/VMMarketplaceInvalidInput_ACG

symptom: Unable to create Azure Elastic SAN resources; resource creation blocked or access denied
rootCause: Elastic SAN creation is protected by AFEC feature flag (ElasticSanPreview Access). Subscription must be registered.
solution: Register the subscription for AFEC (ElasticSanPreview Access) to enable Elastic SAN resource creation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/How Tos/Elastic SAN Overview_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN networking validation fails when creating or updating volume group with VNET rules
rootCause: Service endpoint not registered on the VNET, or AFEC not set for VNETs across different regions.
solution: Register the Microsoft.Storage or Microsoft.ElasticSan service endpoint on the target VNET subnet. If VNETs are cross-region, ensure AFEC is set.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/How Tos/Elastic SAN Overview_Storage

symptom: Unable to create Elastic SAN Volume Group or Volume; operation fails with permission/authorization error
rootCause: User does not have the required RBAC role assignment (Elastic SAN Owner, Volume Group Owner, or Network Admin).
solution: Assign the appropriate Elastic SAN RBAC role: Elastic SAN Owner (full access), Volume Group Owner (volume group level), or Network Admin (private endpoints). Check role assignments in Subscription > Access Control.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/How Tos/Elastic SAN Overview_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN resource creation takes a long time; provisioning slow or appears stuck
rootCause: Intermittent delays during underlying storage account creation by the Elastic SAN resource provider.
solution: Wait for the operation to complete. If consistently slow, check Jarvis ServiceApiQosEvent for the specific operation correlationId to identify backend delays.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/How Tos/Elastic SAN Overview_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN volume creation fails when multiple parallel volume operations are in flight
rootCause: Limits on concurrent volume create operations that can be handled simultaneously by the Elastic SAN RP.
solution: Reduce the number of parallel volume creation operations. Serialize volume create requests or retry failed ones after the concurrent batch completes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/How Tos/Elastic SAN Overview_Storage

symptom: Incorrect Elastic SAN billing continues after Elastic SAN resource is deleted
rootCause: In rare cases, Elastic SAN billing meters are not stopped upon resource deletion due to a backend issue.
solution: Verify billing usage via Kusto (xstore.kusto.windows.net / xdataanalytics). If billing persists after deletion, escalate to the Elastic SAN PG via ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/How Tos/Elastic SAN Overview_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN scale out fails in Azure Portal with Service Error when updating SAN size (e.g. Canada Central)
rootCause: Bug in Elastic SAN RP related to Volume Groups with missing subnets in VNET configuration. Hotfix rolled out August 2024. ICM 529832765.
solution: Workaround 1: Fix missing subnets in Volume Group VNET rules, then retry update from portal. Workaround 2: Use PowerShell: Update-AzElasticSan -Name <name> -ResourceGroupName <rg> -PublicNetworkAccess Enabled -BaseSizeTiB <val> -ExtendedCapacitySizeTiB <val>. If issue recurs, escalate via ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/Known Issues or Bug/Elastic SAN Emerging Issues

symptom: Elastic SAN I/O timeouts after successful iSCSI login; disk disappears from Windows VM
rootCause: Windows iSCSI initiator unmounts disk when I/O times out. Windows Event 157 is logged. If initiator retry also fails, disk disappears entirely.
solution: Check Windows Event Viewer for Event 157. Initiator typically retries login automatically. If retry fails and disk disappears, reconnect the iSCSI target manually. Investigate underlying network connectivity to the Elastic SAN endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Connectivity_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN iSCSI login fails with Authentication Error or Access Denied
rootCause: Customer did not correctly configure VNET ACL on the Elastic SAN Volume Group. Network security rules must allow the client VNET/subnet.
solution: Verify VNET ACL configuration on the Volume Group. Ensure the client VM subnet has a service endpoint registered and is added to the Volume Group network rules. Use Jarvis server-side telemetry (ServiceApiQosEvent) with correlationId to trace the authentication failure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Connectivity_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN iSCSI login fails with Connection Failed error; target not processing login
rootCause: Network error between client and Elastic SAN target, or target did not process the login (login packet dropped). No server-side logs available for Connection Failed.
solution: Gather client-side diagnostics: Windows Event Viewer / Linux syslogs, nslookup for DNS, PsPing (Windows) or tcp traceroute (Linux) to target endpoint, network trace (Wireshark/tcpdump). Check VNET/NSG rules and network path.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Connectivity_Storage

symptom: Customer requests recovery of deleted Elastic SAN volume, volume group, or snapshot
rootCause: Elastic SAN has an internal soft delete feature with a 10-day retention period. Granular file-level data cannot be recovered.
solution: Collect Elastic SAN Resource ID, Region, deletion date from ASC Operations tab. If within 10-day retention: create ICM to SAN RP team with resource details and OperationID. For volume group recovery, customer must recreate volume group with same name and encryption config. For volume/snapshot, ensure no naming conflict. Recovery is best-effort, never guaranteed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Data Recovery_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN disk unexpectedly unmounts during Windows Failover Cluster restart. iSCSI initiator fails to re-establish path after cluster node restart, MPIO tries failover to another failing path and unmounts the disk.
rootCause: iSCSI initiator session timeout (LinkDownTime) defaults to 15 seconds, which is too short for cluster restart scenarios. When multiple paths go down, the timeout expires before paths can be re-established.
solution: Increase iSCSI initiator session timeout (LinkDownTime) to 30 seconds. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/elastic-san/elastic-san-best-practices#iscsi
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Disk Unmount Unexpectedly_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN volume created from Managed Disk snapshot via PowerShell shows as not initialized. Issue does not occur when using Azure Portal.
rootCause: PowerShell command missing -CreationDataCreateSource DiskSnapshot switch. Without this parameter, the volume is created without snapshot source metadata, causing it to appear uninitialized.
solution: Add -CreationDataCreateSource DiskSnapshot parameter when creating Elastic SAN volume from managed disk snapshot via PowerShell.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Managed Disk Not Initialized_Storage

symptom: Elastic SAN volume shows lower than expected performance (bandwidth/IOPS) when running sequential workloads, despite provisioned size suggesting higher limits.
rootCause: Product limitation: shard level performance limits of 5,000 IOPS and 256 MB/s are lower than volume level targets. Sequential workloads hit single shard bottleneck. (Internal - do not share with customer)
solution: Workaround: stripe multiple volumes to achieve target performance. Use Storage Spaces on Windows: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/storage/storage-spaces/deploy-standalone-storage-spaces
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Performance_Storage

symptom: Customer requests quota increase for Elastic SAN (e.g., maximum number of Elastic SANs per subscription per region, currently limited to 5).
solution: File ICM to ElasticSANRP team with title "Quota Increase for Customer Subscription | <Case number>". Include: Customer Company Name, Business Justification, Timeline, Subscription ID, Region, current/requested Elastic SAN count.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Quota Increase_Storage

symptom: Customer reports being billed for Elastic SAN after creation failed or SAN was deleted. Billing continues because master storage account was created but not cleaned up.
rootCause: During Elastic SAN creation failure, certain resources (master storage account) may still get created, causing ongoing billing even though the Elastic SAN creation overall failed.
solution: 1) Find operationId from ElasticSanRP dgrep logs. 2) Find master storage account via operationId or Kusto. 3) File IcM to delete master storage account using SRPTool (Delete-RSRPAccount). Check master account metadata on XPortal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Troubleshoot_Storage

symptom: Customer encounters "Active sessions present on Volume. Close active sessions before modifying" error when deleting or modifying Elastic SAN volume.
rootCause: Active iSCSI sessions exist to the volume from one or more VMs, preventing deletion or modification.
solution: Customer must terminate iSCSI sessions from all VMs that have active connections to the volume. Note: future releases will include a "force delete" option.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Troubleshoot_Storage

symptom: Updating networking configuration for Elastic SAN volume group with >40 volumes fails with HTTP 409 "NetworkAcls failed on some of the existing Volumes of Volume Group."
rootCause: Update request gets throttled by Storage Resource Provider when volume group has many volumes (>40), causing partial failure of network ACL updates.
solution: Retry the update command. The networking changes should be applied on subsequent attempts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Troubleshoot_Storage

symptom: "Storage Resource Unavailable" error when creating Elastic SAN in certain regions or availability zones.
rootCause: Elastic SAN is not available in all regions/zones. Some regions only support LRS (not ZRS), and some regions may not have Elastic SAN enabled on all availability zones.
solution: 1) Check available SKUs with Get-AzElasticSanSkuList. 2) Ensure SkuName matches region capabilities. 3) Try other availability zones. 4) Use GetSanRpSubscription to find logicalZone to physicalZone mappings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Troubleshoot_Storage

symptom: After attempting to update Elastic SAN volume size, portal shows incorrect/stale volume details instead of reverting to previous values.
rootCause: Volume size update fails due to active iSCSI sessions, but portal may not immediately reflect the rollback to original values (caching issue).
solution: Refresh the volume details in the portal to obtain the correct (original) volume size.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Elastic SAN/TSGs/Elastic SAN Troubleshoot_Storage

symptom: ADE encryption operation succeeds from Azure portal but disk appears as non-encrypted in ASC and unencrypted in Windows Explorer. VM has Dynamic Volumes configured.
rootCause: Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) does not support Dynamic Volumes. This is a documented unsupported scenario.
solution: Customer must first detach or set offline the dynamic volume, then encrypt the VM with ADE, then set the dynamic volume back online. Verify dynamic volumes via Disk Management (look for Dynamic label on the disk type).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/How To Troubleshoot Dynamic Volumes ADE_Encryption

symptom: Enabling ADE on a VM with SSE+CMK (Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Managed Keys) configured fails with error: OperationNotAllowed - Disk encryption set resource cannot be added to VM having disks that were encrypted with Azure Disk Encryption.
rootCause: SSE+CMK is incompatible with ADE. Disks that are part of an Encryption Set for SSE+CMK cannot be encrypted using ADE. There is no direct migration path.
solution: Create a copy of the disk(s) without encryption settings to remove encryption metadata, then apply ADE to the new disk. Steps: 1) Remove SSE+CMK encryption set from disk 2) Create a disk copy without encryption metadata 3) Apply ADE to the new disk.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/How to Migrate (SSE+CMK to ADE) & (ADE to SSE+CMK)_Encryption

symptom: Applying SSE+CMK to a disk previously encrypted with ADE fails with error: Disk cannot have both Azure Disk Encryption and Encryption at rest with customer-managed key enabled. It is currently encrypted with Azure Disk Encryption.
rootCause: Managed disks currently or previously encrypted using ADE cannot be encrypted using customer-managed keys (SSE+CMK). The ADE encryption metadata (UDE flag) persists even after decryption.
solution: Create a copy of the disk(s) without encryption settings to remove the UDE flag, then apply SSE+CMK. Steps: 1) Disable ADE on the disk 2) Create a new disk copy without encryption metadata 3) Apply SSE+CMK encryption set to the new disk.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/How to Migrate (SSE+CMK to ADE) & (ADE to SSE+CMK)_Encryption

symptom: Commvault backup jobs fail on ADE-encrypted VMs reporting ADE extension in failed state: 'Encryption status of one or more disks doesn't match the setting in ADE Extension of VM. Found AzureDiskEncryption extension in failed state'
rootCause: After VM reboot, the guest agent starts ~90 seconds after container starts and takes 30+ more seconds to upload ADE extension status, missing the CRP 2-minute timeout window. CRP marks ADE extension as provisioning failed. Commvault queries ProvisioningState and sees it as failed.
solution: 1) Have Commvault query Extension InstanceView API instead of ProvisioningState (InstanceView is always up-to-date). 2) Alternatively, reapply VM goal state after reboots (can be automated) - CRP uses 90-minute timeout for reapply vs 2-minute for initial status. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/compute/virtual-machines/instance-view
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Known Issues or Bug/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Commvault reports failure for VMs with ADE Extension In 'FailedState'_Encryption

symptom: Disk Encryption Set (DES) unable to update to new key version or perform operations - key vault access fails after the associated system-assigned managed identity was deleted
rootCause: The system-assigned managed identity used by DES for authenticating with Key Vault was deleted (e.g. accidental deletion or AAD cleanup), breaking DES-to-KeyVault authentication chain.
solution: CLI: 1) az disk-encryption-set identity remove --system-assigned 2) az disk-encryption-set identity assign --system-assigned (expect initial error - normal, new MI lacks permissions) 3) Grant new MI wrapkey/unwrapkey/get on keyvault (RBAC: assign 'Key Vault Crypto Service Encryption User' role, or vault policy: az keyvault set-policy --key-permissions wrapkey unwrapkey get) 4) Re-run identity assign. Verify with az disk-encryption-set identity show.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/Update DiskEncryptionSet ManagedIdentity_Encryption

symptom: Need to switch Disk Encryption Set from system-assigned to user-assigned managed identity, or DES operations fail and customer wants user-assigned MI for centralized management
rootCause: Customer requires user-assigned managed identity for DES (centralized identity management or after system-assigned MI issues).
