solution: CLI: 1) az disk-encryption-set identity remove --system-assigned 2) az identity create -g RG -n MIName 3) Grant MI wrapkey/unwrapkey/get on keyvault 4) az disk-encryption-set identity assign --user-assigned MIId. CRITICAL WARNING: Switch from system-assigned to user-assigned is IRREVERSIBLE. Does NOT work if Ultra disks are associated with this DES.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/Update DiskEncryptionSet ManagedIdentity_Encryption

symptom: Cannot enable Encryption at Host on a VM that currently or ever had Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) enabled - operation is blocked by platform
rootCause: ADE and Encryption at Host are mutually exclusive by design. The restriction applies even if ADE was previously enabled and then removed from the VM/VMSS.
solution: To migrate from ADE to Encryption at Host: 1) Follow ADE-to-SSE+CMK migration steps to completely remove ADE 2) Deallocate VM 3) Enable Encryption at Host. For existing VMSS, only new VMs created after enabling are auto-encrypted; existing VMs must be deallocated and reallocated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/Managed Disk Server Side Encryption at Host_Encryption

symptom: Enabling Encryption at Host fails because VM size does not support the EncryptionAtHost feature
rootCause: Not all VM sizes support Encryption at Host. The VM must use a size with EncryptionAtHostSupported capability set to True.
solution: Check VM size support: 1) REST API: Resource Skus API, check EncryptionAtHostSupported capability 2) PowerShell: Get-AzComputeResourceSku | filter EncryptionAtHostSupported=true 3) CLI: az vm list-skus. Also register feature: Register-AzProviderFeature -FeatureName EncryptionAtHost -ProviderNamespace Microsoft.Compute. Resize VM to supported size if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/Managed Disk Server Side Encryption at Host_Encryption

symptom: Azure Key Vault key or secret expiration causes ADE or SSE+CMK encrypted VMs to fail to start without customer awareness
rootCause: No alert mechanism configured to notify when Key Vault keys or secrets approach expiration
solution: Configure Event Grid integration with Logic App on Key Vault to monitor CertificateNearExpiry, KeyNearExpiry, SecretNearExpiry events. Register Microsoft.EventGrid and Microsoft.Web resource providers first. Create Logic App with Event Grid trigger filtering on key vault events, then add email notification action.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/How to set up alerts for Azure Key Vaults_Encryption

symptom: VM using legacy ADE Dual Pass (with AAD) encryption needs migration to current Single Pass (without AAD) version; ADE extension version Windows 1.1.* or Linux 0.1.*
rootCause: ADE Dual Pass (with AAD) is deprecated legacy version that relies on Azure AD for authentication
solution: Run Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -ResourceGroupName <rg> -VMName <vm> -Migrate (requires Az module >= 5.9.0). VM will reboot during migration. Verify current version via Get-AzVM -Status or az vm get-instance-view. Target version: Windows 2.2, Linux 1.2. Only use on VMs already encrypted with ADE (with AAD).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Migrate from Dual Pass to Single Pass Extension_Encryption

symptom: Encrypted disk cannot be unlocked on rescue VM; BEK key in manage-bde protectors output does not match Disk Encryption Key Secret URL shown in ASC/platform
rootCause: Encryption settings in Azure platform (DiskEncryptionKey/KeyEncryptionKey on managed disk) are out of sync with actual BitLocker protectors on the disk
solution: Compare manage-bde -protectors -get <drive>: output (External Key File Name) with ASC Disk Encryption Key Secret URL. Verify BEK file exists in Key Vault (not expired/disabled). Check Content Type: BEK=secret only, Wrapped BEK=KEK layer. For Single Pass: use Update-AzDisk with -DiskEncryptionKey (secretUrl+sourceVaultId) and -KeyEncryptionKey (keyUrl+sourceVaultId). For Dual Pass: use Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension with AAD credentials. Detach disk from rescue VM before updating.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Modify Encryption Settings_Encryption

symptom: VM fails to start or ADE-encrypted VM becomes inaccessible because the Azure Key Vault key or secret used for encryption has expired
rootCause: Customer was not aware of key/secret expiration in Azure Key Vault. There is no built-in Azure Monitor alert for key expiration; it requires Event Grid integration.
solution: Set up Key Vault expiration alerts using Event Grid + Logic App: 1) Register Microsoft.EventGrid resource provider, 2) Create a Logic App triggered by Key Vault events (KeyNearExpiry, KeyExpired, SecretNearExpiry, SecretExpired, CertificateNearExpiry), 3) Configure Send Mail action to notify customer before expiration (triggered 30 days before expiry date).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/How to set up alerts for Azure Key Vaults_Encryption

symptom: Server Side Encryption with Customer Managed Keys (SSE+CMK) fails to configure or DiskEncryptionSet creation fails. Managed Disk encryption with CMK is not working.
rootCause: Azure Key Vault used for CMK does not have Soft Delete and Purge Protection enabled. SSE+CMK is only supported with Key Vaults that have both features enabled. Once soft-delete is set to true, it cannot be changed or removed.
solution: Enable Soft Delete and Purge Protection on Key Vault. PowerShell (new KV): New-AzKeyVault -Name ContosoVault -ResourceGroupName ContosoRG -Location westus -EnableSoftDelete -EnablePurgeProtection. CLI (existing KV): az keyvault update --name ContosoVault --resource-group ContosoRG --enable-soft-delete true --enable-purge-protection true. Verify with: az keyvault show --name <VaultName> and check enableSoftDelete and enablePurgeProtection are both true.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Managed Disk Storage Service Encryption with CMK_Encryption

symptom: VM is running ADE Dual Pass (with AAD) encryption - older ADE version (Windows: 1.1.*, Linux: 0.1.*). Need to migrate to Single Pass (without AAD) for continued support.
rootCause: VM was encrypted with the older ADE version that relied on Azure Active Directory for authentication. The current version does not require AAD.
solution: Migrate using PowerShell: Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -ResourceGroupName <rgName> -VMName <vmName> -Migrate. Requires Az module version >= 5.9.0. The ADE version will be updated and VM rebooted. Check current version: az vm get-instance-view or Get-AzVM -Status, look for TypeHandlerVersion in AzureDiskEncryption extension. Windows target: 2.2, Linux target: 1.2.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Migrate from Dual Pass to Single Pass Extension_Encryption

symptom: Encrypted disk cannot be unlocked because the BEK file referenced by the platform (ASC Disk Encryption Key Secret URL) does not match the BEK protector shown by manage-bde -protectors -get inside the VM. External Key File Name differs from Secret URL.
rootCause: The encryption settings stored on the disk (platform side) are out of sync with the actual BitLocker protector on the OS disk. This can happen after disk operations or failed encryption updates.
solution: Update encryption settings to align with actual BEK protector. For KEK-encrypted disk: use New-AzDiskUpdateConfig -EncryptionSettingsEnabled $true, Set-AzDiskUpdateDiskEncryptionKey with correct $secretUrl/$secretId, Set-AzDiskUpdateKeyEncryptionKey with $keyUrl/$keyId, set EncryptionSettingsVersion=1.1, then Update-AzDisk. For BEK-only disk: same but skip KeyEncryptionKey step. Find $secretUrl in Key Vault > Secrets (Secret Identifier), $secretId in Key Vault > Properties > Resource ID. Check Content Type: BEK=secret only, Wrapped BEK=has KEK.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Modify Encryption Settings_Encryption

symptom: Cannot attach ADE Dual Pass encrypted managed disk as a data disk to rescue VM. Azure Portal shows error: disk contains encryption settings and cannot be used as a data disk.
rootCause: Dual Pass encrypted managed disks have encryption settings metadata that prevents them from being attached as data disks through normal methods.
solution: Clear encryption settings before attaching. PowerShell (Az): $disk = Get-AzDisk -ResourceGroupName $rgName -DiskName $diskName; $disk.EncryptionSettingsCollection = @{}; $disk | Update-AzDisk. Or (AzureRM): New-AzureRmDiskUpdateConfig -EncryptionSettingsEnabled $false | Update-AzureRmDisk -diskName $osDiskName -ResourceGroupName $rgName. Then attach to rescue VM using Add-AzVMDataDisk. Verify cleared: $disk.EncryptionSettingsCollection.EncryptionSettings | ConvertTo-Json should return null.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Unlock Encrypted Windows Disk_Encryption

symptom: manage-bde -unlock fails with ERROR: An error occurred while attempting to read the key from disk when trying to unlock ADE encrypted Windows disk using BEK file.
rootCause: The .bek file extension in the command path causes manage-bde to fail reading the key file in certain environments.
solution: Drop the .bek file extension from the recovery key file path when running manage-bde -unlock. Example: instead of manage-bde -unlock E: -RecoveryKey C:\BEK\TestKEK.bek, use manage-bde -unlock E: -RecoveryKey C:\BEK\TestKEK.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Unlock Encrypted Windows Disk_Encryption

symptom: BEK volume does not appear on rescue VM after attaching ADE encrypted disk. Cannot find BEK file to unlock the encrypted disk for offline repair.
rootCause: The encrypted disk was attached after VM creation. BEK volume is only provisioned when the encrypted disk is detected during VM creation, not when attached later.
solution: Must attach the encrypted disk DURING rescue VM creation (in the Disks blade of Create VM wizard), not after. The system detects encryption settings during creation and automatically fetches ADE key from Key Vault to create BEK volume. If BEK volume is present but BEK file is missing, reboot the rescue VM. Alternative: use az vm repair create -g {rg} -n {vm} --unlock-encrypted-vm --verbose for automated approach.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Unlock Encrypted Windows Disk_Encryption

symptom: Encrypted Windows VM or VMSS fails to start with error: secret does not have the DiskEncryptionKeyFileName or DiskEncryptionKeyEncryptionAlgorithm tags. Error code: DiskEncryptionKeySecretMissingTags.
rootCause: The Key Vault secret used by ADE is missing required tags (DiskEncryptionKeyFileName or DiskEncryptionKeyEncryptionAlgorithm) on its current version. This can happen after secret rotation, manual edits, or migration.
solution: Navigate to Key Vault > secret current version > Tags. Add missing tag: DiskEncryptionKeyFileName = {SecretName}.BEK (extract SecretName from the KV URL), or DiskEncryptionKeyEncryptionAlgorithm = RSA-OAEP (default). Save and retry VM start.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Client error occured DiskEncryptionKeySecretMissingTags_Encryption_Windows

symptom: Azure Disk Encryption fails with Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension long running operation failed. Bitlocker log shows: An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host (System.Net.Sockets.SocketException).
rootCause: TLS 1.1 is disabled on the VM. ADE requires TLS 1.1+ to communicate with Azure Key Vault and storage endpoints.
solution: Check TLS 1.1 status: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server" /v Enabled. If key missing or value is 0, enable it: reg add ... /v Enabled /t REG_DWORD /d 1. Retry encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Connection was Forcibly Closed by the Remote Host_Encryption

symptom: Azure Disk Encryption fails with connection forcibly closed by remote host. waagent.log shows UriNotAllowed or StorageException: Unable to connect to the remote server.
rootCause: Proxy service or stateful packet inspection (firewall/IPS) on the VM is blocking traffic to required Azure endpoints (Key Vault, Storage).
solution: Test encryption from a VM on a different network (not behind the corporate proxy/firewall). If successful, configure proxy/firewall rules to allow traffic to Azure Key Vault and Storage endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Connection was Forcibly Closed by the Remote Host_Encryption

symptom: Attaching an ADE-encrypted managed disk as a data disk to another VM fails with: Disk contains encryption settings and cannot be used as a data disk.
rootCause: Disk-level encryption settings prevent attaching an encrypted OS disk as a data disk to a non-encrypted VM or a VM encrypted with different BEK/KEK.
solution: Copy the managed disk VHD to a storage account (Grant-AzDiskAccess + Start-AzStorageBlobCopy), create a new managed disk from the VHD, attach to target VM. Unlock the drive using the original BEK from Key Vault (manage-bde -protectors -get <volume>).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Contains Encryption Settings and Cannot be used as a Data Disk_Encryption

symptom: Data disks are silently skipped during ADE encryption. Bitlocker log shows: Skipping Volume Backed by VHD. Storage Spaces disks and Windows Server 2012 data disks are at higher risk.
rootCause: Regression bug in ADE extension version 2.2.0.37 causes storage pool virtual disks to be mistakenly considered as VHD-backed, leading to encryption skip.
solution: Remove the 2.2.0.37 extension (Remove-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension), then re-add with version 2.1 and -DisableAutoUpgradeMinorVersion flag: Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -TypeHandlerVersion 2.1 -DisableAutoUpgradeMinorVersion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Data Disks get Skipped During Encryption_Encryption

symptom: Encrypted VM fails to boot with DiskEncryptionKeySecretRetrievalFailed. Azure Activity Log shows: Error encountered when retrieving secret from Key Vault. VM remains unavailable as OS disk cannot be unlocked.
rootCause: The Key Vault or disk encryption secret is no longer accessible to ADE. Causes: KV deleted (soft-deleted or permanently), secret deleted/expired/version mismatch, RBAC/access policy changes blocking access, subscription/tenant/resource moves breaking trust path.
solution: Check KV state: az keyvault list-deleted, recover if soft-deleted (az keyvault recover). Check secret: az keyvault secret show/recover. If permanently deleted: restore VM from backup/snapshot. Enable soft-delete + purge protection as preventive measure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/DiskEncryptionKeySecretRetrievalFailed VMStartupFailure_Encryption

symptom: Linux VM data disk secrets lost after boot: LinuxPassPhraseFileName_1_0 missing from /mnt/azure_bek_disk. Guest OS prompts "Please enter passphrase for disk". Data disks not mounted. Intermittent on VM with ADE-encrypted data disks.
rootCause: Race condition between ADE and Fast Attach/Detach (FAD) during VM creation. CRP sends data disk info after tenant creation, but Fabric creates BEK disk based on initial CCF (no data disks). When start command arrives with data disks, BEK disk is not recreated.
solution: Deallocate and start the VM to remount BEK volume with data disk secrets. For non-trusted-launch VMs, disable FAD by adding VM tag: DisableFastDiskAttachDetach=true. PG can also stop FAD at subscription level via IcM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Encryption Secrets of Data Disks Go Missing_Encryption

symptom: New VM deployment with Encryption at Host (EAH) enabled fails with OS Provisioning Timed Out. Same deployment succeeds without EAH. No OS guest logs generated as VM never reaches OS boot phase.
rootCause: Encryption at Host infrastructure prerequisites not met. Typical causes: unsupported disk type/sector size (512e disks must be created after 2023-05-13), VM size does not support EAH, disks previously had ADE enabled, region/host capacity constraints, old snapshots/images.
solution: Verify disk compatibility: az disk show --query "{sku:sku.name, created:timeCreated}". Check VM size supports EAH: az vm list-skus -l <region>. Use newly created disks, avoid disks with prior ADE history. Try different region/size.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/EncryptionAtHost OSProvisioningTimeout_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError: [2.4.0.21] BEK disk is missing or not initialized. Extension log shows GetBekVolumeObject: Bek Volume not found, SetEncryptableBekVolume: Unable to find Bek Volume using label.
rootCause: BEK volume is offline or not initialized. Often caused by NoAutoMount registry setting blocking volumes from mounting automatically. BEK disk may show offline in diskpart even when online in Disk Management.
solution: Check Disk Management and diskpart for BEK volume status. If offline, make it online (diskpart > select disk N > online disk). If NoAutoMount registry is set, update/remove it. Retrigger encryption via Portal/CLI/PowerShell. Verify with manage-bde -status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Error message [2.4.0.21] BEK disk is missing or not initialized_Encryption

symptom: ADE decrypt or encrypt operation fails with error: Decrypt/Encrypt failed with 2147942421 (Device appears as not ready). Volume shows Encryption Paused status in manage-bde -status.
rootCause: The disk/mount point is not in ready state. Typically the encryption or decryption on a volume was paused for an extended period, leaving BitLocker in an incomplete state that blocks subsequent operations.
solution: Check manage-bde -status for paused volumes. Resume paused encryption (manage-bde -resume <volume>) or ensure disk is in ready state via Disk Management. Fix underlying disk health issue, then retry ADE encrypt/decrypt operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker Exception 2147942421_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError: ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2150694929. The drive cannot be encrypted because it does not have enough free space.
rootCause: Insufficient free disk space on the target volume. Azure Disk Encryption requires 10-20% free space on each disk being encrypted for BitLocker to create its metadata and key protectors.
solution: Increase disk space or ask customer to delete unnecessary data on the affected drive to create at least 10-20% free space, then retry encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker Exception 2150694929_Encryption

symptom: Defender for Cloud shows non-compliance alerts (Windows/Linux VMs should enable ADE or EncryptionAtHost) even when disk encryption is already enabled via SSE+CMK or EncryptionAtHost
rootCause: VM missing system-assigned managed identity or AzurePolicyforWindows/AzurePolicyforLinux guest configuration extension not installed; the policy evaluation requires both to detect encryption status correctly
solution: Enable system-assigned identity on VM (Identity > ON), install Azure Machine Configuration extension (search AzurePolicyforWindows/Linux in Extensions), register Microsoft.GuestConfiguration provider if needed. Fix unhealthy resource under Guest Configuration extension recommendation. Changes take up to 24 hours to reflect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Known Issues or Bug/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Encryption Policy in Defender for Cloud_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with 0xc142506f RUNTIME_E_KEYVAULT_SECRET_WRAP_WITH_KEK_FAILED or Bad Length error
rootCause: KEK (Key Encryption Key) in Key Vault is expired or disabled, preventing ADE from wrapping the BEK secret
solution: Check Key Vault key status: if expired, renew the key; if disabled, re-enable it. Then retry encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/0xc142506f RUNTIME E KEYVAULT SECRET WRAP WITH KEK FAILED_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with 0xc142506f RUNTIME_E_KEYVAULT_SECRET_WRAP_WITH_KEK_FAILED Bad Length on Windows Server 2022 or Windows 11
rootCause: Windows Server 2022/Windows 11 use a new BitLocker version that requires RSA key size > 2048 for KEK, but customer is using RSA-2048 key
solution: Create a new KEK with RSA key size greater than 2048 (e.g., RSA-3072 or RSA-4096) in Key Vault, then retry encryption with the new key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/0xc142506f RUNTIME E KEYVAULT SECRET WRAP WITH KEK FAILED_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with 0xc1425072 RUNTIME_E_KEYVAULT_SET_SECRET_FAILED Failed to set secret to KeyVault
rootCause: Key Vault missing required access permissions: Wrap/Unwrap key permissions not granted, or Azure Disk Encryption for volume encryption not enabled in Key Vault Access Configuration
solution: In Key Vault Access Configuration: enable Azure Disk Encryption for volume encryption. For ARM deployments also enable Azure Virtual Machines for deployment. Ensure Access Policy includes Wrap and Unwrap key permissions for the ADE service principal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/0xc1425072 RUNTIME E KEYVAULT SET SECRET FAILED_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with 0xc1425072 RUNTIME_E_KEYVAULT_SET_SECRET_FAILED and Key Vault log shows 'Secret was disallowed by policy'
rootCause: Azure Policy 'Key Vault secrets should have an expiration date' with Deny effect blocks ADE from creating secrets without expiry date
solution: Go to Azure Policy > Definitions, find the policy requiring secret expiration dates. Remove the policy assignment or change effect from Deny to Audit. Then retry encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/0xc1425072 RUNTIME E KEYVAULT SET SECRET FAILED_Encryption

symptom: Disabling ADE via CLI fails with InvalidParameter typeHandlerVersion even with correct command syntax
rootCause: ADE was previously enabled using a custom/non-standard VM extension name (e.g. Azure-Disk-Encryption-1 instead of AzureDiskEncryption), causing platform extension state mismatch
solution: List VM extensions (az vm extension list) to find non-standard ADE extension name. Delete the custom extension (az vm extension delete --name <CustomName>). Azure auto-reinstalls correct AzureDiskEncryption extension. Retry ADE disable. If still failing, run Update-AzVM to refresh VM model state, or do controlled enable-disable cycle.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ADE Disable Failure Custom Extension_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with 'A semi colon character was expected' or 'Whitespace is not allowed at this location'
rootCause: Disk volume labels contain special characters (semicolon, ampersand &, excessive whitespace) that ADE BitLocker extension cannot parse when sending encryption settings to host
solution: Identify volumes with special characters in labels from BitLocker extension logs or Get-Volume. Rename affected volume labels to remove special characters (e.g., 'Application & Binaries' -> 'Application and Binaries'). Retry encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ADE Extension Errors Semicolon or Whitespace in Volume Labels_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with 'Failed to configure bitlocker as expected. Exception: Access denied'
rootCause: Key Vault Access Policy missing required permissions (WrapKey, Set) for the ADE AAD application/service principal used during encryption
solution: Identify the Client ID (Application ID) from SPIOoSEvent/extension logs. Set Key Vault Access Policy: Set-AzKeyVaultAccessPolicy -VaultName <kv> -ServicePrincipalName <clientId> -PermissionsToKeys WrapKey -PermissionsToSecrets Set. Or add ApplicationID via portal Key Vault Access Policies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Access Denied_Encryption

symptom: ADE-encrypted Windows VM shows 'Bitlocker needs your recovery key' or 'You are locked out' at boot screen after multiple incorrect password attempts
rootCause: GPO policy MaxDevicePasswordFailedAttempts triggered BitLocker recovery mode after exceeding failed login limit, disabling the External Protector which cannot be re-enabled
solution: Prevention: disable MaxDevicePasswordFailedAttempts policy (set to Not configured) or set high threshold (>=16). Recovery: if VM is AAD-joined, retrieve recovery key from Azure AD > Devices > Bitlocker Keys. Otherwise VM may require redeployment from backup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Bitlocker Needs your Recovery Key_Encryption

symptom: ADE-encrypted VM requests BitLocker Recovery Key or USB drive with key at boot, VM cannot locate BEK file to unlock encrypted OS disk
rootCause: VM cannot locate the BitLocker Recovery Key (BEK) file during boot operation, BEK volume not mounting or BEK file corrupted/missing from Key Vault
solution: Use rescue VM approach: attach encrypted OS disk to rescue VM, retrieve BEK from Key Vault, manually unlock the disk. See related guides for ADE encrypted disk unlock procedures.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Bitlocker Needs your Recovery Key_Encryption

symptom: RHEL 9 VM enters emergency mode after enabling ADE, boot filesystem /boot mount failure with empty bootuuid parameter
rootCause: grub2-mkconfig on RHEL9 with grub2-tools >= 2.06-69 requires --update-bls-cmdline option to update BLS cmdline boot parameters, but ADE extension only adds this flag for RHEL >= 9.3, leaving bootuuid empty on older RHEL9 versions with updated grub2-tools package
solution: Attach disk to rescue VM, chroot into the filesystem. Run: grub2-mkconfig --update-bls-cmdline -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg. Alternative: downgrade grub2-tools (yum downgrade -y grub2-tools-2.06-61.el9) then run grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Boot Filesystem mount failure RHEL9_Encryption

symptom: Starting an ADE-encrypted VM (stopped/deallocated) fails with DiskEncryptionInternalError: Internal error occurred while processing encryptionSettings. Secret or key retrieval from Key Vault fails.
rootCause: The secret and/or key used for Azure Disk Encryption has been deleted from the Key Vault, or the Key Vault access policies (Wrap/Unwrap key permissions, volume encryption checkbox) have been removed while the VM was in a stopped state.
solution: 1) Re-enable Key Vault access policies: enable Azure Disk Encryption for volume encryption, ensure Wrap and Unwrap key permissions. 2) If secret/key was deleted, use Manage deleted secrets/keys to Recover. 3) If recovery is impossible (purged), VM is unrecoverable -- restore from backup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Internal error occured while processing encryptionSettings_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with error: secret does not have DiskEncryptionKeyEncryptionAlgorithm tags. Key Vault has incorrect tenant ID after subscription was moved between tenants.
rootCause: When an Azure subscription is moved from tenant A to tenant B, existing Key Vaults retain the old tenant ID and access policies still reference tenant A, causing ADE to fail retrieving secrets.
solution: 1) Update Key Vault tenant ID: Set-AzureRmResource to change TenantId to new tenant. 2) Clear old access policies from VM model using Set-EncryptionSettings.ps1 script. 3) Re-add access policies with Set-AzureRmKeyVaultAccessPolicy for the new tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Key Vault Associated to Wrong TenantID_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption fails with error: Keyvault not found in the directory.
rootCause: The Key Vault is not in the same Azure AD tenant as the AD Application used for ADE.
solution: Ensure the Key Vault and the AD Application are in the same Azure AD tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Key Vault not Found in the Directory_Encryption

symptom: VM Start/Stop/PUT/PATCH operations fail with 403 Forbidden: The key vault key is not found to unwrap the encryption key. Affects VMs with unmanaged disks using storage account Customer Managed Keys encryption.
rootCause: Storage account uses Customer Managed Keys (CMK) for encryption at rest, and the key in Key Vault is not accessible. Possible causes: key version is outdated, key was deleted, or storage account access was removed from Key Vault access policies.
solution: 1) Verify Key Vault has the correct key name and latest version matching ASC. 2) If key deleted, recover with Undo-AzKeyVaultKeyRemoval. 3) If key version mismatch, update storage account Encryption to point to latest key version. 4) If storage account not listed in KV access policies, re-add with Set-AzKeyVaultAccessPolicy (permissions: wrapkey, unwrapkey, get, recover). May need Set-AzStorageAccount -AssignIdentity first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Key Vault not Found to Unwrap Encryption Key_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption on Linux VM fails with No space left on device. Guest Agent reports Not Ready with VM status blob exceeding 1 MB limit. cp error during OS volume encryption.
rootCause: Root file system usage is too large relative to available RAM. Linux ADE requires RAM >= 2x the root filesystem usage when encrypting both OS and data volumes.
solution: Resize the VM to have RAM that is at least 2x the size of the root partition usage. For example, 16 GB root filesystem requires at least 32 GB RAM. Reference: Linux ADE disk-encryption-overview.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/No space left on device_Encryption

symptom: Microsoft Defender shows VM as Not Compliant for encryption policy even after ADE or Encryption at Host is enabled. Secure Score recommendation persists.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) VM is deallocated (known issue -- Defender cannot audit stopped VMs). 2) Missing prerequisites: System Assigned Managed Identity not enabled, Guest Configuration extension not installed. 3) Issue with the Policy definition itself.
solution: 1) Start the VM if deallocated. 2) Enable System Assigned Managed Identity. 3) Verify Guest Configuration/Policy extension is installed. 4) Check Guest Assignments exist. 5) If still non-compliant after 30 min, escalate to Defender team via SAP: Azure/Microsoft Defender for Cloud/Secure Score and Recommendations/Encryption recommendations. 6) Customer can exempt the resource if they do not want extensions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Policy issue ADE or Encryption at Host_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2147942512. BitLocker cannot encrypt volume.
rootCause: One of the volumes to be encrypted by BitLocker is full or does not have enough free space for encryption. Error 2147942512 translates to insufficient disk space.
solution: 1) Check Disk Management to identify which volume has insufficient free space. 2) Extend the volume or add more space to provide the free space needed by BitLocker. 3) Once sufficient space is available on all disks, retry the ADE encryption process.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ProtectKeyWithExternalKey Failed with 2147942512_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2150695003. BitLocker startup options conflict in Group Policy.
rootCause: Conflict in BitLocker startup options group policy (or local policy). One UseTPM* registry entry is set to 0x1 (Require) while other UseTPM* entries are set to 0x2 (Allow), causing ADE to fail securing the volume encryption key.
solution: 1) Check registry at HKLM\software\policies\microsoft\fve for UseTPM, UseTPMPIN, UseTPMKey, UseTPMKeyPIN values. 2) Fix the conflict: either set one to 0x1 (Require) and all others to 0x0 (Don't allow), or set all to 0x2 (Allow). 3) May require coordination with customer AD administrator to modify group policy. 4) Retry ADE after policy fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ProtectKeyWithExternalKey Failed with 2150695003_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2150695006 during OS volume encryption.
rootCause: Group Policy Object is not correctly configured to backup BitLocker recovery keys in Active Directory Domain Services (ADDS).
solution: Ensure the Group Policy Object is correctly configured to backup keys in ADDS. Verify GPO settings for BitLocker key backup requirements.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ProtectKeyWithExternalKey Failed with 2150695006_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2150695023. Error: The drive is too small to be protected using BitLocker Drive Encryption.
rootCause: One of the drives (e.g. F: drive) has less than 250MB of free space, which is below the BitLocker minimum requirement for encryption.
solution: 1) Request customer to deallocate and restart VM to check if encryption status updates. 2) Check manage-bde -status output and Disk Management. 3) Identify drives with < 250MB free space. 4) Set the problematic drive to offline, then retry encryption. 5) Alternative: customer can delete the problematic partition if unused. BitLocker partitioning requirements: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/manufacture/desktop/bitlocker-drive-encryption
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ProtectKeyWithExternalKey Failed with 2150695023_Encryption

symptom: VM Start/Stop/PUT/PATCH operations fail with 'The key vault key is not found to unwrap the encryption key' and 403 Forbidden error, when VM uses unmanaged disks with Customer Managed Keys (CMK) on storage account.
rootCause: Storage account uses Customer Managed Keys for encryption at rest, and the key version is not latest, the key was deleted/disabled, or key vault access policies are misconfigured.
solution: Verify the Key Vault key version is current, ensure the key is not deleted/disabled, check key vault access policies and firewall. If using CMK, ensure the storage account can access the key vault key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Key Vault not Found to Unwrap Encryption Key_Encryption

symptom: ADE encryption on Linux VM fails with 'No space left on device' during OS volume encryption. Guest Agent shows Not Ready with 'Size of VM status blob is larger than allowed limit of 1 MB'.
rootCause: Linux VM does not have enough RAM for ADE OS disk encryption. Linux VMs require RAM >= 2x the root partition size when encrypting both data and OS volumes.
solution: Resize the VM to have RAM that is at least 2x the size of the root partition. For example, 16 GB root filesystem usage requires at least 32 GB of RAM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/No space left on device_Encryption

symptom: Microsoft Defender shows VM as non-compliant for encryption policy/recommendation even after enabling ADE or Encryption at Host. VM is deallocated.
rootCause: Known issue: Defender cannot assess encryption compliance for deallocated VMs.
solution: Start the VM so that Defender can recognize it as compliant. If still non-compliant after 30 min with VM running, verify prerequisites (SAMI, Policy extension, Guest Assignments). If all met, cut collab to Defender (SAP: Azure/Microsoft Defender for Cloud/Secure Score and Recommendations/Encryption recommendations).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Policy issue ADE or Encryption at Host_Encryption

symptom: Microsoft Defender shows VM as non-compliant for encryption policy even after enabling ADE or Encryption at Host. VM is running but missing prerequisites.
rootCause: Missing prerequisites: System Assigned Managed Identity not enabled, Policy extension not installed, or Guest Configuration assignments not present.
solution: Enable System Assigned Managed Identity on the VM. Verify the Policy extension is installed (name varies by OS). Ensure Guest Assignments exist for the policy. Customer can also exempt the recommendation if they prefer not to install extensions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Policy issue ADE or Encryption at Host_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2147942512 during BitLocker encryption.
rootCause: One of the volumes to be encrypted by BitLocker is full or does not have enough free space for encryption to proceed.
solution: Add more space or extend the volume to provide the free space needed by BitLocker (check system requirements). Once all disks have sufficient space, retry the ADE process.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ProtectKeyWithExternalKey Failed with 2147942512_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2150695003 during BitLocker encryption.
rootCause: Conflict in BitLocker startup options group policy. UseTPM registry entries have conflicting values - one authentication method is required (0x1) while other methods are allowed (0x2).
solution: Fix GPO conflict: either (1) set one UseTPM* to 0x1 (Require) and all others to 0x0 (Dont allow), or (2) set all UseTPM* entries to 0x2 (Allow). May need customer AD administrator to modify group policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ProtectKeyWithExternalKey Failed with 2150695003_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with ProtectKeyWithExternalKey failed with 2150695006 during BitLocker encryption.
rootCause: Group Policy Object (GPO) is not correctly configured to backup BitLocker keys in Active Directory Domain Services (ADDS).
solution: Ensure the Group Policy Object is correctly configured to backup keys in ADDS. Review and fix the GPO settings for BitLocker key backup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ProtectKeyWithExternalKey Failed with 2150695006_Encryption

symptom: ADE operation fails with error: Cannot update encryption properties for disk because it already has incremental restore point(s).
rootCause: Existing incremental restore points on the source disk prevent ADE from updating encryption properties.
solution: Delete the incremental restore point(s): go to Restore Points in portal, find the collection with the VM disk, if backup needed create a disk from restore point first, delete the restore points, then rerun ADE operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Restorepoints ADE operation error_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with SaveExternalKeyToFile failed with 2147942419. Error: The media is write protected.
rootCause: BitLocker GPO 'Deny write access to removable drives not protected by BitLocker' is enabled, or registry key FDVDenyWriteAccess is set to 1, preventing ADE from saving the external key file.
solution: Disable the GPO: Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > BitLocker Drive Encryption > Removable Data Drives > turn off 'Deny write access to removable drives not protected by BitLocker'. Set registry FDVDenyWriteAccess=0. Then retry ADE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/SaveExternalKeyToFile Failed with 2147942419_Or_Disk_is_Write_Protected_Encryption

symptom: Data disk shows 'The disk is write-protected. Remove the write-protection or use another disk' when trying to copy/delete/create/edit files. VM may or may not use ADE.
rootCause: A GPO or persisted (tattooed) GPO is in effect: 'Deny write access to removable drives not protected by BitLocker' under Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > BitLocker Drive Encryption > Removable Data Drives.
solution: Disable the GPO via gpedit.msc or gpmc.msc. Check and modify registry keys under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE and HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE, set FDVDenyWriteAccess to 0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/SaveExternalKeyToFile Failed with 2147942419_Or_Disk_is_Write_Protected_Encryption

symptom: ADE enable fails with "Failed to configure bitlocker as expected. Exception: SaveExternalKeyToFile failed with 2147942419" - error indicates media is write-protected.
rootCause: GPO or registry setting FDVDenyWriteAccess=1 (Deny write access to removable drives not protected by BitLocker) at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE blocks ADE from saving the external key file.
solution: Set registry FDVDenyWriteAccess to 0: reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE" /v FDVDenyWriteAccess /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f, then restart VM. Also check gpedit.msc > Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > BitLocker Drive Encryption > Removable Data Drives and turn the policy off. If GPO is domain-enforced, customer must engage Directory Services team to disable GPO first. gpupdate /force will NOT resolve it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/SaveExternalKeyToFile Failed with 2147942419_Or_Disk_is_Write_Protected_Encryption

symptom: Data disks show "The disk is write-protected. Remove the write-protection or use another disk" when copying/deleting/creating files. Disks prompt for BitLocker encryption when brought online in Disk Management.
rootCause: GPO or tattooed GPO setting FDVDenyWriteAccess=1 at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE denies write access to removable drives not protected by BitLocker. This can affect VMs not using ADE.
solution: Remove registry: reg delete "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE" /f, or set FDVDenyWriteAccess to 0, then restart VM. If GPO is domain-enforced, customer must engage Directory Services team to disable GPO. Verify with: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE". Note: gpupdate /force will not resolve if GPO is still active.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/SaveExternalKeyToFile Failed with 2147942419_Or_Disk_is_Write_Protected_Encryption

symptom: Attaching data disk or updating VM fails with "User encryption settings in the VM model are not supported. Please upgrade Azure Disk Encryption extension version and clear encryption settings in the VM model."
rootCause: VM was previously encrypted with Dual Pass ADE. After disabling Dual Pass and re-encrypting with Single Pass, legacy encryption settings (DiskEncryptionSettings object with Enabled=False) remain in the VM model OsDisk.EncryptionSettings.
solution: Clear encryption settings via PowerShell (only after confirming: DP fully disabled, EncryptionSettings Enabled=False, Single Pass completed successfully): $vm = Get-AzVM -ResourceGroupName $rg -VMName $vmName; $vm.StorageProfile.OsDisk.EncryptionSettings=$null; $vm | Update-AzVM. After clearing, EncryptionSettings should show as null.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/User Encryption Settings in the VM Model are not Supported_Encryption

symptom: ADE OS disk encryption fails with "Cannot open WinRM service on computer '.'. System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: Access is denied" in BitlockerExtension OnEnable.
rootCause: A Group Policy changed the default Windows Remote Management (WS-Management) service permissions, removing required access for Administrators (Full Control), SYSTEM (Full Control), and INTERACTIVE (Read) accounts.
solution: Collect gpresult /h gpreport.html and check Computer Details > Settings > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > System Services for Windows Remote Management entry. Edit the GPO to restore WinRM service permissions: Administrators - Full Control, SYSTEM - Full Control, INTERACTIVE - Read.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/WinRM AccessDenied_Encryption

symptom: Disk Encryption Set with Confidential disk encryption + CMK fails to grant KeyVault permissions with error: "Failed to grant permissions to the key vault. Error: Invalid value found at accessPolicies[N].ObjectId."
rootCause: Confidential VM Orchestrator service principal (AppId bf7b6499-ff71-4aa2-97a4-f372087be7f0) is not registered in the tenant. This is required by design for the Secure Key Release (SKR) workflow where IGVMAgent needs an AAD enterprise application identity.
solution: Register the service principal using Microsoft Graph SDK: Connect-Graph -Tenant "tenantId" Application.ReadWrite.All; New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId bf7b6499-ff71-4aa2-97a4-f372087be7f0 -DisplayName "Confidential VM Orchestrator". Then retry granting permissions from the DES error pop-up in portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Confidential VMs Encryption/Confidential Disk Encryption With SSE+CMK Disk Encryption Set shows Invalid ObjectId_Encryption

symptom: CMK storage account update fails with ManagedServiceIdentityNotFound error after the associated managed identity (system-assigned or user-assigned) has been deleted
rootCause: The managed identity associated with the storage account for CMK encryption was deleted from Azure AD, causing MSI lookup to fail
solution: Regenerate the managed identity: Set-AzStorageAccount -IdentityType None, then set back to SystemAssigned or create new UserAssigned identity. Grant required Key Vault permissions (WrapKey, UnwrapKey, Get) via access policy or RBAC (Key Vault Crypto Service Encryption User role). If customer switched back to MMK and MSI is missing, simply set IdentityType to None.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/MSINotFound_Encryption

symptom: SSE+CMK auto key rotation fails: Key Vault rotation policy creates new key version but DES does not reflect the change, disks remain using expired key until it expires and operations fail
rootCause: Key rotation policy created a new key version in Key Vault but the Disk Encryption Set did not automatically pick up the rotated key version
solution: Customer should rotate the key manually (docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-registry/tutorial-rotate-revoke-customer-managed-keys). For RCA, use Kusto: cluster(disks.kusto.windows.net).database(Disks).DiskManagerApiQoSEvent filtered by subscriptionId and resourceGroupName. Check UpdateDiskEncryptionSetOperation and DiskEncryptionSets.ResourceOperation.PUT. If server error (resultType=2), get operationId and query DiskManagerContextActivityEvent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Autokey rotation failures

symptom: Disk Encryption Set provisioning failed: Previous identity update is in failed state. Cannot update DES to use new identity when previous system-assigned managed identity was soft-deleted.
rootCause: Previous system-assigned managed identity associated with the DES was soft-deleted in Azure AD. When trying to update DES (e.g., to cross-tenant SSE+CMK with user-assigned identity), the platform detects the failed identity state and blocks the update.
solution: 1) Engage AAD Team to verify the system-assigned managed identity is soft-deleted using SAP: Azure/Microsoft Entra Directories, Domains, and Objects/Microsoft Entra Roles and Administrators. 2) Once confirmed, open an ICM with the product team. Customer needs to update existing DES with new system-assigned managed identity using steps from PG. Reference ICMs: 494792919, 466925296.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Disk Encryption Set Shows Previous Identity Update is in Failed State_Encryption

symptom: Disk Encryption Set shows Invalid value found at accessPolicies TenantId error after moving VM/DES/Key Vault to another tenant with SSE+CMK. VM fails to start.
rootCause: System-assigned managed identity does not move across tenants. After subscription/resource transfer, the DES identity can no longer access the Key Vault in the new tenant.
solution: 1) Set DES identity type to None via ARM template (Export template > Deploy > Edit template > set identity type to None). 2) Set identity type back to SystemAssigned (will fail with internal error - expected). 3) Fetch new service principal: az ad sp list --display-name <DESname>. 4) Grant Get/Wrap/Unwrap Key Vault permissions to the new identity. 5) Wait 15 minutes and retry VM start.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Disk Encryption Set shows Invalid TenantID_Encryption

symptom: SSE+CMK disk encryption update fails: Cannot update encryption properties for disk because it already has incremental restore point(s). Delete all existing incremental restore points to update encryption.
rootCause: Azure platform restriction: managed disk and all associated incremental snapshots/restore points must maintain consistent encryption metadata. Changing encryption while restore points exist would create inconsistency.
solution: Option A (Azure Disk Backup): Go to Resiliency > Protection inventory > Protected items, select affected disk backup instance, Stop Backup with Delete backup data. Option B (Restore Point Collection): az restore-point collection delete --resource-group <rg> --collection-name <rpcName> --yes. After deletion, retry CMK/DES update, then re-enable backup protection.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Failed CMK Enablement Due To Incremental Restore Points_Encryption

symptom: SSE+CMK fails for PremiumV2_LRS or UltraSSD_LRS disk: DiskEncryptionSet with UserAssigned identity type is not supported for disk since it has direct drive SKU (DirectDriveDiskNotSupportUserAssignedIdentityDes)
rootCause: User-assigned managed identities are not supported for Ultra Disks and Premium SSD v2 disks encrypted with customer-managed keys. This is a documented platform restriction.
solution: Create a new DiskEncryptionSet using system-assigned managed identity instead of user-assigned identity, then encrypt the disk with SSE+CMK using that DES. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disk-encryption#restrictions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Fails to Encrypt PremiumSSDv2 and Ultra Disks_Encryption

symptom: SSE+CMK encrypted VM in failed state, start fails with KeyVaultAccessForbidden. DES managed identity lacks required Key Vault permissions (Get, Unwrap Key, Wrap Key).
rootCause: Disk Encryption Set system-assigned managed identity does not have the required Key Vault permissions (Get, Unwrap Key, Wrap Key) to access the encryption key
solution: For KV Access Policy model: Navigate to DES overview, click the error to auto-grant permissions, or manually add Get/Unwrap Key/Wrap Key in KV access policies. For Azure RBAC model: Assign Key Vault Crypto Service Encryption User role to the DES identity on the Key Vault scope. Then start the VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Fails with 22KeyVaultAccessForbidden_Encryption

symptom: SSE+CMK encrypted VM fails to start: Unable to access key - Key is expired. Key Vault key used by Disk Encryption Set has passed its expiration date.
rootCause: The Key Vault key associated with the Disk Encryption Set has expired, preventing the DES from performing wrap/unwrap operations needed for disk encryption/decryption
solution: Rotate the key: In Azure portal, go to Key Vault > Keys, select the expired key > Rotation policy > click Rotate now. Or set up automatic key rotation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disk-encryption#automatic-key-rotation-of-customer-managed-keys
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Fails with 22KeyVaultAccessForbidden_Encryption

symptom: SSE+CMK encrypted VM fails to start with KeyVaultAccessTokenCannotBeAcquired error (403 Forbidden). Unable to acquire access token for Key Vault from Azure AD using resource identity.
rootCause: MSI credential for Disk Encryption Set requires periodic backend updates by an automatic job. When demand exceeds job capacity, credential becomes stale and token acquisition fails. Internal platform issue requiring PG intervention.
solution: First rule out: 1) Missing KV permissions (check Get/List/Unwrap/Wrap Key) 2) Key expiration. Verify via Kusto: cluster(azcrp.kusto.windows.net).database(crp_allprod).VMApiQosEvent. If permissions and expiration are correct, file IcM to Support/EEE AzureRT via https://aka.ms/CRI-AzureRT. PG will manually run the credential update job. After PG confirmation, customer retries VM start. If IcM stalls >24h, contact Encryption SMEs via OneVM Encryption Ava channel.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Fails with KeyVaultAccessTokenCannotBeAcquired

symptom: Azure Files SMB: Security recommended action about potentially unintended elevation of privileges for wildcard (*) RBAC roles after oAuth REST API and MSI support release
rootCause: Custom RBAC role definitions using wildcard (*) for Actions or DataActions on Azure Files SMB grant all current and future data actions including new backup/restore privileges (SeBackupPrivilege/SeRestorePrivilege) that supersede file/folder ACLs storage-account-wide
solution: Update RBAC role assignments to replace wildcard (*) with explicit Storage Files RBAC roles. Declare actions and data actions explicitly to prevent unintended privilege elevation. Reference tracking ID: 2MP7-JP0. See docs: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/files/storage-files-identity-ad-ds-assign-permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Emerging Issues/Azure Files RBAC SMB TrackingID_2MP7 JP0_Storage

symptom: After removing ADE extension, platform cannot report encryption status or provision VM properly
rootCause: ADE extension was removed (Remove-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension) before disk encryption was disabled (Disable-AzVMDiskEncryption), breaking platform-level encryption settings and VM model consistency
solution: Always run Disable-AzVMDiskEncryption first to unencrypt the disk, then Remove-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension to remove the extension. Correct sequence: 1) Disable-AzVMDiskEncryption -ResourceGroupName $RG -VMName $VM -VolumeType "all" 2) Remove-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -ResourceGroupName $RG -VMName $VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 11 Decrypt a VM_Encryption

symptom: Need to migrate VM from Dual Pass ADE (with AAD/Service Principal) to Single Pass ADE (without AAD)
solution: Use Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -ResourceGroupName $RG -VMName $VM -Migrate. Requires Azure PowerShell Az module >=5.9.0. VM will be rebooted during migration. Verify with Get-AzVmDiskEncryptionStatus. After migration, ADE extension version changes from 0.x to 1.x (Linux) or 1.x to 2.x (Windows).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 12 Migrate from Dual Pass to Single Pass_Encryption

symptom: Need to verify if SSE+CMK (Customer Managed Key) encryption is enabled on an Azure managed disk
solution: PowerShell: $disk = Get-AzDisk -ResourceGroupName $RG -DiskName $DiskName; $disk.Encryption.Type. Result "EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerKey" confirms SSE+CMK is active. In ASC, search for disk name and check "EncryptionType" property. Note: This method only checks CMK; for ADE encryption status, check VM > Disks in ASC instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 3 How to Check if CMK is Enabled ASC and PowerShell_Encryption

symptom: Need to determine if a VM uses Dual Pass or Single Pass Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)
solution: Check ADE extension version in VM Extensions blade: Windows - version 1.x = Dual Pass, version 2.x = Single Pass. Linux - version 0.x = Dual Pass, version 1.x = Single Pass. In Dual Pass, encryption settings are stamped in VM Model (not individual disk). Dual Pass uses AAD App (aadClientID/aadClientSecret) and requires Key Vault Access Policies; Single Pass does not require AAD App.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 9 Dual Pass Encryption with Linux Using PowerShell_Encryption

symptom: Windows VM is encrypted with BEK only (no KEK) and needs to be re-encrypted with KEK for enhanced key management
solution: Re-encrypt with KEK using PowerShell: $keyEncryptionKeyUrl = (Get-AzKeyVaultKey -VaultName $KVName -Name $keyName).Key.kid; Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -ResourceGroupName $RG -VMName $VM -DiskEncryptionKeyVaultUrl $vaultUri -DiskEncryptionKeyVaultId $vaultId -KeyEncryptionKeyUrl $keyEncryptionKeyUrl -KeyEncryptionKeyVaultId $vaultId -VolumeType "OS". Verify with Get-AzVmDiskEncryptionStatus or manage-bde -status from within the VM via RDP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 7 Windows Single Pass Encryption using Cloud Shell or PowerShell_Encryption

symptom: Need to enable or disable Encryption at Host for a Windows VM to encrypt data at the VM host level
solution: Prerequisites: Register feature with Register-AzProviderFeature -FeatureName "EncryptionAtHost" -ProviderNamespace "Microsoft.Compute". Check registration status with Get-AzProviderFeature. VM must be stopped/deallocated before enabling. Portal: VM > Disks > Additional Settings > Encryption at host = Yes > Save. To disable: same path, set to No. Verify: VM Overview > Properties > Disk section shows Encryption at host flag.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 4 Encrypt a Windows VM with Encryption at Host_Encryption

symptom: After removing ADE extension, Azure portal shows incorrect encryption status and VM provisioning fails. Platform cannot report encryption state properly.
rootCause: ADE decryption commands executed in wrong order: Remove-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension was run before Disable-AzVMDiskEncryption. The Remove command deletes the extension without properly decrypting the disk first, leaving platform-level encryption settings in an inconsistent state.
solution: Always follow correct sequence: 1) Disable-AzVMDiskEncryption -ResourceGroupName RG -VMName VM -VolumeType "all" (decrypts disk), 2) Remove-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -ResourceGroupName RG -VMName VM (removes extension). Never reverse the order.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 11 Decrypt a VM_Encryption

symptom: Azure Support Center (ASC) disk search shows EncryptionType for SSE+CMK encrypted disks, but does not display ADE encryption information. Engineer cannot verify ADE status from ASC.
rootCause: ASC EncryptionType property (e.g. EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerKey) only reflects SSE/CMK encryption state. ADE encryption uses a different mechanism (BitLocker/DM-Crypt) and is not reported via the same EncryptionType field.
solution: To check CMK: use PowerShell Get-AzDisk and inspect $disk.Encryption.Type, or search disk in ASC and look for EncryptionType property. To check ADE: go to VM > Disks blade in ASC (not disk resource directly). ADE status is shown on VM-level disk view, not on individual disk resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 3 How to Check if CMK is Enabled ASC and PowerShell_Encryption

symptom: Enabling Encryption at Host on a VM fails. The option is not available or returns an error in Azure Portal or PowerShell.
rootCause: The EncryptionAtHost feature is not registered for the subscription. This feature requires explicit opt-in registration via Azure Provider Feature registration before it can be used.
solution: Register the feature: Register-AzProviderFeature -FeatureName "EncryptionAtHost" -ProviderNamespace "Microsoft.Compute". Check status: Get-AzProviderFeature -FeatureName "EncryptionAtHost" -ProviderNamespace "Microsoft.Compute". Wait for status to show Registered. VM must be deallocated, then enable via Disks > Additional Settings > Encryption at host > Yes > Save.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 4 Encrypt a Windows VM with Encryption at Host_Encryption

symptom: Customer needs to migrate VM from ADE Dual Pass (with AAD) to Single Pass (without AAD) encryption. Migration path is unclear or customer is unsure how to upgrade.
solution: Use PowerShell: Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -ResourceGroupName RG -VMName VM -Migrate. Requires Az module >= 5.9.0. Confirm with Y when prompted. VM will reboot. Verify: Get-AzVmDiskEncryptionStatus. After migration, ADE extension version changes to 1.x (Single Pass). Check portal Extensions tab to confirm version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 12 Migrate from Dual Pass to Single Pass_Encryption

symptom: Customer has ADE-encrypted Windows VM with BEK only and needs to upgrade to KEK (Key Encryption Key) protection. Need to re-encrypt with KEK without decrypting first.
solution: Re-run Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension with KEK parameters on already BEK-encrypted VM: Get KeyVault URI and ResourceId, get KEK URL via Get-AzKeyVaultKey, then Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension with -KeyEncryptionKeyUrl and -KeyEncryptionKeyVaultId. Use -SequenceVersion (New-Guid). Verify: Get-AzVmDiskEncryptionStatus, Portal Disks blade, or RDP + manage-bde -status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 7 Windows Single Pass Encryption using Cloud Shell or PowerShell_Encryption

symptom: Need to encrypt Linux VM data disks with ADE using KEK via Azure CLI. Customer prefers CLI over PowerShell for Linux encryption.
solution: az vm encryption enable --resource-group RG --name VM --disk-encryption-keyvault KVName --key-encryption-key KeyName --volume-type DATA --encrypt-format-all -o table. Verify: az vm encryption show -n VM -g RG -o table. On VM: lsblk to confirm encrypted partitions. Warning: --encrypt-format-all formats data disks (data loss on existing data).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 8 Single Pass Encryption with Linux using CLI_Encryption

symptom: ADE Dual Pass encryption setup fails or customer is confused about AAD App and Service Principal requirements for legacy Dual Pass encryption on Linux.
rootCause: Dual Pass ADE (legacy) requires an AAD Application registration with Service Principal and a client secret, plus Contributor role assignment on the subscription. This is different from modern Single Pass which does not need AAD app.
solution: Create AAD App: az ad sp create-for-rbac --name AppName --role Contributor --scopes /subscriptions/SubId. Save appId and password. Configure Key Vault access policies for the app. Then encrypt using Dual Pass cmdlets with -AadClientId and -AadClientSecret parameters. Recommendation: migrate to Single Pass if possible (Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension -Migrate).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/Trainings/Labs/Lv. 200 Lab 9 Dual Pass Encryption with Linux Using PowerShell_Encryption

symptom: Azure Files SMB mount via UNC path fails with STATUS_OBJECT_NAME_NOT_FOUND instead of prompting for credentials. Network authentication prompt does not appear when mounting file share in Windows Explorer.
rootCause: Azure Files Storage service bug (starting 8/14/2023) changed SMB Session Setup error code from STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE to STATUS_OBJECT_NAME_NOT_FOUND. Windows relies on STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE as signal to prompt for alternate credentials. The new error code bypasses this prompt.
solution: Provide credentials explicitly: (1) Use net use command with credential parameters, or (2) Use cmdkey /add:<account>.file.core.windows.net /user:<account> /pass:<key> to pre-configure credentials before mounting. Azure Files deployed a fix via Safe Deployment Practices. Ref: ICM 417195141.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Emerging Issues/Emerging Issue Network credential prompt not loading mount Azure Files UNC path_Storage

symptom: Slow performance after mounting Azure file share on Windows Server 2012 R2 or Windows 8.1.
rootCause: Known OS-level issue in Windows Server 2012 R2 and Windows 8.1 affecting SMB file share performance. Resolved in April 2014 cumulative update.
solution: Apply the April 2014 cumulative update for Windows Server 2012 R2 and Windows 8.1 (KB3114025). See: https://support.microsoft.com/kb/3114025
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files FAQ_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync recall failures with RecallToDiskType: No errors. Tiered files fail to download fully. Event ID 1110 shows recall details.
rootCause: Default 10% memory allocation for Azure File Sync recalls is insufficient. When cache usage exceeds 60% of allocated memory, recalls are processed in-memory without writing to disk.
solution: Increase allowed memory for recalls by modifying registry settings to allocate 30% memory, then restart Azure File Sync services. Verify data recall sizes and review Event ID 1110.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files FAQ_Storage

symptom: Network drive mapping fails when using Microsoft Entra ID authentication for Azure Files. Mount command fails after switching to Entra ID auth.
rootCause: Stale/cached credentials from previous authentication methods conflict with new Microsoft Entra ID authentication flow.
solution: 1) List stored credentials: cmdkey /list; 2) Remove stale credential: cmdkey /delete:<account>.file.core.windows.net; 3) Re-mount using PowerShell Test-NetConnection + New-PSDrive.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files FAQ_Storage

symptom: Failed to delete/modify file on Azure File Share: The specified resource is marked for deletion by an SMB client. File handle prevents modification or deletion.
rootCause: Open/orphaned SMB file handle preventing file or directory from being modified or deleted. Handles can be leaked or lost track of by SMB clients.
solution: Use PowerShell: Get-AzStorageFileHandle -ShareName <share> -Recursive -Context $ctx to list handles, then Close-AzStorageFileHandle -ShareName <share> -CloseAll -Recursive -Context $ctx to close. If blocked by permissions, use ASC Force Close File Handle via Jarvis with JIT. Note: Az CLI has bug (ICM 743859154, Feb 2026), use PowerShell.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Handles Closure_Storage

symptom: Close-AzStorageFileHandle returns HTTP 404 ParentNotFound even with correct path when closing Azure File Share handles.
rootCause: Azure Storage Account Firewall is enabled, blocking the PowerShell file handle operation with misleading 404 error instead of 403.
solution: Temporarily set storage account networking to Enabled from all networks, retry Close-AzStorageFileHandle, then re-enable firewall restrictions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Handles Closure_Storage

symptom: Get-AzStorageFileHandle returns 403 AuthorizationFailure when running from Azure Serial Console or Cloud Shell for Azure Files handle operations.
rootCause: MSI-based authentication from Serial Console/Cloud Shell uses managed identity tokens that lack required permissions for Azure Files data plane handle operations.
solution: Run handle commands from a local machine or Azure VM with proper interactive Connect-AzAccount authentication, not from Serial Console/Cloud Shell. Use New-AzStorageContext with storage account name and key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Handles Closure_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share shows high latency and slow performance for metadata-heavy operations (createfile, openfile, closefile, queryinfo, querydirectory) on both standard and premium tiers
rootCause: Internal resource limitations on the Azure platform side cause higher latency for metadata operations compared to read/write operations on Azure File Shares
solution: Mount a VHD on the file share and perform file operations against the VHD. In Disk Management create VHD with location set to mapped Azure File Share, initialize disk and create volume. Metadata operations become local with performance similar to directly attached storage. Limitation: only works for single writer/reader or multiple read-only scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Mount VHD on FileShare_Storage

symptom: Cannot mount Azure File Share from on-premises or different Azure region. Mount fails on Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 (SMB 2.1 only) or Linux kernel before 4.11
rootCause: Mounting Azure File Share outside hosting region requires SMB 3.0 with encryption. SMB 2.1 clients and Linux kernels before 4.11 do not support SMB 3.0 encryption. Secure transit setting disallows SMB 2.1.
solution: Upgrade to OS supporting SMB 3.0+: Windows Server 2012+, Windows 8.1+, Linux kernel 4.11+. SMB 2.1 clients can only access shares from VMs in same Azure region. For Linux ensure cifs-utils is installed and use _netdev mount option in fstab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/OS Restrictions for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: Cannot directly convert between standard file share and premium file share in Azure Files
rootCause: Direct conversion between standard and premium Azure Files tiers is not supported. Premium uses FileStorage account kind with SSD-backed storage.
solution: Create a new file share in the target tier and manually copy data using Robocopy or AzCopy. Premium requires FileStorage account kind, min 100 GiB. IOPS: Baseline=100+1*provisionedGiB (max 100K). Burst up to 3x baseline for 60+ min.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Premium Files_Storage

symptom: Access Denied error when mounting Azure File Share using storage account key after SMB security settings modified on storage account
rootCause: Storage account SMB security settings configured to allow only Kerberos, disabling NTLMv2. Storage account key mounting requires NTLMv2 enabled.
solution: Update SMB security settings: PowerShell Update-AzStorageFileServiceProperty -SmbAuthenticationMethod "NTLMv2;Kerberos". CLI: az storage account file-service-properties update --auth-methods NTLMv2;Kerberos. Also verify SMB version, channel encryption, Kerberos ticket encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Security settings for SMB Protocols in Azure File Shares_Storage

symptom: Azure Files AD DS authentication fails after a period of time; storage account Kerberos key expired due to AD password expiration policy on the OU where the storage account identity was registered
rootCause: The AD account (service logon or computer account) representing the storage account was created under an OU with password expiration policy, and the password was not rotated before the maximum password age
solution: Run Update-AzStorageAccountADObjectPassword -RotateToKerbKey kerb2 -ResourceGroupName <rg> -StorageAccountName <sa> from the AzFilesHybrid module. The cmdlet gets the second Kerberos key, updates the AD account password, then regenerates the primary Kerberos key. Set up a scheduled rotation before password expiration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/AD Authentication for Files Overview_Storage

symptom: Azure Files mount fails with private endpoint configured; DNS resolves storage account to public IP instead of private IP; nslookup returns outdated DNS records
rootCause: Private DNS zone (privatelink.file.core.windows.net) not associated with the private endpoint, or virtual network link not configured between the private DNS zone and the client VM VNet
solution: 1) Verify private DNS zone (privatelink.file.core.windows.net) is associated with the private endpoint. 2) Configure virtual network link from private DNS zone to the client VM VNet. 3) Use Resolve-DnsName (more reliable than nslookup) to verify resolution to private IP. 4) If DNS resolves correctly but connection fails, check VNet peering, NSG rules, and port 445.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Private Endpoint_Storage

symptom: Azure Files AD DS Kerberos authentication fails when mounting using privatelink subdomain URL (*.privatelink.file.core.windows.net) or raw private IP address instead of standard FQDN
rootCause: Kerberos authentication requires the standard FQDN (*.file.core.windows.net) for SPN matching; privatelink subdomain or IP address bypasses Kerberos SPN lookup and ticket validation
solution: Always mount using the standard FQDN: net use * \\<storage-account>.file.core.windows.net\<share-name>. Never use the privatelink subdomain or private IP for the mount command. Private endpoint DNS resolution handles routing to the private IP transparently when configured correctly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Private Endpoint_Storage

symptom: Azure Files AD DS authentication or NTFS authorization fails for synced user or group despite correct RBAC share-level permissions being assigned
rootCause: OnPremisesSecurityIdentifier attribute not correctly synchronized between on-premises Active Directory and Azure AD via AD Connect; SID mismatch between on-prem AD user/group and Azure AD object
solution: 1) Get on-prem SID: whoami /user or Get-ADUser -Identity <user>. 2) Get Azure AD SID: Connect-AzureAD, then Get-AzureADUser -objectid <oid> | fl to check OnPremisesSecurityIdentifier. 3) For groups: Get-AzureADGroup -objectid <oid> | fl. 4) If SID mismatch, review AD Connect sync configuration and attribute flow for OnPremisesSecurityIdentifier.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/How To Check A User Or Group Has A Valid SID_Storage

symptom: Azure Files Kerberos authentication fails; storage account SPN (cifs/storageaccount.file.core.windows.net) not found in Active Directory Domain Services when running setspn query
rootCause: Storage account was not properly domain-joined to ADDS via Join-AzStorageAccountForAuth, or the computer/service logon account representing the storage account was deleted or moved in AD
solution: 1) Verify SPN exists: setspn -q cifs/<storageaccount>.file.core.windows.net. If 'No Such SPN found', re-run Join-AzStorageAccountForAuth. 2) Alternative check: Get-AzStorageAccount -ResourceGroupName <rg> -Name <sa> | Get-AzStorageAccountADObject.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/How To Check The Azure Storage Account Service Principle Name_Storage

symptom: Azure Files klist get cifs/<sa>.file.core.windows.net fails with error 0xc000018b/-1073741429: The SAM database on the Windows Server does not have a computer account for this workstation trust relationship
rootCause: Client machine computer account trust with the domain controller is broken, or the storage account computer/service logon account in ADDS is missing or its trust relationship is corrupted
