solution: 1) Verify client is domain-joined: dsregcmd /status. 2) Check storage SPN: setspn -q cifs/<sa>.file.core.windows.net. 3) If SPN missing, re-run Join-AzStorageAccountForAuth. 4) If client trust broken, rejoin machine to domain. 5) Use Debug-AzStorageAccountAuth from AzFilesHybrid for automated validation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/How to Check Kerberos Ticket_Storage

symptom: Azure Files klist get cifs/<sa>.file.core.windows.net fails with error 0x80090342/-2146892990: The encryption type requested is not supported by the KDC; Get-AzStorageKerberosTicketStatus shows Unhealthy
rootCause: Mismatch between encryption types supported by the client, the storage account AD object, and the KDC. Common when AES-256 is required but AD object only supports RC4, or domain GPO restricts encryption types
solution: 1) Run Get-AzStorageKerberosTicketStatus (AzFilesHybrid module) to check health status. 2) Verify storage account AD object supports AES-256-CTS-HMAC-SHA1-96. 3) Check domain Group Policy for Kerberos encryption type restrictions. 4) Update AD object supported encryption types if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/How to Check Kerberos Ticket_Storage

symptom: High latency and slow performance on Azure File Share during metadata-heavy operations (createfile, openfile, closefile, queryinfo, querydirectory). ASC insight often identifies this scenario.
rootCause: Known Azure platform internal resource limitations cause metadata operations to have higher latency than read/write operations on Azure File Shares (both standard and premium).
solution: Create a VHD on the Azure File Share and mount it from the client. The client-owned filesystem makes metadata operations local, offering performance similar to directly attached storage. Limitation: single writer/reader only; multiple machines require all read-only access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Mount VHD on FileShare_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share mount fails when accessing from on-premises or a different Azure region. Client cannot connect to the file share remotely.
rootCause: Client OS only supports SMB 2.1 (e.g., Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7, or Linux kernel < 4.11) which lacks encryption. Cross-region and on-premises access requires SMB 3.0+ with encryption.
solution: Upgrade client OS to one supporting SMB 3.0 with encryption: Windows Server 2012+, Windows 8.1+, or Linux kernel 4.11+. For same-region access, SMB 2.1 is sufficient.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/OS Restrictions for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share mount fails on Linux with DNS lookup failure for Azure Files endpoint.
rootCause: Technologies like Mesosphere manipulate DNS configuration, causing DNS lookup of the Azure Files location to fail.
solution: Mount directly via IP address, or add DNS name and IP mapping into /etc/hosts to bypass manipulated DNS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/OS Restrictions for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share mount fails on Linux during system boot. The fstab entry for the file share fails to mount at startup.
rootCause: Entries in /etc/fstab are processed before networking is enabled, causing mount failure because the Azure Files endpoint is not reachable yet.
solution: Add the _netdev option to the Azure File Share entry in /etc/fstab so it is not mounted until networking is available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/OS Restrictions for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: Customer wants to convert existing standard file share to premium (or vice versa) but no direct conversion option exists.
rootCause: Azure Files does not support direct conversion between standard and premium file share tiers. They use different storage account types (StorageV2 vs FileStorage).
solution: Create a new file share in the desired tier, then manually copy data using Robocopy or AzCopy. Note: azcopy is only supported for SMB, not NFS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Premium Files_Storage

symptom: Access Denied error when trying to mount Azure File Share using storage account key (NTLMv2 authentication).
rootCause: Storage account SMB security settings restrict authentication methods (e.g., only Kerberos allowed, NTLMv2 disabled). When NTLMv2 is removed, mounting with storage account key fails.
solution: Check and adjust SMB security settings: PowerShell Update-AzStorageFileServiceProperty -SmbAuthenticationMethod "NTLMv2;Kerberos" or CLI az storage account file-service-properties update --auth-methods NTLMv2;Kerberos.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Security settings for SMB Protocols in Azure File Shares_Storage

symptom: File Explorer tab with Azure File Share mount (mapped drive via GPO) closes unexpectedly every ~90 minutes; only the File Explorer tab closes, not opened files on the share
rootCause: GPO Drive Map policy configured with Replace action causes the mapped drive to disconnect and remount during GPUpdate cycle (~90 minutes), closing the File Explorer tab each time
solution: Change the GPO Drive Map action from Replace to Update in New Drive Properties dialog. Update modifies existing mapping settings without disconnecting/remounting the drive, preventing File Explorer from closing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure File Share Mount Issue Randomly Closing_Storage

symptom: Azure SMB file share mounted on Linux VM suddenly fails with unknown error 524; kernel log shows CIFS STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE (0xc000006d) and return code -13
rootCause: Azure storage stamp migration caused the mounted file share to become inaccessible while other shares from the same storage account may still work
solution: Verify storage stamp migration using the LosingMountafterStampMigration TSG (wiki/577390). Remount the share after migration completes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure File Share Mount Issue Unknown error 524_Storage

symptom: Azure SMB file share mounted on Linux VM fails with unknown error 524 and CIFS STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE; other shares from same storage account work fine; remounting also fails with error 524
rootCause: Stale connection to storage account - newer connection attempts reuse an already opened socket that is no longer operational
solution: Add nosharesock option to FSTAB entry: //sa.file.core.windows.net/share /mnt/dir cifs nofail,nosharesock,vers=3.0,dir_mode=0775,file_mode=0775 0 0. Then run mount -a. The nosharesock parameter is included in official Azure CLI mount script.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure File Share Mount Issue Unknown error 524_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share operations fail with STATUS_QUOTA_EXCEEDED (0xc0000044) / ERROR_NOT_ENOUGH_QUOTA (1816) on Windows, or Disk quota exceeded / permission denied on Linux; data-plane failures and potential slowness
rootCause: Exceeding maximum concurrent file handles limit per file/directory (default 2,000; 10,000 with preview). Internal error XS_STATUS_SMB_TOO_MANY_HANDLES (0x830a1824) or XS_STATUS_SMB_TOO_MANY_LOCKS (0x830a1825)
solution: Query Storage Front End Logs XSMBPerfMetric table for InternalXsError 830a1824/830a1825. Investigate client-side handle usage with ProcMon. Consider registering for increased file handle limits preview (10,000/file). Customer can close opened handles via Azure portal/CLI but may cause application errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure File Share QUOTA_EXCEEDED During File Access_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share operations fail with STATUS_QUOTA_EXCEEDED when applications register excessive File Change Notify operations (e.g., IIS monitoring file changes)
rootCause: Exceeding internal limit of 1,000 File Change Notify operations per SMB share (MS INTERNAL, not publicly documented). Internal error XS_STATUS_SMB_TOO_MANY_NOTIFICATIONS (0x830a1826)
solution: For IIS: disable File Change Notifications and lower W3WP worker process polling interval per MS docs. Query Storage Front End Logs for InternalXsError 830a1826. Reduce ChangeNotify operations from client applications. Collaborate with IIS CSS if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure File Share QUOTA_EXCEEDED During File Access_Storage

symptom: After a long-running Azure Storage outage, STATUS_QUOTA_EXCEEDED errors persist on Azure File Share even after outage resolution
rootCause: During storage outage, application (e.g., IIS) handle close operations fail, resulting in leaked handles that persist and count against handle limits until manually closed
solution: Confirm Azure Storage outage occurred affecting the Storage Account. List opened handles to evaluate which are stale (using outage timestamp). Stop the application using the file share, then close leaked handles via Azure portal/CLI. Warning: forcefully closing handles may cause unexpected application behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure File Share QUOTA_EXCEEDED During File Access_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share mounted via PowerShell (New-PSDrive) is accessible in PowerShell session but returns error when accessing via File Explorer; Used/Free GB not reported correctly in drive properties
rootCause: New-PSDrive command was used without the -Persist option, creating a session-only drive visible only in the current PowerShell session, not in File Explorer or other applications
solution: Mount with -Persist flag: New-PSDrive -Name Z -PSProvider FileSystem -Root '\\<sa>.file.core.windows.net\<share>' -Persist. Alternatively use net use: net use Z: \\<sa>.file.core.windows.net\<share> <accesskey> /user:<sa>. Note: Azure Portal connection script includes -Persist by default.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure File Share Unable to Access via FileExplorer_Storage

symptom: Mount of Azure File Share on RHEL 8.2 fails with: mount: bad option; for several filesystems (e.g. nfs, cifs) you might need a /sbin/mount.<type> helper program
rootCause: cifs-utils RPM package is not installed by default on RHEL 8.2, so /sbin/mount.cifs binary is missing. Verified by: ls -l /sbin/mount.cifs returns No such file or directory
solution: Install cifs-utils package: yum install cifs-utils. Verify installation: ls -l /sbin/mount.cifs. Then retry mounting the Azure File Share. The package provides mount.cifs binary required for CIFS/SMB mounts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure FileShare Mount Issue Bad Option_Storage

symptom: Fiddler Classic unable to decrypt HTTPS traffic for Entra Kerberos debugging - requests to login.microsoftonline.com appear greyed out and cannot be inspected
rootCause: Fiddler certificate configuration is corrupt or not properly trusted by the system, preventing HTTPS traffic decryption
solution: In Fiddler go to Tools > Options > HTTPS > Actions > Reset All Certificates. Accept all popups, wait for completion, then restart Fiddler.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/How to Execute Fiddler Kerberos extension to debug Entra Kerberos issues_Storage

symptom: klist get cifs/<storageaccount>.file.core.windows.net fails with error 0x51f / 0xc000005e/-1073741730 after using Fiddler for Kerberos debugging
rootCause: Fiddler proxy settings (port 8888) not cleaned up correctly after process exit, leaving stale proxy configuration blocking Kerberos ticket retrieval
solution: Run: (1) netsh winhttp reset autoproxy, (2) netsh winhttp reset proxy, (3) In registry HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\iphlpsvc\Parameters\ProxyMgr delete subentries with port :8888, (4) Restart machine.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Identity/How to Execute Fiddler Kerberos extension to debug Entra Kerberos issues_Storage

symptom: Windows 7 or Windows Server 2008 R2 client fails to mount Azure File Share from external location with STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED during SMB session setup
rootCause: SMB 2.1 (max protocol on Win7/2008R2) does not support encryption. Azure Files requires encrypted connections for external access.
solution: Use a client OS supporting SMB 3.0+ (Windows 8/Server 2012+) for external Azure Files access, or place the client VM in the same Azure region as the storage account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Windows 7 & Windows Server 2008 R2_Storage

symptom: Windows 7 or Windows Server 2008 R2 client fails to mount Azure File Share with Access Denied even from SAME datacenter when Secure Transfer Required is enabled
rootCause: Secure Transfer Required forces encrypted connections. SMB 2.1 on Win7/2008R2 cannot handle encryption, so Azure rejects even same-region clients.
solution: Disable Secure Transfer Required on the storage account (if security policy allows), or upgrade client OS to SMB 3.0+ (Windows 8/Server 2012+).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Azure Files Windows 7 & Windows Server 2008 R2_Storage

symptom: Application or service cannot connect to mounted Azure Files drive. Mapped drive not visible when app runs under different user context.
rootCause: Mapped drives are mounted per user. If the application/service runs under a different user context, it cannot see the Azure Files mounted drive.
solution: Mount the Azure File share from the same user context as the application using PsExec (psexec.exe -i -u <UserName> powershell), then use cmdkey + New-PSDrive to map the drive. For System account: psexec.exe -i -s powershell.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Drive Mapped Under Different User_Storage

symptom: Error System 86 "The specified network password is not correct" when mounting Azure File Share configured with Entra ID Kerberos on Windows client using net use command.
rootCause: Server closed the authentication connection because no Kerberos tickets were presented from the client side. Stale cached credentials in Windows Credential Manager interfere with Kerberos authentication.
solution: Remove the stale entry from Windows Credential Manager for the storage account. Also verify: supported OS prerequisites, storage account Entra Kerberos config, client Entra joined/hybrid joined, hybrid identity, CloudKerberosTicketRetrievalEnabled, Debug-AzStorageAccountAuth cmdlet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AAD_Kerb_Auth/Error 86 The Specified Network Password is not Correct_Storage

symptom: Error "The request to microsoft graph failed with code BadRequest" / "Credential type not allowed as per assigned policy" when joining storage account to Microsoft Entra Kerberos.
rootCause: Entra ID app management policies ("Block password addition" and "Restrict max password lifetime") block the Storage Resource Provider from creating the service principal.
solution: Exclude the Storage Resource Provider in the Excluded callers section of the applied app management policies (Entra ID > Enterprise applications > app management policies). Requires Attribute Definition Administrator and Attribute Assignment Administrator roles.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AAD_Kerb_Auth/Microsoft Entra Kerberos Join Graph BadRequest_Storage

symptom: klist failed with 0xc000009a/-1073741670 "Insufficient system resources" when running klist get cifs/<storageaccount>.file.core.windows.net. Client gets Microsoft TGT but not storage CIFS ticket. Net use returns System error 1326.
rootCause: Customer disabled Microsoft Entra Kerberos on the storage account without deleting the associated Entra app, then re-enabled it. Old app identifier no longer matches (errorCode 700016: Application not found in directory).
solution: Disable Microsoft Entra ID Kerberos for the storage account, delete the corresponding app in Entra ID, wait 15-30 minutes for sync, then re-enable Kerberos.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AAD_Kerb_Auth/Microsoft Entra Kerberos authentication Unable to Retrieve Storage token_Storage

symptom: Unable to mount Azure File Share with Microsoft Entra Kerberos. Entra sign-in logs show error AADSTS50105: user is blocked because not assigned to the application.
rootCause: "Assignment required" is enabled on the enterprise application representing the storage account. Entitlements are not populated in the Kerberos ticket, so even assigned users fail.
solution: Do not select "Assignment required" for the Microsoft Entra application representing the storage account. If an existing policy enforces it, configure an exception for the storage account app.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AAD_Kerb_Auth/Microsoft Entra Kerberos authentication Unable to Retrieve Storage token_Storage

symptom: Unable to mount Azure File Share with Entra Kerberos. Entra sign-in logs show errorCode 50158: "External security challenge not satisfied."
rootCause: External security challenge (terms of use, third-party MFA provider, or conditional access MFA policy) blocks Kerberos authentication for the storage account app.
solution: Disable MFA on the Microsoft Entra app representing the storage account. Create conditional access policy exclusion for the storage account service principal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AAD_Kerb_Auth/Microsoft Entra Kerberos authentication Unable to Retrieve Storage token_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share mount error 1219: Multiple connections to a server or shared resource by the same user, using more than one user name, are not allowed
rootCause: Open file handle on a previously mounted file share that needs to be closed
solution: Wait for auto-resolution (handle timeout). Check for open file handles and close them. If issue persists, investigate Kerberos STATUS_ACCOUNT_DISABLED (storage account computer object may be disabled in AD).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/1219 Mount Error_Storage

symptom: System error 64 when mounting Azure File Share: The specified network name is no longer available. Proxy or NAT device blocking.
rootCause: Proxy or NAT device in customer local network blocking connection from on-premises to Azure for mapping Azure File Share
solution: Add Storage Account IP/range in proxy or NAT device. Run ipconfig /flushdns and /registerdns. In Local Security Policy, disable "Microsoft network client: Digitally sign communications".
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/64 Mount Error_Storage

symptom: System error 64 mounting Azure File Share; SMB negotiate met with TCP RST from storage IP but TTL is suspicious (64 instead of ~128); SMB request never reached Azure Storage backend
rootCause: Client-side network element (firewall) intercepting and sending TCP RST with different TTL, not Azure Storage. SMB negotiate never reaches backend.
solution: Confirm suspicious TTL in Wireshark (Windows TTL=128; 64 indicates interception). Verify in XSMBPerfMetric logs no request from client IP reached backend. Involve Networking team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/64 Mount Error_Storage

symptom: Azure Files AD DS auth mount fails with Kerberos STATUS_ACCOUNT_DISABLED; klist fails with 0xc000018b; KRB5KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN in network trace
rootCause: Storage account computer object in Active Directory is disabled or accidentally deleted
solution: Open AD Users and Computers, locate and enable the storage account computer object. If deleted, restore from AD Recycle Bin via AD Administrative Center. Run "klist purge" then "klist get cifs/<storage>.file.core.windows.net" and retry mount.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Azure Files Kerberos_Error_STATUS_ACCOUNT_DISABLED_Storage

symptom: Restoring files from Azure File Share backup does not restore manually assigned NTFS ACLs; only inherited permissions restored after backup restore
rootCause: Storage Account Firewall is set to Disabled with Private Endpoints configured. Backup restore does not rehydrate manual NTFS ACLs in this config.
solution: Set Storage Account Firewall to "Enabled from all networks" or "Enabled from selected virtual networks and IP addresses". If still failing, engage Backup team (MSaaS Azure Backup and Recovery Service).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Backup Doesn't Restore Manually Assigned NTFS ACLs_Storage

symptom: User without proper RBAC/NTFS permissions can unexpectedly mount Azure File Share; Wireshark shows Azure\StorageAccountName instead of user@domain.com
rootCause: Storage account key credentials cached on the client machine from a previous session in Windows Credential Manager
solution: Delete cached credentials via Windows Credential Manager (Control Panel > Credential Manager). Verify with Wireshark that subsequent mount uses Kerberos domain identity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Cached Credentials_Storage

symptom: Join-AzStorageAccountforAuth fails with "Cannot bind positional parameters because no names were given" when joining Azure Storage account to AD DS
rootCause: Syntax error in command, commonly misspelled parameter "-OrganizationUnitName" (correct: "-OrganizationalUnitName") from outdated documentation
solution: Check command for misspellings. Ensure parameter is "-OrganizationalUnitName" (not "-OrganizationUnitName"). If correct, submit AVA request to AD Auth for Az Files Teams Channel.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Cannot Bind Positional Parameters_Storage

symptom: Cannot take ownership of Azure File Share or set NTFS ACLs via domain identity; Robocopy with security info fails with access denied
rootCause: Platform limitation: AD-authenticated domain identity lacks TakeOwnership/WRITE_DAC privilege without explicit ACL grant. No admin-like RBAC role for domain identities.
solution: Mount file share using storage account name and key (super-user access) for lift-and-shift scenarios. This bypasses ACL checks and allows setting ownership and permissions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Cannot Take Ownership_Storage

symptom: On-premises domain not visible in "From this location" when setting NTFS permissions on Azure File Share after AD DS domain join; only Storage Account URL shown
rootCause: Known transient issue after initial domain join of storage account to AD DS
solution: Wait a few hours and retry. If persists beyond a few hours, create ICM to Azure Storage team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Domain Does Not Populate_Storage

symptom: Azure Files REST API call (ARM or SRP) returns empty value array with nextLink present; no apparent results in first page
rootCause: By design - REST API enumeration can return partial or empty results depending on internal storage layout; consumer must follow continuation token (nextLink) to get complete results, even if maxpagesize is not reached
solution: Follow nextLink pagination: send GET requests to the URL in nextLink property until it no longer contains a URL. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/azure/#async-operations-throttling-and-paging
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files RestAPI Empty Value Response_Storage

symptom: Accessing Azure file share via UNC path in Windows Explorer takes more than 5 minutes; same issue in Excel/Word/PowerPoint via UNC path; but mounting as network drive works fine
rootCause: Windows with Client for NFS feature installed tries connecting via port 111 (sunrpc/nfsclnt.exe); NFS Azure File Share is not supported on Windows, causing TCP retransmissions and timeouts
solution: Remove Client for NFS feature from Windows (uncheck in Remove Features screen) and restart the machine
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Slow Access Via UNCPath_Storage

symptom: ls command hangs or returns 'Cannot access FilePath: Input/output error' when listing files in Azure file share on Linux
rootCause: Missing Linux OS kernel fixes for Azure Files SMB client
solution: Upgrade Linux kernel to 4.4.87+, 4.9.48+, 4.12.11+, or any version >= 4.13
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Cannot Access File Path Input Output Error_Storage

symptom: net use command fails with 'The option /key== is unknown' when mapping Azure file share network drive and storage account key contains forward slash
rootCause: cmd.exe interprets forward slash (/) in storage account key as a command line option
solution: Use PowerShell New-SmbMapping instead of net use: New-SmbMapping -LocalPath z: -RemotePath \\server\share -UserName account -Password "/key"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Cannot Map Drive Storage Account Forward Slash_Storage

symptom: Cannot mount Azure file share on macOS via Finder when storage account key contains '/' special character
rootCause: macOS Finder does not properly handle forward slash '/' character in storage account key, causing connection failure
solution: Mount via Terminal in 2-stage process: (1) mkdir mount_point, (2) mount_smbfs //username@server/share mount_point/ and enter password at prompt. Alternatively use: open smb://account:key@account.file.core.windows.net/share. Or regenerate storage key without '/'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Cannot mount file share to MacOS no route to host_Storage

symptom: 'File already exists' error when copying files to mounted Azure file share using Windows File Explorer; some files copy successfully while others fail
rootCause: Files have NTFS Alternate Data Streams which Azure File Shares do not support
solution: Remove alternate data streams before copying: Remove-Item <filepath> -Stream *; then copy again. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-file-acls-preservation#alternate-data-streams-and-extended-attributes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Copy Files File Explorer File Already Exists Error_Storage

symptom: RequesterAppId, RequesterUpn, RequesterUserName empty in StorageFileLogs diagnostic logs when using AD DS authentication for Azure Files; only SmbPrimarySID is populated
rootCause: When using on-premises Active Directory authentication, only the Security Identifier (SID) is passed to the storage account; UPN and username are not shared by AD during the Kerberos authentication flow
solution: Use Get-AdUser <SID> PowerShell command to resolve the SID to a username for audit purposes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Diagnostic Settings Kerberos Authentication_Storage

symptom: Azure Files REST API call through ARM (management.azure.com) or SRP returns empty value array with no results, but nextLink property is present containing continuation token
rootCause: By design - REST APIs depending on enumeration can return partial results including empty result sets even when data exists. Consumer must follow continuation tokens (nextLink) to get complete results.
solution: Always follow continuation tokens: send GET requests to the nextLink URL until it no longer contains a URL in the response. See Paging in REST API docs for implementation details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files RestAPI Empty Value Response_Storage

symptom: Accessing Azure Files through Explorer using UNC path takes more than 5 minutes. Applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) accessing UNC path are also slow. However, mounting as network drive (mapped drive) works fine.
rootCause: Windows with Client for NFS feature installed attempts to connect via port 111 (sunrpc). NFS Azure File Share is not supported on Windows, causing repeated TCP retransmissions to port 111 and timeouts before falling back to SMB.
solution: Remove the Client for NFS feature: open Remove Features screen, uncheck Client for NFS, and restart the machine. Verify with Procmon (nfsclnt.exe connecting to port 111) or Wireshark (TCP retransmission to port 111, RST from storage).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Slow Access Via UNCPath_Storage

symptom: Customer cannot see a specific Availability Zone during Azure Files Premium (LRS) storage account creation with Zonal Placement feature
rootCause: Azure intentionally hides zones that have current capacity or infrastructure constraints. Only zones with high probability of successful deployment are shown.
solution: Explain that zones reappear automatically when capacity is restored. Do not provide ETAs. For large strategic customers, escalate internally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Zonal Placement/Azure File Share Zonal Placement TSG_Storage

symptom: Azure Files Premium LRS to ZRS redundancy conversion fails when the storage account has Zonal Placement enabled
rootCause: ZRS conversion is blocked when the storage account is zone-pinned via Zonal Placement feature. The two features are mutually exclusive.
solution: 1. Set Availability Zone to None in Storage Account > Availability. 2. Navigate to Data Management > Redundancy. 3. Convert to ZRS. Portal now provides inline guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Zonal Placement/Azure File Share Zonal Placement TSG_Storage

symptom: Customer pinned Azure Files Premium storage account to a specific zone via Zonal Placement but wants to move to a different zone. Toggling between Azure-selected zone and None does not move data.
rootCause: Customers cannot self-select another zone after pinning. Zone-to-zone migration is not a customer-facing operation.
solution: For large customers (>50 TB, S500): 1. Customer unpins (set to None). 2. Open ICM to backend team. 3. Backend moves account. SLA ~1 week, no downtime.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Zonal Placement/Azure File Share Zonal Placement TSG_Storage

symptom: Linux ls command hangs when listing Azure Files directory, or returns error: ls: cannot access FilePath: Input/output error
rootCause: Missing Linux OS kernel fixes for Azure Files SMB client operations
solution: Upgrade the Linux kernel to one of these fixed versions: 4.4.87+, 4.9.48+, 4.12.11+, or any version >= 4.13
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Cannot Access File Path Input Output Error_Storage

symptom: Mapping network drive with net use fails with error: The option /StorageAccountKey== is unknown, when storage account key contains a forward slash character
rootCause: Command Interpreter (cmd.exe) interprets the forward slash (/) in the storage account key as a command line option switch
solution: Use PowerShell instead of cmd.exe: New-SmbMapping -LocalPath z: -RemotePath \\storageaccountname.file.core.windows.net\sharename -UserName storageaccountname -Password with the key. For scripts: pipe through powershell -command -
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Cannot Map Drive Storage Account Forward Slash_Storage

symptom: Cannot mount/map Azure File Share drive to macOS. Connection fails when using Finder and storage account key contains special character like forward slash
rootCause: macOS Finder does not properly handle the forward slash (/) character in the storage account key, causing authentication/connection failure
solution: Mount in 2-stage process via Terminal: 1. Create mount point (mkdir share_name). 2. Mount without inline password (mount_smbfs //username@server/share share/) and enter password at prompt. Alternatively use open smb:// command in Terminal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Cannot mount file share to MacOS no route to host_Storage

symptom: Copying files into mounted Azure File Share using Windows File Explorer shows File already exists or Replace or Skip Files dialog then fails. Some files copy but others fail.
rootCause: Source files have Alternate Data Streams (ADS). Azure File Shares do not support alternate data streams, causing the copy operation to fail for files with ADS.
solution: Check for ADS with Get-Item <file> -Stream *. If streams beyond :$DATA exist, remove them with Remove-Item <file> -Stream * before copying to Azure File Share.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Copy Files File Explorer File Already Exists Error_Storage

symptom: Azure Files StorageFileLogs diagnostic logs show only SmbPrimarySID but RequesterUserName, RequesterUpn, and RequesterAppId properties are empty/not populated when using AD authentication
rootCause: When using on-premises Active Directory authentication, AD only shares the Security Identifier (SID) with Azure Storage during Kerberos authentication. No UPN or username information is passed.
solution: Use PowerShell command Get-AdUser <SID> to map the SmbPrimarySID from logs to the actual on-premises Active Directory username.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Diagnostic Settings Kerberos Authentication_Storage

symptom: Azure Files AAD Kerberos clients connecting through VPN fail to retrieve Kerberos token. klist returns error: klist failed with 0x80090303/-2146893053: The specified target is unknown or unreachable
rootCause: VPN misconfiguration preventing Windows clients from reaching AAD Kerberos service over HTTPS. AAD Kerberos uses HTTPS (not traditional Kerberos ports), so WireShark cannot inspect this traffic.
solution: 1) Verify client meets prerequisites (Win10/Server 2022 with required patches). 2) Check AAD join status and line-of-sight to on-prem domain. 3) Verify storage account SPN registration in AAD with admin consent and MFA exclusion. 4) Collect Fiddler trace with Kerberos.NET extension. 5) Recommend customer reconfigure VPN. 6) Collaborate with Azure Networking Team if needed (SAP: Azure\VPN Gateway\Configuration and Setup).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Klist Failed With 0x80090303 2146893053 The specified target is unknown or unreachable_Storage

symptom: Azure Files AAD Kerberos mount prompts for credentials repeatedly. klist returns error 0x80090342 SEC_E_KDC_UNKNOWN_ETYPE (The encryption type requested is not supported by the KDC).
rootCause: RC4 encryption is not enabled on the customer domain, causing Kerberos ticket retrieval to fail with unsupported encryption type.
solution: Enable RC4 encryption on the domain. See related TSG: 1396 - The target account name is incorrect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/User Prompted for Credentials Repeatedly_Storage

symptom: Azure Files AAD Kerberos mount prompts for credentials with error: The SAM database on the Windows Server does not have a computer account for this workstation trust relationship.
rootCause: Storage account AD object is missing or disabled in Active Directory.
solution: 1) Run Get-AzStorageAccountADObject to verify object exists. 2) If missing, rejoin storage account via PowerShell to recreate AD object. 3) If exists, check if account is disabled (right-click to enable). 4) If exists and enabled but issue persists, collect WireShark trace and escalate to XSMB product team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/User Prompted for Credentials Repeatedly_Storage

symptom: User with file share level RBAC permissions navigates to Azure portal file share browse but gets access denied. Cannot view file share data despite having share-level permissions.
rootCause: User lacks reader-level RBAC permissions at the storage account scope or higher. Share-level permissions alone are insufficient to browse in the portal.
solution: Assign at least Reader role at storage account level or higher scope (e.g. subscription level). Verify permissions via portal IAM blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure_Active_Directory_Authentication_over_REST/TSGs/AAD Files OAuth REST ASC Unable to view data Share level_Storage

symptom: Error when browsing Azure file share in portal: You do not have permission to use the access key to list data. Key-based authentication is not permitted on this storage account.
rootCause: Storage account key-based access is disabled at the account level in the configuration settings.
solution: Enable key-based access at the storage account level in Configuration settings via the Azure portal, PowerShell, or CLI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure_Active_Directory_Authentication_over_REST/TSGs/AAD Files OAuth REST ASC restrict key based access_Storage

symptom: AuthorizationPermissionMismatch error when accessing Azure File Share over REST using OAuth: This request is not authorized to perform this operation using this permission.
rootCause: User/group/service principal lacks required RBAC permissions/role assignments for the requested data action. Failures logged in DGrep FileFrontEndSummaryPerfLogs with HTTP 403.
solution: 1) Query DGrep FileFrontEndSummaryPerfLogs for HTTP 403 + AuthorizationPermissionMismatch. 2) Get UserObjectId from logs. 3) Check verbose logs (Nephos.File) to identify required data action. 4) Use ASC to verify RBAC role assignments (Storage Roles and Storage Sub Resources Roles tabs). 5) Check user group memberships. 6) Assign Storage File Data Privileged Reader or Contributor role. Role takes up to 10 mins to take effect. 7) If correct roles exist but issue persists, escalate to TAs via AVA teams channel.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure_Active_Directory_Authentication_over_REST/TSGs/AAD Files OAuth REST AuthorizationPermissionMismatch error_Storage

symptom: Join-AzStorageAccountforAuth fails with 'The directory service was unable to allocate a relative identifier' when domain joining Azure Storage Account
rootCause: RID Master domain controller is unavailable or was removed from domain and restored from backup
solution: Verify all DCs in Active Directory Users and Computers > Domain Controllers container are running. Bring unavailable DC online. If issue persists after restoring DC, collaborate with Directory Services team. See KB822053 for DC restored from backup scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Unable to Allocate Relative Identifier_Storage

symptom: Join-AzStorageAccount cmdlet fails with 'Get-ADDomain: Unable to contact the server. This may be because this server does not exist, it is currently down, or it does not have the Active Directory Web Services running'
rootCause: Active Directory Web Services (ADWS) is not running on domain controller, or port 9389 is blocked by firewall
solution: 1) Test port 9389: Test-NetConnection <DomainName> -port 9389. 2) Check ADWS in services.msc. 3) If stopped: Start-Service ADWS. 4) If running: Restart-Service ADWS. 5) Set startup to AutomaticDelayedStart. 6) If issue persists, collaborate with Windows Identity Team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Unable to contact the server or it does not have the Active Directory Web Services running_Storage

symptom: Access Denied when trying to update NTFS permissions on Azure File Share via identity-based authentication
rootCause: Customer is using cached AD credentials instead of Storage Account Access Key when mounting the share for NTFS permission changes. XDiagnostics may show kerberosclientothererror access denied
solution: 1) Drop existing mount. 2) Clear cached credentials via Windows Credential Manager and cmdkey /list. 3) Purge Kerberos tickets: klist purge. 4) Reboot (required). 5) Re-mount using Storage Account Access Key, then retry NTFS permission changes. Note: Storage File Data SMB Elevated Contributor RBAC role also allows NTFS modification.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Access Denied while trying to update file share NTFS permission_Storage

symptom: Access Denied for root or specific folders in Azure File Share while child folders are accessible with identity-based auth
rootCause: Default NTFS ACLs are missing on the file share or folder
solution: Mount file share using storage key and verify default ACLs exist: BUILTIN\Administrators:(OI)(CI)(F), BUILTIN\Users:(RX), NT AUTHORITY\Authenticated Users:(OI)(CI)(M), NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM:(OI)(CI)(F), CREATOR OWNER:(OI)(CI)(IO)(F). Restore missing ACLs via security tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Access Denied while trying to update file share NTFS permission_Storage

symptom: Net use error code 1326 'The username or password is incorrect' when accessing Azure File Share with AD auth, especially from system processes like Windows Defender
rootCause: Applications running as SYSTEM/Network Service use computer account SID which has no corresponding Azure RBAC identity, causing logon failure on TreeConnect. XDS logs show FilterWellKnownSids Found matching non-alerting Sid
solution: Computer accounts require default share level permissions for AD Auth. Configure via Share-level permissions for all authenticated identities. To detect: use Process Monitor to check process SID, or check XDS logs for computer SID detection.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/1326 Mount Error_Storage

symptom: System error 5 'Access is denied' when accessing Azure File Share with identity-based auth due to missing RBAC or NTFS permissions
rootCause: User missing either share-level RBAC permissions (SMB Reader/Contributor/Elevated) or file/directory-level NTFS permissions. Both levels required for access.
solution: 1) Verify RBAC via ASC Resource Explorer at storage account/file share level. 2) For NTFS: mount share with account key first, assign permissions: icacls <drive>: /grant <user-email>:(f). See RBAC+NTFS permission matrix for expected behavior combinations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/5 Mount Error_Storage

symptom: System error 5 'Access is denied' when accessing Azure File Share - on-prem SID does not match Azure AD SID
rootCause: On-premises user SID does not match the corresponding Azure AD onPremisesSecurityIdentifier. Only supported against the AD forest that storage account is registered to.
solution: Compare on-prem SID (whoami /user) with Azure AD SID (Get-AzureADUser -objectid <id> | fl). User must login via the AD identity matching the onPremisesSecurityIdentifier shown in AAD. For multi-forest, see multi-forest AD auth configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/5 Mount Error_Storage

symptom: System error 5 'Access is denied' when mounting Azure File Share due to SMB cipher order mismatch (Win11/Win2022)
rootCause: Windows 11/Server 2022 default SMB cipher order (e.g. AES-128-CCM first) conflicts with storage account Maximum Security setting which only allows AES-256-GCM. Negotiation fails before account name is known.
solution: Change cipher order on client: Set-SmbClientConfiguration -EncryptionCiphers 'AES_256_GCM, AES_256_CCM, AES_128_GCM, AES_128_CCM'. Check current: get-SmbClientConfiguration | select encryptionciphers. Keep all ciphers for compatibility.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/5 Mount Error_Storage

symptom: Access denied (0xc0000022) when mounting Azure File Share - XDS log shows 'Account does not list SMBx.x as allowed dialect'
rootCause: Client attempting to connect with SMB version not in storage account allowed SMB dialects list
solution: Configure client SMB version via registry: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters\MinSMB2Dialect and MaxSMB2Dialect. Values: 0x202(2.0.2), 0x210(2.1), 0x300(3.0), 0x302(3.0.2), 0x311(3.1.1). Or add required version to storage account allowed dialects.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Access is denied - Mount Error_Storage

symptom: Access denied when mounting Azure File Share with Kerberos - wrong encryption type (e.g. RC4 when only AES-256 permitted)
rootCause: Kerberos ticket encrypted with unsupported encryption type for the storage account
solution: Use correct encryption type or allow required type at storage account level. See Kerberos encryption configuration at storage-files-identity-ad-ds-enable. Reference: TSG 1396 Mount Error for detailed steps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Access is denied - Mount Error_Storage

symptom: Error 1396 'The target account name is incorrect' / KRB_AP_ERR_MODIFIED when mounting Azure File Share - Kerberos key password mismatch
rootCause: Storage account identity password in AD does not match either kerb1 or kerb2 keys on storage account. Azure Files cannot decrypt the Kerberos service ticket.
solution: 1) Verify: Test-AzStorageAccountADObjectPasswordIsKerbKey. 2) If mismatch, rotate: Update-AzStorageAccountADObjectPassword -RotateToKerbKey kerb2. Wait hours, rotate back to kerb1. Alt: use Set-ADAccountPassword directly (requires Windows PowerShell 5.1 elevated).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Error 1396 The target account name is incorrect_Storage

symptom: Error 1396 after Windows Update (Nov 2022+) disabled RC4 encryption on DC, breaking previously working Azure Files AD auth
rootCause: Windows patches (KB5021131, CVE-2022-37966) disabled RC4-HMAC and enabled AES-256 on DCs. SamAccountName may not match between AD object and storage account.
solution: 1) Verify SamAccountName matches: Get-ADUser vs Get-AzStorageAccount properties. 2) Re-run Join-AzStorageAccount with latest AzFilesHybrid module, or manually set SamAccountName with Set-AzStorageAccount. 3) Rotate password. 4) klist purge, retry mount.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Error 1396 The target account name is incorrect_Storage

symptom: Error 1396 when AES256 not properly enabled on Azure Files storage account - xportal shows no keytab entries
rootCause: AES256 encryption not enabled on storage account, possibly due to outdated AzFilesHybrid module (prior to Jan 2023). XPORTAL shows no keytab1/keytab2.
solution: 1) Check XPORTAL > storage account > Basic Info > search keytab. 2) If not found, enable AES256 per docs. 3) Verify keytab1/keytab2 appear. 4) klist purge, retry mount.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Error 1396 The target account name is incorrect_Storage

symptom: Error 1396 with AAD DS storage account - mount fails after SRP automatic Kerberos key rotation (~30 days)
rootCause: Customer followed AD DS steps to manually create objects in AAD DS domain. SRP rotates kerb key but cannot update manually created object password.
solution: 1) Verify AAD DS in ASC Resource Explorer. 2) Check setspn -Q - if object not in OU=AzureFilesConfig, manually created. 3) Remove conflicting object, disable feature, wait 10 min, re-enable, wait 10 min, retry mount.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Error 1396 The target account name is incorrect_Storage

symptom: Error 1396 / KRB_AP_ERR_MODIFIED after manual domain join - missing UPN on AD object for storage account
rootCause: Manual AD domain join omits setting UPN on AD object. Causes kerbkey mismatch after second key reset when configuring AES-256.
solution: Set UPN: Set-ADUser -Identity <name> -UserPrincipalName cifs/<sa>.file.core.windows.net@<UPN suffix>. Then perform additional kerbkey reset. Even without AES-256, second key reset needed after changing UPN manually.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/Azure Active Directory (AAD) Kerberos Authentication/Error 1396 The target account name is incorrect_Storage

symptom: Linux mount error(115): Operation now in progress, or mount error: could not resolve address <StorageAccountName>: Unknown error when mounting Azure Files SMB share
rootCause: Linux client unable to communicate with Azure Files server due to DNS resolution failure, SMB version incompatibility (must support SMB 2.1+), or network connectivity issues (TCP port 445 blocked)
solution: 1) Verify Linux and CIFS client support SMB 2.1+. 2) Ensure VM is in same region as file share (SMB 2.1 requires same datacenter). 3) Check mount command syntax. 4) Verify DNS resolution: ping the Files URL, use telnet to test TCP 445 connectivity, check iptables rules for outbound port 445 blocking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 115 Linux_Storage

symptom: Linux mount error(13): Permission denied when mounting Azure Files SMB share
rootCause: Wrong storage account username or password used in mount command
solution: Verify the username (must be the storage account name) and password (must be a valid storage account access key). Example: sudo mount -t cifs //<StorageAccountName>.file.core.windows.net/<ShareName> <mount-point> -o vers=3.0,user=<StorageAccountName>,password=<StorageAccountKey>,dir_mode=0777,file_mode=0777,serverino
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 13 Linux VM_Storage

symptom: Linux CIFS mount returns STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE (error -13) on existing Azure Files SMB mounts. dmesg shows "Send error in SessSetup = -13" and STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE. Resolved IP in /proc/mounts differs from current nslookup result.
rootCause: Storage account migrated to a different storage cluster (load balancing or customer-initiated failover). DNS updated to new cluster IP, but Linux SMB kernel client retains stale IP in kernel DNS cache and keeps communicating with the old cluster.
solution: Clear kernel DNS resolver cache: 1) grep .dns_resolver /proc/keys to get key ID. 2) sudo keyctl clear $((16#<key_id>)). 3) Verify cache is empty. 4) Unmount and remount the share. For permanent fix: upgrade Linux distro to version with storage account migration support. Check Xportal Account > Basic Info for FinalizeMigrateCompleteTime to confirm migration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 13 Linux VM_Storage

symptom: Linux mount error(2): mount(2) error system call failed: No route to host when mounting Azure Files SMB share using mount -t cifs
rootCause: cifs-utils package (and its dependency keyutils) not installed on Linux. These packages are prerequisites for SMB/CIFS mounts but are not installed by default.
solution: Install cifs-utils using the distro package manager: Ubuntu: sudo apt install cifs-utils | RHEL 8+: sudo dnf install cifs-utils | Older RHEL: sudo yum install cifs-utils | SLES: sudo zypper install cifs-utils. Update repos first to get latest version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 2 Linux_Storage

symptom: System Error 1326 when mounting AD-joined Azure file share using Storage Account Access Keys on Windows
rootCause: NTLM authentication is disabled on the AD-joined Storage Account (only Kerberos allowed). Access key authentication requires NTLM, so it fails when NTLM is not in FileSmbAuthenticationMethods.
solution: Enable NTLMv2 authentication: Update-AzStorageFileServiceProperty -ResourceGroupName <RG> -StorageAccountName <SA> -SmbAuthenticationMethod Kerberos,NTLMv2. Can also be changed from Azure Portal under file share settings. Verify via Xportal FileSmbAuthenticationMethods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error System Error 1326 with Access Keys_Storage

symptom: Net Use or New-SmbMapping succeeds but no drive letter appears in Windows File Explorer / My Computer
rootCause: Windows File Explorer does not run as administrator by default. Network drives mapped from an administrative command prompt are not visible to the non-elevated user session because mapped drives are user-centric.
solution: Option 1: Mount the share from a non-administrator command line. Option 2: Configure EnableLinkedConnections registry value at HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System, create DWORD EnableLinkedConnections=1, restart computer. This shares network connections between filtered and full admin access tokens.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Succeeds Drive Letter Does Not Show_Storage

symptom: The network path was not found (error 0x80070035) when mounting Azure file share on Windows, even though nslookup and Test-NetConnection to port 445 both succeed. No DNS resolution packets in network capture. Windows OS is lower than Server 2022.
rootCause: TCP/IP NetBIOS Helper (lmhosts) service is disabled. Windows 2019 and older depend on this service for Kernel Name Resolution (KNR) used by SMB Client. On Server 2022+ this dependency moved to DNS Client service.
solution: Enable and start the TCP/IP NetBIOS Helper (lmhosts) service. Note: disabling lmhosts does not disconnect existing SMB connections; it only impacts new mount attempts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount error Network path was not found on Windows

symptom: Error: Multiple connections to a server or shared resource by the same user, using more than one user name, are not allowed when accessing mounted Azure file share drive on Windows
rootCause: Windows does not allow multiple connections to the same server with different credentials from the same user session.
solution: Use cmdkey to store credentials with AZURE domain prefix: cmdkey /add:<StorageAccount>.file.core.windows.net /user:AZURE\<StorageAccount> /pass:<Key>. Then map drive with /savecred and no username: net use K: \\<StorageAccount>.file.core.windows.net\sharename /persistent:yes /savecred
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Multiple Connections to a Server_Storage

symptom: Azure Files NFS shares become inaccessible after VM reboot on RHEL. Mounts time out or hang with I/O errors. Kernel log shows: NFS: nfs4_reclaim_open_state: unhandled error -10019, NFSv4: state recovery failed for open file.
rootCause: Storage account migration between clusters caused redirection mechanism failure. Normally Azure Files traffic is redirected seamlessly after DNS updates, but the redirection failed after the designated period, causing requests to be unprocessed until DNS propagation completed.
solution: Confirm storage migration via Xportal (Account > Basic Info: FinalizeMigrateCompleteTime, MigrationLastPrimaryStamp), Jarvis, or ASI (Storage Tools > Storage Account > Properties: migrationDestinationStamp, LlamState). To prevent recurrence, open ICM to add account to do-not-migrate list. For permanent fix, wait for platform resolution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Network File Systems (NFS)/Cannot access or mount NFS nfs4 reclaim open state error=10019_Storage

symptom: After rebooting RHEL VM, df -h shows incorrect/same storage account name for all Azure Files NFS shares instead of the correct individual storage account names configured in /etc/fstab.
rootCause: NFS default sharecache behavior: without nosharecache mount option, a single cache is used for all mount points accessing the same export. The mount options from the first mount take effect for subsequent concurrent mounts, causing all shares to appear under the same storage account name.
solution: Edit /etc/fstab and add "nosharecache" option to each NFS mount entry. Example: <sa>.file.core.windows.net:/<sa>/<share> /mountpoint nfs defaults,nosharecache 0 0. Note: nosharecache is legacy caching behavior and maintains separate share caches for each mount.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Network File Systems (NFS)/Incorrect storage account names in df h output for Azure files NFS shares mounted on RHEL after reboot_Storage

symptom: Azure Files mount error(115) "Operation now in progress" or "could not resolve address: Unknown error" on Linux when mounting SMB share
rootCause: Linux client cannot communicate with Azure Files server due to DNS resolution failure, port 445 blocked by iptables/NSG, SMB version mismatch, or VM not in same region as storage account
solution: 1) Verify Linux/CIFS client supports SMB 2.1+; 2) Ensure VM is in same region as share (SMB 2.1 requires same datacenter); 3) Verify mount command syntax; 4) Check DNS resolution (ping/nslookup); 5) Test TCP port 445 connectivity (telnet); 6) Check iptables rules and NSG for port 445 blocking
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 115 Linux_Storage

symptom: Azure Files mount error(13) "Permission denied" on Linux when mounting SMB share
rootCause: Wrong storage account username or password used in mount command
solution: Verify username is the storage account name and password is the correct storage account key. Example: sudo mount -t cifs //StorageAccountName.file.core.windows.net/ShareName /mnt -o vers=3.0,user=StorageAccountName,password=StorageAccountKey
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 13 Linux VM_Storage

symptom: Azure Files STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE / Send error in SessSetup = -13 on Linux after storage account migration or failover, existing mounts fail with 0xc000006d
rootCause: Linux kernel DNS resolver bug: after storage account cluster migration or geo-failover, kernel DNS cache retains old IP address and keeps sending traffic to old cluster. Regressing commit: 39299bdd2546. Confirmed via mismatched IPs in /proc/mounts vs nslookup.
solution: Workaround: 1) Clear kernel DNS resolver cache: sudo keyctl clear $((16#$(grep .dns_resolver /proc/keys | cut -f1 -d" "))); 2) Unmount and remount share. Permanent fix: Upgrade to Linux kernel with fix (commit 9da27fb65a14). For AKS: delete pod to trigger SMB remount. Alternative: Enable private endpoint for static IP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 13 Linux VM_Storage

symptom: Azure Files mount error(13) Permission denied on Linux when storage account uses CMK with Managed Service Identity
rootCause: Managed Service Identity (MSI) is no longer working correctly for the storage account using CMK encryption. Symptom: modifying storage account networking config complains MSI not found.
solution: Step 1: Renew MSI - Set-AzStorageAccount -IdentityType None then Set-AzStorageAccount -IdentityType SystemAssigned. Step 2: Reauthorize new MSI to Key Vault - Set-AzKeyVaultAccessPolicy with wrapkey,unwrapkey,get permissions for the new PrincipalId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 13 Linux VM_Storage

symptom: Azure Files mount error(2) "No route to host" on Linux when mounting SMB share with -t cifs
rootCause: cifs-utils package (and its dependency keyutils) not installed on the Linux VM. These are prerequisites for CIFS/SMB mounts and are not installed by default.
solution: Install cifs-utils using the distro package manager: Ubuntu: sudo apt install cifs-utils; RHEL 8+: sudo dnf install cifs-utils; Older RHEL: sudo yum install cifs-utils; SLES: sudo zypper install cifs-utils. Update repos first for latest version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Error 2 Linux_Storage

symptom: Net Use or New-SmbMapping succeeds but mapped drive letter does not appear in Windows File Explorer / My Computer
rootCause: Drive mapped from an elevated (administrator) command prompt. Windows mapped drives are user-centric; drives mounted under admin token are not visible to the standard user session in File Explorer due to UAC token separation.
solution: Resolution 1: Mount the share from a non-administrator command line. Resolution 2: Configure EnableLinkedConnections registry value at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System (DWORD=1) and restart.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount Succeeds Drive Letter Does Not Show_Storage

symptom: "The network path was not found" (error 0x80070035) when mounting Azure File share on Windows, despite nslookup and Test-NetConnection succeeding on port 445
rootCause: TCP/IP NetBIOS Helper (lmhosts) service is disabled. Windows Server 2019 and older depend on this service for Kernel Name Resolution (KNR) used by SMB client. No DNS resolution packet seen in network trace. Windows Server 2022+ moved this to DNS Client.
solution: Enable and start the TCP/IP NetBIOS Helper (lmhosts) service. Note: Disabling lmhosts does not disconnect existing SMB connections, only impacts new mount attempts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Mount error Network path was not found on Windows

symptom: "Multiple connections to a server or shared resource by the same user, using more than one user name, are not allowed" when accessing mounted Azure File share on Windows
rootCause: Windows prevents multiple concurrent connections to the same server with different user credentials. This occurs when trying to mount multiple shares from same storage account with different credentials.
solution: Use cmdkey to add credentials with AZURE\ domain prefix: cmdkey /add:StorageAccount.file.core.windows.net /user:AZURE\StorageAccount /pass:Key. Then map drive with /savecred without specifying username: net use K: \\StorageAccount.file.core.windows.net\share /persistent:yes /savecred
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Multiple Connections to a Server_Storage

symptom: Azure Files NFS shares become inaccessible on RHEL after VM reboot, kernel logs show "nfs4_reclaim_open_state: unhandled error -10019" and "NFSv4: state recovery failed for open file"
rootCause: Storage account live migration between clusters caused redirection mechanism failure. After DNS updates, internal networking did not consume changes promptly, causing requests to fail until DNS propagation completed. Confirm via Xportal FinalizeMigrateCompleteTime, Jarvis stamp change, or ASI migrationDestinationStamp/LlamState.
solution: Check for storage account migration via Xportal/Jarvis/ASI. To prevent recurrence, open ICM to add account to do-not-migrate list. For immediate mitigation, unmount and remount NFS shares after DNS propagation. Customer may proceed with LRS-to-ZRS conversions unless issue recurs frequently.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Network File Systems (NFS)/Cannot access or mount NFS nfs4 reclaim open state error=10019_Storage

symptom: After VM reboot on RHEL, df -h shows incorrect (same) storage account name for all NFS-mounted Azure File shares, despite /etc/fstab having correct distinct storage account entries
rootCause: NFS sharecache sharing: without nosharecache mount option, all mounts to the same export share a single cache. The first mount options and identity take effect for subsequent concurrent mounts, causing df -h to display the first storage account name for all.
solution: Edit /etc/fstab and add nosharecache option to each NFS mount entry (e.g., "nfs defaults,nosharecache 0 0"). Note: nosharecache creates separate cache per mount, which is legacy behavior and may have data consistency implications with local updates.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Network File Systems (NFS)/Incorrect storage account names in df h output for Azure files NFS shares mounted on RHEL after reboot_Storage

symptom: Users unable to access Azure Files shares configured for AD DS authentication from machines where Microsoft Entra Kerberos is enabled. Client retrieves Kerberos ticket from Entra instead of on-prem AD KDC, causing authentication failure.
rootCause: Client machine is configured to use Cloud Kerberos (Microsoft Entra Kerberos) and requests Kerberos TGS ticket from Microsoft Entra ID instead of on-premises domain controllers. Since storage account is joined to on-prem AD DS, it requires tickets issued by on-prem KDC.
solution: Create Realm-To-Host mapping for each storage account joined to AD DS. Options: (1) Intune Policy CSP: configure Kerberos/HostToRealm; (2) Group Policy: Administrative Templates > System > Kerberos > Define host name-to-Kerberos realm mappings; (3) Registry/ksetup: ksetup /addhosttorealmmap <storageaccount>.file.core.windows.net <REALMNAME>. Verify via: reg query HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Kerberos\Domain_realm
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/How Tos/Coexistence feature enablement with on prem AD_Storage

symptom: Error 'resource was disallowed by policy' when running Set-AzStorageAccount to enable SMB OAuth for Azure Files managed identity authentication.
rootCause: Azure Policy on subscription blocking Set-AzStorageAccount operation.
solution: Retry with -AllowBlobPublicAccess $false flag: Set-AzStorageAccount -ResourceGroupName <rg> -Name <name> -EnableSmbOAuth $true -AllowBlobPublicAccess $false
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/How Tos/Azure Files Managed Identity Support for SMB HowTo

symptom: SuSE NFS client VMs with newer kernels (e.g. 4.4.162-94.72) cannot connect to NFS server running older kernel 4.4.138-94.39, while clients with same older kernel work fine
rootCause: Unknown bug in older SuSE kernel 4.4.138-94.39 on NFS server causes connectivity failures with newer-kernel clients
solution: Upgrade kernel on NFS server: sudo zypper update kernel-default. Ensure backup/snapshot before upgrading.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Network File Systems (NFS)/NFS Not Working SuSE_Storage

symptom: Error NfsFileShare is not supported for the account when creating NFS file shares in newly created storage account after NFS feature registration
rootCause: Storage account created right after NFS feature enablement may land on stamp where NFS not enabled (race condition)
solution: Verify stamp via nslookup. If not NFS-supported, recreate storage account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Network File Systems (NFS)/NfsFileShares is Not Supported for the Account_Storage

symptom: Error The option ID == is unknown when mapping Azure File Share with net use, storage account key contains forward slash
rootCause: cmd.exe interprets forward slash in storage account key as command line option switch
solution: Use PowerShell New-SmbMapping instead of cmd net use to map the drive.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Option is Unknown Using Command Net Use_Storage

symptom: Opening Excel files from mounted Azure File Share or UNC path is significantly slower than locally
rootCause: Multiple causes: network latency, xlsx format overhead, Excel unsupported ops, add-ons timeout, linked sheets auth delays, Protected View extra traffic
solution: Use XLS/XLSB, disable Protected View, add storage FQDN to Trusted Sites, verify port 80, avoid linked sheets
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Slow Open Office Excel Files_Storage

symptom: Azure File Share access fails with Resource temporarily unavailable or mount error(11). STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES 0xc000009a in logs
rootCause: Client app opens excessive file handles without releasing, exceeding 256000 max per SMB session
solution: Temp: reboot VM. Permanent: adjust app to release idle handles. Diagnose: ASC File Handle, Get-AzStorageFileHandle, Jarvis XSMBPerfMetric.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Status Insufficient Resources_Storage

symptom: System Error 5 Access Denied on Windows or mount error(13) Permission denied on Linux when mounting Azure File Share
rootCause: Cross-region: client lacks SMB 3.0 encryption (Win2008R2=SMB2.1, Linux<4.11). Same-region: Secure transfer required enabled with unencrypted SMB.
solution: Cross-region: use Win Server 2012+ or Linux kernel 4.11+. Same-region: disable Secure transfer required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/System Error 5 Access Denied Mount Command_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server registration using Register-AzStorageSyncServer fails with error 0x8000FFFF (E_UNEXPECTED) No system-assigned Managed Identity was found for this resource
rootCause: After upgrading AFS agent from v17.x to v18.x, the ServerType registry value under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync is set to an unexpected value because the v17.x server was unregistered prior to the upgrade attempt
solution: Delete the ServerType registry value: reg delete HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync /v ServerType /f, then restart FileSyncSvc (net stop filesyncsvc && net start filesyncsvc), and retry server registration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/AFS 0x8000FFFF no system assigned Managed Identity was found for this resource_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync v16 agent enters Low Disk Space mode and aggressively tiers files even though disk is not actually low on space, especially on large volumes
rootCause: Regression in v16 agent: low disk mode uses percentage of volume free space without considering actual volume size, causing unnecessary tiering on large volumes (e.g. 5% of 4TB = 200GB threshold). Bug was fixed in v15.1 but regressed in v16
solution: Set registry value to disable low disk space mode: reg add HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync /v LowDiskModeFreeSpacePercentThreshold /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f. Upgrade to v17 when available for permanent fix
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/Azure File Sync v16 agent bug low disk space mode_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent causes high CPU usage up to 100% due to repeatedly spawning MonAgentLauncher.exe and AzureStorageSyncMonitor.exe processes without terminating previous instances, making server unstable or unresponsive
rootCause: Bug in AFS agent fails to properly shut down previously running MonAgentLauncher.exe and AzureStorageSyncMonitor.exe when other MonAgentLauncher.exe processes from Azure Monitor or Log Analytics are running on the server
solution: Kill AFS-owned MonAgentLauncher.exe processes using PowerShell (Get-Process MonAgentLauncher, check path against InstallDir, Stop-Process), then restart FileSyncSvc service. Permanent fix in agent v18.0
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/Emerging Issue Azure File Sync Agent MonAgentLauncher.exe and AzureStorageSyncMonitor.exe_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync Agent v17 server registration using ServerRegistration.exe fails with Failed to register the server and ManagementCode NoRegisteredProviderFound for API version 2022-09-01
rootCause: Regression in v17 agent: API version 2022-09-01 was not registered/enabled in all regions for storageSyncServices/registeredServers resource type
solution: Use PowerShell cmdlets as workaround: Connect-AzAccount -Subscription guid -Tenant guid, then Register-AzStorageSyncServer -ResourceGroupName rg -StorageSyncServiceName name. Service-side fix deployed by 3/9/2024
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/Emerging Issue Azure File Sync Agent v17 Server Registration using ServerRegistration.exe fails_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync Agent v15.1 FileSyncSvc consumes excessive memory and crashes periodically after upgrade; recalls fail with ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE; telemetry shows event ids 9091 9092 9093 9094
rootCause: Deep GC feature in v15.1 uses ESE database with long-running transactions; when commit fails, rollback of extended transaction exposes ESE bug causing excessive memory consumption; service restart reloads database and repeats the rollback crash loop
solution: 1) Stop FileSyncSvc 2) Use PSExec to access System Volume Information\HFS\HeatStore\DB 3) Robocopy empty folder to clear HeatStore DB and DeepGC dirs 4) Set registry to postpone DeepGC (MinDaysToRunStableVersionDeepGc=600, MaxDaysToRunStableVersionDeepGc=1000) 5) Restart FileSyncSvc 6) Upgrade to v15.2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/Emerging Issue File Sync Agent v15.1_Storage

symptom: Azure Files SMB mount fails with managed identity - smbOAuthSettings.isSmbOAuthEnabled not set to true on storage account
rootCause: Managed Identity for SMB feature is not enabled on the Storage account; smbOAuthSettings.isSmbOAuthEnabled property is false
solution: Enable Managed Identity for SMB on the Storage account. Verify via Xportal that smbOAuthSettings.isSmbOAuthEnabled is set to true. Install AzFilesSMBMIClient PowerShell module and run AzFilesSMBMIClient.exe refresh to retrieve OAuth token
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/TSGs/Azure File Managed Identity Support for SMB TSG

symptom: AzFilesSMBMIClient.exe refresh command fails when retrieving OAuth token for managed identity SMB mount
rootCause: Storage Account network ACLs do not allow network connectivity from the client VM; firewall rules or private endpoint misconfiguration
solution: Ensure appropriate firewall rules are configured on the storage account to allow connectivity from the client VM. If using private endpoints, verify private endpoint connectivity from client to storage account
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/TSGs/Azure File Managed Identity Support for SMB TSG

symptom: Missing Kerberos Service Ticket when mounting Azure Files SMB with Entra-Only Kerberos; klist shows no service ticket for storage account
rootCause: Admin Consent not granted on Entra application for Azure Files SMB authentication, or client setting for fetching Kerberos tickets not enabled
solution: 1) Grant Admin Consent on the Entra app registration. 2) Enable client setting to fetch Kerberos tickets via registry or Intune policy. 3) Verify with klist that TGT and Service Ticket for storage account exist, issued by microsoftonline.com
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/TSGs/MS Auth Entra Only Kerberos/Azure Files SMB Authentication with Entra Only Kerberos TSG_Storage

symptom: Access Denied when mounting Azure Files SMB with Entra-Only Kerberos cloud-only identities
rootCause: Default Share Permissions not configured on the storage account; RBAC is not supported for cloud-only identities in non-canary regions
solution: Configure Default Share Permissions on the storage account. In non-canary regions, use Default Share Permission + file-level ACLs instead of RBAC for cloud-only identities
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/TSGs/MS Auth Entra Only Kerberos/Azure Files SMB Authentication with Entra Only Kerberos TSG_Storage

symptom: Cannot use cloud-only Entra groups for file-level permissions on Azure Files SMB share
