rootCause: Application Tag for cloud-only SIDs preview not enabled in Entra app manifest
solution: Enable the cloud-only SIDs preview tag in the Entra application manifest file. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/files/storage-files-identity-auth-hybrid-identities-enable
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/TSGs/MS Auth Entra Only Kerberos/Azure Files SMB Authentication with Entra Only Kerberos TSG_Storage

symptom: Azure Files SMB session disconnects after 12 hours when using Entra-Only Kerberos authentication
rootCause: Windows session limitation with Kerberos token expiration; known issue with Entra-Only Kerberos preview
solution: Lock/unlock or reboot the session to re-establish the Kerberos ticket. A Windows change is planned (Scenario 49475334)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Identity/TSGs/MS Auth Entra Only Kerberos/Azure Files SMB Authentication with Entra Only Kerberos TSG_Storage

symptom: FileSyncSvc fails to start with error 0x80070057 (ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER) after upgrading Azure File Sync agent to v17.3+ or v18.1+. Server telemetry event 19048 shows MitigationRedirectionPolicy failure with HResult -2147024809. Register-AzStorageSyncServer and ServerRegistration.exe may also fail.
rootCause: AFS introduced SetProcessMitigationPolicy call for PROCESS_MITIGATION_REDIRECTION_TRUST_POLICY which requires specific OS updates (KB5021653 for 2012R2, cumulative updates for 2016/2019). Without these updates, the API call fails with ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER.
solution: Install required OS updates: Windows Server 2012 R2 (KB5021653), 2016/2019 (latest cumulative update), 2022 (no update needed). Temporary workaround: reg add HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync /v MitigationRedirectionPolicy /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f, then restart FileSyncSvc.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/Emerging Issue FileSyncSvc failed to start_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync tiered file recall fails with error 0x80072f8f (WININET_E_DECODING_FAILED). Customer cannot access tiered files.
rootCause: Proxy/firewall/gateway blocking or modifying responses from AFS service. In Sep 2023, certificate revocation server outage also caused widespread occurrences (IcM 421550806).
solution: Ensure firewall/proxy allows Microsoft PKI URLs (www.microsoft.com/pki/mscorp/cps, crl.microsoft.com, ocsp.msocsp.com, ocsp.digicert.com). If no proxy: set registry SkipSslCertificateRevocationCheck=1 and SkipVerifyingPinnedRootCertificate=1 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync, then restart FileSyncSvc.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/Emerging Issue TSG AFS WININET E DECODING FAILED_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync ACL regression affecting servers created Sep 30-Dec 5, 2024 or Feb 5-Mar 19, 2025. Portal notification about ACL regression. Keywords: permissions, ACL, ICM 611929002.
rootCause: Security issue in Azure File Sync causing ACL mismatches on affected servers during specific creation timeframes.
solution: Use Microsoft-provided PowerShell script (CompareDatasets.ps1) and user guide to restore ACLs. Escalate to PG engineering team. PG has deployed fix for majority of affected customers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/File Sync Access Control Regression_Storage

symptom: After upgrading Azure File Sync agent from v16 to v17, the Bytes Synced metric in Azure portal overreports data synced (e.g., 4GB actual shows as 22GB).
rootCause: Bug in Azure File Sync v17 release causing bytes synced metric to overreport.
solution: No customer-side workaround. PG working on service-side fix (IcM 477088437). Metric is cosmetic only; actual sync not affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/Emerging Issues/File Sync Agent v17 bytes synced metric unexpected increase bug_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync error ECS_E_SYNC_METADATA_KNOWLEDGE_LIMIT_REACHED (HRESULT 0x80c8021c / -2134375908). Sync fails with this error and the public doc says to open a support case.
rootCause: The metadata database for Azure File Sync objects is full. Once this database fills up, no new entries can be inserted, causing sync to fail.
solution: 1. Check Per-Item Errors in ASC for the Sync Service. 2. Resolve per-item errors (see MS Learn troubleshooting per-file/directory sync errors). 3. If unresolved, escalate to Engineering via ICM: confirm server endpoint has authoritative data, collect AFSDiag and FileSyncErrorsReport via Debug-StorageSyncServer cmdlet. Related soft-limit error: ECS_E_SYNC_METADATA_KNOWLEDGE_SOFT_LIMIT_REACHED (0x80c8023b).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS 2134375908 ECS_E_SYNC_METADATA_KNOWLEDGE_LIMIT_REACHED_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent installation hangs at starting FileSyncSvc service. Error 1920: Service Storage Sync Agent (FileSyncSvc) failed to start. Verify that you have sufficient privileges to start system services.
rootCause: FileSyncSvc service fails to start during agent installation or re-installation due to insufficient privileges, a stuck service from previous installation, or corrupted service state.
solution: 1. Run ProcMon while installation is running; when installation hangs, capture ProcMon output and retry. 2. Collect FileSyncSvc dump: run "sc query filesyncsvc" from command prompt to check service state. 3. If service is stuck, collect the service dump before retry. 4. Reinstall after cleaning up stuck service state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Agent Hung_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server registration fails with certificate error CRYPT_E_NOT_FOUND (0x80092004) or ECS_E_SERVER_INVALID_OR_EXPIRED_CERTIFICATE (0x80C80050). Certificate is not present, deleted, or expired.
rootCause: The server certificate required for AFS registration is missing (deleted) or has expired, preventing the server from registering with the Storage Sync Service.
solution: Run in admin PowerShell: 1. Remove-ItemProperty HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync\ServerSetting -Name ServerSetting.Server=Localhost -Force. 2. Import StorageSync modules from C:\Program Files\Azure\StorageSyncAgent\. 3. Reset-StorageSyncServer to reset the serverID. 4. Retry server registration with verbose flag.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Certificate Related Errors_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync sync/recall fails with ECS_E_AUTH_SRV_CERT_EXPIRED or ECS_E_AUTH_SRV_CERT_NOT_FOUND on a registered server with active server endpoints.
rootCause: Server certificate has expired on a previously registered and provisioned server, causing authentication failures for sync and recall operations.
solution: Run: 1. Import-Module "C:\Program Files\Azure\StorageSyncAgent\StorageSync.Management.PowerShell.Cmdlets.dll". 2. Reset-AzStorageSyncServerCertificate -ResourceGroupName <rg> -StorageSyncServiceName <sss>. Sync/Recall/Management should recover after certificate rollover.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Certificate Related Errors_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync re-registration fails with generic error MgmtServerJobExpired (0x80C87013). FileSync-Management event logs show Keyset does not exist (NTE_BAD_KEYSET 0x80090016). Typically occurs when server was registered before or machine is a clone.
rootCause: Server was previously registered or is a clone of a registered machine. The keyset for the original certificate no longer exists, preventing re-registration.
solution: Same recovery as expired cert: 1. Import StorageSync PowerShell module. 2. Reset-AzStorageSyncServerCertificate -ResourceGroupName <rg> -StorageSyncServiceName <sss>. Management should recover after certificate rollover.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Certificate Related Errors_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync cloud endpoint creation fails with MgmtFileShareAuthorizationFailed / ECS_E_MGMT_FILE_LOCKS_OPERATION_ERROR (0x80c8707b). Error: Server failed to authenticate the request including the Authorization header signature.
rootCause: Storage Access key mismatch between client and server side. The AFS control plane failed to recognize the need to refresh storage account keys after key rotation.
solution: 1. Verify storage account and Azure file share exist. 2. Confirm AFS has access to storage account. 3. Check firewall/VNet settings. 4. Validate RBAC permissions. 5. Ensure Microsoft.StorageSync has Reader and Data Access role. 6. Check key rotation timestamps in ASC/Activity logs. 7. If key rotation is recent and keys are out of sync, escalate via ICM to XSync/Kailani team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS ECS E MGMT FILE LOCKS OPERATION ERROR_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync fails to download with ECS_E_NOT_ENOUGH_LOCAL_STORAGE (0x80c8031a) or JET_errLogDiskFull (0x8e5e0211). Error: This path does not have enough free space to sync.
rootCause: Volume has insufficient disk space, NTFS Quota has been reached, or File Server Resource Manager (FSRM) Quota has been reached on the configured directory.
solution: 1. Check volume size via Disk Management/fsutil. 2. Check NTFS Quota via fsutil quota query. 3. Check FSRM Quota Management. 4. If volume full: increase disk size or free up space. 5. If quota met: increase quota. Use Debug-StorageSyncServer -FileSyncErrorsReport to identify failing files.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS ECS_E_NOT_ENOUGH_LOCAL_STORAGE Not Enough Space to Sync_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync gaps for prolonged periods. ECS_E_SERVER_CREDENTIAL_NEEDED (0x80c80300) in Telemetry EventId 9450 and ECS_E_MGMT_ACCESS_DENIED (0x80c8700c) in Event 4104.
rootCause: Local server time is out of sync, causing authentication failures with the AFS management endpoint.
solution: Set the local server time correctly. Verify NTP synchronization is working.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS ECS_E_SERVER_CREDENTIAL_NEEDED_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync initial upload experiences long delays. Error: ECS_E_SYNC_CANCELLED_BY_VSS (-2134375780) - sync session cancelled by volume snapshot sync session.
rootCause: VSS sync scheduled task runs daily and cancels the ongoing initial upload sync session.
solution: Disable the VSS sync scheduled task until initial upload is completed. Re-enable it once initial upload finishes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS ECS_E_SYNC_CANCELLED_BY_VSS_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync cloud endpoint creation fails with MgmtForbidden - Failed to provision a replica group. Storage Account Firewall public network access is disabled.
rootCause: Storage Account Firewall public network access is set to Disabled, blocking AFS access to the storage account.
solution: Set Storage Account Firewall to Enabled from all networks, or Enabled from selected VNets/IPs with Allow Azure services on the trusted services list checkbox enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS MgmtForbidden Failed to provision a replica group_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync cloud endpoint provisioning fails with MgmtStorageAccountInaccessible when storage account was created using Bicep template with publicNetworkAccess set to Disabled.
rootCause: Bicep template omits networkAcls parameter. Default networkAcls has defaultAction=Allow and bypass=None. When publicNetworkAccess=Disabled, AFS checks networkAcls to determine connection method and incorrectly attempts public IP connection.
solution: Add networkAcls to Bicep template: networkAcls: { bypass: AzureServices, defaultAction: Deny (when publicNetworkAccess=Disabled), ipRules: [], virtualNetworkRules: [] }
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS MgmtStorageAccountInaccessible_Storage

symptom: File in use error when trying to delete tiered files in Azure File Sync. Error occurs for all tiered files with no open file handles found.
rootCause: During initial sync, Stable Version Promotion and Fast DR steps block moving tiered files out of the sync namespace by design. File deletion (move to recycle bin) is also blocked.
solution: 1. Wait for Stable Version Promotion to finish (check EventID 9118 TotalRemainingFiles count, EventID 9117 when done). 2. Workaround: use Shift+Delete (permanent delete) or recall the file first then delete normally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Unable to Delete Tiered File_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync Server Registration UI shows Automatic discovery of your accessible cloud environments did not work. Test-StorageSyncNetworkConnectivity fails. Error: FileNotFoundException for System.Net.Http Version=4.2.0.0.
rootCause: Missing .NET assembly System.Net.Http (Version=4.2.0.0) due to missing Windows updates or incomplete/corrupted AFS agent installation.
solution: 1. Verify pre-requisites per AFS deployment guide. 2. Install required Windows updates: WS2012R2 KB5021653, WS2016 KB5040562, WS2019 KB5005112+KB5040430. 3. If still failing: uninstall AFS agent, reboot, reinstall latest version. 4. Review proxy/firewall/DNS settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Unable to Register Azure File Sync Server Endpoint_Storage

symptom: Error 0x800705AA (Insufficient system resources exist to complete the requested service) when renaming files on Azure File Sync server endpoint drives.
rootCause: Bug in AFS agent version 20.0.0.0 where renames fail when filesyncsvc is not connected.
solution: Upgrade AFS agent to version 21.2 or later from Microsoft Update Catalog. Collect AFSDiag logs if issue persists after upgrade, then escalate via ICM to XSync/Kailani Frontline DRI team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Unable to rename a file 0x800705AA_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync upload operations fail with error code -2147024891 (Access Denied). Download operations work fine. Resource Explorer in ASC shows AgentAccessDenied.
rootCause: Permissions on the System Volume Information (SVI) folder are incorrect; NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM is missing Full Control permission. Extra inherited permissions (e.g. Everyone with Read-only) on SVI can also cause access denied.
solution: Grant SYSTEM full control: run PsExec.exe -s -I -d cmd, then cacls "<drive>:\system volume information" /T /E /G "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM:F". If access denied, first take ownership with takeown /A /R /F. Remove inherited permissions with icacls "<vol:>\System Volume Information" /inheritance:r /T /C.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFS Uploading Failed with Access Denied_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server endpoint health status shows 'No Activity' in portal, while Registered Servers shows server as Online.
rootCause: Server endpoint has not logged sync activity in past two hours. Possible causes: max concurrent sync sessions reached (2 per processor, max 8 per server), or an active VSS sync session (SnapshotSync) is blocking other sync sessions.
solution: Check current sync activity per docs. If max concurrent sessions reached, wait for sessions to complete. If VSS sync session active, wait for it to finish. Use event monitoring to track progress of current sync session.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFSS Sync Issues_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync sessions fail with ERROR_CANCELLED (E_CANCELLED). Sync sessions are repeatedly cancelled.
rootCause: Sync sessions cancelled due to VSS snapshots (SnapshotSync), server restarts, or other transient conditions. If previous SyncScenario was SnapshotSync, the cancellation is expected behavior.
solution: Check if sync still failing after couple hours. If previous SyncScenario was SnapshotSync, this is transient - ignore. Query event ID 9302 to check AppliedFileCount and AppliedSizeBytes. If numbers increasing, progress is being made. If not increasing, escalate for deeper investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFSS Sync Issues_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync fails with JET_errWriteConflict (HEX 0x8e5e044e / DEC -1906441138) during sync operations.
rootCause: Database corruption in the Azure File Sync metadata database.
solution: If the customer continually hits this error, escalate to engineering (PG). This is not a customer-remediable issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/AFSS Sync Issues_Storage

symptom: System Error 5 'Access Denied' or 'mount error(13): Permission denied' when trying to mount or map Azure file share.
rootCause: Client OS only supports SMB 2.1 (not SMB 3.0) and is connecting across regions, or Azure File share has 'Secure transfer required' enabled which forces SMB 3.0. Also occurs with forced tunneling where VM traffic routes through customer edge router/proxy.
solution: Use Windows Server 2012+ or Windows 8+ for SMB 3.0 support. Alternatively disable 'Secure transfer required' on storage account. For forced tunneling, run Azure Files connectivity troubleshooter to verify public IP and network path.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Access Denied During Mount or Map of AFS_Storage_TSG

symptom: AzureFileSyncAgentExtension installation fails on Arc-enabled server. Extension deployment failure visible in Azure portal.
rootCause: Extension deployment failure due to Arc platform issues (payload download/auth/signature), AFS prerequisites not met (unsupported OS, pending reboot, AFS version < v22), or missing certificates.
solution: Navigate to Arc Server > Extensions > AzureFileSyncAgentExtension > View Details to see error. Save error details before uninstalling (deleted on uninstall). Uninstall, remediate, then reinstall.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Azure Arc Extension/Azure Arc Extension Troubleshooting_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync Arc extension shows succeeded in portal, but Storage Sync Service shows Server Offline. No heartbeat detected after extension install.
rootCause: Outbound access to Storage Sync service endpoints blocked by firewall, local firewall blocking agent egress, or AFS service crashes (faulting module logged in event logs).
solution: Open outbound port 443 and required URLs for Azure region. Create explicit allow rules for agent executables in Windows Defender Firewall. Check event logs for crashes. Update to latest agent via extension update and verify after reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Azure Arc Extension/Azure Arc Extension Troubleshooting_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync Agent update fails with 'Azure File Sync Agent update failed. Continuing with the Older Version'. Uninstall attempts fail with 'path not found' or 'file already exists'.
rootCause: Cached installer file is missing or not found in the MSI database on the server, preventing upgrade or uninstall.
solution: Download latest MSI from Microsoft Update Catalog. Run elevated: msiexec.exe /i <msi> REINSTALLMODE=vamus /l*v Repair.log. REINSTALLMODE=vamus reinstalls missing/older files (v), forces all files reinstall (a), rewrites registry (m,u), reinstalls shortcuts (s). Reboot if prompted. If .MSP package, execute directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Azure File Sync Agent Upgrade Issue_Storage

symptom: Cannot register Windows Server with Storage Sync Service. Security alert: 'Revocation information for the security certificate for this site is not available'. Observed on Windows Server 2012, Agent Version 17.3.
rootCause: CRYPT_E_NO_REVOCATION_DLL - registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\OID\EncodingType 1\CertDllVerifyRevocation\DEFAULT missing Dll value pointing to cryptnet.dll.
solution: Open Registry Editor. Navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\OID\EncodingType 1\CertDllVerifyRevocation\DEFAULT. Check/create Dll value (Type REG_MULTI_SZ, value C:\Windows\System32\cryptnet.dll).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Azure File Sync addresses certificate revocation issues_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync shows ECS_E_SYNC_CONSTRAINT_CONFLICT and ECS_E_SYNC_FILE_IN_USE upload errors in portal. Constraint conflict errors indicate dependent files failing.
rootCause: ECS_E_SYNC_CONSTRAINT_CONFLICT occurs when another error (FILE_IN_USE, INVALID_NAME, FILE_NOT_FOUND) causes dependent folder/files to fail. Constraint conflict is a secondary error.
solution: Upgrade to latest File Sync agent. Run FileSyncErrorReport.ps1 to identify locked/problematic files. Close locked files. Fix invalid filenames. Constraint conflicts auto-resolve once root cause errors fixed. Use Jarvis query with ServerEventId 9121 for investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Conflict Issues_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync download errors with ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS and ECS_E_SYNC_MERGE_TOMBSTONE_CHECKS_FAILED, causing 100+ dependent files to fail with constraint conflicts.
rootCause: ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS: cannot create a file when it already exists (name collision). ECS_E_SYNC_MERGE_TOMBSTONE_CHECKS_FAILED: merge tombstone data consistency error for files marked for deletion.
solution: Run FileSyncErrorReport.ps1 to identify specific files. Resolve ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS by renaming/removing conflicting files. For tombstone errors, allow sync to reconcile. Constraint conflicts auto-resolve once root cause errors fixed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Conflict Issues_Storage

symptom: Set-AzStorageSyncServiceIdentity cmdlet does not configure server to use system-assigned managed identity for Azure File Sync
rootCause: Server does not meet prerequisites: requires v19 agent, system-assigned managed identity, and dataset without custom ACEs
solution: Verify the server meets all prerequisites (v19+ agent, system-assigned MI enabled, no custom ACEs on dataset)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Expected Issues for Managed Identities on Azure File Sync_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync cloud endpoint creation fails with error MgmtForbidden2 when using managed identities
rootCause: Portal does not support managed identities for endpoint creation, or Storage Sync Service MI was granted Owner role instead of required roles
solution: Use PowerShell to create cloud/server endpoints instead of portal. If Owner role was assigned, remove it and follow docs for correct role assignment
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Expected Issues for Managed Identities on Azure File Sync_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync session fails with error 0x80c8305f (ECS_E_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED) after enabling managed identities
rootCause: Service-managed identity does not have access to the storage account
solution: Run Set-AzStorageSyncCloudEndpointPermission cmdlet to fix permissions for the service-managed identity
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Expected Issues for Managed Identities on Azure File Sync_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync files fail to sync with error 0x80c86063 (ECS_E_AZURE_AUTHORIZATION_PERMISSION_MISMATCH) or 0x80c86053 (ECS_E_AZURE_FILE_SHARE_NOT_ACCESSIBLE)
rootCause: Server-managed identity or service-managed identity does not have access to the Azure file share
solution: Run Set-AzStorageSyncServerEndpointPermission (for server MI) or Set-AzStorageSyncCloudEndpointPermission (for service MI) to fix permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Expected Issues for Managed Identities on Azure File Sync_Storage

symptom: Test-NetworkConnectivity cmdlet fails with 0x80131500 (COR_E_EXCEPTION) when using managed identities with Azure File Sync
rootCause: Allow Azure services on the trusted services list to access this storage account exception is not enabled on the storage account
solution: Enable the trusted services exception on the storage account firewall settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Expected Issues for Managed Identities on Azure File Sync_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync upload fails with error ECS_E_NOT_ENOUGH_REMOTE_STORAGE - file share at 5TB limit
rootCause: Azure file share has reached its 5TB size limit and cannot accept more data from server endpoint upload
solution: Ask customer to expand file share size to Large File Share via Azure portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/File Not Syncing from Server to File Share_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent installation fails or server registration fails after agent install with certificate error
rootCause: Older agent version conflict or expired/invalid server certificate prevents registration
solution: Uninstall old agent first, install latest. For cert errors: Import-Module StorageSync, Login-AzureRmStorageSync, then Reset-AzureRmStorageSyncServerCertificate. Install latest .NET Framework if 404 error occurs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/File Sync Agent Installation Issues_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent installation fails with error 0x80070080 - SYSTEM account does not have Full Control permissions
rootCause: SYSTEM account lacks Full Control permissions on the installation folder, possibly caused by registry or OS corruption
solution: Grant SYSTEM account Full Control on the target installation directory. Check for OS/registry corruption if permissions are already correct.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/File Sync Agent Installation Issues_Storage

symptom: AFSUpdater hangs indefinitely during Azure File Sync agent update, no installer logs generated, agent version < 16.2.0
rootCause: Known bug in Storage Sync Agent versions < 16.2.0 where LogMan process blocks the updater
solution: Workaround: Open Task Manager, kill LogMan process repeatedly until installation progresses. Permanent fix: upgrade to agent v17+ (fixed in v16.2.0+)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/File Sync Agent Updater Hang_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server registration fails - AfsSrvRegistration log shows error, possibly network or certificate related
rootCause: Network connectivity issues or invalid/expired server certificate preventing registration with Storage Sync Service
solution: Run Debug-StorageSyncServer -Diagnose and Debug-StorageSyncServer -TestNetworkConnectivity. Check AfsSrvRegistration*.log under %LocalAppData%/Temp for error details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/File sync troubleshoot agent registration failures_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server registration does not list all Azure Subscriptions in the dropdown
rootCause: ServerRegistration.exe only retrieves subscriptions from first 5 Microsoft Entra tenants by default
solution: Create DWORD ServerRegistrationTenantLimit under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync with value > 5, or use PowerShell: Connect-AzAccount -Subscription <guid> -Tenant <guid> then Register-AzStorageSyncServer
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/File sync troubleshoot agent registration failures_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server registration displays: This server is already registered
rootCause: Server was previously registered with a different Storage Sync Service and the registration was not properly cleaned up
solution: Run Import-Module StorageSync.Management.ServerCmdlets.dll then Reset-StorageSyncServer to reset server ID. Then re-register. For cluster, add -CleanClusterRegistration parameter.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/File sync troubleshoot agent registration failures_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync auto-update policy (InstallLatest) does not auto-update agent to latest version
rootCause: InstallLatest policy only applies during agent flighting phase - setting it after a version is fully released will not trigger auto-update
solution: Use alternative update methods: 1) Configure Microsoft Update for automatic downloads, 2) Run AfsUpdater.exe manually, 3) Download .msp from Microsoft Update Catalog, 4) Download latest installer from Microsoft Download Center and reinstall.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Filesync Agent Autoupdater Policy_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync tiered files show grey X icon in Windows Explorer
rootCause: Expected behavior: Windows Explorer displays grey X for any files with the offline attribute set. Tiered files have the offline attribute.
solution: No action needed - this is by design. The grey X appears because tiered files have the offline attribute. Over SMB the X is visible; on the local server files appear greyed without X.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Grey 'X' on Tiered Files_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent installation fails with error 1603. PowerShell custom action SetRegPIIAclSettings fails with exit code -2147287035 (ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED). PowerShell reports: The shell cannot be started due to an error in the initialization. System.Net.ServicePointManager threw an exception.
rootCause: PowerShell process on the server is broken (System.Net.ServicePointManager initialization failure), preventing AFS installer custom actions from executing.
solution: Engage Windows Support to investigate and fix the PowerShell issue before reattempting the AFS agent installation. Verify by running: powershell.exe -NoProfile -NonInteractive -InputFormat None -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& New-Item -ItemType directory -Path C:\temp\test -Force"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 222 AFS Agent Installation troubleshooting_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent installation fails with error 0x80070002 (NOT_FOUND). All PowerShell custom actions in MSI log fail with 0x80070002. Running powershell.exe from command line also fails.
rootCause: The %PATH% environment variable does not contain the PowerShell directory (%SystemRoot%\system32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0), so the installer cannot find powershell.exe.
solution: Add "%SystemRoot%\system32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0" to the system PATH environment variable: Control Panel > System > Advanced System Settings > Environment Variables > System variables > Path > New > add the PowerShell path. Then retry installation from a new command prompt.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 222 AFS Agent Installation troubleshooting_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent update (MSP) fails with error 1603. Log shows CompareProductVersion custom action fails: "Cannot proceed with an upgrade as agent is already running this or a later update."
rootCause: The agent update package version is the same or older than the currently installed version. The CompareProductVersion custom action blocks downgrade/reinstall.
solution: Verify the currently installed agent version and ensure the update package is a newer version. If attempting to reinstall the same version, uninstall and reinstall instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 222 AFS Agent Installation troubleshooting_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent update fails with Error 1921: Service Storage Sync Agent (FileSyncSvc) could not be stopped during InstallFinalize phase.
rootCause: The FileSyncSvc service cannot be stopped during agent patching, possibly due to active sync operations, permission issues, or hung processes.
solution: Manually stop FileSyncSvc before retrying: net stop FileSyncSvc. If service hangs, use taskkill /f /im FileSyncSvc.exe. Verify sufficient privileges to stop system services. Reboot if necessary.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 222 AFS Agent Installation troubleshooting_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync sync/management operations fail with error 0x80c83099 (PrivateEndpointAccessBlocked) or HTTP Unreachable errors after configuring private endpoints.
rootCause: Private endpoint configuration is incomplete or incorrect: missing/unapproved PE connection, incorrect Private DNS Zone setup, NSG/firewall blocking traffic, or agent version < 10.1 which does not support PE.
solution: Verify: (1) Agent is v10.1+, (2) PE connection is in Approved state, (3) Private DNS Zones (privatelink.afs.azure.net) are correctly configured, (4) NSG/firewall allows traffic to PE IPs. Use Jarvis Ifx3Operation table to check if traffic uses PE (IsPrivateIp=True, PvtLinkId present). Check allowInternetTraffic policy (AllowAllTraffic vs AllowVirtualNetworksOnly).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 372 AFS How to Troubleshoot Private Endpoint failures_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync upload sync session fails with error 0x80c8029c (ECS_E_SYNC_CANCELLED_BY_VSS). Sync session is cancelled once per day.
rootCause: VssSyncScheduledTask runs once daily to sync files with open handles. It cancels the current upload session, creates a VSS snapshot, and starts a new upload session.
solution: No action required - this is expected behavior and sync will auto-resume. If initial upload is in progress and interruption is undesirable, temporarily disable VssSyncScheduledTask: Disable-ScheduledTask -TaskName VssSyncScheduledTask -TaskPath \Microsoft\StorageSync\ then restart FileSyncSvc. Re-enable after initial upload completes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 428 AFS VSS Sync_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync reports per-item error ECS_E_DIRECTORY_RENAME_FAILED on a directory in upload direction. Directory rename fails to sync.
rootCause: When syncing a directory rename (e.g., \A -> \B), the cloud share has an open file handle on a child file under the source directory, preventing the rename operation.
solution: Customer must close all open file handles under the directory that failed to rename on the destination side of sync. After handles are released, sync will automatically retry and succeed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 504 AFS ECS E DIRECTORY RENAME FAILED per item upload errors_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync reports widespread ECS_E_DIRECTORY_RENAME_FAILED per-item errors in upload direction across multiple directories, not explained by customer file handles.
rootCause: XFiles bug causes leaked marker handles in the cloud share. These leaked handles prevent directory rename operations from completing during sync.
solution: Contact XSMBDev and Daniewo for the dev tool to clean up leaked marker handles. If experienced with PartitionRepair tools, run CleanupXFilesLeakedMarkerHandles mode with appropriate keylow/keyhigh. Alternatively, create a Sev3 ICM for XStore/SMB team with account name and container name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 504 AFS ECS E DIRECTORY RENAME FAILED per item upload errors_Storage

symptom: Need to determine why a specific file was tiered by Azure File Sync cloud tiering
rootCause: Files can be tiered for multiple reasons: VolumePolicy (free space threshold), DatePolicy (file age), CloudTieringCmdlet (manual tiering via PowerShell), DownloadSync (fastdr/ghostedsync), or PriorAgent
solution: Run Get-StorageSyncHeatStoreInformation -FilePath <path> to check GhostingReason field. VolumePolicy/DatePolicy = background tiering (check 9016 events around LastGhostingTimeUtc). DownloadSync = fastdr or ghostedsync. CloudTieringCmdlet = manual tiering cmdlet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS Cloud Tiering Check why a file is tiered_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync cloud tiering date policy not tiering files as expected because background processes (e.g. fsdmhost.exe for dedup) update the last access time
rootCause: Heat tracking counts file access from all processes including background services. Dedup optimization (fsdmhost.exe) or other background apps access files before tiering, making them appear recently accessed.
solution: Set registry to exclude process names from heat tracking. Agent v11-v12: HeatTrackingProcessNameExclusionList (REG_MULTI_SZ). Agent v13+: HeatTrackingProcessNamesExclusionList (REG_SZ pipe-separated). Dedup/FSRM auto-excluded. Optionally adjust HeatTrackingProcessNameExclusionRefreshMins (default 5 min).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS Cloud Tiering Heat tracking Process name exclusion from last access time tracking_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync heatstore corruption detected - cloud tiering not working, Event ID 9013 shows Heatstore corruption detected HRESULT 0x8e5e01fe Unable to read from or write to the database
rootCause: ESE database (heatstore) corruption on the server volume
solution: Query Event 9013 via Kusto: cluster(Xfiles).database(xsynctelemetrysf).ServerTelemetryEvents where ServerEventId == 9013. If corruption confirmed, delete ONLY the heatstore DB (not sync DB or change tracking DB). Follow TSG 196 AFS Delete an ESE database. PG does NOT require a copy of the corrupted DB.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS Cloud Tiering Identify a corrupt heatstore_Storage

symptom: Recalling Azure File Sync tiered files on macOS via SMB takes much longer than the identical copy on Windows
rootCause: macOS Finder bug (rdar://97728008): Finder reads SF_DATALESS (tiered) files with 4KB IO request size causing extremely slow recalls compared to Windows SMB clients
solution: No Apple workaround available. For AFS agent v15.0+ partial mitigation: enable cache manager reads via registry HfsKmDisableCCReadAhead=0 under HKLM SYSTEM CurrentControlSet Services StorageSync then fltmc unload/load storagesync. Improvement is small (prefetching).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS MacOS slow file recall tiering_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync recall results in partial content download. Event 1110 shows RecallToDiskType No (File system cache usage reached maximum threshold). DataTransferHresult is 0 but data not written to disk.
rootCause: OS-level memory constraint. By default AFS reserves 10% of physical memory for recall. When recalls exceed 60% of assigned recall memory, data is served from memory but not written to disk. Happens when apps/scripts attempt aggressive simultaneous recalls.
solution: Increase memory allocation via registry: BottomlessFilterSharedMemSizePercentage=30 and BottomlessFilterCacheUsagePercentage=70 under HKLM SOFTWARE Microsoft Azure StorageSync. Then fltmc unload/load StorageSync + restart filesyncsvc. Use Invoke-StorageSyncFileRecall with -ThreadCount for controlled recalls. Consult AFS PG before suggesting registry edits.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS Recall Error File system cache usage reached maximum threshold_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync tiered multimedia files show partial content download when recalled - file not fully downloaded to disk
rootCause: Expected behavior (by design). Azure File Sync only recalls the requested data range for multimedia files. Media players use range requests and only read portions of the file.
solution: No action needed - by design for multimedia files. AFS efficiently recalls only requested data. To force full recall use Invoke-StorageSyncFileRecall cmdlet or copy the file to trigger a full read.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS Recall Error File system cache usage reached maximum threshold_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server registration fails with CRYPT_E_NOT_FOUND (0x80092004) or ECS_E_SERVER_INVALID_OR_EXPIRED_CERTIFICATE (0x80C80050) when server is not registered and certificate is missing, deleted, or expired
rootCause: Server certificate is not present, has been deleted, or has expired, preventing AFS agent from authenticating with the Storage Sync Service
solution:
1) Open PowerShell as admin
2) Remove-ItemProperty HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync\ServerSetting -Name ServerSetting.Server=Localhost -Force
3) Import StorageSync modules
4) Run Reset-StorageSyncServer to reset serverID
5) Retry server registration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS Server Certificate Issues_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync server sync/recall fails with ECS_E_AUTH_SRV_CERT_EXPIRED or ECS_E_AUTH_SRV_CERT_NOT_FOUND on a registered server with active server endpoints
rootCause: Server certificate has expired on a registered and provisioned AFS server, causing authentication failures for sync and recall operations
solution:
1) Import-Module StorageSync.Management.PowerShell.Cmdlets.dll
2) Run Reset-AzStorageSyncServerCertificate -ResourceGroupName <RG> -StorageSyncServiceName <name>
3) Sync/Recall/Management should recover after certificate rollover
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG AFS Server Certificate Issues_Storage

symptom: Drive becomes inaccessible after installing Azure File Sync Agent with error Location is not available the parameter is incorrect - primarily on Windows Server 2012 R2
rootCause: Windows Server 2012 R2 bug or third-party filter driver interop issue with Azure File Sync filter driver (StorageSync.sys)
solution:
1) Ensure Windows 2012 R2 is on latest build with all updates (KB2919355)
2) Check for third-party filter drivers in ASC under Registered Servers > Filter Data
3) Try uninstalling conflicting third-party filter drivers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Unable to Access Drive After Installing AFS Agent_Storage

symptom: AFS server endpoint cannot recall files from cloud endpoint with error 0x80072f8f (-2147012721), network connectivity test shows Content decoding has failed
rootCause: Firewall proxy or gateway blocks access to PKI/OCSP URLs required for SSL certificate revocation checking (microsoft.com/pki crl.microsoft.com ocsp.msocsp.com ocsp.digicert.com)
solution: Ensure server can access PKI URLs: microsoft.com/pki/mscorp/cps, crl.microsoft.com, mscrl.microsoft.com, ocsp.msocsp.com, ocsp.digicert.com, crl3.digicert.com. Last resort: set registry HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync SkipSslCertificateRevocationCheck=1 (DWORD)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Unable to Recall due to Certificate Revocation_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync download fails with -2134375877 (ECS_E_SYNC_METADATA_KNOWLEDGE_SOFT_LIMIT_REACHED) and per-item error 0x80070780 (ERROR_CANT_ACCESS_FILE) with reparse tag 0x80000013 found on files
rootCause: Data was copied from a server with Data Deduplication enabled creating dedup reparse points (tag 0x80000013) but the destination server does not have the Deduplication role installed
solution: Install Data Deduplication role on the server endpoint: Install-WindowsFeature -Name FS-Data-Deduplication. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/storage/data-deduplication/install-enable
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Unable to Sync with ERROR CANT ACCESS FILE_Storage

symptom: 20-30 second delay when browsing Azure File shares configured with DFS namespace showing contacting dfsname share message and network traces show connection attempts on TCP ports 139 and 80 in addition to 445
rootCause: Windows client redirector sequentially tries SMB (445) then NetBIOS (139) then WebDAV (80). WebClient service causes delays when DFS namespace is used because Azure Files responds on port 80 but rejects WebDAV with Bad request
solution: Disable the WebClient service on the client machine. Azure Files only supports SMB 3.0 over TCP port 445; connections via NetBIOS or WebDAV are not supported
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Windows Behavior Connecting to AFS_Storage

symptom: AIB build fails with VMMarketplaceInvalidInput error when using Azure Compute Gallery custom image (sourced from CIS marketplace image) as source for AIB template. Adding planInfo to template also fails with unmarshalling error.
rootCause: ACG images with plan information (3rd party marketplace images like CIS) cannot be used as source for AIB templates. The planInfo field is not supported in AIB template source configuration for shared image versions.
solution: Use the original marketplace image directly as source in the AIB template with all required customizations, then distribute to ACG. Do not use ACG images from 3rd party marketplace as AIB source.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/AIB Build failing while using Custom Images from ACG

symptom: AIB build fails with InternalOperationError. Kusto AsyncContextActivity/PackerizerContextActivity logs show ACI container deployment failure. Container instance fails to mount share from AIB staging storage account.
rootCause: Customer had Azure Policy adding NSG rules to the subnet used by AIB container instance, blocking all inbound/outbound traffic. This prevented the container from communicating with the storage account.
solution: Remove the restrictive policy or add an exception for Azure Image Builder resources. Set the containerInstanceSubnetId parameter in the image template to use a subnet without restrictive NSG rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/Build Failing With Error Internal Error Occurred_AIB

symptom: AIB build fails with WinRM timeout error (Timeout waiting for WinRM) when using CIS-hardened Windows Server image as source. customization.log shows packer WinRM connection timeout.
rootCause: CIS hardening disrupts WinRM connectivity. AIB/Packer relies on WinRM to communicate with the build VM. CIS firewall rules and security policies block or restrict WinRM.
solution: Known limitation as of June 2025 - no direct automated solution within AIB. No fix on roadmap per AIB PM team. Consider alternative non-CIS-hardened base images and apply hardening as post-build customization step.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/Build Failures with CIS Hardened Images_AIB

symptom: AIB isolated image build with BYO subnet completes without errors, but auto-generated VNET/network resources are created instead of using the specified subnets for ACI.
rootCause: New-AzImageBuilderTemplate PowerShell cmdlet does not support the apiVersion property, causing requests with older API version 2022-07-01 instead of required 2024-02-01 for isolated builds with containerInstanceSubnetId.
solution: Use custom ARM template deployment via Azure Portal or REST API instead of New-AzImageBuilderTemplate cmdlet, specifying API version 2024-02-01 or later.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/Build incomplete due to api version

symptom: AIB build with customer VNET fails after ~30 minutes with WinRM/SSH connection timeout via proxy VM. Error: proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp IP:60000: i/o timeout. Customer has restrictive NSG with DenyAll inbound rule.
rootCause: Traffic to proxy VM comes from two sources: AzureLoadBalancer and private endpoint (VNET IP). NSG rule allowing only AzureLoadBalancer source does not cover private endpoint traffic, which gets blocked by DenyAll rule.
solution: Add two NSG inbound rules before DenyAll: (1) TCP 60000-60001 from AzureLoadBalancer to VirtualNetwork, (2) TCP 60000-60001,22,5986 from VirtualNetwork to VirtualNetwork.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/ConnectionError_AIB

symptom: AIB build times out before reaching AIB timeout period when using GitHub Actions with OpenID tokens. customization.log shows: Future#WaitForCompletion: context has been cancelled: context deadline exceeded.
rootCause: OpenID token issued by GitHub Actions expires before AIB build completes, or GitHub Action workflow is misconfigured. This is a GitHub-side issue, not an AIB service problem.
solution: Redirect customer to GitHub Support (https://support.github.com). Contact CSAM to re-route. Verify via customization log from ASC that context timeout is the issue before redirecting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/GitHubActions OpenID_AIB

symptom: AIB build fails early with NoCustomizerScript provisioning error code. No customization.log file is created. ASC shows: Image template is not provisioned; cannot get packer logs.
rootCause: Azure Policy restricts creation of AIB staging resources (IT_ staging resource group, storage account). Policy deny actions visible in subscription-level Operations logs as Microsoft.Authorization/policies/deny/action.
solution: Check subscription-level Operations tab in ASC, filter by resource group to find policy deny actions. Identify blocking Azure Policy. Customer must update Azure Policy to allow AIB staging resource creation per prerequisite documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/NoCustomizerScript_AIB

symptom: AIB build fails with VMMarketplaceInvalidInput when using CIS hardened or 3rd-party Marketplace images stored in Azure Compute Gallery (ACG) as source. Passing planInfo in template returns unmarshalling error: unknown field planInfo.
rootCause: AIB does not support using ACG images that require plan information (3rd-party Marketplace images like CIS) as source for image templates. The planInfo field is not recognized in the ImageTemplateSharedImageVersionSource schema.
solution: Use the original Marketplace image directly as the source in the AIB template (type: PlatformImage) instead of the ACG copy. Include all required customizations in the template and distribute the output to Azure Compute Gallery.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/AIB Build failing while using Custom Images from ACG

symptom: AIB build fails with InternalOperationError. Kusto AsyncContextActivity and PackerizerContextActivity logs show ACI container instance fails to mount storage account share. ASI shows container group deployment failure.
rootCause: Customer had an Azure Policy that added NSG rules to the NSG attached to the AIB container instance subnet, blocking all inbound and outbound traffic. This prevented the container from communicating with the storage account created by AIB.
solution: Remove the restrictive policy or add an exception for Azure Image Builder resources. Alternatively, set the containerInstanceSubnetId parameter in the image template to use a subnet without restrictive NSG policies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/Build Failing With Error Internal Error Occurred_AIB

symptom: AIB build fails with runState failed when using CIS-hardened Windows Server image as source. Customization.log shows WinRM timeout: Timeout waiting for WinRM. Build does not complete customization steps.
rootCause: CIS hardening modifies firewall rules, service configurations, and security policies that block or restrict WinRM connectivity. AIB/Packer relies on WinRM for communication with the build VM during customization. No built-in solution or roadmap item exists as of June 2025.
solution: Known limitation with no automated fix. Gather AIB template and customization.log for investigation. Consider using non-CIS base image with post-build hardening, or apply CIS hardening as a customization step that preserves WinRM connectivity during build.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/Build Failures with CIS Hardened Images_AIB

symptom: AIB Isolated Image build completes without errors, but auto-generated VNET and network resources are created instead of using customer-specified BYO Build VM subnet and ACI subnet. Build VM uses correct subnet but ACI does not.
rootCause: New-AzImageBuilderTemplate PowerShell cmdlet does not support the apiVersion property, causing requests to use older API version 2022-07-01 instead of required 2024-01-01 (or 2024-02-01) which supports isolated builds with containerInstanceSubnetId.
solution: Use custom ARM template deployment via Azure Portal or REST API instead of New-AzImageBuilderTemplate, explicitly specifying API version 2024-02-01 or later. Verify via ARM HttpIncomingRequests Kusto query that correct API version is used.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/Build incomplete due to api version

symptom: AIB build with custom VNET fails after ~30 minutes with connection timeout. Windows: WinRM proxyconnect timeout to port 60000. Linux: SSH bastion dial timeout to port 60000. Customer has restrictive NSG with DenyAll rule and added AIB LoadBalancer allow rule per docs but still fails.
rootCause: AIB proxy VM receives traffic from two sources on ports 60000-60001: Azure Load Balancer (168.63.129.16) and private endpoint (VNET address). The documented AzureLoadBalancer source rule only covers LB traffic. Private endpoint traffic from VNET source is blocked by the DenyAll rule.
solution: Add two NSG inbound rules before the DenyAll rule: (1) TCP 60000-60001 from AzureLoadBalancer to VirtualNetwork, (2) TCP 60000-60001,22,5986 from VirtualNetwork to VirtualNetwork (or Any to Any).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/ConnectionError_AIB

symptom: AIB build times out or fails before AIB timeout period is reached. Customization.log shows: Future#WaitForCompletion context has been cancelled StatusCode=200 Original Error context deadline exceeded. Customer is using GitHub Actions with OpenID tokens.
rootCause: GitHub Actions OpenID token expires or GitHub Action is misconfigured, causing the authentication context to time out before the AIB build completes. This is outside AIB scope as GitHub Actions/OpenID are managed by GitHub.
solution: Redirect customer to GitHub support (https://support.github.com). Contact CSAM to re-route the case. Verify with customer they are using GitHub Actions before applying this TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/GitHubActions OpenID_AIB

symptom: AIB build fails early with NoCustomizerScript error code. No customization.log file is created. ASC shows: Image template is not provisioned, cannot get packer logs. Provisioning error message is generic InternalOperationError.
rootCause: Azure Policy restricts creation of necessary AIB staging resources (IT_ staging resource group, storage account for logs, or other required resources). Policy deny actions are visible in subscription-level Operations logs.
solution: Check subscription Operations tab filtered by the image template resource group (includes IT_ staging RG). Identify the Azure Policy causing deny actions. Customer needs to add an exemption for AIB staging resources or modify the policy. Collaborate with Azure Policy team via AVA request if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/NoCustomizerScript_AIB

symptom: AIB start build fails with PurchasePlanNotProvided error when using Third-party Azure Marketplace image as source. Error: Purchase Plan was not provided for image.
rootCause: PlanInfo is incorrectly defined as a standalone property instead of inside the Source property block, or the marketplace image terms have not been accepted.
solution: Move PlanInfo inside the Source property in the ARM template. If issue persists, run az vm image terms show --urn <image-urn> to check acceptance, then az vm image terms accept if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/PurchasePlanTSG_AIB

symptom: AIB start build fails with Error setting up networking pipeline for communication with build VM when using a customized staging resource group. No IT_ resource group created, no packer logs.
rootCause: Azure Virtual Machine Image Builder service principal is missing Contributor role assignment on the customized staging resource group.
solution: Use correlationId from Activity Logs to trace error via AIB Kusto (azcrp.kusto.windows.net, vmimagebuilder DB, AsyncContextActivity table). Identify objectId from AuthorizationFailed error, then assign Contributor role to Azure Virtual Machine Image Builder on the staging RG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/StartBuild fail with customized StagingResourceGroup_AIB

symptom: AIB start build fails sporadically with RedHat 9.x image when customizer step runs sudo dnf update -y. Packer log shows FileNotFoundError for cached RPM package in /var/cache/dnf/.
rootCause: Package manager access conflict between dnf update and VM configuration/extension installation (e.g., cloud-init SSH key setup). Both require exclusive access to the package manager, causing race condition.
solution: Wrap dnf update in a background command with delay: ( sleep 5m && sudo dnf update -y ) &. This allows cloud-init and extensions to complete first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/StartBuild fail with customizer shellcommandscript dnf update_AIB

symptom: AIB start build fails with Provided staging resource group may be used by another image template Conflict error when using an existing staging resource group.
rootCause: Staging RG has tags imageTemplateResourceGroupName and imageTemplateName from a previous/different image template. AIB verifies these tags match the current template; mismatch causes conflict. RG must also be empty, same region, with Contributor/Owner role.
solution: Remove the existing imageTemplateResourceGroupName and imageTemplateName tags from the staging resource group, ensure the RG is empty, then retry the build.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/StartBuild fail with existing StagingResourceGroup_AIB

symptom: AIB provisioning fails with PrivateLinkService Network Policy is not disabled for the given subnet when using existing VNet. Portal shows Private Endpoint policy as Disabled but issue persists.
rootCause: Subnet has privateLinkServiceNetworkPolicies set to Enabled. Azure Portal only exposes privateEndpointNetworkPolicies (for Private Endpoints), not privateLinkServiceNetworkPolicies (for Private Link Services). These are separate settings.
solution: Disable via CLI (cannot be done via Portal): az network vnet subnet update --name <subnet> --vnet-name <vnet> --resource-group <rg> --disable-private-link-service-network-policies true
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/Submission_Failing_privateLinkServiceNetworkPolicies_AIB

symptom: AIB errorHandling property does not work or is not recognized in image template configuration.
rootCause: API version is below 2022-07-01 (minimum required for errorHandling), or property name is misspelled. AIB service is case-sensitive as of API version 2024-02-01.
solution: Ensure API version >= 2022-07-01. Use exact spelling errorHandling with correct camelCase. Values: onCustomizerError and onValidationError, each accepting cleanup (default) or abort. For API >= 2024-02-01, all property names are case-sensitive.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/TSGs/errorHandling_AIB

symptom: Domain Controller VM fails to boot with error 0xC00002E2 STATUS_DS_INIT_FAILURE; VM cannot access local Active Directory database (NTDS.DIT); AD database corruption or AD data disk not auto-onlined
rootCause: Active Directory database corruption, the volume/disk associated with AD database is not set to come online automatically, or the domain controller cannot access NTDS.DIT file
solution: Boot VM into DSRM (Directory Services Restore Mode) by setting safeboot to dsrepair via bcdedit: attach OS disk to rescue VM, run bcdedit /store <Drive>:\boot\bcd /set {identifier} safeboot dsrepair. For single DC environments, also disable NLA (SecurityLayer=0). Automated: use az vm repair create + az vm repair run --run-id win-toggle-safe-mode --parameters DC=$true. Login with .\administrator and DSRM password.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Boot Domain Controller (DC) into DSRM_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM fails to boot after post-installation changes or system configuration modifications that break normal OS boot process
rootCause: Recent OS configuration changes corrupted the current ControlSet, preventing the VM from booting with the active configuration
solution: Attach OS disk to rescue VM as data disk. Load the SYSTEM hive: reg load HKLM\BROKENSYSTEM f:\windows\system32\config\SYSTEM. Increment all ControlSet Select values (Current, Default, Failed, LastKnownGood) by 1 to boot from the LastKnownGood configuration. Unload hive, detach disk, reassemble VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Boot from Last Known Good Configuration_RDP SSH

symptom: VM connectivity degraded: slow connection, random disconnections, black screen over RDP, or VM completely unreachable due to User Defined Route (UDR) misconfiguration
rootCause: Misconfigured User Defined Routes (UDR) causing network loops, or traffic routed to virtual appliances/firewalls with improperly configured rules that block required ports or all network connectivity
solution: Check UDR via ASC Resource Explorer -> VM -> Virtual Network -> Subnet -> Route Table. Verify next hop type and destination for routes covering the VM IP range. Use ASC Test Traffic tool (Stateless test) to identify routing blocks. If traffic routes through virtual appliance, verify appliance firewall rules allow required traffic. Check for routes with NextHopType pointing back to the VM itself (causes routing block).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Check User Defined Route Settings_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM default display resolution is 1024x768. Customer needs higher native display resolution for applications that require it. Changing resolution via MSTSC client only affects remote session, not native OS resolution.
rootCause: Azure VMs default to 1024x768 native display resolution. The MSTSC client resolution setting only changes the RDP session display, not the underlying OS display configuration.
solution: Workaround 1: Use MSTSC client Options > Display to change RDP session resolution, or run mstsc /w:1920 /h:1080. Workaround 2: Create rescue VM with nested virtualization, attach the disk, boot in Hyper-V environment, change display resolution in OS Display settings, save, detach disk, and swap back to target VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/How To Increase display resolution of Azure VM_RDP SSH

symptom: Unable to install Azure PowerShell modules (Az/AzureRM) on Windows Server 2012R2 or 2016. Install-Module fails with NuGet provider is required and Unable to download from URI https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=627338. Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet also fails.
rootCause: PowerShell Gallery requires TLS 1.2 since April 2020. Older Windows Server versions (2012R2, 2016) default to SSL3 and TLS 1.0 security protocols, which cannot connect to the PowerShell Gallery endpoints.
solution: Mitigation 1: Add [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12 to PowerShell profile. Mitigation 2: Set .NET strong crypto registry keys: Set-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NetFramework\v4.0.30319 -Name SchUseStrongCrypto -Value 1 -Type DWord (64-bit) and same for 32-bit path, then restart PowerShell. Mitigation 3: Run [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol -bor [Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12 to add TLS 1.2 without overwriting existing protocols.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Install Azure PowerShell on Azure VM_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM because the Network Security Group (NSG) is missing the default RDP inbound allow rule on port 3389
rootCause: Creating a new NSG from portal or PowerShell does not automatically create the default RDP rule; the rule must be manually added
solution: Use PowerShell (Add-AzureRmNetworkSecurityRuleConfig) to recreate the default RDP allow rule: Priority 1000, TCP, port 3389, inbound, allow all source/destination
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Recreate NSG RDP Rule_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot access Azure VM remotely because the trusted communication between the VM and the Active Directory domain is broken (secure channel failure)
rootCause: The computer account trust relationship with the AD domain has been broken, preventing domain authentication
solution: Rejoin the VM to the domain using Custom Script Extension (netdom remove + netdom join), Run Command (Remove-Computer + Add-Computer), or Serial Console. For local admin only: isolate VM with NSG deny-all rules first, then use Run Command to unjoin/rejoin
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Rejoin VM to Domain using CSE_RDP SSH

symptom: RDP connection fails with Internal Error or unable to connect; the RDP self-signed certificate is not renewing as expected
rootCause: The RDP self-signed certificate has expired or become corrupt, blocking RDP connection attempts
solution: From a VM with connectivity: open remote MMC Certificates snap-in (Computer account), navigate to Remote Desktop/Certificates, delete the expired cert, restart SessionEnv service (net stop/start SessionEnv) to auto-regenerate new certificate
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Renew RDP Certificate Remotely_RDP SSH

symptom: Linux VM has network connectivity issues because the guest OS is not configured to use DHCP for IP assignment
rootCause: The network interface config file (ifcfg-eth0) has BOOTPROTO set to static instead of dhcp
solution: Attach OS disk to recovery VM, mount, edit /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-eth0 setting BOOTPROTO=dhcp (DEVICE=eth0, ONBOOT=yes, TYPE=Ethernet), unmount, detach disk, recreate VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Setup DHCP on Linux VM_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM because NSG is missing default RDP inbound rule (port 3389). Creating a new NSG from portal or PowerShell does not automatically create the default RDP allow rule.
rootCause: NSG was recreated or newly created without the default RDP (port 3389) inbound allow rule. Unlike the default NSG created with a VM, manually created NSGs do not include this rule.
solution: Use PowerShell to add the RDP rule: Get-AzureRmNetworkSecurityGroup then Add-AzureRmNetworkSecurityRuleConfig -Name DefaultRDP-Recreate -Access Allow -DestinationPortRange 3389 -Direction Inbound -Priority 1000 -Protocol TCP -SourceAddressPrefix * then Set-AzureRmNetworkSecurityGroup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Recreate NSG RDP Rule_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot access Azure VM remotely. Trusted communication between VM and Active Directory domain is broken.
rootCause: The secure channel between the VM computer account and the AD domain controller has been broken.
solution: Rejoin VM to domain using: (1) CustomScriptExtension with netdom remove/join, (2) Run Command with Remove-Computer/Add-Computer, (3) Serial Console. For local admin only: isolate via NSG deny-all, unjoin with local creds, remove NSG, rejoin.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Rejoin VM to Domain using CSE_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM with Internal Error or connection failure. RDP self-signed certificate is not renewing automatically.
rootCause: The RDP self-signed certificate has expired or become corrupted and automatic renewal failed.
solution: From another VM: (1) Open MMC, add Certificates snap-in for remote computer (internal IP), (2) Navigate to Remote Desktop/Certificates, (3) Delete expired cert, (4) Restart SessionEnv service. Certificate auto-regenerates.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Renew RDP Certificate Remotely_RDP SSH

symptom: Linux VM loses network connectivity. Static IP in guest OS instead of DHCP causes network issues after VM recreation.
rootCause: ifcfg-eth0 has BOOTPROTO set to static instead of dhcp.
solution: Attach OS disk to rescue VM, edit /recoverymnt/etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-eth0: BOOTPROTO=dhcp, ONBOOT=yes, TYPE=Ethernet. Unmount, detach, recreate VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Setup DHCP on Linux VM_RDP SSH

symptom: VM displays Fatal error C0000034 applying update operations on boot screen after installing a Windows Cumulative Update - Windows update fails to complete and VM cannot boot
rootCause: Corrupted pending update operations after Windows Cumulative Update installation cause boot failure
solution: OFFLINE: Create rescue VM or nested environment, run dism /image:G:\ /cleanup-image /revertpendingactions /scratchdir:G:\temp to revert pending actions; if unsuccessful, use PowerShell to rename/remove pending.xml (data recovery workaround only, machine must be rebuilt afterward). See also: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/windows/boot-error-0xc0000034
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/Known Issues or Bug/Error C0000034 applying update operations_RDP SSH

symptom: TLS/SSL connection handshakes fail with SEC_E_ILLEGAL_MESSAGE after installing October 2022 Windows Updates (KB5018427, KB5018418, KB5018421, KB5018410, KB5018412, KB5018419, KB5018411, KB5018425, and related KBs)
rootCause: Bug in ISC state transition in 2022 10B servicing branch - code fails to check if bytes have been processed (cbBytesProcessed) and returns SEC_E_INCOMPLETE_MESSAGE when multiple TLS frames are in input buffer and final frame has less than 5 bytes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/Known Issues or Bug/Error SEC_E_ILLEGAL_MESSAGE_RDP SSH

symptom: After installing a non-English language pack (e.g. English UK) on Windows 10 AVD image and restarting, VM loses network connectivity - Windows Firewall service (mpssvc) fails to start with error 1168 Element not found, RDP connection fails
rootCause: Bug triggered by language pack installation on specific Windows 10 AVD images (win10-21h2-avd-g2, 20h2-evd) causes Windows Firewall service mpssvc to fail on startup with service-specific error 1066/1168
solution: 1) Disable Firewall via Serial Console: netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state off; 2) Connect to VM; 3) Install KB5016688 from Microsoft Update Catalog; 4) Restart VM; 5) Re-enable Firewall: netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state on
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/Known Issues or Bug/Non English US Language pack_RDP SSH

symptom: Standard_D96ds_v5 or Standard_D96ads_v5 (96 vCPU) VM with Trusted Launch and Secure Boot fails to boot - VM immediately stops and deallocates after starting with fatal virtual firmware errors on host node
rootCause: Firmware bug in virtual firmware layer that only manifests with 96 vCPU SKUs (Standard_D96ds_v5/Standard_D96ads_v5) under Trusted Launch with Secure Boot enabled
solution: Resize VM to a non-96 vCPU SKU (e.g. Standard_D64ds_v5) as workaround; for permanent fix, file ICM to EEE RDOS team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/Known Issues or Bug/Standard D96ds v5 SecureBoot VM fails to boot due to Firmware bug_RDP SSH

symptom: Container/VM loses network connectivity on Gen 8 host nodes with SwitchPortNotEnabled errors - NM programming pending, UD walk pending for NM programming on multiple instances
rootCause: Expired SoC machinefunction certificate (APPKI CA) causes host-to-SoC control plane connectivity failure - NMAgent on host cannot communicate with vfpremoteapi on SoC via mTLS because VRA is using an expired certificate on Overlake (Gen 8) nodes
solution: File ICM to EEE Cloudnet team for triage and mitigation; verify by checking NMAGENT_PORT_STATE in NetVMA and querying InterfaceProgramEndFiveMinuteTable in aznwmds for SwitchPortNotEnabled pattern across all containers on the node
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/Known Issues or Bug/SwitchPortNotEnabled on Node Gen 8 due to VRA certificate expiration_RDP SSH

symptom: VM unexpectedly loses network connectivity with SwitchPortNotEnabled errors during network component update on the host node - VFP_REPLACE_ADD_LAYERS_FAILED followed by ProgramNewVFP Failed 0x80070002 and SwitchPortNotEnabled on VM ports
rootCause: Network component update on host node triggers VFP layer programming failure (VFP_REPLACE_ADD_LAYERS_FAILED, ProgramNewVFP Failed: 0x80070002), causing SwitchPortNotEnabled state on VM network ports (ICM 298389774)
solution: Redeploy the VM or deallocate and restart the VM to restore network connectivity
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/Known Issues or Bug/VM unexpectedly lost network connectivity due to SwitchPortNotEnabled_RDP SSH

symptom: Gen2 Trusted Launch VM with Secure Boot hangs in reboot loop - Windows.VmBoot failures lasting 40min-1hr, VM stays in Failed state, console screenshot shows Windows Setup could not configure Windows to run on this computers hardware
rootCause: Race condition / timing issue in the Firmware / HAL layer of guest VM with Trusted Launch Secure Boot - issue does not reproduce on all reboots or deployments, intermittent
solution: Restart from Azure portal or reboot the VM (issue is intermittent and usually resolves on retry); if persisting, file ICM to EEE RDOS team with results of diagnostic Kusto query checking for Gen2 VMs in reboot loop (EventId 18514 count > 5)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/Known Issues or Bug/Windows hangs on Secure Boot Trusted Launch_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM. No connectivity on VIP/DIP. Guest agent logs show EndpointNotFoundException: no endpoint listening at http://168.63.129.16/?comp=versions. SocketException: connected host has failed to respond 168.63.129.16:80.
rootCause: MAC preservation feature bug: RNM team enabled MAC preservation on subset of subscriptions. After enabling, NMAgent DHCP response was not RFC compliant - responded with new IP without sending NAK, causing clients to continue using previous IP address, breaking network connectivity.
solution: Stop and start the VM to get a new MAC/IP assignment. Alternatively, delete the deployment (keeping the disk) and recreate the VM. The MAC preservation flag was disabled globally to mitigate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/GA Endpoint not Found Exception_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to VM. No NIC visible in ipconfig. Only loopback interface in route print. UserPnp Event 20001 with status 0x5AA. VM screenshot shows OS at login screen but no network connectivity.
rootCause: Ghost/hidden NICs accumulated beyond threshold (~1204). Every MAC address change (e.g. after stop/start) creates a new NIC entry in Windows registry while leaving old entries as ghost devices. When threshold is exceeded, new vNIC installation fails with error 0x5AA. VMs with Accelerated Networking and frequent stop/start are most susceptible.
solution: Remove ghost NICs using PowerShell or Device Manager (show hidden devices). For VMs with Accelerated Networking, periodically clean ghost NICs as routine maintenance. Alternative: disable Accelerated Networking if recurrence is a concern. Close code: link Bug 41164659.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Ghost NIC_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Windows VM. VM screenshot shows OS at login screen. No inbound/outbound network traffic in Azure Portal. WinGuestAnalyzer Health Signal shows RDP traffic denied in remoteaccess section.
rootCause: Guest OS firewall RDP rule (Remote Desktop TCP-In) is disabled or not configured to allow RDP traffic. Common after migration from on-premises without proper firewall preparation.
solution: Online: Enable the RDP firewall rule via Serial Console: netsh advfirewall firewall set rule name="Remote Desktop - User Mode (TCP-In)" new enable=yes. Also enable WinRM (HTTP-In) and File/Printer Sharing rules for troubleshooting. Offline: Attach OS disk to rescue VM and modify firewall rules via registry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Guest OS Firewall Blocking Inbound Traffic_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Windows VM. All inbound connections blocked. VM screenshot shows OS at login. No connections in Guest OS logs. WaAppAgent heartbeat is normal but WinGuestAnalyzer shows RDP denied.
rootCause: One or more Guest OS firewall profiles (Domain/Private/Public) configured with BlockAllInboundConnections=True, overriding individual allow rules including RDP. May be set by GPO on corp domain-joined machines.
solution: Online via Serial Console: netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state off (temporary, re-enable after fixing rules). Or reset: netsh advfirewall set allprofiles firewallpolicy blockinbound,allowoutbound. Offline: Modify firewall policy in registry on rescue VM. For corp domain machines with SAW/network restrictions, redirect customer to MSIT.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Guest OS Firewall Blocking Inbound Traffic_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM. netsh advfirewall show allprofiles state returns error 0x45b. Windows Defender Firewall service (mpssvc) stuck in StartPending, repeatedly starting and stopping in System Event Log. Registry DebugedLoopbackApps has ~683 duplicate SID entries.
