rootCause: Duplicate SIDs accumulated in registry HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\mpssvc\Parameters\AppCs\DebugedLoopbackApps. Each admin login creates a new SID entry regardless of duplicates. At ~683 entries the firewall service enters a start/stop loop. Linked to Bug 37474982, supposedly fixed in KB5014019 but may recur on Server 2022.
solution: Create rescue nested VM (Hyper-V). Open regedit, navigate to HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\mpssvc\Parameters\AppCs. Rename DebugedLoopbackApps to DebugedLoopbackApps_ (change ownership to local admin if permission denied). Restart VM. Verify with netsh advfirewall show allprofiles state. Swap disk back to original VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Guest OS Firewall Stop and Start loop (Windows Defender Firewall) Error 0x45b_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to VM migrated from on-premises (via VHD upload or ASR). No NIC in ipconfig, only loopback. Guest agent reports no DHCP-enabled interface. System Event Log shows Hyper-V services (VMSMP, VM Management, Networking Management) starting up.
rootCause: The migrated VM was a Hyper-V host. The Hyper-V virtual switch (VMSMP) conflicts with Azure networking - needs a virtual NAT switch between Guest and Host hypervisors. Machine was not properly prepared before migration; Hyper-V role is incompatible without nested virtualization support.
solution: Do not migrate Hyper-V hosts directly to Azure. Create a new VM with nested virtualization support (supported sizes/regions, Windows Server 2016+ or Windows 10+). Prepare VHD properly before upload per docs.microsoft.com. For ASR migrations, additional preparation steps are required beyond what ASR handles automatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Hyper-V Host Migrated to Azure_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot SSH to Linux VM. No connectivity on VIP/DIP. Console serial log shows different DIP IP than Azure portal. Network config file has static IP (BOOTPROTO=static) that differs from Azure-assigned IP.
rootCause: Static IP hardcoded in Linux network configuration file does not match Azure DHCP-assigned IP. All NIC settings should be managed from Azure Portal. Only valid scenario for static IP in Guest OS is multiple IPs on one NIC.
solution: Change network config to DHCP. RHEL/CentOS: edit /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-eth0, set BOOTPROTO=dhcp. Ubuntu <18.04: edit /etc/network/interfaces, use iface eth0 inet dhcp. Ubuntu 18.04+: edit /etc/netplan/50-cloud-init.yaml, set dhcp4: true. SUSE: edit /etc/sysconfig/network/ifcfg-eth-id-*, set BOOTPROTO=dhcp. Restart networking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/IP Hardcoded on NIC Linux_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Windows VM. WinGuestAnalyzer Health Signal shows EnableDHCP=FALSE on primary NIC. ipconfig shows static IP different from Azure portal DIP. Guest Agent not ready (cannot communicate with WireServer). No internet icon on VM console screenshot.
rootCause: Static IP configured on Windows NIC inside Guest OS differs from Azure platform-assigned IP. Azure DHCP assigns the private IPv4 address; manually setting a different IP causes mismatch and breaks connectivity. Common after migration from on-premises.
solution: Online via Serial Console: netsh interface ip set address Ethernet dhcp. Or PowerShell: Set-NetIPInterface -InterfaceAlias Ethernet -Dhcp Enabled. Offline: Attach OS disk to rescue VM, load SYSTEM registry hive, navigate to ControlSet001\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces\{GUID}, set EnableDHCP=1 and delete IPAddress/SubnetMask/DefaultGateway values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/IP Hardcoded on NIC Windows_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Windows VM. VM screenshot shows OS at login screen. No connections in Guest OS logs. WaAppAgent heartbeat normal. VM has outbound connectivity but no inbound RDP/SSH access.
rootCause: Restrictive IPSec policy configured on the Guest OS blocking all inbound traffic. May be set via Group Policy, NAP client enforcement, or local IPSec policy.
solution: Online (2008/2008R2): netsh nap client set enforcement ID=79619 ADMIN=DISABLE. Online (2016+): PowerShell Disable-NetIPsecRule -All, or netsh advfirewall consec delete rule name=all. Offline: Load BROKENSOFTWARE registry hive, navigate to Policies\Microsoft\Windows\IPSEC\Policy\Local, delete ActivePolicy entry and all child keys. Unload hives and reassemble VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/IPSEC Policy Blocking Incoming Traffic_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP/SSH to Azure VM via VIP. VM screenshot shows OS at login. RDP via jumpbox to DIP works fine. Generic Remote Desktop connection error when connecting via VIP.
rootCause: Azure endpoint (Classic) misconfiguration: either (1) an ACL on the endpoint blocks traffic from client IP range, or (2) Floating IP (Direct Server Return) enabled on RDP/SSH endpoint, which should only be used for load-balanced cluster/SQLAlwaysON scenarios.
solution: For ACL issue: Go to Azure portal endpoint configuration, review and remove restrictive ACLs to restore access. For Floating IP: Delete the endpoint and recreate it with Floating IP disabled (default). This is a Classic VM scenario.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Incorrect Endpoint Config_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP/SSH to Azure VM. OS is fully loaded at CAD screen but VM is unreachable. ASC Stateful Test shows traffic blocked by NSG default rule DenyAllInBound or Azure Virtual Network Manager rule BlockHighRiskTCPPortsFromInternet.
rootCause: NSG security rules are blocking inbound RDP/SSH traffic. Either no Allow rule exists for port 3389/22 (default DenyAllInBound takes effect), or Azure Virtual Network Manager (AVNM) admin rule BlockHighRiskTCPPortsFromInternet is blocking high-risk TCP ports with higher priority than user-defined NSG rules.
solution: For standard NSG: Review Effective Security Rules and add/modify a customer-defined security rule to allow inbound RDP/SSH traffic. For AVNM rule: Contact the Azure network administrator who manages AVNM rules to modify or exempt the blocking rule. Use ASC Traffic Test or Portal Network Security Group Test blade to verify which rule is blocking.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Incorrect NSG Config_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM that belongs to Microsoft Corp Domain (internal MSFT employee). OS is fully loaded but VM is unreachable from non-SAW devices or specific network segments.
rootCause: AD Group Policy is blocking all connections except from specific network subnets and SAW devices, or the source and destination machines are in different network segments that cannot reach each other by design (Microsoft internal network policy).
solution: Verify AD policy by checking registry keys under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\WindowsFirewall\ConSecRules and related Phase/Crypto sets. If AD policy is blocking: customer must file a case with MSIT (not Azure scope). If network segments are unreachable: run tracert both ways to confirm, then redirect to MSIT for internal network routing issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/MS Corp Domain Joined VMs_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP/SSH to Azure VM. VM may be stuck booting or have no connectivity. MSConfig was used to disable services; registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Shared Tools\MSConfig\state\services has value different from 0. Network services like BFE/Windows Firewall/DHCP may show Stopped in WinGuestAnalyzer.
rootCause: Customer used MSConfig to customize system services startup, disabling critical services needed for OS boot, network stack, RDP service, or authentication.
solution: Restore to Normal Startup: ONLINE via Serial Console/RunCommands - re-enable disabled services (sc config <service> start=auto). OFFLINE - load BROKENSYSTEM registry hive, navigate to ControlSet00x\Services\<service>\Start and change from 4 (disabled) to 2 (auto) or 3 (manual). After fix, educate customer about MSConfig impact on Azure VMs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/MSConfig Disabled Service_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM. OS is fully loaded at CAD screen. No inbound connections possible. Brief connectivity (~1 minute) after VM restart then kicked out. McAfee VirusScan Enterprise 8.7/8.8 is installed.
rootCause: McAfee VirusScan Enterprise software is blocking all inbound connections due to misconfiguration.
solution: ONLINE: 1) Stop/disable McAfee services via sc stop/sc config start=disabled. 2) Uninstall via PowerShell: (Get-WmiObject -Class Win32_Product -Filter 'Name = McAfee VirusScan Enterprise').Uninstall(). OFFLINE: Create GPO startup script (gpt.ini + scripts.ini + FixAzureVM.cmd) with msiexec /x {product-GUID} REMOVE=ALL REBOOT=R /q to auto-uninstall at boot. GUIDs: 8.8={CE15D1B6-19B6-4D4D-8F43-CF5D2C3356FF}, 8.7i={147BCE03-C0F1-4C9F-8157-6A89B6D2D973}.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/McAfee Blocking Incoming Traffic_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM. OS loaded and waiting for credentials. No VIP/DIP connectivity. Event ID 7044 warning: Message Queuing service taking more than 16 minutes to start. Event ID 7022 error: Message Queuing service hung on starting. Other dependent services may also fail to start.
rootCause: Message Queuing (MSMQ) service is taking too long to start or hung, blocking the boot sequence and dependent services including network stack components.
solution: ONLINE: sc config msmq start=demand (set to manual). Check for other services in Start_Pending state: sc query | more, change 3rd-party ones to manual too. Restart VM from EMS. OFFLINE: Load BROKENSYSTEM hive, change HKLM\BROKENSYSTEM\ControlSet00x\Services\msmq\Start from 2 to 3. After recovery: reinstall MSMQ (uninstall from Roles/Features, reboot, reinstall). For further MSMQ analysis, cut case to BizTalk queue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Message Queueing Service not Running_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot SSH to Debian 9.1 (Stretch) Linux VM created from a specialized disk. No VIP/DIP connectivity. Serial Console log shows no network connectivity.
rootCause: Debian uses ens33 (or ens33p0) interface names instead of eth0, which violates Azure Linux network configuration requirements. The NIC is not recognized by the Azure fabric.
solution: Attach OS disk to rescue VM and chroot. 1) Fix GRUB: edit /etc/default/grub, set GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX with net.ifnames=0 biosdevname=0, then run update-grub. 2) Fix network config: edit /etc/network/interfaces, change interface name to eth0 with DHCP (auto eth0 / iface eth0 inet dhcp). Unmount, reassemble VM. Educate customer to follow Azure networking requirements for specialized images.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Misconfigured NIC Name_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM after configuring multiple IPs per NIC feature. Guest OS NIC set to static IP (DHCP disabled). Guest Agent log shows DHCP enable: NO and fails to discover fabric address. Additional secondary IPs visible in ipconfig.
rootCause: The primary IP for the primary NIC has a mismatched IP address between the Azure portal (fabric) configuration and the Guest OS static IP configuration. Setting static IPs in the Guest OS breaks Azure fabric communication.
solution: Re-enable DHCP on the primary NIC in the Guest OS. Azure VMs require DHCP mode for the primary IP; static IPs should be managed from the Azure Portal. For multiple IPs per NIC, follow the official Azure documentation to configure secondary IPs while keeping the primary on DHCP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Multiple IPs per NIC_RDP SSH

symptom: Linux VM deployed with multiple NICs but only primary NIC is visible/up; additional NICs are not configured or reachable.
rootCause: Additional NICs are not auto-configured depending on Linux distro: Ubuntu 16.04 had a regression, RHEL/CentOS 6 requires manual config, Fedora gallery image is pre-configured for single NIC only.
solution: Configure additional NIC manually in /etc/network/interfaces.d/50-cloud-init.cfg (add auto eth1 / iface eth1 inet dhcp). Add routing table entries: echo 200 test >> /etc/iproute2/rt_tables, ip rule add from <secondary-IP> table test, ip route add default via <gateway> dev eth1 table test. For Fedora: create custom image with multi-NIC support or use custom-data/cloud-init. Reboot after changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Multiple NICs_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM. NIC is disabled in Guest OS. Event ID 6 shows Miniport NIC is halting. WaAppAgent.log shows no active network card (only Software Loopback Interface). WinGuestAnalyzer NetworkAdapters section shows no active NIC.
rootCause: The network interface card (NIC) was manually disabled in the Guest OS, removing all network connectivity. NIC configuration should be managed from the Azure Portal, not inside the Guest OS.
solution: ONLINE via Serial Console: 1) netsh interface show interface (verify NIC is Disabled). 2) netsh interface set interface name=<NIC Name> admin=enable. OFFLINE: Attach disk to rescue VM, load BROKENSYSTEM hive, re-enable the NIC via registry. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-rdp-nic-disabled
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/NIC Disabled_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure VM. OS loaded at CAD screen. Red cross/yellow bang on network card icon or no icon. Guest OS logs show NIC initialized successfully but WaAppAgent.log shows no active network card (only Loopback). No VIP/DIP/PA connectivity.
rootCause: Network stack is corrupted - the system cannot properly process the network interface despite it being installed at the hardware level. The corruption prevents the NIC from obtaining an IP address and establishing connectivity.
solution: ONLINE: Mitigation 1: netsh winhttp reset proxy + powercfg /setactive SCHEME_MIN, reboot. Mitigation 2: netsh int ip reset c:\WindowsAzure\logs\NetworkStackReset.log, reboot. If still failing, check the reset log and contact RDP SME. OFFLINE: Do NOT remove tcpip\parameters\adapters or tcpip\parameters\interfaces subkeys (will worsen state). Use offline registry hive repair approach.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Network Stack Corrupted_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM Bugcheck 7B. Boot driver .sys NOT found in System32/drivers per 7Bchecks.
rootCause: Missing driver .sys in System32/drivers from corruption/update failure.
solution: Copy from DriverStore/FileRepository or identical server. SFC scan.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Use 7Bchecks.iso to troubleshoot Bugcheck 7B_Windows_Tools_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM Bugcheck 7B. Third-party filter drivers in Upperfilter/LowerFilter per 7Bchecks.
rootCause: Filter drivers interfere with boot device access.
solution: Remove third-party entries from HKLM ControlSet Services Upperfilter/LowerFilter.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Use 7Bchecks.iso to troubleshoot Bugcheck 7B_Windows_Tools_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM Bugcheck 7B. Pending/Staged updates per 7Bchecks DISM check.
rootCause: Pending updates left system inconsistent.
solution: dism /cleanup-image /revertpendingactions or /Remove-Package.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Use 7Bchecks.iso to troubleshoot Bugcheck 7B_Windows_Tools_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM Bugcheck 7B. NoBootDeviceCheck: driver Start value NOT BOOT.
rootCause: Driver Start not 0 (disabled/misconfigured).
solution: Load SYSTEM hive, set Start=0 for the driver, recreate VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Use NobootDeviceChecks tool to troubleshoot Bugcheck 7B_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP with local account despite correct password.
rootCause: Wrong auth context format (dot-backslash or bare username).
solution: Use backslash-username, DOMAIN-backslash-username, IP-backslash-username, or username@IP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/VM Password Reset_RDP SSH

symptom: Built-in admin disabled per WinGuestAnalyzer Health Signal.
rootCause: Admin disabled by GPO or manual action.
solution: VMAccess or Portal Reset Password. Or EnableAdminAccount Run Command.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/VM Password Reset_RDP SSH

symptom: Password reset fails. VMAccess cannot install/execute.
rootCause: VM Agent not installed/running/broken.
solution: Check agent in Health Signal. Start via Serial Console. Reinstall if missing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/VM Password Reset_RDP SSH

symptom: APIPA address 169.254.x.x. DHCP Client service disabled.
rootCause: DHCP Client service disabled/broken.
solution: sc config dhcp start=auto; net start dhcp via Serial Console.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Validate Container Initiated DHCP_RDP SSH

symptom: APIPA address. DHCP enabled but no IP. Fabric discovery failure.
rootCause: NmProgramming or Network Allocation incomplete.
solution: Check ASC IsNmProgrammingComplete. NetVMA for TOR. Engage Network team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Validate Container Initiated DHCP_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM has no RDP connectivity. VM screenshot shows OS fully loaded at credentials screen. WinHTTP Web Proxy Auto-Discovery Service (WinHttpAutoProxySvc) is not running. Event logs show Event ID 7022 (service hung on starting), Event ID 7001 (dependent DHCP Client service failed), or Event ID 7000 (account mismatch error).
rootCause: WinHttpAutoProxySvc service is not running due to: (1) service set to disabled, (2) service crashing, (3) service hanging, or (4) dependent service (DHCP Client) not running. Without this service, the VM cannot communicate with the Azure fabric wireserver.
solution: OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to rescue VM. Fix 1 (disabled): Enable service via registry HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\WinHttpAutoProxySvc - set Start=3 (Manual). Fix 2 (DHCP dependency): Also enable DHCP service. Fix 3 (account mismatch): Reset service logon account to LocalService. ONLINE: Use Run Command or Serial Console to start the service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/WinHTTP Web Proxy not Running_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM has no RDP connectivity. VM screenshot shows OS at credentials screen. Windows Firewall service (MpsSvc) is in Stopped state. Event logs may show Event ID 7022 (service hung), Event ID 7000 (service failed to start with error 1079), Event ID 7023 (service terminated with error), or no service start events at all (disabled).
rootCause: Windows Firewall service (MpsSvc) is not running due to: (1) service disabled, (2) service crashing, (3) service hanging, or (4) required dependent service not running. Note: Starting Windows Server 2016/Windows 10, display name changed to Windows Defender Firewall but service name remains MpsSvc.
solution: OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to rescue VM. Fix 1 (disabled): Enable service via registry HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\MpsSvc - set Start=2 (Automatic). Fix 2 (error 1079): Reset logon account via sc.exe config. Fix 3 (crash/hang): Collect dump for analysis. ONLINE: Use Run Command or Serial Console to diagnose and restart service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Windows Firewall not Running_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot SSH into Azure Linux VM, connection times out. No connectivity on VIP or DIP verified with VM Port Scanner. UFW firewall logs in /var/log/ufw.log show '[UFW BLOCK]' entries blocking inbound TCP traffic to DPT=22 (SSH port).
rootCause: UFW (Uncomplicated Firewall) is the guest OS firewall on the Linux VM and does not have rules configured to allow SSH traffic on port 22.
solution: Connect via Serial Console or Run Command. Allow SSH: 'sudo ufw allow 22/tcp' or 'sudo ufw allow OpenSSH'. Alternatively, temporarily disable UFW: 'sudo ufw disable'. Verify with 'sudo ufw status verbose'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/ufw Blocking SSH Traffic_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM shows BSOD with stop code 0x000000FC (ATTEMPTED_EXECUTE_OF_NOEXECUTE_MEMORY). VM screenshot displays: 'Your PC ran into a problem and needs to restart... ATTEMPTED_EXECUTE_OF_NOEXECUTE_MEMORY'. VM is in a boot loop.
rootCause: An attempt was made to execute non-executable memory, typically caused by a faulty driver. The driver name may be printed on the bugcheck screen or found in KiBugCheckDriver. Stack trace analysis of the kernel dump is needed to identify the specific driver.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required (guest OS is not operational). Attach OS disk to rescue VM. Collect kernel memory dump for analysis. Identify the faulting driver via dump analysis. Mitigation: uninstall/update the faulty driver, or restore from backup. Reference: Bug Check 0xFC documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/ATTEMPTED EXECUTE OF NOEXECUTE MEMORY 0x000000FC_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM screenshot shows an administrative CMD window with title 'Administrator: ERROR HANDLER'. The VM is stuck and not progressing to the login screen. This occurs after customer ran SYSPREP on the VM.
rootCause: ErrorHandler.CMD is part of the IaaS provisioning agent that should only run during initial provisioning from a generalized image. The cleanup steps (removing Unattend.wsf answer file and unmounting ISO from CDRom) were not performed after the initial provisioning. When customer ran SYSPREP and restarted, the leftover answer file triggered the provisioning agent again, causing it to get stuck.
solution: The image is likely broken. Change support topic to: Product: Azure Virtual Machine - Windows, Support Topic: Cannot create a VM > I am unable to deploy a captured or generalized image. Follow the Error Handler TSG for image recovery. May need to recreate the VM from a clean image.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Administrator Error Handler_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM screenshot shows: 'An operating system wasn't found. Try disconnecting any drivers that don't contain an operating system. Press Ctrl+Alt+Del to restart'. VM cannot boot.
rootCause: The OS boot process could not locate an active system partition. The system partition has been deactivated (e.g., by diskpart, third-party tool, or accidental modification).
solution: OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to rescue VM. Use diskpart to identify and activate the system partition: 'select disk 0' > 'list partition' > 'select partition <system_partition>' > 'active'. Detach and reboot original VM. Close code: Disk Partition Issues > Partition deactivated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/An OS Wasn't Found_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM screenshot shows: 'An operating system wasn't found'. VM cannot boot.
rootCause: Ransomware attack has encrypted or destroyed the boot sector and/or OS partition, making the operating system undetectable.
solution: Restore from backup if available. Check Azure Backup vault for recovery points. If no backup, attempt offline disk analysis to assess encryption extent. Consider engaging Microsoft Security Response team. Customer may need to redeploy from clean image.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/An OS Wasn't Found_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM is stuck during boot displaying 'Applying Audit Policy Configuration policy' message. VM does not progress to login screen. Seen on Windows Server 2012 R2 and 2016.
rootCause: Known OS Bug 5880648 - GPO (Group Policy Object) processing deadlock during boot. Can also be caused by 'delete profiles older than...' policy during profile load resulting in deadlock hang during OS startup.
solution: ONLINE: Use Run Command or Serial Console to disable the problematic GPO. OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to rescue VM, navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon and check GPExtensions. Remove or disable problematic audit policy GPO. References: SBSL topics 2667069, 2703143. Close code: Active Directory issues > GPO preventing RDP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Applying Audit Policy Configuration Policy_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM is stuck during boot displaying 'Applying computer settings' message. VM does not progress to login screen.
rootCause: Known OS Bug 5880648 - GPO processing deadlock during boot. Can also be caused by 'delete profiles older than...' policy during profile load resulting in deadlock hang during OS startup. Root cause 2 depends on dump analysis.
solution: ONLINE: Use Run Command or Serial Console to disable problematic GPO. OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to rescue VM via OSDisk Swap API or manual attach. Check and disable problematic GPO in registry. Collect dump for RCA 2 analysis. References: SBSL topics 2667069, 2703143. Close code: Active Directory issues > GPO preventing RDP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Applying Computer Settings_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM is stuck during boot displaying 'Applying Group Policy Environment policy' message. VM does not progress to login screen.
rootCause: Group Policy Environment policy processing hang during boot. Specific root cause depends on kernel dump analysis from GES (Guest Escalation Support).
solution: ONLINE only (OFFLINE not possible - scenario needs in-state analysis). Use Serial Console or Run Command to collect NMI crash dump. Engage Connectivity SMEs for dump analysis. Alternative: Use Nested Virtualization for full console access if datacenter supports it. Escalate to GES if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Applying Group Policy Environment Policy_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM not booting, stuck at "Applying Group Policy Local Users and Groups" on boot screenshot. OS is not fully up.
rootCause: OS Bug 5880648 - GPO Local Users and Groups policy processing causes hang/deadlock during boot. Can also be caused by profile load deadlock when "delete profiles older than" policy is set.
solution: 1) Backup OS disk. 2) ONLINE: Use hang scenario mitigation template (NMI crash dump + analysis). 3) OFFLINE: Mount OS disk on rescue VM, load BROKENSYSTEM hive, enable crash dump (CrashDumpEnabled=2, NMICrashDump=1), reassemble VM. 4) Escalate to Windows Devices and Deployment if needed. See also: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/windows/unresponsive-vm-apply-group-policy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Applying Group Policy Local Users and Groups_RDP SSH

solution: ONLINE troubleshooting: Use hang scenario mitigation template (run command / NMI for crash dump). OFFLINE not applicable. Escalate to RDP SME or Windows Devices and Deployment team for dump analysis.

symptom: Azure VM not booting, boot screenshot shows "Error ####### applying update operations ##### of #####" during Windows Update.
rootCause: OS unable to complete KB installation; failing in primitive operation queue executor phase. Specific root cause depends on the error code shown on screen.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required (Guest OS not operational): 1) Collect WU data. 2) Check error code: C0000265 -> WU failing due to hardlinks (wiki /495320). C01A001D -> ServicingError (wiki /499727) or lack of space (wiki /495321). Other codes -> general WU TSG (wiki /495322). Escalate to Windows Devices and Deployment if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Applying Update Operation_RDP SSH

symptom: VM screenshot shows BOOTMGR image is corrupt. The system cannot boot.
rootCause: BCD store is corrupted or inaccessible, usually after an unexpected VM restart caused by Azure Platform issue.
solution: Offline troubleshooting: stop/deallocate VM, snapshot OS disk, attach to rescue VM, rebuild BCD using bcdedit commands. For Gen1: bcdedit /store <Boot partition>:\boot\bcd /rebuildbcd. For Gen2: access EFI partition via diskpart, then rebuild BCD. Enable SAC and dump setup during repair.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/BOOTMGR Image Corrupt_RDP SSH

symptom: VM screenshot shows BOOTMGR is missing, Press Ctrl+Alt+Del to restart.
rootCause: OS boot process could not locate an active system partition. The partition holding BCD is not marked as active.
solution: Offline troubleshooting: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Use diskpart to set the system partition as active: select disk > select partition > active. Then rebuild BCD with bootrec /rebuildbcd.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/BOOTMGR Missing_RDP SSH

symptom: VM shows BSOD with stop code MSSECCORE_ASSERTION_FAILURE.
rootCause: Low disk space on the OS drive causes assertion failures in msseccore.
solution: Create rescue VM using az vm repair create, attach OS disk, increase OS drive size from Disk Management, then swap disk back to original VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/BSOD MSSECCORE ASSERTION FAILURE_RDP SSH

symptom: VM screenshot shows BitLocker recovery key prompt at boot: Plug in USB driver with BitLocker key, or Enter recovery key to get going again.
rootCause: VM unable to locate BitLocker BEK file to decrypt encrypted disk, typically because VM cannot access the Key Vault where encryption key is stored.
solution: First try stop/deallocate/start VM to force BEK retrieval from Key Vault (allow up to 15 min). If that fails, manually restore BEK: snapshot OS disk, attach to rescue VM (use New-AzDiskUpdateConfig -EncryptionSettingsEnabled $false to bypass encryption error), retrieve BEK from Key Vault using PowerShell (Get-AzKeyVaultSecret), download and write BEK to volume using manage-bde.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/BitLocker_RDP SSH

symptom: After ASR test failover, target VM fails to boot with grub2 files missing: /grub2/i386-pc/normal.mod not found, Entering rescue mode.
rootCause: OS disk was swapped while VM replication was enabled without reconfiguring replication, causing partial files missing in destination VM during failover.
solution: Cleanup test failover, disable replication, re-enable replication (reconfigure), then perform test failover again. Engage ABRS team if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Boot Failure ASR Failover_RDP SSH

symptom: VM screenshot shows: Boot failure. Reboot and Select proper Boot device or Insert Boot Media in selected Boot device.
rootCause: BCD store partition is not active, or BCD corruption causing OS unable to locate boot sector.
solution: Stop/deallocate/start VM first. If persists, offline troubleshooting: attach OS disk to rescue VM, use diskpart to set partition active, rebuild BCD with bootrec /fixmbr, bootrec /fixboot, bootrec /rebuildbcd. For Gen2 VMs, access EFI partition via diskpart assign.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Boot Failure_RDP SSH

symptom: Windows 10 VM crashes with Bugcheck 0x0000009F DRIVER_POWER_STATE_FAILURE, first parameter is 3.
rootCause: Code defect in Windows 10 (OS Bug 19651151): Turn off Hard Drives after 20mins feature enabled causes crash when OS returns from hibernation.
solution: Online fix: go to Power Options and set Turn off hard drives after value to 0 (disabled). If VM is not accessible (non-boot), use offline troubleshooting to collect memory dump and engage Windows on Azure SMEs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck 0x0000009F DRIVER POWER STATE FAILURE_RDP SSH

symptom: After installing July 2018 security patches (KB4338825, KB4338814, etc.), VMs running network workloads crash with Bugcheck 0x000000D1 DRIVER_IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL due to race condition.
rootCause: Regression caused by MSRC case 44653 shipped in July 2018 (07B) patches, introducing a race condition in network stack.
solution: If VM is accessible (online): install resolving KB (e.g., KB4345421 for RS4, KB4345419 for RS3, KB4345418 for RS1). If VM is non-boot (offline): use DISM to apply fix KB or uninstall breaking KB via rescue VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck 0x000000D1 MSRC 44653_RDP SSH

symptom: VM shows BSOD with stop code UNMOUNTABLE_BOOT_VOLUME (0x000000ED).
rootCause: I/O subsystem attempted to mount the boot volume and failed, typically due to disk corruption on the OS disk.
solution: Offline troubleshooting: attach OS disk to rescue VM, run chkdsk f: /f on OS disk. If VM has data disks hosting Windows service paths, also run chkdsk on data disks as they may have boot dependencies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck 0x000000ED UNMOUNTABLE BOOT VOLUME_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0xC0000139 (STATUS_ENTRY_POINT_NOT_FOUND). VM screenshot shows blue screen with error code 0xc0000139.
rootCause: Depends on memory dump analysis. The error code 0xC0000139 indicates a required entry point was not found in a DLL during system initialization.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required: (1) Backup OS disk, (2) Create rescue VM via OSDisk Swap API, (3) Enable SAC and configure dump setup (CrashDumpEnabled=2, NMICrashDump=1), (4) Swap OS disk back, (5) Reproduce and collect memory dump for analysis. See Gen1/Gen2 specific BCD configuration steps in TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck 0xC0000139_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0xC0000145. VM screenshot shows blue screen with error code 0xC0000145.
rootCause: Depends on memory dump analysis.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required: (1) Backup OS disk, (2) Create rescue VM via OSDisk Swap API, (3) Enable SAC and configure dump setup, (4) Collect memory dump for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck 0xC0000145_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x00000019 (BAD_POOL_HEADER). VM screenshot shows BAD_POOL_HEADER stop error.
rootCause: Pool is already corrupted at the time of the current request. This may or may not be due to the caller. A dump analysis is needed to identify what is causing the pool corruption.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required: Backup OS disk, create rescue VM via OSDisk Swap API, enable SAC and configure dump, collect and analyze memory dump to identify pool corruption source. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/bug-check-0x19--bad-pool-header
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck BAD POOL HEADER_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x00000074 (BAD_SYSTEM_CONFIG_INFO). VM screenshot shows BAD_SYSTEM_CONFIG_INFO stop error.
rootCause: SYSTEM registry hive is corrupt, or critical registry keys and values are missing. Can be caused by manual registry edits or an application/service corrupting the registry.
solution: OFFLINE mitigation: (1) Run CHKREG to repair registry corruption on rescue VM, (2) Restore registry from RegBack backup folder. Both require rescue VM via OSDisk Swap API. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/bug-check-0x74--bad-system-config-info
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck BAD SYSTEM CONFIG INFO_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x000000C5 (DRIVER_CORRUPTED_EXPOOL). VM screenshot shows DRIVER_CORRUPTED_EXPOOL stop error.
rootCause: Kernel attempted to access pageable memory (or invalid memory) when IRQL was too high. Almost certainly caused by a driver that has corrupted the system pool.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required: Backup OS disk, create rescue VM, collect and analyze memory dump to identify which driver is corrupting the system pool. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/bug-check-0xc5--driver-corrupted-expool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck DRIVER CORRUPTED EXPOOL_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x000000D1 (DRIVER_IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL) after applying July (07B) security patches.
rootCause: Regression caused by MSRC case 44653 shipped in July 07B patches.
solution: Uninstall the problematic July 07B security update via rescue VM (OSDisk Swap API). Follow WUP (Windows Update Problem) mitigation template to remove the offending KB.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck DRIVER IRQL NOT LESS OR EQUAL_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x000000D1 (DRIVER_IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL). VM screenshot shows DRIVER_IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL error. If "What failed: myfault.sys" is shown, crash was intentional via NotMyFault.
rootCause: A driver accessed pageable memory at too high IRQL. Depends on dump analysis to identify the faulty driver.
solution: If screenshot shows "What failed: myfault.sys", the crash was intentional via NotMyFault application - no analysis needed, dismiss. Otherwise OFFLINE troubleshooting: backup OS disk, rescue VM, collect and analyze memory dump. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/bug-check-0xd1--driver-irql-not-less-or-equal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck DRIVER IRQL NOT LESS OR EQUAL_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x000000CE (DRIVER_UNLOADED_WITHOUT_CANCELLING_PENDING_OPERATIONS). VM screenshot shows the error with a .sys file reference.
rootCause: A driver failed to cancel lookaside lists, DPCs, worker threads, or other pending operations before unloading.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required: Backup OS disk, create rescue VM, collect and analyze memory dump to identify the culprit driver. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/bug-check-0xce--driver-unloaded-without-cancelling-pending-operations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck DRIVER UNLOADED WITHOUT CANCELLING PENDING OPERATIONS_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x0000000A (IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL). VM has more than 64 vCPUs configured with an unsupported Guest OS.
rootCause: The Guest OS does not support the number of vCPUs configured on the VM. More than 64 vCPUs require Windows Server 2016, Ubuntu 16.04 LTS, SLES 12 SP2, RHEL, CentOS 7.3, or Oracle Linux 7.3 with LIS 4.2.1.
solution: Mitigation 1: Resize the VM to fewer than 64 vCPUs and start the VM. Mitigation 2: Rebuild the VM using a supported Guest OS (Windows Server 2016 Datacenter, Ubuntu 16.04 LTS, SLES 12 SP2, RHEL, CentOS 7.3, or Oracle Linux 7.3 with LIS 4.2.1).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck IRQL NOT LESS OR EQUAL_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x0000000A (IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL). VM screenshot shows IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL stop error.
rootCause: Depends on dump analysis.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting: Backup OS disk, create rescue VM via OSDisk Swap API, enable SAC and configure dump, collect memory dump for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck IRQL NOT LESS OR EQUAL_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x00000139 (KERNEL_SECURITY_CHECK_FAILURE). VM screenshot shows KERNEL_SECURITY_CHECK_FAILURE stop error.
rootCause: Kernel detected corruption of a critical data structure. The first parameter of the bugcheck indicates the type of corruption. Dump analysis needed to identify corruption source.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required: Backup OS disk, create rescue VM, collect memory dump, analyze dump to identify bugcheck parameters and corruption source. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/bug-check-0x139--kernel-security-check-failure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck KERNEL SECURITY CHECK FAILURE_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM crashes with bugcheck 0x0000001E (KMODE_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED). VM screenshot shows KMODE_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED with a .sys file reference.
rootCause: A kernel-mode program generated an exception which the error handler did not catch. Dump analysis needed to identify which exception was generated.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting required: Backup OS disk, create rescue VM, collect and analyze memory dump to identify the unhandled exception. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/bug-check-0x1e--kmode-exception-not-handled
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck KMODE EXCEPTION NOT HANDLED_RDP SSH

symptom: Windows VM screenshot shows CHKDSK is in progress during boot, VM is stuck at disk check screen
rootCause: Dirty flag was set on the OS disk (by user action or NTFS corruption), forcing CHKDSK to run on restart
solution: Wait for CHKDSK to complete (can take hours on large disks). If VM recovered, check Application Event Log ID 1001 for CHKDSK report. If stuck, use offline rescue VM to run chkdsk on attached disk. Public doc: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/troubleshoot-check-disk-boot-error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/CHKDSK In Progress_RDP SSH

symptom: Windows VM BSOD with error 0x00000067 CONFIG_INITIALIZATION_FAILED, VM cannot boot
rootCause: IMC (Initial Machine Configuration) reference exists in Boot Loader (imcdevice, imchivename) but corresponding registry keys are missing
solution: Offline: Attach OS disk to rescue VM. Mitigation 1: delete IMC entries from BCD via bcdedit /store <drive>:\boot\bcd /deletevalue {identifier} imcdevice AND imchivename. Mitigation 2: Create ErrorControlPolicy (REG_DWORD=0) under HKLM\BROKENSYSTEM\ControlSet00X\Control\InitialMachineConfig\_Global and \System
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/CONFIG INITIALIZATION FAILED_RDP SSH

symptom: VM screenshot shows "A disk read error occurred. Press Ctrl+Alt+Del to restart"
rootCause: OS disk structure is corrupted and unreadable
solution: Stop-deallocate and start the VM. If persists, attach OS disk to rescue VM, backup disk, enable SAC and crash dump, run offline disk repair (chkdsk). Close code: Disk Corruption - Chkdsk
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Disk Read Error Occurred_RDP SSH

symptom: Active Directory domain controller VM in reboot loop with error 0xC00002E2 (STATUS_DS_INIT_FAILURE) or 0xC00002E1 (STATUS_DS_CANT_START), LSASS.exe triggers automatic restart
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) No access to disk holding NTDS.DIT, (2) No free space on NTDS.DIT disk, (3) NTDS.DIT file missing, (4) SAN policy not ONLINEALL so data disks offline, (5) NTDS.DIT corrupt
solution: Offline: (RCA1) Check disk/storage access; (RCA2) Expand disk or free space if <300MB; (RCA3) Check NTDS.DIT via registry HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\NTDS\parameters; (RCA4) Set SAN policy OnlineAll via diskpart; (RCA5) AD DB repair. Public doc: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshoot-directory-service-initialization-failure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Domain Controller in Reboot Loop_RDP SSH

symptom: VM shows error 0xC0000605 "A component of the operating system has expired" and cannot boot
rootCause: VM was built from a preview/trial OS image (not RTM release), trial period has expired. Preview images have designated lifecycle (e.g. 180 days for Windows)
solution: No fix. Customer must delete VM (keep disks), mount OS disk to another VM to copy data, rebuild from production RTM image. Azure preview images are not for production. Close code: Unsupported OS
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error 0xC0000605_RDP SSH

symptom: Windows VM stuck during boot with "Error C0000265 applying update operations" during Windows Update, SFC fails with "system repair pending"
rootCause: Core file during KB installation reached NTFS hardlink limit. PendingDeletes entries in WinSxS\Temp\PendingDeletes accumulate and block primitive operations queue executor
solution: Offline: (1) Open \windows\winsxs\poqexec.log, find line with error c0000265 to identify blocked file. (2) fsutil hardlink list <drive>:\<file path> to see all hardlinks. (3) Delete accumulated $$DeleteMe.* entries from WinSxS\Temp\PendingDeletes to reduce hardlink count below limit
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error C0000265 applying update operations Due to Hardlinks_RDP SSH

symptom: Windows VM stuck during boot with "Error C01A001D applying update operations" during Windows Update due to lack of disk space
rootCause: OS disk is full, system cannot write files during KB installation causing primitive operations queue to fail
solution: Offline: Attach OS disk to rescue VM, free disk space (delete temp files, old updates, expand disk). After freeing space, reassemble VM. Close code: OS Disk Space
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error C01A001D applying update operations Due to Lack of Space_RDP SSH

symptom: Windows VM stuck with "Error C01A001D applying update operations" after June 2021 patch, Event 7023 "Log space is exhausted", CBS.LOG shows ERROR_LOG_FULL (0x800719e4)
rootCause: CLFS transaction log creation fails. Base container file (txr\*.TxR.blf) has SE_SACL_PROTECTED flag, system account lacks SeSecurityPrivilege to create new .regtrans-ms files. 5MB containers fill up, CLFS cannot add new ones
solution: Offline: (1) dism.exe /image:<drive>:\ /cleanup-image /revertpendingactions. (2) Load SYSTEM hive, check active ControlSet. (3) reg add HKLM\brokenSYSTEM\ControlSet00X\Policies\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides /v 816927371 /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f. (4) Unload hive, reassemble VM, continue June update. ICM 245605242. Doc: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/unresponsive-vm-apply-windows-update
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error C01A001D applying update operations_RDP SSH

symptom: Linux VM shows GRUB minimal BASH prompt "Minimal BASH-like line editing is supported", cannot SSH to VM
rootCause: GRUB unable to start: (1) grub.cfg missing, (2) incorrect GRUB config, or (3) /boot partition or contents missing
solution: Method 1: Rescue VM + chroot. RHEL/CentOS Gen1: grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg. Gen2: yum reinstall grub2-efi-x64 shim-x64 then grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg then sed -i s/hd2/hd0/g. Ubuntu: grub-install /dev/sdX then update-grub. Method 2: az vm repair create + run --run-id linux-alar2 --parameters grubfix(Gen1)/efifix(Gen2). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/linux/troubleshoot-vm-boot-error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error Minimal BASH like line editing is supported_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM not booting - screenshot shows CHKDSK (disk consistency check) is in progress, VM stuck at disk verification stages
rootCause: Disk consistency check (CHKDSK) was triggered on the OS disk or data disk, possibly due to improper shutdown, disk corruption, or scheduled check. VM cannot proceed until CHKDSK completes.
solution: Wait for CHKDSK to complete. If VM comes back up, check Application event log for Event ID 1001 (Microsoft-Windows-Wininit) to review CHKDSK results. If CHKDSK is stuck, consider offline troubleshooting: attach OS disk to rescue VM and run chkdsk /f on the affected volume.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/CHKDSK In Progress_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM BSOD with Stop Error 0x00000067 CONFIG_INITIALIZATION_FAILED - VM cannot boot, blue screen displayed
rootCause: IMC (Initial Machine Configuration) reference is set in the Boot Loader (bcdedit shows imcdevice and imchivename entries pointing to IMC.hiv) but the corresponding registry references are missing, causing the OS to fail initialization.
solution: Offline fix: attach OS disk to rescue VM, open elevated CMD, run bcdedit /store <drive>:\boot\bcd /enum to confirm imcdevice/imchivename entries exist, then delete them with: bcdedit /store <drive>:\boot\bcd /deletevalue {identifier} imcdevice && bcdedit /store <drive>:\boot\bcd /deletevalue {identifier} imchivename. Reassemble original VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/CONFIG INITIALIZATION FAILED_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM displays "A disk read error occurred. Press Ctrl+Alt+Del to restart" - VM cannot boot
rootCause: The OS disk structure is corrupted and unreadable, preventing the boot loader from reading the disk contents.
solution: Stop/deallocate and start the VM. If persists, attach OS disk to rescue VM (offline). Run chkdsk /f on the OS partition. Enable SAC and configure dump collection for further analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Disk Read Error Occurred_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM displays "A disk read error occurred" - boot partition deactivated
rootCause: The partition holding the boot configuration data (BCD) is not set as active, so the BIOS/firmware cannot find the boot loader.
solution: Offline fix: attach OS disk to rescue VM, open diskpart, select the BCD/boot partition, run 'active' command to mark it active. Reassemble original VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Disk Read Error Occurred_RDP SSH

symptom: Domain Controller VM in reboot loop with 0xC00002E2 (STATUS_DS_INIT_FAILURE) or 0xC00002E1 (STATUS_DS_CANT_START) - LSASS.exe crashes and restarts OS
rootCause: The Active Directory database (NTDS.DIT) is inaccessible, missing, or corrupt. The Domain Controller OS cannot initialize directory services without read/write access to its local AD database.
solution: Offline: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Verify NTDS.DIT file exists at the path in registry (HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\DSA Database File). If missing, restore from backup. If corrupt, attempt repair with ntdsutil or esentutl /p. If unrecoverable, use DSRM boot mode for data recovery. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshoot-directory-service-initialization-failure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Domain Controller in Reboot Loop_RDP SSH

symptom: Domain Controller VM in reboot loop with 0xC00002E2/0xC00002E1 - no free space on disk holding NTDS.DIT
rootCause: The disk holding the Active Directory database (NTDS.DIT) has run out of free space, preventing the OS from performing read/write operations on the AD database.
solution: Offline: attach OS disk to rescue VM, expand the disk or clean up space on the volume holding NTDS.DIT. Then reassemble the VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Domain Controller in Reboot Loop_RDP SSH

symptom: Domain Controller VM in reboot loop with 0xC00002E2/0xC00002E1 - data disks showing offline in Disk Management
rootCause: SAN policy is not set to ONLINEALL, causing non-OS disks (which may hold NTDS.DIT) to be attached in OFFLINE mode. The DC cannot access its AD database on the offline disk.
solution: Offline: attach OS disk to rescue VM, load SYSTEM registry hive, set SAN policy to OnlineAll: reg add HKLM\BROKENSYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\partmgr\Parameters /v SanPolicy /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/prepare-for-upload-vhd-image
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Domain Controller in Reboot Loop_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM fails to boot with error 0xC0000605 "A component of the operating system has expired" - Windows preview image trial period ended
rootCause: The VM was built from a Windows Server preview/trial image (not RTM). Preview images have a designated lifecycle (typically 180-day trial) and after expiration the OS refuses to boot.
solution: This cannot be mitigated. The customer must delete the VM (keep disks), mount the OS disk to another VM to copy data, and rebuild using a supported RTM image. Ref: KB4010131.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error 0xC0000605_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM stuck during Windows Update with error C0000265 applying update operations - hardlink limit reached, SFC fails with "system repair pending"
rootCause: During KB installation, a core file cannot be created because the filesystem hardlink limit has been reached. The poqexec.log shows error c0000265 on a HardLinkFile operation. Pending deletion entries in WinSxS\Temp\PendingDeletes accumulate and block new hardlinks.
solution: Offline: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Open poqexec.log (\windows\winsxs\poqexec.log) to identify the blocked file. Run fsutil hardlink list <drive>:\<file path> to see all hardlinks. Delete stale PendingDeletes entries ($$DeleteMe.* files in WinSxS\Temp\PendingDeletes) to free hardlink slots. Reassemble VM and retry update.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error C0000265 applying update operations Due to Hardlinks_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM stuck during Windows Update with error C01A001D applying update operations - unable to write files to disk
rootCause: The OS disk is full, preventing Windows Update from writing files during KB installation. The primitive operations queue executor fails because there is no space to create/modify files.
solution: Offline: attach OS disk to rescue VM, free up disk space (delete temp files, expand OS disk, move pagefile). Reassemble VM and retry update.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error C01A001D applying update operations Due to Lack of Space_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM stuck during Windows Update with error C01A001D, Service Control Manager event 7023 "Log space is exhausted", CBS.log shows ERROR_LOG_FULL (0x800719e4) - specific to June 2021 patches
rootCause: During patch installation, poqexec creates many registry transaction keys. CLFS tries to create a new transaction file but fails because the base container file (TxR.blf) has SE_SACL_PROTECTED flag. System account lacks SeSecurityPrivilege, causing CreateFile to fail with STATUS_PRIVILEGE_NOT_HELD.
solution: Offline: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Fix the security descriptor on the CLFS base container file (C:\windows\system32\config\txr\*.TxR.blf) to remove SE_SACL_PROTECTED flag. Ref: ICM 245605242, public doc: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/unresponsive-vm-apply-windows-update
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error C01A001D applying update operations_RDP SSH

symptom: Linux VM cannot boot via SSH, drops to GRUB shell showing "Minimal BASH-like line editing is supported" prompt
rootCause: GRUB bootloader is unable to start because: (1) GRUB configuration file (/boot/grub2/grub.cfg or /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg) is missing, (2) incorrect GRUB configuration, or (3) /boot partition or its contents are missing.
solution: Create rescue VM, enter chroot environment. Regenerate GRUB config: RHEL/CentOS Gen1: grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg; Gen2 UEFI: yum reinstall grub2-efi-x64 shim-x64 && grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg && sed -i 's/hd2/hd0/g' /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg; Ubuntu: grub-install /dev/sdX && update-grub; SLES: grub2-install /dev/sdX && grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Error Minimal BASH like line editing is supported_RDP SSH

symptom: ACSS SAP system registration fails with error code SIDNotFoundOnGivenAscsVm; error message states SID not found on given ASCS VM with possible cause that SAP hostctrl agent and sapstartsrv process are not running
rootCause: sapstartsrv process not running on ASCS VM for hostctrl agent and/or SAP instances; SAP services for the specific SID not running on ASCS/App/DB VMs; or incorrect ASCS VM input (SID not present on that VM)
solution: 1) Check if sapstartsrv running: ps -ef | grep sapstartsrv. 2) Start hostctrl: <hostctrlexepath>/hostexecstart -start. 3) Start instance: <hostctrlexepath>/sapcontrol -nr <AscsInstanceNr> -function StartService {SID}. 4) Verify SID on VM: saphostctrl -function ListInstances. 5) Retry Register. If SID name is wrong, delete VIS and re-register.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Register SIDNotFoundOnGivenAscsVm_ACSS

symptom: ACSS VIS resource status changes to Unknown after customer changes managed storage account public network access to selected networks; HealthAndStatusMonitoringExtensionStatusUnknown error in VIS overview; extension Microsoft.Azure.Workloads.MonitoringExtensionLinux shows Provisioning failed with UnableToFetchMonitoringScriptFromStorageAccount
rootCause: ACSS health monitoring loop runs at 30-second frequency uploading VM status to managed storage account. When storage account is switched to selected networks, VIS and script running inside VM are unaware, causing storage not reachable failures and VIS status to go unknown
solution: Run: az workloads sap-virtual-instance update --ids <resource-id-VIS> --managed-resources-network-access-type private. This automatically updates storage account configuration. Then delete the monitoring extension (MonitoringExtensionLinux or Windows) from central server instance VM - service auto-reinstalls within 4 hours. Ensure user-assigned managed identity has ACSS service role on Compute RG and Reader role on VNet RG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Register VIS Status Unknown After Restricting StorageAccount Network Access_ACSS

symptom: ACSS registration fails with VmExtensionContainerNotFound; Workloads VM extension on VM cannot find the storage account container (e.g. DiscoverDetailsFromScsInstance.ps1) in the Managed Resource Group storage account
rootCause: During registration, Workloads extension unable to reach blobs on referenced managed storage account. Possible causes: managed resource group storage account container was deleted, or VM is in hung state (df -h command hangs)
solution: 1) Verify all registration pre-requisites at learn.microsoft.com/azure/sap/center-sap-solutions/register-existing-system. 2) Run df -h on VM; if it hangs, reboot VM and confirm command works. 3) Retry Register. 4) If container was deleted, delete the VIS resource and re-register. Escalate via ICM to ACSS SMEs if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Register VmExtensionContainerNotFound_ACSS

symptom: ACSS SAP system registration fails when creating managed Storage account; error: You must enable Azure Storage service endpoint on subnets of VM corresponding to this SAP system; public internet connectivity is blocked in customer architecture
rootCause: ACSS creates managed Storage account without private endpoint during registration. When public access is disabled and Microsoft.Storage service endpoint is not configured on SAP subnets, VM cannot connect to managed storage account. Private endpoints are NOT supported for managed Storage account in ACSS registration.
solution: Enable Microsoft.Storage service endpoint on ALL SAP subnets: Portal > Virtual Network > Subnet > Service Endpoints > Add Microsoft.Storage. Check NSG has no outbound deny rules blocking Azure Storage traffic and UDRs do not redirect to unsupported paths. Private endpoints are not supported here. Retry registration. Escalate to Azure Workloads Platform as Service team via ICM if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/SAP Registration Failure Due to Storage Endpoint Configuration in ACSS_ACSS

symptom: SAP software download via ACSS Ansible playbook (SDAF) fails with errors when S-user account password contains special characters such as $
rootCause: SAP login API does not support special characters in passwords when passed as a command-line argument in the Ansible playbook command
solution: Set SPASS environment variable with single quotes before running the Ansible playbook: export SPASS='password_with_special_chars'. Then use -e "s_password=$SPASS" in the ansible-playbook command instead of passing password directly. Reference: learn.microsoft.com/azure/sap/center-sap-solutions/install-software#special-characters-like--in-s-user-password
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Software Download Common issues when download ansible_ACSS

symptom: SAP software download via ACSS Ansible playbook (SDAF) fails because S-user ID does not have authorization to download SAP software
rootCause: S-user account lacks required permissions/authorization to download SAP software from the SAP portal
solution: Validate S-user access by logging into SAP portal and verifying download permissions. Obtain proper authorization for the S-user account to download SAP software. Then retry the Ansible playbook.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Deployment TSGs/Software Download Common issues when download ansible_ACSS

symptom: ACSS infrastructure deployment fails and VIS resource is left in error state
rootCause: Transient deployment failure or misconfiguration during infrastructure creation via Azure Center for SAP Solutions
solution: Delete the old VIS in error state via VIS overview page > Delete button. Note: deletion is permanent, removes VIS/ABAP SAP Central Service/App and Database Instances but NOT physical resources (VMs, Disks, NICs). After deletion, recreate the infrastructure. Previously installed systems can be re-registered via Add option on ACSS Center Hub.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Known Issues_ACSS

symptom: ACSS SAP software installation fails with AnsibleTaskExecutionFailed; variants: scs-ha-install ERS Install SAPInst failed, Execute hdblcm on failed, SCS HA Install Assign ownership failed; possible causes: non-zero return code or transient issue
rootCause: Transient Ansible task execution failure or non-zero return code during SAP installation via ACSS
solution: Retry the installation from Azure portal. If issue persists, delete the VIS and create the infrastructure again, then retry installation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Known Issues_ACSS

symptom: ACSS SAP installation fails with Ansible task error: roles-os/1.3-repository 1.3 Repository Check that zypper repos are registered failed on host
rootCause: SUSE IP addresses are not allowed in NSG or firewall for app and DB subnets, preventing access to SUSE repositories
solution: Allow SUSE IP addresses in NSG/Firewall for app and DB subnets following: docs.microsoft.com/azure/center-sap-solutions/prepare-network#allowlist-suse-or-red-hat-endpoints. Retry install from portal. If issue persists, delete VIS, recreate infrastructure, and retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Known Issues_ACSS

symptom: ACSS SAP installation fails with Ansible task error: roles-os/1.4-packages 1.4 Packages Update OS packages REDHAT-8 failed on host
rootCause: RedHat IP addresses are not allowed in NSG or firewall for app and DB subnets, preventing access to Red Hat package repositories
solution: Allow RedHat IP addresses in NSG/Firewall for app and DB subnets following: docs.microsoft.com/azure/center-sap-solutions/prepare-network#allowlist-suse-or-red-hat-endpoints. Retry install from portal. If issue persists, delete VIS, recreate infrastructure, and retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Known Issues_ACSS

symptom: ACSS registration fails with error code AzureVmIsNotInDesiredPowerState; error message states VM is in undesired power state and recommends ensuring VM power status is running
rootCause: The VM referenced in the error message is not in Running state - it is in Stopped (deallocated) state
solution: Start the virtual machine mentioned in the error: Go to Virtual Machines > select the VM > Start. Verify VM power status in VM > Settings > Properties > Status. After VM is running, retry Register.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Monitoring TSGs/Azure VM Is Not in Desired Power State_ACSS

symptom: Database status shown as Unavailable in Azure Portal ACSS VIS overview despite database actually running normally
rootCause: Database list not passed to Health and Status Script Arguments during discovery. Occurs when: (1) database is non-HANA DB (DB details not discovered in discovery workflow), or (2) customer using multi-tenant database cluster which is not supported by ACSS health monitoring
solution: Inform customer that multi-tenant database cluster architecture is not supported for ACSS health monitoring. Non-HANA databases are also not fully supported for status discovery. Reference ICM: Incident-414904514.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Center for SAP Solutions (ACSS)/TSGs/Monitoring TSGs/Database Showing Unavailable_ACSS

symptom: ADE-encrypted VM shows encryption status mismatch (manage-bde reports 100% encrypted internally but Azure Portal/PS/CLI shows not encrypted, or vice versa) after Azure Site Recovery (ASR) failover to secondary region
rootCause: ADE extension is not automatically reinstalled on the failover VM after ASR failover. ASR replication does not preserve the ADE extension on the target VM, causing reported encryption status to diverge from actual BitLocker state.
solution: 1. RDP into failover VM, run manage-bde -status to confirm actual encryption state. 2. Check ASC Extensions tab to verify if ADE extension is installed on failover VM. 3. If extension missing: install it - With AAD (ver 1.1): Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension with -AadClientID/-AadClientSecret. Without AAD (ver 2.2): standard extension install. 4. After install, verify status matches in Portal, PS, CLI, and internal manage-bde. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/site-recovery/azure-to-azure-how-to-enable-replication-ade-vms
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/ADE with ASR_Encryption

symptom: VM shows phantom/stale ADE encryption status in Azure Portal or PowerShell (reports Enabled) even after customer attempted to disable ADE and remove extension - actual BitLocker shows Protection Off in manage-bde -status or lsblk
rootCause: ADE removal process was incomplete or platform was not properly updated during removal, leaving encryption metadata (EncryptionSettings) still stamped in the VM model or disk resource despite BitLocker actually being disabled.
solution: Validate BitLocker disabled first (manage-bde -status shows Protection Off for all volumes; backup VM). Dual Pass: $vm=Get-AzVM; $vm.storageprofile.osdisk.encryptionsettings=$null; $vm | Update-AzVM. Single Pass: Detach disk, run $disk.EncryptionSettingsCollection=@{}; $disk | Update-AzDisk, then reattach/swap OS disk. For OS disk take snapshot first before clearing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Clear Encryption Settings_Encryption

symptom: Attempt to disable ADE encryption on Windows VM OS disk only fails or has no effect - Disable-AzVMDiskEncryption with VolumeType OS on Windows VM does not work or behaves unexpectedly
rootCause: Azure Disk Encryption does not support disabling encryption on the OS disk only for Windows VMs - this is an architectural limitation of ADE for Windows.
solution: Use VolumeType ALL to disable encryption on both OS and Data disks together. PowerShell: Disable-AzVMDiskEncryption -VolumeType ALL. CLI: az vm encryption disable --volume-type ALL. Then remove extension: Remove-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension. Note: On Linux, disabling Data disk only via Portal IS supported (Disks > Additional Settings > change to None).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/How Tos/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Disable ADE on Data Disks_Encryption

symptom: Windows VM ADE encryption fails with error: [2.2.x] Failed to configure bitlocker as expected. Exception: Encrypt failed with 2147942487 (0x80070057 - invalid parameter). Extension logs in C:\WindowsAzure\Logs\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Security.AzureDiskEncryption show this error.
rootCause: Incompatible BitLocker Group Policy (GPO/Local GPO) settings: (1) policy does not allow 256-bit recovery key required by ADE, (2) TPM protectors enforced - ADE uses external key protector not TPM, (3) MBAM group policy features enabled which are incompatible with ADE, (4) domain policy blocks AES-CBC cipher used by BitLocker with ADE.
solution: 1. Collect: extension logs, registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE and HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE, gpresult /h gpreport.html. 2. Fix BitLocker policy: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > BitLocker Drive Encryption > Choose how BitLocker-protected drives can be recovered > Allow 256-bit recovery key. 3. Remove any GPO that enforces TPM protectors. 4. Disable MBAM group policy features. 5. Ensure domain policy does not block AES-CBC. 6. Run gpupdate /force, reboot if needed, retry ADE. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/disk-encryption-overview#group-policy-requirements
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker as Expected Exception 2147942487_Encryption

symptom: Windows VM ADE encryption fails with Encrypt failed with 2147942487 and no obvious BitLocker GPO configuration conflicts are found after checking all policy settings
rootCause: BitLocker WMI provider corruption: the win32_encryptablevolume WMI class is not properly registered or the MOF file is damaged, preventing ADE from interacting with BitLocker via WMI.
solution: Recompile the BitLocker WMI MOF file from an elevated command prompt: Mofcomp.exe C:\Windows\System32\wbem\win32_encryptablevolume.mof - then reboot the VM and retry ADE encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker as Expected Exception 2147942487_Encryption

symptom: Windows VM ADE Dual Pass encryption fails with Failed to configure BitLocker as expected. Exception: AADSTS70002: Error validating credentials. AADSTS50012: Invalid client secret is provided - when running Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension with -AadClientID/-AadClientSecret
rootCause: (1) Wrong AadClientSecret (client secret/password) string provided for the AAD App registration used for Dual Pass ADE. (2) AAD App credential (client secret) has expired - default lifetime is 1 year when created.
solution: 1. Verify correct AadClientSecret: confirm variable has the right password string. 2. Check credential expiry: $ADEApp=Get-AzADApplication -DisplayName AppName; Get-AzADAppCredential -ObjectId $ADEApp.ObjectId - check EndDate field. 3. If expired: New-AzADAppCredential -ObjectId $ADEApp.ObjectId -Password $newSecurePassword. 4. Retry Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension with new -AadClientSecret value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker as Expected Exception AADSTS_Encryption

symptom: Windows VM ADE encryption fails with Failed to configure bitlocker as expected. Exception: System.Net.WebException: An error occurred while sending the request. InnerException: The underlying connection was closed: An unexpected error occurred on a receive
rootCause: .NET Framework on the VM is configured to use legacy SSL protocols (SSL 2.0+) rather than TLS 1.2. TLS 1.2 is required for ADE extension communication with Azure Key Vault, causing the SSL/TLS handshake to fail.
solution: 1. Deselect TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1. 2. Add registry keys to force .NET Framework to use TLS 1.2: set SchUseStrongCrypto=dword:00000001 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727, HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319, HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319, HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727. 3. Reboot the VM. 4. Retry ADE encryption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker as Expected_Encryption

symptom: RoboCopy fails with access denied when copying files to Azure Files share using AD (Kerberos) authentication; robocopy /sec fails on WRITE_OWNER (Take Ownership) permission check
rootCause: No AD user has Take Ownership (WRITE_OWNER) permission on Azure Files shares during AD Authentication preview; access check for setting file owner fails for all AD users
solution: Mount Azure Files using storage account key instead of AD credentials for robocopy operations - storage account key grants super-user access and skips all access checks. To confirm WRITE_OWNER failure, collect Process Monitor trace filtered to robocopy and robocopy /log:<logfile>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/RoboCopy Limitations_Storage

symptom: The specified network password is not correct error when mounting Azure Files share with ADDS identity-based authentication; credentials are verified correct
rootCause: Authentication method mismatch: client attempts NTLM but storage account is configured to allow only Kerberos (or vice versa). XDS logs show NTLMv2 authentication is disabled and Allowed authentication methods 2 (SmbAuthentication_Kerberos=0x2)
solution: Use dgrep to find SessionSetup SMB failure and get activity ID. Use Log Collector with activity ID to pull XDS server logs. Check Allowed authentication methods value in logs. Either connect using the allowed auth type or update storage account security setting to permit the needed auth type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/Specified Network Password is not Correct - Mount Error_Storage

symptom: System Error 86 The specified network password is not correct on Windows 7 or Windows Server 2008 R2 clients when mounting Azure Files with ADDS Authentication
rootCause: Windows 7 and Windows 2008 use SMB 2.1 which does not natively support encryption in transit. Additional configuration required for mounting with AD identity on these legacy OS versions.
solution: (1) If client is outside Azure: use private endpoint with VPN - SMB 2.1 data encrypted over VPN wire. (2) Disable Secure transfer required on the storage account. (3) Additional configuration steps needed for AD identity mount on Win7. Primary recommendation: upgrade to Windows 10 as Windows 7 is end-of-life since Jan 2020.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Azure Files Identity/AD DS with AD Connect Syncing to AAD with AAD DS/System Error 86 on Windows 7 or 2008_Storage

symptom: You are copying a file to a destination that does not support encryption error when copying files to Azure Files
rootCause: Azure Files does not support NTFS EFS (Encrypting File System). Only Bitlocker-encrypted files can be copied. EFS copy operations fail unless the file is first decrypted.
solution: Decrypt the file before copying using: (1) copy /d command in local cmd prompt, or (2) set registry HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\CopyFileAllowDecryptedRemoteDestination = 1 (DWORD, type REG_DWORD). Note: registry key affects all copy operations to network shares.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Does Not Support Encryption_Storage

symptom: A file share named _Empty appears in Azure Portal file share metrics for a storage account even though no such share exists; real shares also show correctly alongside _Empty entry
rootCause: Day-0 platform bug: GetFileServiceProperties is a service-level API that does not take container name as input. When called with empty container name, the platform emits _Empty string for the Container dimension in metrics. This behavior will not change.
solution: Ignore the _Empty entry in file share metrics - it does not represent a real file share and is expected platform behavior. Reference ICM 440527147.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Empty File Share Metrics Dimension_In_Portal_Storage

symptom: System error 53 (The network path was not found) or system error 67 (The network name cannot be found) when mounting Azure Files from on-premises or different datacenter
rootCause: Outbound TCP port 445 to Azure Files datacenter is blocked by ISP or firewall
solution: Test with Test-NetConnection -ComputerName <storage>.file.core.windows.net -Port 445 or AzFileDiagnostics tool. Work with ISP or network admin to unblock outbound TCP port 445.
