LabBox available at aka.ms/LabBox for lab reproduction. See ISP port-445 allowance list at social.technet.microsoft.com/wiki/...
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Error 53, 67,87 or 123_Storage

symptom: System error 53 (The network path was not found) or system error 87 (The parameter is incorrect) when mounting Azure Files share
rootCause: NTLMv1 is enabled on the client (LmCompatibilityLevel < 3). Azure Files requires NTLMv2 authentication. May also be caused by RestrictSendingNTLMTraffic set to non-default value or DNS Client service disabled.
solution: Set HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\LmCompatibilityLevel to 3 or higher. Verify HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\MSV1_0\RestrictSendingNTLMTraffic is at default (0). Also check DNS Client service is running (required for SMB name resolution).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Error 53, 67,87 or 123_Storage

symptom: System error 123 (The file name, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect) when mounting Azure Files in AD DS scenarios; may see Kerberos error KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN in Windows event log
rootCause: Duplicate SPN for the Azure file share FQDN in AD DS, preventing Kerberos authentication from selecting the correct service principal
solution: Run: setspn -q cifs/storageaccount.file.core.windows.net to identify duplicate SPNs. Remove duplicate entries or reconfigure AD DS authentication for the file share.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/TSGs/Error 53, 67,87 or 123_Storage

symptom: ADE extension fails with: AddMountPoint failed with 3 during BitLocker encryption; BEK volume mount point assigned is not supported by ADE
rootCause: ADE does not support mount points on the BEK (BitLocker Encryption Key) volume. If a mount point is assigned to the BEK volume, the AddMountPoint call fails with error code 3, aborting encryption.
solution: 1. RDP to VM and open diskmgmt.msc. 2. Locate the BEK volume, right-click > Change Drive Letter and Paths > remove any mount point. 3. If BEK volume is absent/corrupted, delete C:\Packages\Plugins\Microsoft.Azure.Security.AzureDiskEncryption\{ver}\BekMountDir. 4. Reboot or re-enable ADE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker as expected Exception AddMountPoint failed with 3_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with: EnableAutoUnlock failed with 2147942401 during BitLocker encryption of data volumes
rootCause: Common causes: (1) Group Policy restrictions blocking auto-unlock or recovery key creation for fixed data drives; (2) Corrupted auto-unlock store or BEK mount directory; (3) Extension sequence mismatch after VM resize or disk addition.
solution: 1. Validate BitLocker Group Policies (Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > BitLocker > Fixed Data Drives) to allow auto-unlock and recovery key creation. 2. Decrypt disks and reapply ADE. 3. Re-run Set-AzVMDiskEncryptionExtension with a new SequenceVersion (New-Guid). 4. If unresolved, raise ICM to ADE PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to Configure Bitlocker as expected Exception EnableAutoUnlock failed with 2147942401_Encryption

symptom: ADE extension fails with: SaveExternalKeyToFile failed with 2147942419 during OS disk encryption; BitLocker unable to save BEK key to BEK volume
rootCause: The BEK disk is write-protected by a registry policy under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE, preventing the ADE extension from saving the BitLocker key to the BEK volume.
solution: 1. Mount BEK disk with drive letter in Disk Management. 2. Test write permissions: cmd /r dir E: /a:h /b /s; try creating a text file on BEK disk. 3. Check registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE and HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FVE. 4. Run gpresult /h gpreport.html to identify the policy. 5. Remove write-protection group policy and retry ADE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to configure bitlocker as expected Exception SaveExternalKeyToFile failed_Encryption

symptom: ADE enable fails with: When enabledForDiskEncryption is true networkAcls.bypass must include AzureServices -- Key Vault network ACL blocks ADE
rootCause: Key Vault has network ACLs restricting access, but the bypass property does not include AzureServices. ADE requires trusted Azure services to bypass the Key Vault firewall.
solution: 1. If KV uses selected networks: In KV Networking > Firewall, check Allow trusted Microsoft services to bypass this firewall under Exceptions. 2. If KV allows all public networks but still fails, run: az keyvault update --name <kvname> --bypass AzureServices. 3. Retry ADE enable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failed to enable ADE due to networkAcls bypass property_Encryption

symptom: ADE fails with: Failed to send DiskEncryptionData Check KeyVault inputs ResourceIds and retry encryption operation -- KV configuration issues (wrong region/subscription, typo, permissions, special chars)
rootCause: Possible causes: (1) Key Vault in different region or subscription than VM; (2) Typo in KV name/URL or KEK resource ID; (3) Special characters in resource names; (4) Missing KV access policy for Azure Disk Encryption (Get/Wrap/Unwrap Key); (5) Unsupported scenario such as virtual disk mounted inside VM.
solution: 1. Verify KV and VM are in same region AND subscription. 2. Check for typos in KV name, key name, resource IDs. 3. Remove special characters from resource names. 4. In KV > Access Policies, ensure Azure Disk Encryption for volume encryption is enabled with Wrap/Unwrap Key permissions. 5. Check for unsupported scenarios. For Windows Server 2022, check ADE Emerging Issue wiki page.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failure to Send Disk Encryption Data_Encryption

symptom: ADE fails with Failed to send DiskEncryptionData and extension logs show 504 Gateway Timeout contacting 168.63.129.16 -- proxy or NSG blocks platform endpoint
rootCause: A proxy server or NSG blocks the ADE extension from reaching http://168.63.129.16:80/machine?comp=diskEncryptionData. ADE v2.2+ no longer needs direct KV access from VM, but requires unblocked access to Azure platform IPs 168.63.129.16 and 169.254.169.254.
solution: Update proxy settings to allow unaltered HTTP traffic to 168.63.129.16 and 169.254.169.254. Verify NSG/firewall rules do not block outbound to these IPs. Reference: ADE behind Firewall and Azure IMDS documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Failure to Send Disk Encryption Data_Encryption

symptom: ADE for Linux (AzureDiskEncryptionForLinux) fails with: AttributeError module platform has no attribute linux_distribution -- Non-zero exit code 1 during extension install on RHEL
rootCause: The ADE extension script runs with Python 3.9+ (which removed platform.linux_distribution), even though Python 3.6 appears installed. The system update-alternatives sets Python 3.9 as primary, overriding the Python 3.6 symlink.
solution: 1. Run /usr/sbin/update-alternatives --display python to identify actual primary Python. 2. Check: update-alternatives --list python. 3. If Python 3.6 absent: sudo update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/unversioned-python python /usr/bin/python3.6 1. 4. Reconfigure: update-alternatives --config python, select Python 3.6. 5. Retry ADE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Has no attribute linux_distribution_Encryption

symptom: Extension installation fails on VM with Encryption at Host enabled that was previously ADE-encrypted: OperationNotAllowed -- Encryption at host is not allowed for a VM having disks that were encrypted with Azure Disk Encryption
rootCause: When a VM is ADE-encrypted, the platform sets a Unified Disk Encryption (UDE) flag. Disabling ADE does not clear this flag. Any CRP operation on a VM with Encryption at Host enabled triggers a conflict check against the UDE flag, causing failure. This is an unsupported migration scenario.
solution: Workaround (best-effort, unsupported): 1. Stop VM. 2. Create a NEW managed OS disk by copying via azcopy (NOT snapshot -- snapshot preserves UDE flag). Use PowerShell script to create SAS tokens and azcopy disk data. 3. Create new VM from new disk (no UDE flag). 4. Repeat for data disks. PG tracking feature to allow EAH+ADE coexistence (no ETA).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)/Installation of extensions fail on VM which is encrypted at host but was previously ADE encrypted_Encryption

symptom: VM fails to start with error: KeyVaultSecretDoesNotExist -- The Key Vault secret with URL ... does not exist; for SSE+CMK encrypted VM
rootCause: The Key Vault secret (certificate) referenced by the VM has been disabled. CRP cannot retrieve the secret to decrypt the disk, preventing VM start.
solution: In Azure Portal: navigate to Key Vault > Secrets > find the referenced secret > open current version > change Enabled from No to Yes. Retry starting the VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK Fails with KeyVaultSecretDoesNotExist_Encryption

symptom: VM fails to start with error: KeyVaultKeyNotEnabled -- The key vault key used for disk encryption set must be in enabled state; for SSE+CMK encrypted VM
rootCause: The Key Vault key configured in the Disk Encryption Set was disabled. Azure cannot access the key to unwrap the DEK, preventing disk decryption and VM startup.
solution: 1. Navigate to Key Vault in the error message. 2. Keys > select the key > open Current Version. 3. Change Enabled from No to Yes > Save. 4. Start the VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK VM Start Fails With DiskEncryptionSet KeyDisabled_Encryption

symptom: VM fails to start with error: InternalDiskManagementError -- The given encryption scope is not available; for SSE+CMK encrypted disk; DiskEncryptionSet managed identity lacks Key Vault permissions
rootCause: The DiskEncryptionSet system-assigned managed identity requires Get/Wrap Key/Unwrap Key permissions on the associated Key Vault. These permissions were not granted or were revoked, preventing Azure from decrypting disk data.
solution: 1. In Azure Portal, go to Disk Encryption Set > Overview. If an error is shown, click it to auto-grant required KV permissions. 2. Alternatively, KV > Access Policies > find DES managed identity > grant Get, Unwrap Key, Wrap Key > Save. 3. Start the VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/SSE+CMK VM Start Fails with InternalDiskManagementError_Encryption

symptom: VM operations on Managed Disks fail with 'An unexpected error occurred while processing disk' and 'The remote server returned an error: (403) Forbidden' when using DiskEncryptionSets (SSE+CMK)
rootCause: The Key Vault referenced by the DiskEncryptionSet has been deleted. CRP ContextActivity shows 'The Key Vault vault is not found'. Storage cannot retrieve the CMK, blocking all disk operations.
solution: 1) Query CRP ContextActivity table with activityId to confirm error 'The Key Vault vault is not found'. 2) Pull VM Model View from Jarvis to identify the diskEncryptionSet resource ID. 3) Get DiskEncryptionSet details from ARM/Jarvis to find the Key Vault name and keyUrl. 4) Check ASC to confirm Key Vault is missing. 5) Run Get-AzKeyVault -InRemovedState to find soft-deleted KV (recoverable within 90 days if SoftDelete+PurgeProtection were enabled). 6) If RG also deleted, recreate RG with same name first. 7) Recover KV: Undo-AzKeyVaultRemoval -VaultName <kv> -ResourceGroupName <rg> -Location <loc>. 8) Ask customer to retry the original VM operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Encryption/TSGs/Server Side Encryption (SSE)/Unexpected Error Processing Disk_Encryption

symptom: Azure Files share tier change fails with error 'Cannot change access tier at this moment. Please wait for the current tier transition to complete' (HTTP 409 Conflict)
rootCause: SetShareProperties API call is blocked while a previous tier transition is still in progress. The API returns HTTP 409 until the previous transition completes.
solution: Wait for the current tier transition to complete. Transition rate is approximately 30 seconds per GB of share size. Check the Tier Transition State ETA via ASC tier properties (Storage Account -> File -> Search File Share). No workaround exists; opening a CRI/ICM will not help.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/How Tos/Hot & Cool Tiers for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: Customer wants to copy SIG (Shared Image Gallery) images across tenants using PowerShell -- operation fails or is unsupported
rootCause: Cross-tenant SIG image copy is not supported via PowerShell. The feature only works with Azure CLI using service principal authentication.
solution: Use Azure CLI: (1) az account clear; (2) az login --service-principal -u '<appID>' -p '<secret>' --tenant '<tenant1_ID>'; (3) az login --service-principal for tenant 2; (4) az sig image-version create --resource-group DestRG --gallery-name DestGallery --gallery-image-definition DestDef --gallery-image-version 1.0.0 --managed-image '/subscriptions/<SubID>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Compute/galleries/<Gallery>/images/<Def>/versions/1.0.0'. Requires app registration with Reader role on source gallery and Contributor role on destination RG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Copy SIG Images Across Tenants_ACG

symptom: Customer needs to create a VM from a Shared Image Gallery (SIG) image that resides in a different Azure AD tenant
solution: Create an app registration and grant it Reader on the source gallery (Tenant 1) and Contributor on destination RG (Tenant 2). Sign in with service principal for both tenants via az login --service-principal. Run: az vm create --resource-group <RG> --name <VM> --image '/subscriptions/<Tenant1Sub>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Compute/galleries/<Gallery>/images/<Def>/versions/<ver>'. Alternatively use az group deployment create with --aux-subs for ARM template deployments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Create VM Different Tenant_ACG

symptom: Customer needs to deploy a VM from an Azure Compute Gallery Community Gallery image, or doesn't know how to use communityGalleryImageId in VM deployment
solution: 1) Install extension: az extension add --name image-gallery --upgrade. 2) List community gallery image versions: az sig image-version list-community --public-gallery-name <name> --gallery-image-definition <def> --location <region>. 3) Use communityGalleryImageId in imageReference for VM deployment (API 2021-07-01+): {"communityGalleryImageId": "/communityGalleries/<publicName>/images/<def>/versions/1.0.0"}. CLI: az vm create --name <VM> --resource-group <RG> --image /CommunityGalleries/<publicName>/images/<imageName>/versions/latest --location <region>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Deploy VM From Community Gallery Image_ACG

symptom: VMSS scale-out operation fails after Azure Compute Gallery (ACG) or Shared Image Gallery is deleted; existing VMs continue running but new scale-out instances cannot be provisioned
rootCause: VMSS relies on the source image ID reference stored in its model. When the ACG is deleted, this reference becomes invalid. Existing VM instances are unaffected (they have persistent OS disks), but VMSS scale-out requires accessing the gallery image to create new instances.
solution: Restore or recreate the deleted Azure Compute Gallery and image definition/version (using the same names if possible so existing VMSS model references remain valid). Alternatively, update the VMSS model via portal or CLI (az vmss update --image) to reference a new valid image in an existing gallery.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Frequently Asked Questions(FAQ's)_ACG

symptom: Customer cannot replicate Azure Compute Gallery image versions to the South West Africa region; replication option is unavailable or fails
rootCause: Image definition replication is not supported for the South West Africa region. This region serves as a disaster recovery (DR) site for South Africa North only and does not support gallery image publishing or cross-region replication as a target.
solution: Use South Africa North as the target replication region instead of South West Africa.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Frequently Asked Questions(FAQ's)_ACG

symptom: Customer wants to convert an existing private (RBAC-based) Azure Compute Gallery to a Community Gallery -- operation fails or option is not available
rootCause: Community gallery type (sharingProfile.permissions=Community) must be set at gallery creation time. This property is immutable and cannot be updated on an existing gallery.
solution: Create a new Azure Compute Gallery with Community sharing from the start: Portal: select sharing method 'Community' during gallery creation; CLI: az sig create --gallery-name <name> --permissions community --resource-group <RG> --publisher-uri <uri> --publisher-email <email> --eula <url> --public-name-prefix <prefix>. Then republish image definitions and versions to the new community gallery.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Share Gallery with Community_ACG

symptom: Customer tries to share VM Applications (VM App definitions or versions) through Azure Compute Gallery Community sharing -- VM Apps not visible to community users or sharing fails
rootCause: Sharing VM Applications to Community Gallery is not yet supported. Only VM image definitions and versions can be made publicly available via Community Gallery.
solution: No workaround for community sharing of VM Applications. Use RBAC-based gallery sharing to share VM Applications with specific users, groups, or service principals within the same or different subscriptions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Share Gallery with Community_ACG

symptom: Customer cannot add encrypted image versions to an Azure Compute Gallery Community Gallery, or gets error when trying to create encrypted images within a community-enabled gallery
rootCause: Encrypted images are not supported in Community Gallery. Public distribution is incompatible with encryption. Encrypted images cannot be created within a gallery already configured for Community sharing.
solution: Remove encryption from the image before publishing to a Community Gallery. For images requiring encryption, use RBAC-based gallery sharing (share with specific subscriptions or users) instead of Community sharing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Compute Gallery (ACG)/How Tos/Share Gallery with Community_ACG

symptom: AFS (Azure File Sync) server generates unexpectedly large uploads to Azure service storage account due to diagnostic pipeline being enabled
rootCause: AFS diagnostic pipeline enabled via EnableDiagnostics registry key (value=1) under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync\Agent\InstallOptions, causing agent to continuously upload diagnostic data to the Hybrid Monitoring storage account
solution: Disable AFS diagnostics by setting EnableDiagnostics to 0: reg ADD "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync\Agent\InstallOptions" /v EnableDiagnostics /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f. No FileSyncSvc service restart required. To re-enable diagnostics, set value back to 1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/TSG 174 AFS Enabling or Disabling diagnostics on Customer server_Storage

symptom: Azure Image Builder (AIB) image validation property "validate" not working or image validation fails with errors during image build
rootCause: API version below minimum 2022-07-01 required for validate feature, or incorrect spelling/capitalization of "validate" property in image template JSON (AIB service is case-sensitive starting API version 2024-02-01)
solution: Ensure AIB template uses API version 2022-07-01 or above. Verify exact spelling "validate" in template JSON. Note: case-sensitivity applies from API version 2024-02-01 onwards. Validators execute in specified order; if one fails, entire validation fails. Do not put sensitive data in validation scripts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/How Tos/ImageValidation_AIB

symptom: Azure Image Builder (AIB) Isolated Image Build fails; Azure Container Instance (ACI) resources not provisioned or accessible in staging resource group
rootCause: Microsoft.ContainerInstance resource provider not registered in the customer subscription. Isolated Image Builds requires ACI to execute Packer within dedicated container resources for compute/network isolation.
solution: Register Microsoft.ContainerInstance provider: (Azure CLI) az provider register -n Microsoft.ContainerInstance; (PowerShell) Register-AzResourceProvider -ProviderNamespace Microsoft.ContainerInstance. Minimum API version 2022-07-01 required. Customization logs remain available in storage account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/How Tos/IsolatedImageBuilds_AIB

symptom: Azure Image Builder (AIB) template provisioning fails in Microsoft internal subscriptions with security policy or security control errors during deployment
rootCause: Microsoft internal subscription security controls conflict with AIB requirements. AIB creates staging resource groups (IT_* prefix) and needs to deploy/manage resources within them; internal security policies block this operation.
solution: Deploy ARM policy via Azure portal "Deploy a custom template" that adds SecurityControl=Ignore tag to resource groups starting with IT_ prefix. CRITICAL: do NOT use wildcard (*) by itself in policy target rule - scope specifically to IT_ prefixed resource groups only to avoid broad security impact.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/How Tos/Test AIB Using Internal Subscription_AIB

symptom: Azure Image Builder (AIB) build fails with WinRM or SSH connection errors to build VM when using custom source images; connection failures visible in customization.log
rootCause: Custom source image does not have WinRM (Windows) or SSH (Linux) properly configured or enabled. AIB/Packer requires WinRM/SSH connectivity to the build VM to execute customization scripts, commands, and reboots.
solution: Ensure WinRM is configured and enabled on custom Windows source images before using with AIB. For Linux, verify SSH is accessible. Review customization.log for WinRM/SSH error patterns. Use Azure Image Builder Customization.log Analyzer tool (zebra-ai-web-prd.ait.microsoft.com) for quick log analysis - validate results with SME.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Image Builder (AIB)/How Tos/Understanding Customization Log_AIB

symptom: RDP connection fails due to incorrect, expired, or missing certificate bound to the RDP listener on Windows Server
rootCause: RDP listener certificate not properly configured; default self-signed cert expired or custom certificate thumbprint not applied to Win32_TSGeneralSetting
solution: Apply correct certificate thumbprint to RDP listener using WMIC: wmic /namespace:\root\cimv2\TerminalServices PATH Win32_TSGeneralSetting Set SSLCertificateSHA1Hash='THUMBPRINT'. Remove ASCII control characters before running. Applies to Windows Server 2012/2012R2/2016.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Configure RDP Listener Cert_RDP SSH

symptom: VM stuck in reboot loop and the BSOD/stop error message is hidden because AutoReboot is enabled, preventing crash analysis
rootCause: AutoReboot registry value set to 1 causes the system to automatically restart after a critical crash (BSOD), clearing the error screen before it can be read or a dump generated
solution: Offline: load SYSTEM hive on recovery VM (reg load HKLM\BROKENSYSTEM <osdrive>:\Windows\System32\Config\SYSTEM), set AutoReboot to 0 (reg add HKLM\BROKENSYSTEM\ControlSet001\Control\CrashControl /v AutoReboot /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f), then unload hive. Online: Set-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl -Name AutoReboot -Value 0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Disable Autoreboot_RDP SSH

symptom: RDP connection to Azure VM fails after customer made a deliberate Windows Guest OS firewall change, or the firewall is blocking RDP traffic
rootCause: Windows Guest OS firewall enabled with misconfigured or overly restrictive rules blocking RDP port or all network traffic; may be enforced via local policy or AD Group Policy
solution: Offline: attach OS disk to recovery VM, load SYSTEM+SOFTWARE hives, set EnableFirewall=0 for DomainProfile/PublicProfile/StandardProfile. Online: use Custom Script Extension or Run Command to push DisableFirewall.ps1 (local firewall) or DisableFirewallGPO.ps1 (GPO-enforced). GPO fix is temporary until policy reapplies; ultimate fix is to modify the AD policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Disable Guest OS Firewall Windows_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM stuck in reboot loop, black screen, or bugcheck (BSOD) without generating a memory dump file despite dump configuration being in place
rootCause: Standard Azure VM dump collection fails during BSOD/bugcheck; crash happens before OS writes the dump file, or standard collection methods are inaccessible when Serial Console and Guest Agent are unavailable
solution: Create nested Hyper-V VM inside Rescue VM using copy of broken OS disk. Power on nested VM, reproduce issue, save VM state to get .VMRS file. Use vm2dmp.exe (from \\emeacssdfs.europe.corp.microsoft.com\netpod\WindowsDiag\SHA\vm2dmp) to convert .VMRS to memory.dmp: vm2dmp.exe -vmrs C:\VM\example.VMRS -dmp C:\tmp\memory.dmp. Analyze with WinDbg. If vm2dmp fails with KdDebuggerDataBlock error, accept Security Alert dialog to allow HTTPS symbol download then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Dump collection in nested Hyper v_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM becomes unreachable via RDP/SSH after Kaspersky Endpoint Security or Kaspersky Security Center is installed; brief connectivity (~1 min) after reboot then all inbound connections blocked; VM screenshot shows OS fully loaded at CAD screen
rootCause: Kaspersky Endpoint Security 10 for Windows or Kaspersky Security Center 10 Network Agent is misconfigured and blocking all inbound connections
solution: ONLINE: Stop/disable Kaspersky services via Serial Console CMD (sc stop then sc config start=disabled for: AVP, avpsus, kl1, klelam, klflt, klhk, KLIF, KLIM6, klnagent, klpd, klwfp); or uninstall via PowerShell WMI. OFFLINE: Mount OS disk, load BROKENSYSTEM hive, set Kaspersky service registry Start=4 (disabled) under HKLM:\BROKENSYSTEM\Controlset00x\Services\.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/AV Kaspersky Blocking Incoming_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM has no RDP/SSH connectivity; WinGuestAnalyzer Health Signal shows Base Filtering Engine (BFE) service in Stopped state; System log Event ID 7022 (BFE hung on starting)
rootCause: Base Filtering Engine (BFE) Windows Firewall core service not running: service disabled, crashing, hanging, or a required dependent service is not running
solution: ONLINE: Start BFE via Serial Console (sc start BFE). Ensure registry Start=2 (auto). OFFLINE: Mount OS disk, load SYSTEM hive, set HKLM:\BROKENSYSTEM\ControlSet00x\Services\BFE Start=2. If crashing/hanging: collect process dump for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Base Filtering Engine Service Not Starting_RDP SSH

symptom: Windows Server 2016 (Build 14393/RS1) or Windows 10 Creator Update (Build 15063/RS2) Azure VM loses network connectivity; NIC shows as Disconnected in WinGuestAnalyzer; issue appears after installing KB3213986, KB3209835, or KB3216755
rootCause: netvsc.sys driver version 10.0.14393.594 (RS1) or 10.0.15063.0 (RS2) requires Media Sense feature on Hyper-V 2016 host which Azure disables to support VMPHU. OS Bugs: 10564224 (RS1), 10887629 (RS2)
solution: Check netvsc.sys version via PowerShell. For RS1 (10.0.14393.594): install KB4073562 or newer. For RS2 (10.0.15063.0): update netvsc.sys to newer driver version. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-rdp-driver-netvsc
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Cable Disconnected Windows_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM OS fully loaded but no network/RDP connectivity; DHCP Client service shown as not Running in WinGuestAnalyzer Health Signal; System Event IDs: 7022 (DHCP Client hung on starting), 7001 (NSI dependency failure), or 7000 (wrong service account)
rootCause: DHCP Client service not running: service was disabled, crashing, hanging, or dependency service (Network Store Interface/NSI) is not running
solution: ONLINE: Check service state (sc query DHCP), enable/start (sc config DHCP start=auto && sc start DHCP). If Event 7001 shows NSI dependency failure, fix NSI service first. If crashing/hanging: collect process dump. Note: DHCP Server role in Azure is unsupported - this TSG covers DHCP Client only.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/DHCP Client not Starting_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM gets APIPA IP (169.254.x.x) or null/no IP at all; no RDP/SSH connectivity; VM Port Scanner shows no open ports on VIP/DIP/PA
rootCause: DHCP request to Azure Wire DHCP Server (168.63.129.16) fails. Multiple causes: 1) Host/hypervisor issue 2) Virtual NIC issue 3) Custom DNS overriding Azure DNS 4) Custom static routes blocking DHCP discovery 5) DHCP Server role enabled (unsupported) 6) Custom DHCP entries configured (unsupported)
solution: 1) Redeploy VM to new host (file ICM to RDOS EEE if resolved). 2) Present new NIC to guest OS. 3) Remove custom DNS, restore Azure default. 4) Remove custom static routes. 5) Check/remove DHCP Server role. 6) Present new NIC to wipe custom DHCP config. Azure DHCP wire server is 168.63.129.16.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/DHCP Request Fails_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM with DHCP Server role installed is experiencing RDP connectivity issues; engineer suspects DHCP Server role is the cause
rootCause: DHCP Server role presence is NOT the cause of RDP issues (red herring). The actual RDP cause requires separate investigation. Note: DHCP server leasing IPs to Azure VMs is unsupported; only leasing to on-premises machines via DHCP relay is supported.
solution: Follow the basic Can't RDP workflow to find actual root cause. For DHCP leasing issues to on-premises machines, involve Azure Networking team. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/how-to-dhcp-azure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/DHCP Server Role Enabled on VM_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM unreachable via RDP; OS screenshot shows login screen; VM heartbeat (WaAppAgent) is normal; ESET Server Security or Endpoint Security installed; stopping ESET services via Serial Console or Run Command returns Access Denied
rootCause: ESET Protect Cloud Policies set to block all inbound communications or triggered automatic/manual network isolation on the VM
solution: ONLINE: Disable existing ESET policies in ESET Management Console to allow RDP connections; manually end network isolation via ESET Management Console. Direct service stop returns Access Denied. OFFLINE: Mount OS disk and remove/disable ESET service registry entries via BROKENSYSTEM hive. Reference: ESET KB7938.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/ESET Blocking Incoming_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM has no RDP/SSH connectivity; WinGuestAnalyzer Health Signal shows Network Store Interface (NSI) service not running; all dependent network services (BFE, Windows Firewall, DHCP Client) also fail to start as cascade effect; System Event IDs 7022/7001/7000
rootCause: Network Store Interface (NSI) - core network component on Windows - not running: service disabled, crashing, hanging, or dependency failure (NSIPROXY kernel driver or RPCSS service). NSI failure causes cascade failure of all network-dependent services.
solution: ONLINE via Serial Console: sc query NSI, sc start NSI. If error 5 (ACCESS DENIED): fix registry ACLs. If error 1 (wrong account): reset ObjectName to NT AUTHORITY\LocalService. OFFLINE: Mount OS disk, set HKLM:\BROKENSYSTEM\ControlSet00x\Services\nsi Start=2, ObjectName=NT AUTHORITY\LocalService. If crashing/hanging: collect NSI process dump.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Network Store Interface not Running_RDP SSH

symptom: Volume runs out of disk space due to ETL traces accumulating in C:\Program Files\Azure\StorageSyncAgent\MAAgent\Diagnostic directory on Azure File Sync server
rootCause: Azure File Sync agent is installed but server is not registered with Storage Sync Service, OR the Storage Sync Monitor (AzureStorageSyncMonitor.exe) process is not running, causing ETL trace files to accumulate without cleanup
solution: If server not registered: register the server with a Storage Sync Service, extra ETL files will be deleted automatically. If AzureStorageSyncMonitor.exe not running: restart the server; if issue recurs after restart, uninstall the Azure File Sync agent, restart, and reinstall the agent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Issues with Azure File Sync Agent AFSS_Storage

symptom: Slow enumeration of files and folders when accessing a mounted Azure File Share in Windows; directory listing takes excessive time
rootCause: Insufficient directory cache on the client machine for large directories; default DirectoryCacheEntrySizeMax value is too small
solution: Adjust DirectoryCacheEntrySizeMax registry value at HKLM\System\CCS\Services\Lanmanworkstation\Parameters (DWORD type). Set to 0x100000 (1MB) and test if performance improves.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Slow Enumeration of Files and Folders_Storage

symptom: Azure File Sync agent installation fails and rolls back; installer log shows SetRegPIIAclSettings custom action error 1603 with AuthorizationManager check failed and STG_E_ACCESSDENIED
rootCause: The user account running the installer does not have sufficient permissions to set registry ACLs; typically occurs when running under a domain account without local admin privileges
solution: 1. Enable verbose logging: StorageSyncAgent.msi /l*v AFSInstaller.log. 2. Collect log from %LocalAppData%\Temp\StorageSyncAgent-SetRegPIIAclSetting*.log. 3. Check registry paths via reg query. 4. Run SetRegPIIAclSettings script manually from elevated PowerShell. 5. Ensure installer runs under account with local admin privileges.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files Sync/TSGs/Sync Agent Installation Rollback_Storage

symptom: RDP connection to Azure Windows VM fails because Guest OS firewall is blocking RDP traffic on port 3389; customer may have deliberately changed firewall rules or firewall profile is misconfigured
rootCause: Windows Firewall rule 'Remote Desktop - User Mode (TCP-In)' is disabled or the firewall profile is blocking inbound port 3389 on the Guest OS
solution: Enable the RDP firewall rule using one of: (1) CSE/RunCommand: netsh advfirewall firewall set rule dir=in name="Remote Desktop - User Mode (TCP-In)" new enable=yes; (2) Remote PowerShell: Enable-NetFirewallRule -Name RemoteDesktop-UserMode-In-TCP; (3) PSTools: psexec to DIP then netsh; (4) Remote Registry: set Active=TRUE in FirewallRules\RemoteDesktop-UserMode-In-TCP; (5) Offline mode: attach OS disk to rescue VM, load BROKENSYSTEM hive, edit registry key under SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy\FirewallRules, rebuild VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Enable Disable Firewall Rule Guest OS_RDP SSH

symptom: VM Agent not installed or broken on Azure Windows VM; extensions (CSE, VMAccess) fail to execute; password reset not possible; GuestAgentStatus property blank
rootCause: Windows Azure Guest Agent (WaAppAgent/RdAgent) binaries or registry entries are missing or corrupted on the VM
solution: Use az vm repair: (1) az vm repair create -g RG -n VM; (2) az vm repair run --run-on-repair --run-id win-GA-fix; (3) az vm repair restore. Manual method: attach OS disk to rescue VM, export WindowsAzureGuestAgent and RdAgent registry keys from healthy VM, rename SYSTEM to BROKENSYSTEM, merge into broken hive, copy C:\WindowsAzure binaries, detach and rebuild VM. For RDFE: set ProvisionGuestAgent=true via Update-AzureVM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Install VM Agent Offline Mode_RDP SSH

symptom: Cannot RDP to Azure Windows VM because all Terminal Services (RDP) sessions are occupied or maxed out; no available session slots for new connections
rootCause: Existing RDP/Terminal Services sessions are consuming all available connection slots on the VM; stale or disconnected sessions not released
solution: Use Remote PowerShell (Enter-PSSession via port 5986/SSL) to connect to VM, run 'query user' to list active sessions, then 'logoff <ID>' to terminate stale sessions. Requires PS endpoint open on Azure load balancer. Use format COMPUTERNAME\USER for credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/How Tos/Kill Terminal Services Sessions_RDP SSH

symptom: NFS Azure File Share snapshot accessed within 30 seconds of creation returns No such file or directory error when running stat /mnt/nfs/.snapshots/<snapshot_name>
rootCause: Server-side snapshot list cache refresh interval is 30 seconds. Accessing the snapshot before the cache updates causes NFS4ERR_NOENT.
solution: Wait 30 seconds after snapshot creation before accessing. Alternatively: (1) run touch /mnt/nfs on the same VM, (2) mount the same share on a different VM and touch, or (3) remount the share.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS File Share Snapshots_Storage

symptom: NFS Azure File Share snapshot enumeration (find command) is very slow and takes long time consistently when the share has a large number of entries
rootCause: Snapshot entries have a different FSID, causing the NFS client cache for snapshot entries to expire faster than for live entries.
solution: Use subdirectory mount instead: mount server-ip:/account/share/.snapshots/<snapshot_timestamp>_snapshot to /mnt/snapshot. This preserves client cache longer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS File Share Snapshots_Storage

symptom: NFS Azure File Share: ls .snapshots returns empty list even though snapshots exist. Happens when snapshots were created right after share creation but before the first NFS mount.
rootCause: Snapshots created before the first NFS mount are not visible in the .snapshots virtual directory. Server log shows READDIR returns NFS4ERR_NOENT.
solution: Delete the snapshots that were created before the first mount. After performing the first mount, snapshots can be created and will be visible normally. PG is working on a fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS File Share Snapshots_Storage

symptom: NFS Azure File Share: after disabling and re-enabling snapshot access on share level, stat .snapshots consistently fails with NFS4ERR_NOENT or NFS4ERR_ACCESS
rootCause: Snapshot LMT (Last Modified Time) update is missed when toggling snapshot access, causing the server cache to not properly refresh.
solution: Workaround: (1) run touch /mnt/nfs on the same VM, (2) mount same share on different VM and touch, or (3) remount. PG fix is undergoing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS File Share Snapshots_Storage

symptom: NFS mount fails with mount.nfs: access denied by server while mounting. Three common causes: (1) secure transfer enabled, (2) VNET not whitelisted, (3) parallel public+private endpoint mount.
rootCause: Cause 1: Secure transfer is enabled on the storage account (must be disabled for NFS). Cause 2: VM VNET not in storage account firewall allowed list. Cause 3: Cannot have both public and private endpoint mounts from same client.
solution: Cause 1: Disable secure transfer in Portal > Storage Account > Configuration. Cause 2: Whitelist VM VNET in storage firewall. Cause 3: Choose one mount method, remove the other mount point first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: NFS mount fails with mount.nfs: Connection timed out
rootCause: Port 2049 is blocked on the customer network (NSG or firewall), or the file share does not exist on the storage account.
solution: Verify port 2049 is open: nmap -PN <storage>.file.core.windows.net -p 2049 (expect state open). Check NSG default rules. Confirm the file share name exists and is correctly spelled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: NFS mount fails with mount.nfs: Remote I/O error
rootCause: The file share is SMB type, not NFS. Customer is attempting to mount an SMB share using NFS protocol.
solution: Verify the share protocol in Azure Portal or ASC Resource Explorer (Files tab > Search File Share). Ensure the share is NFS type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: NFS mount intermittent errors under heavy load from many clients: Remote I/O error, Connection timed out, Stale file handle. Storage FE logs show Could not create file handle for root directory.
rootCause: Server-side NFS worker thread exhaustion due to excessive concurrent load on a single mount point from many clients.
solution: Report to NFS PG via escalation process (ICM). Server-side resource exhaustion issue requiring PG intervention.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: NFS mount fails with mount.nfs: Stale file handle when the storage account uses customer-managed keys (CMK) and the key vault was recently deleted
rootCause: Data encrypted with CMK in Key Vault. Without the key, data is inaccessible. Cannot switch to MS-managed keys and expect existing data to be accessible.
solution: If key vault was soft-deleted, recover it. Otherwise, copy data out to a different storage account with MS-managed key encryption while the key is still accessible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: NFS mount fails with mount: bad option; for several filesystems (e.g. nfs, cifs) you might need a /sbin/mount.<type> helper program
rootCause: NFS client package not installed on the Linux VM.
solution: Install NFS client: Debian/Ubuntu: sudo apt update && sudo apt install nfs-common. CentOS/RHEL: sudo yum install nfs-utils.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: NFS mount fails with mount.nfs: requested NFS version or transport protocol is not supported
rootCause: Private endpoint setup is incorrect. DNS resolution for storage.privatelink.file.core.windows.net returns wrong IP, or private endpoint not approved.
solution: Run nslookup <storage>.file.core.windows.net and verify it resolves to correct private IP matching ASC. Check PE is in Approved state. If DNS IP mismatch, engage networking team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: rsync to NFS Azure File Share fails with rsync: chown filename failed: Invalid argument (22). Occurs when migrating data using rsync.
rootCause: NFS v4.1 on Azure Files only accepts numeric UID/GID. NFS v4 passes identities as username@domain, Azure Files does not support alphanumeric UID/GID.
solution: Disable ID mapping: echo 'options nfs nfs4_disable_idmapping=1' > /etc/modprobe.d/99-nfs.conf (boot-time). Or on-the-fly: echo 1 > /sys/module/nfs/parameters/nfs4_disable_idmapping then remount.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: Azure Files NFS v4.1 shares mounted via public endpoint become inaccessible during storage hardware migration. NFS client mount loses connectivity permanently (unlike SMB which auto-recovers).
rootCause: Storage hardware upgrade changes the public IP. NFS client caches the original IP and does not auto-recover. SMB only experiences temporary disruption, NFS faces permanent disconnection.
solution: Preferred: Switch to private endpoints (static private IP, transparent during migrations). Alternative: Submit MS Forms request to schedule maintenance window, then remount after migration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/Network File System (NFS)/NFS v4 for Azure Files_Storage

symptom: Windows Server 2012/2012 R2 client: SMB Multichannel data loss bug. Azure Files blocks multichannel on these OS. Client logs show The server does not support multichannel even though multichannel is enabled on storage account.
rootCause: Known Windows bug causing data loss with multichannel on Azure Files. Fixed in Win10/Server2016/Server2019 but no fix planned for 2012/2012 R2. Azure Files deliberately blocks multichannel for these OS.
solution: No fix for 2012/2012 R2. Only single-channel communication available. If pressing need, gather business justification and contact azurefiles@microsoft.com. Consider upgrading Windows Server.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/SMB Multichannel_Storage

symptom: SMB Multichannel not triggered on Azure Premium File Share after enabling the feature. Get-SmbMultichannelConnection shows only 1 channel instead of expected 4.
rootCause: After any changes to SMB client or storage account multichannel config, must unmount, wait 60 seconds, remount. Also requires sufficient IO load (QD>=8 for client OS, QD>=1 for server OS).
solution: Unmount, wait 60 seconds, remount. Generate IO load (robocopy /MT or diskspd). Verify with Get-SmbMultichannelConnection | fl. Check SMB Client event logs for channel setup errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Azure Files All Topics/Workflows/SMB Multichannel_Storage

symptom: Azure Windows VM BSOD with bugcheck 0xC000021A STATUS_SYSTEM_PROCESS_TERMINATED. VM screenshot shows 'Your PC ran into a problem' with stop code 0xC000021A. The system process terminated unexpectedly.
rootCause: Critical user-mode process (Winlogon.exe or Csrss.exe) fails. Causes include: mismatched system files installed, failed Service Pack or KB update installation, incomplete backup restore of in-use files, or incompatible third-party program.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting only (Guest OS not operational). 1) Backup OS disk snapshot. 2) Attach to rescue VM. 3) Enable SAC and configure dump (load SYSTEM hive, set bcdedit ems settings, CrashDumpEnabled=2, NMICrashDump=1). 4) Collect memory dump for root cause analysis. 5) Reassemble VM via OSDisk Swap API or VM Recreation. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/windows-stop-error-system-process-terminated
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck STATUS SYSTEM PROCESS TERMINATED_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM BSOD with bugcheck 0x0000009A SYSTEM_LICENSE_VIOLATION. VM screenshot shows stop code SYSTEM LICENSE VIOLATION. VM cannot boot.
rootCause: Software license agreement has been violated. The OS license key is invalid, expired, or not properly activated. Common on newly deployed VMs that were not activated against Azure KMS.
solution: 1) If new Azure VM, change support topic to Deploy a new VM. 2) Setup a valid OS key and activate the OS properly using Azure KMS servers. Refer to Windows Activation TSG in AzureIaaSVM wiki.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck SYSTEM LICENSE VIOLATION_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM BSOD with bugcheck 0x0000003B SYSTEM_SERVICE_EXCEPTION. VM screenshot shows stop code SYSTEM_SERVICE_EXCEPTION. Guest OS not operational.
rootCause: Executing code had an exception and the faulting thread was a system thread. Specific root cause requires memory dump analysis to identify the source of pool corruption.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting only. 1) Backup OS disk. 2) Attach to rescue VM. 3) Enable SAC and configure dump collection (load SYSTEM hive, bcdedit ems settings, CrashDumpEnabled=2, NMICrashDump=1). 4) Reassemble VM via OSDisk Swap API or VM Recreation. 5) Reproduce crash and collect memory dump for WinDbg analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck SYSTEM SERVICE EXCEPTION_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM BSOD with bugcheck 0x0000007E SYSTEM_THREAD_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED. VM screenshot shows stop code or error 0x0000007E. May also show 'The system has rebooted without cleanly shutting down first' with bugcheck 0x0000007E.
rootCause: A system thread generated an exception which the error handler did not catch. Specific root cause depends on memory dump analysis.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting only. 1) Backup OS disk. 2) Attach to rescue VM. 3) Enable SAC + configure dump (load SYSTEM hive, bcdedit ems, CrashDumpEnabled=2, NMICrashDump=1). 4) Reassemble VM. 5) Collect memory dump for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck SYSTEM THREAD EXCEPTION_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM BSOD with bugcheck 0x0000007F UNEXPECTED_KERNEL_MODE_TRAP. VM screenshot shows stop code UNEXPECTED_KERNEL_MODE_TRAP. Guest OS not operational.
rootCause: Kernel encountered an unexpected trap/exception. Specific root cause depends on memory dump analysis.
solution: OFFLINE troubleshooting only. 1) Backup OS disk. 2) Attach to rescue VM. 3) Enable SAC + configure dump (load SYSTEM hive, bcdedit ems, CrashDumpEnabled=2, NMICrashDump=1). 4) Reassemble VM. 5) Collect memory dump for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Bugcheck UNEXPECTED KERNEL MODE TRAP_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Linux VM unable to boot after OS disk resize. Boot diagnostics show GRUB rescue prompt ('Minimal BASH-like line editing is supported'). VM was working before disk resize operation using fdisk.
rootCause: During Linux OS disk resize, customer used fdisk in DOS compatibility mode (cylinders as units instead of sectors). When recreating partition, default start cylinder 1 misaligns the partition start position from the original sector 2048, making filesystem structures inaccessible. The deprecated DOS mode warning was ignored.
solution: 1) Attach problem VHD to rescue VM. 2) Run fdisk with correct flags: fdisk -u=sectors -c=nondos /dev/<device>. 3) Delete the misaligned partition and recreate it using sector 2048 as start, mark as boot. 4) Write changes with 'w' command. 5) Verify by mounting the disk. 6) Run fsck to validate filesystem. 7) Detach and reassemble VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Fdisk Unit Problems after Disk Resize_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Linux VM unreachable via SSH. Boot diagnostics show filesystem errors: 'fsck.ext4 UNEXPECTED INCONSISTENCY; RUN fsck MANUALLY' or 'EXT4-fs write access will be enabled during recovery' or 'Could not remount RDWR because of unprocessed orphan inode list'. VM waiting for console input.
rootCause: File system was not shut down cleanly, resulting in ext4 filesystem corruption. Orphan inodes, journal errors, or inconsistent filesystem state prevents normal boot.
solution: 1) Attach OS disk to rescue VM. 2) Identify problem partition with fdisk -l and df -h. 3) Capture pre-fsck data: fdisk -l, dumpe2fs, tune2fs -l, e2fsck -n (send logs to Microsoft). 4) Run fsck -yM /dev/<partition> on the unmounted problem partition. 5) Detach disk and reassemble original VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/File System Issues_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM stuck on 'Getting devices ready' screen for over 1 hour during boot. VM unreachable via RDP. Boot process interrupted during hardware discovery and driver loading phase.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) OS Bug 5880648 - CleanupProfiles GPO ('Delete user profiles older than N days on system restart') causes deadlock during profile load at OS startup. 2) File system corruption detected in setupapi.log ('Error 19: The media is write protected'). 3) Corrupt or incompatible driver blocking PnP initialization (requires dump analysis).
solution: OFFLINE - For CleanupProfiles deadlock: Load SOFTWARE hive, delete CleanupProfiles key at HKLM\BROKENSOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System, unload hive, reassemble VM. Or disable problematic service (change Start from 2 to 3, or add DelayedAutoStart=1). For driver corruption: Check setupapi.log, rename corrupt binary and copy from working machine, or disable driver (Start=4). For dump-dependent cases: Collect memory dump for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Getting Devices Ready_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM boot diagnostics screenshot shows Getting Ready message; VM not fully booted and RDP unavailable
rootCause: Server performing final reboot after configuration change (Windows updates or roles/features change). Large number of KB updates require extended time to reconfigure.
solution: Wait for the process to complete (online hang scenario troubleshooting). If stuck for extended period, backup OS disk and follow offline troubleshooting. Monitor via boot diagnostics screenshots for progress.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Getting Ready_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM fails to boot after Windows Update; screen shows We could not complete the updates Undoing changes; CBS.log error 0x80070020 ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION and STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION in AutoHive Load
rootCause: Update rollback failed due to STATUS_SHARING_VIOLATION when loading user profile hives. A problematic user profile SID locks the registry hive, preventing CBS from completing pending transactions during the Per-User Registry Installer phase.
solution: Offline repair: attach OS disk to rescue VM, analyze CBS.log to identify the problematic user profile SID causing SHARING_VIOLATION, resolve the locked registry hive conflict. Ref: WindowsDevicesDeployment wiki Error-0x80070020
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Getting Windows Ready Reboot Loop stuck after Windows Update 0x80070020_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM stuck in reboot loop after Windows Update showing Preparing to configure windows or We could not complete the updates Undoing changes; RDP connection fails
rootCause: At restart, boot critical drivers load and registry reads Pending.xml location which holds pending offline update installation info. Corruption or conflict in Pending.xml prevents boot completion, causing continuous rollback loop.
solution: Offline repair: create rescue VM with nested virtualization, boot from recovery ISO (Shift+F10), locate Pending.xml in C:\windows\winSxS (do NOT delete), run Dism /image:C:\ /cleanup-image /revertpendingactions, restart VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Getting Windows Ready Reboot Loop stuck after Windows Update_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM boot diagnostics screenshot shows Getting Windows Ready Dont turn off your computer message; VM not fully booted and RDP unavailable
rootCause: Server performing final reboot after configuration change (Windows updates or roles/features). Large updates require extended reconfiguration time.
solution: Wait for process to complete (online hang scenario). If VM stuck in reboot loop, follow Getting Windows Ready Reboot Loop TSG. Public doc: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-vm-boot-configure-update
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Getting Windows Ready_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Linux VM cannot SSH; boot diagnostics shows /boot/grub2/grub.cfg file not found or Error 15 File not found or initrd/initramfs file not found
rootCause: GRUB unable to start due to missing configuration file (/boot/grub2/grub.cfg), missing initial RAM disk (initrd/initramfs), or missing /boot/grub/menu.lst.
solution: Offline repair: swap OS disk to rescue Linux VM, verify /etc/default/grub, run grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg. Alternative: az vm repair run -g RG -n VM --run-id linux-alar2 --parameters grubfix --run-on-repair.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Grub Config Files Issues_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows Server 2008 R2 VM shows Hardware Malfunction Call your hardware vendor for support The system has halted on boot diagnostics screenshot
rootCause: A Non-Maskable Interrupt (NMI) was sent via Serial Console but the Guest OS was not configured for NMI (NMICrashDump registry key not set).
solution: Restart VM via portal, then add NMICrashDump registry key: REG ADD HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\CrashControl /v NMICrashDump /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f, restart VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Hardware Malfunction_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM shows INACCESSIBLE_BOOT_DEVICE 0x0000007B or 0xC0000001 after failed uninstallation of Azure Site Recovery Mobility Service; MsiInstaller event shows removal status 1603
rootCause: ASR Mobility Service injects drivers/filters in multiple layers. Failed/aborted uninstallation leaves OS with wrong driver/filter configuration rendering OS unbootable. Bug WSSC 13771491.
solution: Offline repair (invasive, backup first): attach OS disk to rescue VM, load BROKENSYSTEM registry hive, enable crash dump, manually restore ASR-modified drivers/filters/BCD to pre-ASR state. For Gen2 VMs handle EFI/BCD via DISKPART.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/INACCESSIBLE_BOOT_DEVICE 0x0000007B ASR Uninstallation Failure_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM boots but RDP fails with generic 3-point error; no network interface shown by netsh; Device Manager shows Microsoft Hyper-V Network Adapter with yellow bang error code 31 (Windows Server 2012/2012R2)
rootCause: Missing registry entry for the net device class installer at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Class\{4D36E972-E325-11CE-BFC1-08002BE10318}\Installer32
solution: Add missing Installer32 registry key (value: NetCfgx.dll,NetClassInstaller) to both ControlSet001 and ControlSet002 via Serial Console (online) or rescue VM (offline). Then reset NIC by changing IP or attaching a new NIC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/No Nic ErrorCode31_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM shows OS at CAD screen but no RDP connectivity; Resource Exhaustion Detector events (1003, 1007, 2004) in System log; SQL.exe consuming all virtual memory causing all other services to fail
rootCause: SQL Server max server memory is set to default maximum (2147483647 MB), causing SQL to consume all available VM memory and starving other services including networking stack
solution: Attach the disk holding SQL master database to another SQL Server, query sys.configurations for max server memory setting, set a proper memory limit, replace the database files, and reattach disk to original VM. Restart the VM to restore connectivity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Performance Degradation on SQL Servers_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM has no connectivity on VIP/DIP/PA; screenshot shows red cross on NIC or black screen; TermService fails with status -2147023812 (cannot start in Safe Mode); SAFEBOOT found in boot options or registry SystemStartOptions
rootCause: VM is booted in Safe Mode (set via MSCONFIG or BCD), which only loads minimal services and does not include Terminal Services (RDP). This is by design in all Safe Mode types (Minimal, Network, Alternate Shell).
solution: Remove the SAFEBOOT flag from BCD configuration. Online: use Serial Console to run bcdedit /deletevalue safeboot. Offline: attach OS disk to rescue VM, load SYSTEM hive, remove SafeBoot value. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-rdp-safe-mode
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Safe Mode_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM no connectivity; network services (Network Store Interface, BFE, DHCP, Windows Firewall) fail to start with Event 7000: The account specified for this service is different from the account specified for other services running in the same process
rootCause: A Windows service sharing a svchost process has a mismatched logon account compared to other services in the same shared process group, preventing all dependent services (including network stack) from starting.
solution: Identify the failing service and its shared svchost group. Fix the logon account to match other services in the same process group. Can be done via Serial Console, Remote Registry, Remote Services Console, Run Commands, or rescue VM. After fixing, restart the VM or restart affected services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Service Failing Service Account_RDP SSH

symptom: Exchange Server VM on Azure shows red cross on NIC with no connectivity for ~40 minutes then recovers; or Exchange VM migrated via ASR has no NIC/disconnected NIC after failover; setupapi.dev.log shows netvsc driver install timeout 0x000005b4
rootCause: Two causes: (1) VM size too small for Exchange (minimum DS3/8GB RAM required), database/logs on OS drive cause heavy I/O starving network driver installation. (2) Exchange services and retry mechanisms interfere with netvsc driver installation during failover, causing timeout.
solution: Mitigation 1: Resize VM to minimum DS3, move Exchange database and logs to Premium Storage RAID, do not use OS drive. Mitigation 2 (ASR failover): Engage Azure Site Recovery team. Ref: Exchange Server virtualization requirements docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Isolation/Slow Startup on Exchange VM_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM fails to boot, screenshot shows: Remove disks or other media. Press any key to restart. Partition holding BCD Store is not active.
rootCause: The OS partition holding the BCD Store has been deactivated, so the boot process cannot locate the boot configuration data.
solution: OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to repair VM. Use diskpart to mark the correct partition as Active (select partition X -> active). Then detach and reattach to original VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Remove Disks or Other Media_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM fails to boot with error 0xC0000242 STATUS_BAD_COMPRESSION_BUFFER: The specified buffer contains ill-formed data.
rootCause: A file with particular content cannot decompress properly, causing File Replication Service (FRS) to fail. The corrupted/incompatible file prevents normal boot process.
solution: OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to repair VM. Recover the missing/corrupted system file using the recovery template (replace from known good source or Windows installation media). Reference: File Replication Service decompression failure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/STATUS BAD COMPRESSION BUFFER 0xC0000242_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM fails to boot with error 0xC0000098 on ACPI.sys after installing KB5058405. CBS.log shows mellanox driver failing with 0x800719e4 ERROR_LOG_FULL during driver uninstall rollback.
rootCause: Mellanox virtual ethernet adapter installation fails because multiple lingering Ghost NICs exist on the OS. The driver uninstall queue encounters ERROR_LOG_FULL, corrupting the ACPI.sys loading process during boot.
solution: Use VMRepair tool with win-remove-patch script to remove the rollup update: az vm repair create -> az vm repair run --run-id win-remove-patch --parameters packageName=Package_for_RollupFix~31bf3856ad364e35~amd64~~22621.5335.1.17 --run-on-repair -> az vm repair restore. OOB fix: KB5062170. See also Iridias message center #1086085.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/STATUS FILE INVALID 0xC0000098 ACPI_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure Windows VM fails to boot with error 0xC0000098 STATUS_FILE_INVALID referencing BCD store. Boot Configuration Data has missing or incomplete bootmgr entry (no default/displayorder).
rootCause: BCD corruption: the {bootmgr} entry is missing the default and displayorder properties, or the entire {bootmgr} entry is gone, so Windows Boot Manager cannot locate the Windows Boot Loader.
solution: OFFLINE: Attach OS disk to repair VM. bcdedit /store <drive>:\boot\bcd /enum to check. If {bootmgr} exists but missing default/displayorder: bcdedit /set {bootmgr} default {<BOOT_LOADER_ID>} and displayorder. If {bootmgr} entirely gone: bcdedit /create {bootmgr}, then set description, locale, inherit, displayorder, default, timeout. If still fails, recreate BCD store entirely.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/STATUS FILE INVALID 0xC0000098_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows OS shutdown with Stopping services message; VM stuck and unresponsive to RDP/SSH during shutdown process
rootCause: Windows shutdown process performing system maintenance (binary updates, role/feature changes). If interrupted, OS corruption is likely. A service in STOP_PENDING state is hanging the shutdown.
solution: Check STOP_PENDING services: Get-Service | Where-Object {$_.Status -eq 'STOP_PENDING'}. Get PID via tasklist /svc. Take memory dump with procdump -s 5 -n 3 -ma <PID>. Kill the hung process: taskkill /PID <PID> /t /f. After OS restarts, run dism /online /cleanup-image /restorehealth to verify OS consistency. If OS does not boot, refer to Non-Boot Scenario Bucket TSG. For RCA, upload dumps to DTM Workspace and engage Windows EE (GES).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Stopping Services_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows VM stuck on Hyper-V screen and not booting past the Hyper-V logo (Windows or Linux)
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) Windows bug check or guest OS issue preventing boot - identifiable via ASC screenshots and serial console. (2) Linux GRUB configuration issue. (3) Underlying host unable to provision resources for the VM.
solution: Take multiple screenshots via ASC to confirm not reboot loop. Check ASC Insights for boot errors. Review serial console. For Windows: try nested virtualization - if VM boots in nested-V, issue is platform-side (request Redeploy); if not, isolate to guest OS and search TSGs. For Linux: investigate GRUB. For host issues: engage PG via CRI. Disable autoreboot to see bugcheck screen.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Stuck on Hyper-V_RDP SSH

symptom: Linux VM cannot be reached via SSH; serial console shows only Enter username: prompt due to GRUB superuser password protection blocking boot
rootCause: GRUB superuser password protection is enabled (set before VHD upload to Azure or configured from Azure VM). Superuser requires console input to unlock GRUB, but Azure has no physical console access, so VM cannot boot past GRUB.
solution: Attach OS disk to a rescue Linux VM. Mount at /rescue. Edit /rescue/boot/grub/grub.cfg - comment out 'set superusers' and 'password_pbkdf2' lines at bottom. Also edit /rescue/etc/grub.d/40_custom and comment out the same lines. Unmount, detach disk, reassemble original VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/SuperUser Enable to Protect Grub_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows Windows setup error: The computer restarted unexpectedly or encountered an unexpected error during generalized image first boot (OOBE failure)
rootCause: First boot of a generalized (sysprepped) image fails to process the unattended answer file. Usually caused by customer using a custom unattended file which is not supported in Azure.
solution: Change support topic to: Product=Azure Virtual Machine Windows, Topic=Cannot create a VM > I am unable to deploy a captured or generalized image. Follow the UnattendXML TSG for resolution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/The Computer Restarted Unexpectedly_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows: This is not a bootable disk. Please insert a bootable floppy and press any key to try again (partition deactivated)
rootCause: The OS boot process could not locate an active system partition - the system partition has been deactivated.
solution: Cannot troubleshoot online (Guest OS not operational). Use OFFLINE approach: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Reactivate the system partition using diskpart (select partition, mark as active). Reassemble VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/This is not a Bootable Disk_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM shows This is not a bootable disk error due to BCD corruption with missing reference to Windows partition
rootCause: BCD (Boot Configuration Data) corruption - missing reference in the BCD store to locate the Windows partition. Can occur on existing VMs or newly migrated VMs lacking proper BCD entries.
solution: OFFLINE approach: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Rebuild BCD store using standard BCD repair commands (bootrec /rebuildbcd, bcdedit). For new VMs migrated to Azure, ensure BCD entries are prepared prior to migration. Reassemble VM after fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/This is not a Bootable Disk_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM in reboot loop; screenshots show boot process interrupted and restarting; Event ID 7007 Level:Error from Service Control Manager: The system reverted to its last known good configuration
rootCause: A third-party service flagged as critical is failing to start, causing OS to restart. Known culprits: RDAgent service (RD Bug 6855611) or AzureWLBackupCoordinatorSvc/AzureWLBackupInquirySvc/AzureWLBackupPluginSvc (WSSC Bug 3091343).
solution: Disable autoreboot first to see the actual bug check code/error. OFFLINE: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Identify the failing critical service. For RDAgent: apply fix per Bug 6855611. For AzureWLBackup services: apply fix per WSSC Bug 3091343. Use registry backup restore (Regback) if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/VM on a Reboot Loop_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM in reboot loop after OS change (KB update, application install, or new policy); or due to file system corruption
rootCause: OS changes (KB/application installation or policy change) or file system corruption causing boot failure loop. Corruption RCA is hard to identify - may be guest OS internal or Azure platform related.
solution: Disable autoreboot to see error. OFFLINE: check Event Logs, CBS.log, WindowsUpdate.log for changes. Uninstall problematic KB update if identified. Restore registry from backup (Regback). For filesystem corruption, investigate if caused by guest OS or platform. If platform suspected, raise CRI to RDOS EEE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/VM on a Reboot Loop_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows VMWare image customization is in progress message on every boot, delaying startup; VM migrated from VMware on-premises environment
rootCause: VMware Image Customization Initialization module is enabled on the VM (similar to Azure provisioning agent but for VMware). Runs a customized Sysprep on every boot. Common on VMs migrated from VMware on-premises environment.
solution: OFFLINE approach: attach OS disk to rescue VM. Disable VMware Customization module following VMware KB 1018378. If customer wants deeper RCA, they need to engage VMware support. If VM will not failback to on-prem, recommend disabling the customization module permanently.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/VMWare Image Customization is In Progress_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows 'Please wait for the Group Policy Client' - OS hangs waiting for Group Policy Client service to start, VM not booting
rootCause: Windows is processing and applying Group Policies. Many or complex policies can cause this process to take extended time, preventing the VM from completing boot.
solution: Wait up to 1 hour for Group Policy processing to complete. If VM is still stuck after 1 hour, collect a memory dump for root cause analysis via online hang scenario troubleshooting (backup OS disk, use run command or serial console). Escalate to Windows PG if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Wait for the Group Policy Client_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows 'Please wait for the Local Session Manager' - OS hangs waiting for Local Session Manager, VM not booting
rootCause: Local Session Manager service is stuck during startup. Root cause depends on memory dump analysis (could be driver conflict, service dependency deadlock, or resource contention).
solution: Use online hang scenario troubleshooting: backup OS disk, collect memory dump via run command or serial console for root cause analysis. Escalate to Windows PG with dump if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Wait for the Local Session Manager_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows 'Please wait for the TrustedInstaller' - OS hangs waiting for TrustedInstaller service, VM not booting
rootCause: TrustedInstaller (Windows Modules Installer) service is stuck during boot, typically related to pending Windows updates or servicing operations. Root cause depends on memory dump analysis.
solution: Use online hang scenario troubleshooting: backup OS disk, collect memory dump via run command or serial console. This scenario needs online mode only (no offline troubleshooting). Escalate to Windows PG with dump if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Wait for the TrustedInstaller_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows Windows Boot Manager menu waiting for user input to select boot partition - VM stuck at boot menu, not booting automatically
rootCause: VM BCD configuration has displaybootmenu enabled with a boot delay, causing VM to wait for user input to select boot partition. In Azure, users cannot interact with the console screen, so the VM hangs indefinitely at the boot menu.
solution: Disable BCD displaybootmenu flag. For CRP machines: set displaybootmenu to 'no' via run command or serial console. For Classic machines: remove the bootems/displaybootmenu flags. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshoot-guide-windows-boot-manager-menu
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Windows Boot Manager Menu_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows 'Windows could not complete the installation. To install Windows in this computer, restart the installation' - sysprep failure during first boot of generalized image
rootCause: Machine is performing first boot of a generalized image and failed to complete the sysprep process. The image is in UNDEPLOYABLE state and cannot be recovered. The generalization was incorrectly performed prior to migration.
solution: Image is unrecoverable. Customer must recreate the generalized image. Change support topic to 'Cannot create a VM > Unable to deploy a captured or generalized image'. Share article: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/sa-upload-generalized
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Windows Could not Complete the Installation_RDP SSH

symptom: Azure VM screenshot shows 'Windows could not finish configuring the system. To attempt to resume configuration, restart the computer' - sysprep failure during first boot of generalized image
rootCause: Machine is performing first boot of a generalized image and failed to complete the sysprep process. The image is in UNDEPLOYABLE state and cannot be recovered. The generalization was incorrectly performed prior to migration.
solution: Image is unrecoverable. Customer must recreate the generalized image. Change support topic to 'Cannot create a VM > Unable to deploy a captured or generalized image'. Share article: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/sa-upload-generalized
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureIaaSVM/AzureIaaSVM:/SME Topics/Cant RDP SSH/TSGs/Non boot/Windows Could not Finish Configuring the System_RDP SSH
